entries.english: This has been your business from the hour of birth: to catch people with the trap of love.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گير و آن مى‏هل ز دام / ‏وين دگر را صيد مى‏كن چون لئام
entries.english: From that pursuit and throng and vainglory and self existence will you get any warp or woof? Try and see!

entries.farsi: بيشتر رفته‏ست و بى‏گاه است روز / تو به جد در صيد خلقانى هنوز
entries.english: Most is gone and the day is late; you are still busy in pursuit of people.

entries.farsi: باز اين را مى‏هل و مى‏جو دگر / اينت لعب كودكان بى‏خبر
entries.english: Go on catching one and releasing another from the trap and pursuing another, like mean folk;

entries.farsi: شب شود در دام تو يك صيد نى / ‏دام بر تو جز صداع و قيد نى
entries.english: Then again release this one and seek the other! Here’s a game of heedless children!

entries.farsi: پس تو خود را صيد مى‏كردى به دام / ‏كه شدى محبوس و محرومى ز كام
entries.english: Night comes, and nothing is caught in your trap: the trap is naught but a headache and shackle to you.

entries.farsi: در زمانه صاحب دامى بود / همچو ما احمق كه صيد خود كند
entries.english: Therefore you were catching yourself with the trap, for you are imprisoned and disappointed of your desire.
Is any owner of a trap in the world such a dolt that, like us, he tries to catch himself?

entries.farsi: چون شكار خوك آمد صيد عام / ‏رنج بى‏حد لقمه خوردن زو حرام
entries.english: Pursuit of the vulgar is like hunting pig: the fatigue is infinite, and it is unlawful to eat a morsel thereof.

entries.farsi: آن كه ارزد صيد را عشق است و بس / ‏ليك او كى گنجد اندر دام كس
entries.english: That which is worth pursuing is Love alone; but how should He be contained in any one’s trap?

entries.farsi: تو مگر آيى و صيد او شوى / ‏دام بگذارى به دام او روى
entries.english: 410. Maybe you may come and be made His prey; you may discard the trap, and go into His trap.

entries.farsi: عشق مى‏گويد به گوشم پست پست / صيد بودن خوشتر از صيادى است
entries.english: Love is saying very softly into my ear, “To be a prey is better than to be a hunter.

entries.farsi: گول من كن خويش را و غره شو / آفتابى را رها كن ذره شو
entries.english: Make yourself My fool and be a dupe: renounce the estate of the sun, become a speck!

entries.farsi: بر درم ساكن شو و بى‏خانه باش / ‏دعوى شمعى مكن پروانه باش
entries.english: Become a dweller at My door and be homeless: do not pretend to be a candle, be a moth,

entries.farsi: تا ببينى چاشنى زندگى / ‏سلطنت بينى نهان در بندگى
entries.english: That you may taste the savour of Life and contemplate the sovereignty hidden in servitude.”

entries.farsi: نعل بينى باژگونه در جهان / ‏تخته بندان را لقب گشته شهان
entries.english: 415. In this world you see the shoes upside down: the title of “kings” is conferred on bondsmen.

entries.farsi: بس طناب اندر گلو و تاج دار / بر وى انبوهى كه اينك تاجدار
entries.english: Many a one who deserves to mount the scaffold with a halter on his throat—a crowd round him, crying, “Behold, an emperor!”

entries.farsi: همچو گور كافران بيرون حلل / ‏اندرون قهر خدا عز و جل
entries.english: Like the tombs of infidels, outwardly the robes of Paradise, within is the wrath of God Almighty and Glorious.

entries.farsi: چون قبور آن را مجصص كرده‏اند / پرده‏ى پندار پيش آورده‏اند
entries.english: He has been plastered like the tombs: the veil of self-conceit has been brought before him.

entries.farsi: طبع مسكينت مجصص از هنر / همچو نخل موم بى‏برگ و ثمر
entries.english: Your miserable nature is plastered with virtues, like a palm-tree of wax without leaves and fruit.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه لطف حق را همه كس داند و قهر حق را همه كس داند و همه از قهر حق گريزانند و ،به لطف حق در آويزان اما حق تعالى قهرها را در لطف پنهان كرد و لطفها را در قهر پنهان كرد نعل باژگونه و تلبيس و مكر اللهَّ بود تا اهل تمييز و ينظر بنور اللهَّ از حالى بينان و ظاهر بينان جدا شوند كه لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْ سَ نُ عَ مَل

sections.title_en: Explaining that everyone knows the mercy of God, and everyone knows the wrath of God; and all are fleeing from the wrath of God and clinging to the mercy of God; but the Most High God has concealed wraths in mercy and mercies in wrath. This is God’s mystification and disguise and contrivance to the end that the discerning who see
by the Light of God may be separated from those who see the present and the visible; for that He might try you, which of you is most righteous in his works

entries.farsi: گفت درويشى به درويشى كه تو / چون بديدى حضرت حق را بگو
entries.english: One dervish said to another, “Tell, what was your vision of the Presence of God?”

entries.farsi: گفت بى‏چون ديدم اما بهر قال / ‏باز گويم مختصر آن را مثال
entries.english: He replied, “My vision was ineffable; but for the sake of argument I will briefly declare a parable thereof.

entries.farsi: ديدمش سوى چپ او آذرى / ‏سوى دست راست جوى كوثرى
entries.english: I beheld Him with a fire on His left, and on the right a stream like Kawthar:

entries.farsi: سوى آن آتش گروهى برده دست / ‏بهر آن كوثر گروهى شاد و مست
entries.english: On His left an exceedingly world-consuming fire, on His right hand a sweet river.

entries.farsi: سوى چپش بس جهان سوز آتشى / ‏سوى دست راستش جوى خوشى
entries.english: One party put forth their hands towards the fire, another party was rejoicing and intoxicated for that Kawthar.

entries.farsi: ليك لعب باژگونه بود سخت / ‏پيش پاى هر شقى و نيك بخت
entries.english: But it was a very topsy-turvy game in the path of every one doomed to perdition or blessed with salvation.

entries.farsi: هر كه در آتش همى‏رفت و شرر / از ميان آب بر مى‏كرد سر
entries.english: Whoever went into the fire and sparks was emerging from the midst of the water;

entries.farsi: هر كه سوى آب مى‏رفت از ميان / ‏او در آتش يافت مى‏شد در زمان
entries.english: Whoever went from the middle towards the water, he was at once found to be in the fire;

entries.farsi: هر كه سوى راست شد و آب زالل / ‏سر ز آتش بر زد از سوى شمال
entries.english: Whoever went towards the right and the limpid water would put forth his head from the fire on the left;

entries.farsi: و انكه شد سوى شمال آتشين / ‏سر برون مى‏كرد از سوى يمين
entries.english: And he who went towards the fiery left, would emerge on the right.

entries.farsi: جز كسى كه بر سرش اقبال ريخت / ‏كاو رها كرد آب و در آتش گريخت
entries.english: Few were they who hit upon the mystery of this occult matter; consequently, seldom would any one go into the fire;

entries.farsi: كم كسى بر سر اين مضمر زدى / ‏الجرم كم كس در آن آتش شدى
entries.english: Except him upon whom felicity was shed, so that he abandoned the water and took refuge in the fire.

entries.farsi: كرده ذوق نقد را معبود خلق / ‏الجرم زين لعب مغبون بود خلق
entries.english: The people made the pleasure that was actually present their object of worship; consequently the people were swindled by this game.

entries.farsi: جوق جوق وصف صف از حرص و شتاب / ‏محترز ز آتش گريزان سوى آب
entries.english: Troop by troop and rank by rank, on their guard against the fire and fleeing greedily and in haste towards the water

entries.farsi: الجرم ز آتش بر آوردند سر / اعتبار الاعتبار اى بى‏خبر
entries.english: Of necessity, they lifted up their heads from the fire. Take warning; take warning, O heedless man!

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏زد آتش اى گيجان گول / ‏من نى‏ام آتش منم چشمه‏ى قبول
entries.english: The fire was crying, ‘O crazy fools, I am not fire, I am a delectable fountain.

entries.farsi: چشم بندى كرده‏اند اى بى‏نظر / در من آى و هيچ مگريز از شرر
entries.english: A spell has been cast on your eyes, O sightless one: come into me and never flee from the sparks.

entries.farsi: اى خليل اينجا شرار و دود نيست / ‏جز كه سحر و خدعه نمرود نيست
entries.english: O Khalíl, here are no sparks and smoke: it is nothing but the sorcery and deceit of Nimrod.

entries.farsi: چون خليل حق اگر فرزانه‏اى / ‏آتش آب تست و تو پروانه‏اى
entries.english: If, like the Friend of God, you are wise, the fire is your water, and you are the moth.’”

entries.farsi: جان پروانه همى‏دارد ندى / ‏كاى دريغا صد هزارم پر بدى
entries.english: The soul of the moth is always crying, “Oh, alas, would that I had a hundred thousand wings,

entries.farsi: بر من آرد رحم جاهل از خرى / ‏من بر او رحم آرم از بينش‏ورى
entries.english: That they might be consumed without mercy by the fire, to the blindness of the eyes and hearts of the profane!

entries.farsi: تا همى‏سوزيد ز آتش بى‏امان / ‏كورى چشم و دل نامحرمان
entries.english: The ignorant man pities me from stupidity: I pity him from clairvoyance.

entries.farsi: خاصه اين آتش كه جان آبهاست / ‏كار پروانه بعكس كار ماست
entries.english: Especially this fire, which is the soul of waters; the behaviour of the moth is contrary to ours.

entries.farsi: او ببيند نور و در نارى رود / دل ببيند نار و در نورى شود
entries.english: It sees the light and goes into a Fire; the heart sees the fire and goes into a Light.”

entries.farsi: اين چنين لعب آمد از رب جليل / تا ببينى كيست از آل خليل
entries.english: Such a game is played by the Glorious God in order that you may see who belongs to the kin of Khalíl.

entries.farsi: آتشى را شكل آبى داده‏اند / و اندر آتش چشمه‏اى بگشاده‏اند
entries.english: A fire has been given the semblance of water, and in the fire a fountain has been opened.

entries.farsi: ساحرى صحن برنجى را به فن / ‏صحن پر كرمى كند در انجمن
entries.english: A magician by his art makes a dish of rice a dish full of worms in the assembly;

entries.farsi: خانه را او پر ز كژدمها نمود / از دم سحر و خود آن كژدم نبود
entries.english: by the breath of magic he has caused a room to appear full of scorpions, though in truth there were no scorpions.

entries.farsi: چون كه جادو مى‏نمايد صد چنين / ‏چون بود دستان جادو آفرين
entries.english: When sorcery produces a hundred such illusions, how must be the cunning of the Creator of sorcery?

entries.farsi: الجرم از سحر يزدان قرن قرن / ‏اندر افتادند چون زن زير پهن
entries.english: Of necessity, through the magic of God generation after generation have fallen down, like a woman flat beneath her husband.

entries.farsi: ساحرانشان بنده بودند و غالم / ‏اندر افتادند چون صعوه به دام
entries.english: Their magicians were slaves and servants, and fell into the trap like wagtails.

entries.farsi: هين بخوان قرآن ببين سحر حالل / ‏سر نگونى مكرهاى كالجبال
entries.english: Listen, read the Qur’an and behold lawful magic the overthrow of plots as the mountains.

entries.farsi: من نى‏ام فرعون كايم سوى نيل / ‏سوى آتش مى‏روم من چون خليل
entries.english: “I am not Pharaoh that I should come to the Nile; I am going towards the fire, like Khalíl.

entries.farsi: نيست آتش هست آن ماء معين / ‏و آن دگر از مكر آب آتشين
entries.english: It is not fire; it is flowing water, the other, through cunning, is water whereof the nature is fire.

entries.farsi: بس نكو گفت آن رسول خوش جواز / ذره‏اى عقلت به از صوم و نماز
entries.english: Excellently well said the complaisant Prophet, “A mote of intelligence is better for you than fasting and performing the ritual prayer,”

entries.farsi: ز انكه عقلت جوهر است اين دو عرض / ‏اين دو در تكميل آن شد مفترض
entries.english: Because your intelligence is the substance, these two are accidents: these two are made obligatory in the full complement of it,

entries.farsi: تا جال باشد مر آن آيينه را / كه صفا آيد ز طاعت سينه را
entries.english: In order that the mirror may have lustre; for purity comes to the breast from piety.

entries.farsi: ليك گر آيينه از بن فاسد است / ‏صيقل او را دير باز آرد به دست
entries.english: But if the mirror is fundamentally depraved, after a long time does the polisher get it back;

entries.farsi: و آن گزين آيينه كه خوش مغرس است / ‏اندكى صيقل‏گرى آن را بس است
entries.english: While the fine mirror, which is a goodly planting-ground, a little polishing is enough for it.

sections.title_fa: تفاوت عقول در اصل فطرت خالف معتزله كه ايشان گويند در اصل عقول جزوى برابرند اين فزونى ‏و تفاوت از تعلم است و رياضت و تجربه
sections.title_en: The diversity of intelligences in their nature as originally created; opposed to the Mu‘tazilites, who assert that particular intelligences are originally equal, and that this superiority and diversity is the result of learning and training and experience.

entries.farsi: اين تفاوت عقلها را نيك دان / ‏در مراتب از زمين تا آسمان
entries.english: Know well that intelligences differ thus in degree from the earth to the sky.

entries.farsi: هست عقلى همچو قرص آفتاب / ‏هست عقلى كمتر از زهره و شهاب
entries.english: There is intelligence like the orb of the sun; there is an intelligence inferior to Venus and the meteor.

entries.farsi: هست عقلى چون چراغى سر خوشى / ‏هست عقلى چون ستاره‏ى آتشى
entries.english: There is intelligence like a tipsy lamp; there is intelligence like a star of fire,

entries.farsi: ز انكه ابر از پيش آن چون وا جهد / نور يزدان بين خردها بر دهد
entries.english: Because, when the cloud is removed from it, it produces intellects that behold the Light of God.

entries.farsi: آن ز خدمت ناز مخدومى بيافت / ‏وين ز مخدومى ز راه عز بتافت
entries.english: The particular intelligence has given the intelligence a bad name: worldly desire has deprived the man of his desire.

entries.farsi: آن ز صيدى حسن صيادى بديد / وين ز صيادى غم صيدى كشيد
entries.english: That, through being a prey, beheld the beauty of the Hunting, while this, through being a hunter , suffered the pain of being a prey.

entries.farsi: عقل جزوى عقل را بد نام كرد / كام دنيا مرد را بى‏كام كرد
entries.english: The former, through service, gained the pride of lordship, while the latter, through lordship, turned from the path of glory.

entries.farsi: آن ز فرعونى اسير آب شد / وز اسيرى سبط صد سهراب شد
entries.english: The latter, through being a Pharaoh, was taken captive by the water, while the Israelites, through captivity, became a hundred Suhrabs.

entries.farsi: لعب معكوس است و فرزين بند سخت / ‏حيله كم كن كار اقبال است و بخت
entries.english: It is a topsy-turvy game and a terrible quandary; do not try cunning: it is a matter of favour and fortune.

entries.farsi: بر خيال و حيله كم تن تار را / كه غنى ره كم دهد مكار را
entries.english: Do not weave plots in vain imagination and cunning; for the Self-sufficient One does not give way to the contriver.

entries.farsi: مكر كن در راه نيكو خدمتى / ‏تا نبوت يابى اندر امتى
entries.english: Contrive, in the way of one who serves God well, that you may gain the position of a prophet in a religious community.

entries.farsi: مكر كن تا وارهى از مكر خود / مكر كن تا فرد گردى از جسد
entries.english: Contrive that you may be delivered from your own contrivance; contrive that you may become detached from the body.

entries.farsi: مكر كن تا كمترين بنده شوى / ‏در كمى رفتى خداونده شوى
entries.english: Contrive that you may become the meanest slave: if you enter into meanness, you will become lordly.

entries.farsi: روبهى و خدمت اى گرگ كهن / ‏هيچ بر قصد خداوندى مكن
entries.english: Never, O old wolf, practise foxiness and perform service with the purpose of lordship;

entries.farsi: ليك چون پروانه در آتش بتاز / كيسه‏اى ز آن بر مدوز و پاك باز
entries.english: But rush into the fire like a moth: do not hoard up that, play for love!

entries.farsi: زور را بگذار و زارى را بگير / رحم سوى زارى آيد اى فقير
entries.english: Renounce power and adopt piteous supplication: mercy comes towards piteous supplication, O dervish.

entries.farsi: زارى مضطر تشنه معنوى است / ‏زارى سرد دروغ آن غوى است
entries.english: The piteous supplication of one sorely distressed and athirst is real; the piteous cold supplication of falsehood is proper to the miscreant.

entries.farsi: گريه‏ى اخوان يوسف حيلت است / ‏كه درونشان پر ز رشك و علت است
entries.english: The weeping of Joseph’s brethren is a trick, for their hearts are full of envy and infirmity.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن اعرابى كه سگ او از گرسنگى مى‏مرد و انبان او پر نان بود و بر سگ نوحه مى‏كرد و ‏شعر مى‏گفت و مى‏گريست و بر سر و رو مى‏زد و دريغش مى‏آمد لقمه اى از انبان به سگ دادن
sections.title_en: Story of the Arab of the desert whose dog was dying of hunger, while his wallet was full of bread;
he was lamenting over the dog and reciting poetry and sobbing and beating his head and face; and yet he grudged the dog a morsel from his wallet.

entries.farsi: آن سگى مى‏مرد و گريان آن عرب / ‏اشك مى‏باريد و مى‏گفت اى كرب
entries.english: The dog was dying, and the Arab sobbing, shedding tears, and crying, “Oh, sorrow!”

entries.farsi: سائلى بگذشت و گفت اين گريه چيست / ‏نوحه و زارى تو از بهر كيست
entries.english: A beggar passed by and asked, “What is this sobbing? For whom are you mourning and lamenting?”

entries.farsi: روز صيادم بد و شب پاسبان / ‏تيز چشم و صيد گير و دزدران
entries.english: He replied, “There was in my possession a dog of excellent disposition. Look, he is dying on the road.

entries.farsi: گفت در ملكم سگى بد نيك خو / نك همى‏ميرد ميان راه او
entries.english: He hunted for me by day and kept watch by night; keen-eyed and catching the prey and driving off thieves”

entries.farsi: گفت رنجش چيست زخمى خورده است / ‏گفت جوع الكلب زارش كرده است
entries.english: He asked, “What ails him? Has he been wounded?” The Arab replied, “Ravenous hunger has made him lamentable.”

entries.farsi: گفت صبرى كن بر اين رنج و حرض / صابران را فضل حق بخشد عوض
entries.english: “Show some patience,” said he, “in this pain and anguish: the grace of God bestows a recompense on those who are patient.”

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش كه اى سالار حر / چيست اندر دستت اين انبان پر
entries.english: Afterwards he said to him, “O noble chief, what is this full wallet in your hand?”

entries.farsi: گفت نان و زاد و لوت دوش من / ‏مى‏كشانم بهر تقويت بدن
entries.english: He replied, “My bread and provender and food left over from last night, I am taking along to nourish my body.”

entries.farsi: گفت چون ندهى بدان سگ نان و زاد / گفت تا اين حد ندارم مهر و داد
entries.english: “Why don’t you give bread and food to the dog?” he asked. He replied, “I have not love and liberality to this extent.

entries.farsi: دست نايد بى‏درم در راه نان / ‏ليك هست آب دو ديده رايگان
entries.english: Bread cannot be obtained on the road without money, but water from the eyes costs nothing.”

entries.farsi: گفت خاكت بر سر اى پر باد مشك / ‏كه لب نان پيش تو بهتر ز اشك
entries.english: He said, “Earth be on your head, O water-skin full of wind! For in your opinion a crust of bread is better than tears.”

entries.farsi: اشك خون است و به غم آبى شده / ‏مى‏نيرزد خاك خون بى‏هده
entries.english: Tears are blood and have been turned by grief into water: idle tears have not the value of earth.

entries.farsi: كل خود را خوار كرد او چون بليس / ‏پاره‏ى اين كل نباشد جز خسيس
entries.english: He made the whole of himself despicable, like Iblís: a piece of this whole is naught but vile.

entries.farsi: من غالم آن كه نفروشد وجود / جز بدان سلطان با افضال و جود
entries.english: I am the slave of him who will not sell his existence save to that bounteous and munificent Sovereign,

entries.farsi: چون بگريد آسمان گريان شود / چون بنالد چرخ يا رب خوان شود
entries.english: When he weeps, heaven begins to weep, and when he moans, the celestial sphere begins to cry, “O Lord!”

entries.farsi: من غالم آن مس همت پرست / ‏كاو به غير كيميا نارد شكست
entries.english: I am the slave of that high-aspiring copper which humbles itself to naught but the Elixir.

entries.farsi: دست اشكسته بر آور در دعا / سوى اشكسته پرد فضل خدا
entries.english: Lift up in prayer a broken hand: the loving kindness of God flies towards the broken.

entries.farsi: گر رهايى بايدت زين چاه تنگ / ‏اى برادر رو بر آذر بى‏درنگ
entries.english: If you have need of deliverance from this narrow dungeon, O brother, go without delay on the fire.

entries.farsi: مكر حق را بين و مكر خود بهل / ‏اى ز مكرش مكر مكاران خجل
entries.english: Regard God’s contrivance and abandon your own contrivance: oh, by His contrivance the contrivance of contrivers is put to shame.

entries.farsi: چون كه مكرت شد فناى مكر رب / ‏بر گشايى يك كمينى بو العجب
entries.english: When your contrivance is negated in the contrivance of the Lord, you will open a most marvellous hiding-place,

entries.farsi: كه كمينه‏ى آن كمين باشد بقا / تا ابد اندر عروج و ارتقا
entries.english: Of which hiding-place the least is everlasting life in ascending and mounting higher.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه هيچ چشم بدى آدمى را چنان مهلك نيست كه چشم پسند خويشتن مگر كه چشم او ‏مبدل شده باشد به نور حق كه بى‏يسمع و بى‏يبصر و خويشتن او بى‏خويشتن شده
sections.title_en: Explaining that no evil eye is so deadly to a man as the eye of self-approval,
unless his eye shall have been transformed by the Light of God, so that “he hears through Me and sees through Me,” and his self shall have become selfless.

entries.farsi: پر طاوست مبين و پاى بين / ‏تا كه سوء العين نگشايد كمين
entries.english: Do not regard your peacock-feathers but regard your feet, in order that the mischief of the eye may not waylay you;

entries.farsi: كه به لغزد كوه از چشم بدان / ‏يزلقونك از نبى بر خوان بدان
entries.english: For a mountain slips at the eye of the wicked: read and mark in the Qur’an they cause you to stumble

entries.farsi: احمد چون كوه لغزيد از نظر / در ميان راه بى‏گل بى‏مطر
entries.english: From looking, Ahmad, like a mountain, slipped in the middle of the road, without mud and without rain.

entries.farsi: در عجب درماند كاين لغزش ز چيست / ‏من نپندارم كه اين حالت تهى است
entries.english: He remained in astonishment, saying, “Why this slipping? I do not think that this occurrence is empty,”

entries.farsi: تا بيامد آيت و آگاه كرد / كان ز چشم بد رسيدت وز نبرد
entries.english: Until the Verse came and made him aware that this had happened to him in consequence of the evil eye and enmity.

entries.farsi: گر بدى غير تو در دم ال شدى / ‏صيد چشم و سخره‏ى افنا شدى
entries.english: “Had it been anyone except you, he would at once have been annihilated: he would have become the prey of the eye and in thrall to destruction;

entries.farsi: ليك آمد عصمتى دامن كشان / ‏وين كه لغزيدى بد از بهر نشان
entries.english: But there came a protection, sweeping along, and your slipping was for a sign.”

entries.farsi: عبرتى گير اندر آن كه كن نگاه / ‏برگ خود عرضه مكن اى كم ز كاه
entries.english: Take a warning, look on that mountain, and do not expose your leaf, O you who are less than a straw.

sections.title_fa: ْتفسير وَ إِنْ يَكادُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ بِأَبْصارِهِم
sections.title_en: Commentary on “And truly those who disbelieve nearly cause you to slip by their eyes.”

entries.farsi: يا رسول اللهَّ در آن نادى كسان / مى‏زنند از چشم بد بر كركسان
entries.english: “O Messenger of Allah, some persons in that assembly smites with their eye the vultures.

entries.farsi: از نظرشان كله‏ى شير عرين / ‏واشكافد تا كند آن شير انين
entries.english: By their looks the head of the lion of the jungle is cloven asunder, so that the lion makes moan.

entries.farsi: بر شتر چشم افكند همچون حمام / ‏و آنگهان بفرستد اندر پى غالم
entries.english: He casts on a camel an eye like death, and then sends a slave after it,

entries.farsi: كه برو از پيه اين اشتر بخر / بيند اشتر را سقط او راه بر
entries.english: Saying, ‘Go, and buy some of the fat of this camel’: he sees the camel fallen dead on the road.

entries.farsi: سر بريده از مرض آن اشترى / ‏كاو بتك با اسب مى‏كردى مرى
entries.english: Mortally stricken by disease the camel that used to vie with a horse in speed;

entries.farsi: كز حسد وز چشم بد بى‏هيچ شك / ‏سير و گردش را بگرداند فلك
entries.english: For, without any doubt, from envy and the evil eye the celestial sphere would alter its course and revolution.”

entries.farsi: آب پنهان است و دوالب آشكار / ليك در گردش بود آب اصل كار
entries.english: The water is hidden and the water-wheel is visible, yet as regards revolution the water is the source of action.

entries.farsi: چشم نيكو شد دواى چشم بد / چشم بد را ال كند زير لگد
entries.english: The remedy of the evil eye is the good eye: it makes the evil eye naught beneath its kick.

entries.farsi: سبق رحمت راست و او از رحمت است / ‏چشم بد محصول قهر و لعنت است
entries.english: Mercy has the precedence: The good eye is from mercy, the evil eye is the product of wrath and a curse.

entries.farsi: رحمتش بر نقمتش غالب شود / چيره زين شد هر نبى بر ضد خود
entries.english: His mercy overcomes His vengeance: hence every prophet prevailed over his adversary;

entries.farsi: حرص بط يك تاست اين پنجاه تاست / ‏حرص شهوت مار و منصب اژدهاست
entries.english: For he is the result of mercy and is the opposite of him: that ill-favoured one was the result of wrath.

entries.farsi: كاو نتيجه‏ى رحمت است و ضد او / از نتيجه‏ى قهر بود آن زشت رو
entries.english: The greed of the duck is single, this is fiftyfold: the greed of lust is a snake, while this eminence is a dragon.

entries.farsi: حرص بط از شهوت حلق است و فرج / ‏در رياست بيست چندان است درج
entries.english: The duck’s greed arises from the appetite of the stomach and genitals; twenty times as much greed is included in ruling.

entries.farsi: از الوهيت زند در جاه الف / ‏طامع شركت كجا باشد معاف
entries.english: He in power pretends to Divinity: how should one ambitious of co-partnership be saved?

entries.farsi: زلت آدم ز اشكم بود و باه / ‏و آن ابليس از تكبر بود و جاه
entries.english: The sin of Adam arose from the belly and sexual intercourse, and that of Iblís from pride and power.

entries.farsi: ال جرم او زود استغفار كرد / و آن لعين از توبه استكبار كرد
entries.english: Consequently, he at once besought pardon, while the accursed disdained to repent.

entries.farsi: حرص حلق و فرج هم خود بد رگى است / ‏ليك منصب نيست آن اشكستگى است
entries.english: The greed of the gullet and pudendum is in truth depravity; but it is not ambition: it is abasement.

entries.farsi: بيخ و شاخ اين رياست را اگر / باز گويم دفترى بايد دگر
entries.english: If I should relate the root and branch of dominion, another Book would be needed.

entries.farsi: اسب سركش را عرب شيطانش خواند / نى ستورى را كه در مرعى بماند
entries.english: The Arabs called a restive horse a “devil” (shaytán); not the beast of burden that stayed in the pasture.

entries.farsi: شيطنت گردن كشى بد در لغت / ‏مستحق لعنت آمد اين صفت
entries.english: “Devilry” (shaytanat) in lexicology is “rebelliousness”: this quality is deserving of a curse.

entries.farsi: صد خورنده گنجد اندر گرد خوان / ‏دو رياست جو نگنجد در جهان
entries.english: There is room for a hundred eaters round a table, there is not room in the world for two seekers of dominion.

entries.farsi: آن نخواهد كاين بود بر پشت خاك / تا ملك بكشد پدر را ز اشتراك
entries.english: The one is not willing that the other should be on the surface of the earth; so that a prince kills his father for partaking with him.

entries.farsi: آن شنيدستى كه الملك عقيم / ‏قطع خويشى كرد ملكت جو ز بيم
entries.english: You have heard that kingship is childless: the seeker of sovereignty has cut relationship because of fear;

entries.farsi: كه عقيم است و و را فرزند نيست / ‏همچو آتش با كسش پيوند نيست
entries.english: For he is childless and has no son: like fire, he has no kinship with anyone.

entries.farsi: هر چه يابد او بسوزد بر درد / چون نيابد هيچ خود را مى‏خورد
entries.english: Whatsoever he finds he destroys and tears to pieces: when he finds nothing, he devours himself.

entries.farsi: هيچ شو واره تو از دندان او / رحم كم جو از دل سندان او
entries.english: Become naught, escape from his teeth: do not seek mercy from his anvil like heart.

entries.farsi: چون كه گشتى هيچ از سندان مترس / ‏هر صباح از فقر مطلق گير درس
entries.english: After you have become naught, do not fear the anvil: take lessons every morning from absolute poverty.

entries.farsi: هست الوهيت رداى ذو الجلال / ‏هر كه در پوشد بر او گردد وبال
entries.english: Divinity is the mantle of the Lord of glory: it becomes a plague to anyone who puts it on.

entries.farsi: اج از آن اوست آن ما كمر / واى او كز حد خود دارد گذر
entries.english: His is the crown, ours the belt: woe to him that passes beyond his proper bound!

entries.farsi: فتنه‏ى تست اين پر طاوسيت / ‏كه اشتراكت بايد و قدوسيت
entries.english: Your peacock-feathers are a temptation to you, for you must needs have co-partnership and All-holiness.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن حكيم كه ديد طاوسى را كه پر زيباى خود را مى‏كند به منقار و مى‏انداخت و تن خود را كل و زشت مى‏كرد از تعجب پرسيد كه دريغت نمى‏آيد گفت مى‏آيد اما پيش من جان از پر عزيزتر است و ‏اين عدوى جان من است
sections.title_en: Story of the Sage who saw a peacock tearing out his handsome feathers with his beak

and dropping them and making himself bald and ugly. In astonishment he asked, “Have you no feeling of regret?” “I have,” said the peacock, “but life is dearer to me than feathers, and these are the enemy of my life.”

entries.farsi: پر خود مى‏كند طاوسى به دشت / ‏يك حكيمى رفته بود آن جا به گشت
entries.english: A peacock was tearing out his feathers in the open country, where a sage had gone for a walk.

entries.farsi: گفت طاوسا چنين پر سنى / ‏بى‏دريغ از بيخ چون بر مى‏كنى
entries.english: He said, “O peacock, how are you tearing out such fine feathers remorselessly from the root?

entries.farsi: خود دلت چون مى‏دهد تا اين حلل / ‏بر كنى اندازى‏اش اندر وحل
entries.english: How indeed is your heart consenting that you should tear off these gorgeous robes and let them fall in the mud?

entries.farsi: هر پرت را از عزيزى و پسند / حافظان در طى مصحف مى‏نهند
entries.english: Those who commit the Qur’an to memory place every feather of yours, on account of its being prized and acceptable, within the folding of the Book.

entries.farsi: بهر تحريك هواى سودمند / از پر تو باد بيزن مى‏كنند
entries.english: For the sake of stirring the healthful air your feathers are used as fans.

entries.farsi: اين چه ناشكرى و چه بى‏باكى است / ‏تو نمى‏دانى كه نقاشش كى است
entries.english: What ingratitude and what recklessness is this! Don’t you know who the decorator is?

entries.farsi: يا همى‏دانى و نازى مى‏كنى / اى بسا نازا كه گردد آن گناه
entries.english: Or do you know and are you showing disdain and purposely tearing out broidery?

entries.farsi: ‏افكند مر بنده را از چشم شاه / ناز كردن خوشتر آيد از شكر
entries.english: Oh, there is many a disdain that becomes a sin and causes the servant to fall from favour with the King.

entries.farsi: ليك كم خايش كه دارد صد خطر / ايمن آباد است آن راه نياز
entries.english: To show disdain is sweeter than sugar; but chew it not, for it has a hundred perils.

entries.farsi: ترك نازش گير و با آن ره بساز / اى بسا ناز آورى زد پر و بال
entries.english: The place of safety is the way of want: abandon disdain and make up with that way.

entries.farsi: ‏آخر الامر آن بر آن كس شد وبال / خوشى ناز ار دمى بفرازدت
entries.english: Oh, many a disdainfulness flapped its wings and plumes, in the end it became a bane to that person.

entries.farsi: ‏بيم و ترس مضمرش بگدازدت / وين نياز ار چه كه الغر مى‏كند
entries.english: If the sweetness of disdain exalts you for a moment, its latent fear and dread consumes you;

entries.farsi: صدر را چون بدر انور مى‏كند / چون ز مرده زنده بيرون مى‏كشد
entries.english: This want, though it make lean, will make your breast like the brilliant full-moon.

entries.farsi: هر كه مرده گشت او دارد رشد / چون ز زنده مرده بيرون مى‏كند
entries.english: Since He draws forth the living from the dead, he that has become dead keeps the right course;

entries.farsi: نفس زنده سوى مرگى مى‏تند / مرده شو تا مخرج الحى الصمد
entries.english: Since He brings forth the dead from the living, the living soul moves towards a state of death.

entries.farsi: زنده‏اى زين مرده بيرون آورد / دى شوى بينى تو اخراج بهار
entries.english: Become dead, that the Lord who brings forth the living may bring forth a living one from this dead one.

entries.farsi: روى مخراش از عزا اى خوب رو / آن چنان رويى كه چون شمس ضحاست
entries.english: You become December, you will experience the bringing forth of Spring; you become night, you will experience the advent of day.

entries.farsi: ليل گردى بينى ايالج نهار / بر مكن آن پر كه نپذيرد رفو
entries.english: Do not tear out your feathers, for it is irreparable: do not cut your face in grief, O beauteous one.

entries.farsi: ‏آن چنان رخ را خراشيدن خطاست / زخم ناخن بر چنان رخ كافرى است
entries.english: Such a face that resembles the morning sun— it is sinful to tear a countenance like that.

entries.farsi: ‏كه رخ مه در فراق او گريست / يا نمى‏بينى تو روى خويش را
entries.english: It is infidelity scratches upon a countenance such that the moon’s countenance wept at parting from it.

entries.farsi: ترك كن خوى لجاج انديش را / روى نفس مطمئنه در جسد
entries.english: Or do not you see your face? Abandon that rebellious disposition.”

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه صفا و سادگى نفس مطمئنه از فكرت‏ها مشوش شود چنان كه بر روى آينه چيزى ‏نويسى يا نقش كنى اگر چه پاك كنى داغى بماند و نقصانى
sections.title_en: Explaining that the purity and simplicity of the tranquil soul are disturbed by thoughts, just as you write or depict anything on the surface of a mirror, though you may obliterate it entirely,
a mark and blemish will remain.

entries.farsi: زخم ناخنهاى فكرت مى‏كشد / فكرت بد ناخن پر زهر دان
entries.english: The face of the tranquil soul in the body suffers wounds inflicted by the nails of thought.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏خراشد در تعمق روى جان / تا گشايد عقده‏ى اشكال را
entries.english: Know that evil thought is a poisonous nail: in deep reflection it rends the face of the soul.

entries.farsi: در حدث كردست زرين بيل را / عقده را بگشاده گير اى منتهى
entries.english: In order that he may untie the knot of a difficulty, he has put a golden spade into ordure.

entries.farsi: عقده‏ى چندى دگر بگشاده گير / عقده‏اى كان بر گلوى ماست سخت
entries.english: Suppose the knot is loosed, O adept: it is a tight knot on an empty purse.

entries.farsi: ‏عقده‏ى سخت است بر كيسه‏ى تهى / در گشاد عقده‏ها گشتى تو پير
entries.english: You have grown old in loosing knots: suppose a few more knots are loosed.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بدانى كه خسى يا نيك بخت / حل اين اشكال كن گر آدمى
entries.english: The knot that is tight on our throat is that you should know whether you are vile or fortunate.

entries.farsi: ‏خرج اين كن دم اگر آدم دمى / حد اعيان و عرض دانسته گير
entries.english: Solve this problem, if you are a man: spend your breath on this, if you have the breath of Adam.

entries.farsi: حد خود را دان كه نبود زين گزير / چون بدانى حد خود زين حد گريز
entries.english: Suppose you know the definitions of substances and accidents, know the definition of yourself, for this is indispensable.

entries.farsi: تا به بى‏حد در رسى اى خاك بيز / عمر در محمول و در موضوع رفت
entries.english: When you know the definition of yourself, flee from this definition that you may attain to Him who has no definition O sifter of dust.

entries.farsi: باطل آمد در نتيجه‏ى خود نگر / جز به مصنوعى نديدى صانعى
entries.english: Life has gone in predicate and subject: life, devoid of insight, has gone in what has been received by hearsay.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏بصيرت عمر در مسموع رفت / هر دليلى بى‏نتيجه و بى‏اثر
entries.english: Every proof without result and effect is vain: consider the result of yourself!

entries.farsi: ‏از داليل باز بر عكسش صفى / اين گريزد از دليل و از حجاب
entries.english: You have never perceived a Maker except by means of a thing made: you are content with a syllogism.

entries.farsi: ‏بر قياس اقترانى قانعى / مى‏فزايد در وسايط فلسفى
entries.english: The philosopher multiplies links of proofs; on the other hand, the elect is contrary to him.

entries.farsi: ‏از پى مدلول سر برده به جيب / گر دخان او را دليل آتش است
entries.english: The latter flees from the proof and from the veil: he has sunk his head in his bosom for the sake of the Object of the proof.

entries.farsi: ‏بى‏دخان ما را در آن آتش خوش است / خاصه اين آتش كه از قرب و وال
entries.english: If to him the smoke is a proof of the fire, to us it is sweet in the fire without the smoke,

entries.farsi: از دخان نزديك‏تر آمد بما / پس سيه كارى بود رفتن ز جان
entries.english: Especially this Fire which, through nearness and fealty, is nearer to us than the smoke.

entries.farsi: ‏بهر تخييالت جان سوى دخان
entries.english: Therefore it is black villainy to go from the Soul towards the smoke for the sake of the phantasies of the soul.

sections.title_fa: ‏در بيان قول رسول عليه السلام ال رهبانيه فى الاسالم
sections.title_en: In explanation of the saying of the Prophet, on whom be peace, “There is no monkery in Islam.”

entries.farsi: بر مكن پر را و دل بر كن از او / ز انكه شرط اين جهاد آمد عدو
entries.english: “Do not tear out your feathers, but detach your heart from them, because the enemy is the necessary condition for this Holy War.

entries.farsi: صبر نبود چون نباشد ميل تو / خصم چون نبود چه حاجت حيل تو
entries.english: When there is no enemy, the Holy War is inconceivable; you have no lust, there can be no obedience.

entries.farsi: چون عدو نبود جهاد آمد محال / ‏شهوتت نبود نباشد امتثال
entries.english: There can be no self-restraint when you have no desire; when there is no adversary, what need for your strength?

entries.farsi: هين مكن خود را خصى رهبان مشو / ز انكه عفت هست شهوت را گرو
entries.english: Listen, do not castrate yourself, do not become a monk; for chastity is in pawn to lust.

entries.farsi: بى‏هوا نهى از هوا ممكن نبود / غازيى بر مردگان نتوان نمود
entries.english: Without sensuality it is impossible to forbid sensuality: heroism cannot be displayed against the dead.

entries.farsi: أَنْفِقُوا گفته است پس كسبى بكن / ‏ز انكه نبود خرج بى‏دخل كهن
entries.english: He has said ‘Spend’: therefore earn something, since there can be no expenditure without an old income.

entries.farsi: گر چه آورد أَنْفِقُوا را مطلق او / تو بخوان كه اكسبوا ثم انفقوا
entries.english: Although He used Spend absolutely, read ‘Earn, then spend.’

entries.farsi: همچنان چون شاه فرمود اصبروا / رغبتى بايد كز آن تابى تو رو
entries.english: Similarly, since the King has given the command ‘Refrain yourselves,’ there must be some desire from which you should avert your face.

entries.farsi: پس كُلُوا از بهر دام شهوت است / ‏بعد از آن ال تُسْ رِفُوا آن عفت است
entries.english: Hence ‘Eat’ is for the sake of the snare of appetite; after that ‘Do not exceed’: that is temperance.

entries.farsi: چون كه محمول به نبود لديه / ‏نيست ممكن بود محمول عليه
entries.english: When there is no ‘predicate’ in him, the existence of the ‘subject’ is impossible.

entries.farsi: چون كه رنج صبر نبود مر ترا / شرط نبود پس فرو نايد جزا
entries.english: When you have not the pain of self-restraint, there is no opening clause: therefore the secondary clause does not follow.

entries.farsi: حبذا آن شرط و شادا آن جزا / آن جزاى دل نواز جان فزا
entries.english: How admirable is that opening clause and how joyful is that secondary clause, a recompense that charms the heart and increases the life of the spirit!

sections.title_fa: ‏در بيان آن كه ثواب عمل عاشق از حق هم حق است
sections.title_en: Explaining that God is the reward bestowed by Him for the work of the lover.

entries.farsi: عاشقان را شادمانى و غم اوست / دست مزد و اجرت خدمت هم اوست
entries.english: For lovers He is joy and sorrow; He is their wages and hire for service.

entries.farsi: غير معشوق ار تماشايى بود / عشق نبود هرزه سودايى بود
entries.english: If there be any spectacle except the Beloved, it is not love: it is an idle passion.

entries.farsi: عشق آن شعله‏ست كاو چون بر فروخت / ‏هر چه جز معشوق باقى جمله سوخت
entries.english: Love is that flame which, when it blazes up, consumes everything else but the Beloved.

entries.farsi: تيغ ال در قتل غير حق براند / در نگر ز آن پس كه بعد ال چه ماند
entries.english: He drives home the sword of Not in order to kill all other than God: thereupon consider what remains after Not.

entries.farsi: ماند إِلاَّ اللهَُّ باقى جمله رفت / ‏شاد باش اى عشق شركت سوز زفت
entries.english: There remains except God: all the rest is gone. Hail, O mighty Love, destroyer of polytheism!

entries.farsi: خود همو بود آخرين و اولين / ‏شرك جز از ديده‏ى احول مبين
entries.english: Truly, He is the First and the Last: do not regard polytheism as arising from aught except the eye that sees double.

entries.farsi: اى عجب حسنى بود جز عكس آن / ‏نيست تن را جنبشى از غير جان
entries.english: Oh, wonderful! Is there any beauty but from the reflection of Him? The body has no movement but from the spirit.

entries.farsi: آن تنى را كه بود در جان خلل / ‏خوش نگردد گر بگيرى در عسل
entries.english: The body that has defect in its spirit will never become sweet, if you smear it with honey.

entries.farsi: اين كسى داند كه روزى زنده بود / از كف اين جان جان جامى ربود
entries.english: This he knows who one day was alive and received a cup from this Soul of the soul;

entries.farsi: وان كه چشم او نديده‏ست آن رخان / ‏پيش او جان است اين تف دخان
entries.english: While to him whose eye has not beheld those cheeks this smoky heat is the spirit.

entries.farsi: چون نديد او عمر عبد العزيز / پيش او عادل بود حجاج نيز
entries.english: Inasmuch as he never saw ‘Umar Abdu ’l-Aziz, to him even Hajjáj seems just.

entries.farsi: چون نديد او مار موسى را ثبات / ‏در حبال سحر پندارد حيات
entries.english: Inasmuch as he never saw the firmness of the dragon of Moses, he fancies life in the magic cords.

entries.farsi: مرغ كاو ناخورده است آب زالل / ‏اندر آب شور دارد پر و بال
entries.english: The bird that has never drunk the limpid water keeps its wings and feathers in the briny water.

entries.farsi: جز به ضد ضد را همى نتوان شناخت / ‏چون ببيند زخم بشناسد نواخت
entries.english: No opposite can be known except through its opposite: when he suffers blows will he know kindness.

entries.farsi: الجرم دنيا مقدم آمده‏ست / ‏ُتا بدانى قدر اقليم أَ لَسْ ت
entries.english: Consequently the present life has come in front, in order that you may appreciate the realm of Alast.

entries.farsi: چون از اينجا وارهى آن جا روى / ‏در شكر خانه‏ى ابد شاكر شوى
entries.english: When you are delivered from this place and go to that place, you will give thanks in the sugar-shop of everlastingness.

entries.farsi: گويى آن جا خاك را مى‏بيختم / ‏زين جهان پاك مى‏بگريختم
entries.english: You will say, ‘There I was sifting dust, I was fleeing from this pure world.

entries.farsi: اى دريغا پيش از اين بوديم اجل / ‏تا عذابم كم بدى اندر وحل
entries.english: Alas, would that I had died before now, so that my being tormented in the mud might have been less!’

sections.title_fa: در تفسير قول رسول صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله ما مات من مات الا و تمنى ان يموت قبل ما مات ان كان ‏برا ليكون الى وصول البر اعجل و ان كان فاجرا ليقل فجوره
sections.title_en: Commentary on the saying of the Prophet, on whom be peace, “None ever died without wishing,
if he was a righteous man that he had died before he died, in order that he might sooner attain unto felicity; and if he was a wicked man, in order that his wickedness might be less.”

entries.farsi: زين بفرموده‏ست آن آگه رسول / ‏كه هر آن كه مرد و كرد از تن نزول
entries.english: Hence the wise Prophet has said that no one who dies and dismounts from the body

entries.farsi: نبود او را حسرت نقالن و موت / ‏ليك باشد حسرت تقصير و فوت
entries.english: Feels grief on account of departure and death, but grieves because of having failed and missed his opportunities.

entries.farsi: هر كه ميرد خود تمنى باشدش / ‏كه بدى زين پيش نقل مقصدش
entries.english: In sooth every one that dies wishes that the departure to his destination had been earlier:

entries.farsi: گر بود بد تا بدى كمتر بدى / ‏ور تقى تا خانه زودتر آمدى
entries.english: If he be wicked, in order that his wickedness might have been less; and if devout, in order that he might have come home sooner.

entries.farsi: گويد آن بد بى‏خبر مى‏بوده‏ام / ‏دم به دم من پرده مى‏افزوده‏ام
entries.english: The wicked man says, ‘I have been heedless, moment by moment I have been adding to the veil.

entries.farsi: از حريصى كم دران روى قنوع / ‏وز تكبر كم دران چهره‏ى خشوع
entries.english: If my passing had taken place sooner, this screen and veil of mine would have been less.’

entries.farsi: گر از اين زودتر مرا معبر بدى / ‏اين حجاب و پرده‏ام كمتر بدى
entries.english: Do not in covetousness rend the face of contentment, and do not in pride rend the visage of humility.

entries.farsi: همچنين از بخل كم در روى جود / وز بليسى چهره‏ى خوب سجود
entries.english: Likewise do not in avarice rend the face of munificence, and in devilishness the beauteous countenance of worship.

entries.farsi: بر مكن آن پر خلد آراى را / بر مكن آن پر ره پيماى ر
entries.english: Do not tear out those feathers which are an ornament to Paradise: do not tear out those feathers which traverse the Way.”

entries.farsi: چون شنيد اين پند در وى بنگريست / ‏بعد از آن در نوحه آمد مى‏گريست
entries.english: When he heard this counsel, he looked at him and, after that, began to lament and weep.

entries.farsi: نوحه و گريه‏ى دراز دردمند / هر كه آن جا بود بر گريه‏اش فگند
entries.english: The long lamentation and weeping of the sorrowful peacock caused everyone who was there to fall a-weeping;

entries.farsi: و انكه مى‏پرسيد پر كندن ز چيست / ‏بى‏جوابى شد پشيمان مى‏گريست
entries.english: And he who was asking the reason of the peacock’s tearing out his feathers, without an answer repented and wept,

entries.farsi: كز فضولى من چرا پرسيدمش / ‏او ز غم پر بود شورانيدمش
entries.english: Saying, “Why did I impertinently ask him? He was full of grief: I made him distraught.”

entries.farsi: مى‏چكيد از چشم تر بر خاك آب / ‏اندر آن هر قطره مدرج صد جواب
entries.english: From his moist eyes the water was trickling to the earth: in every drop were contained a hundred answers.

entries.farsi: گريه‏ى با صدق بر جانها زند / تا كه چرخ و عرش را گريان كند
entries.english: Sincere weeping touches the souls, so that it makes the sky and heaven to weep.

entries.farsi: عقل و دلها بى‏گمانى عرشى‏اند / در حجاب از نور عرشى مى‏زيند
entries.english: Without any doubt, intellects and hearts are celestial, they live debarred from the celestial light.

sections.title_fa: ‏در بيان آن كه عقل و روح در آب و گل محبوس‏اند همچو هاروت و ماروت در چاه بابل
sections.title_en: Explaining that the intellect and spirit are imprisoned in clay, like Harut and Marut in the pit of Babylon.

entries.farsi: همچو هاروت و چو ماروت آن دو پاك / ‏بسته‏اند اينجا به چاه سهمناك
entries.english: Like Harut and Marut, those two pure ones have been confined here in a horrible pit.

entries.farsi: عالم سفلى و شهوانى درند / اندر اين چه گشته‏اند از جرم بند
entries.english: They are in the low and sensual world: they have been confined in this pit on account of sin.

entries.farsi: سحر و ضد سحر را بى‏اختيار / زين دو آموزند نيكان و شرار
entries.english: The good and the evil learn magic and the opposite of magic from these two involuntarily;

entries.farsi: ليك اول پند بدهندش كه هين / ‏سحر را از ما مياموز و مچين
entries.english: But first they admonish him, saying, “Beware, do not learn and pick up magic from us:

entries.farsi: ما بياموزيم اين سحر اى فالن / ‏از براى ابتال و امتحان
entries.english: We teach this magic, O such and such, for the purpose of trial and probation;

entries.farsi: كامتحان را شرط باشد اختيار / اختيارى نبودت بى‏اقتدار
entries.english: For probation necessarily involves free-will, and you cannot have any free-will without the power.”

entries.farsi: ميلها همچون سگان خفته‏اند / اندر ايشان خير و شر بنهفته‏اند
entries.english: Desires are like sleeping dogs: good and evil are hidden in them.

entries.farsi: تا كه مردارى در آيد در ميان / ‏نفخ صور حرص كوبد بر سگان
entries.english: When there is no power, this troop is asleep and silent like faggots,

entries.farsi: چون كه قدرت نيست خفتند اين رده / ‏همچو هيزم پاره‏ها و تن زده
entries.english: Until a carcass comes into view, the blast of the trumpet of greed strikes on the dogs.

entries.farsi: حرصهاى رفته اندر كتم غيب / ‏تاختن آورد سر بر زد ز جيب
entries.english: When the carcass of a donkey appears in the parish, a hundred sleeping dogs are awakened by it.

entries.farsi: چون در آن كوچه خرى مردار شد / صد سگ خفته بدان بيدار شد
entries.english: The greedy desires that had gone into the concealment of the Unseen rush out and display themselves.

entries.farsi: مو به موى هر سگى دندان شده / ‏وز براى حيله دم جنبان شده
entries.english: Every hair on every dog becomes a truth, though they wag their tails for the sake of gaining their object.

entries.farsi: نيم زيرش حيله بالا آن غضب / ‏چون ضعيف آتش كه يابد او حطب
entries.english: His under-half is cunning; the upper is anger, like a poor fire that gets faggots;

entries.farsi: شعله شعله مى‏رسد از المكان / ‏مى‏رود دود لهب تا آسمان
entries.english: Flame on flame reaches from non-spatiality: the smoke of its blaze goes up to the sky.

entries.farsi: صد چنين سگ اندر اين تن خفته‏اند / چون شكارى نيست شان بنهفته‏اند
entries.english: In this body a hundred such dogs are sleeping: when they have no prey, they are hidden.

entries.farsi: يا چو بازان‏اند ديده دوخته / ‏در حجاب از عشق صيدى سوخته
entries.english: Or they resemble falcons with eyes sealed; in the veil consumed with passion for a prey,

entries.farsi: تا كله بر دارد و بيند شكار / آن گهان سازد طواف كوهسار
entries.english: Till he lifts the hood and it sees the prey: then it circles the mountains.

entries.farsi: شهوت رنجور ساكن مى‏بود / خاطر او سوى صحت مى‏رود
entries.english: The appetite of the sick man is quiescent: his thoughts are going towards health.

entries.farsi: چون نبيند نان و سيب و خربزه / ‏در مصاف آيد مزه و خوف بزه
entries.english: When he sees bread and apples and water-melons, his relish and his fear of injury come into conflict.

entries.farsi: گر بود صبار ديدن سود اوست / ‏آن تهيج طبع سستش را نكوست
entries.english: If he be very self-restrained, the sight is a benefit to him: that stimulation is good for his enfeebled constitution;

entries.farsi: ور نباشد صبر پس ناديده به / ‏تير دور اولى ز مرد بى‏زره
entries.english: But if he have not self-restraint, then it was better he had not seen: it is better the arrow should be far from the man who is without a coat of mail.

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن طاوس آن سائل را
sections.title_en: The answer of the peacock to his interrogator.

entries.farsi: چون ز گريه فارغ آمد گفت رو / كه تو رنگ و بوى را هستى گرو
entries.english: When he had finished weeping, he said, “Begone, for you art in pawn to colour and perfume.

entries.farsi: آن نمى‏بينى كه هر سو صد بال / سوى من آيد پى اين بالها
entries.english: Do not you perceive that on account of these feathers a hundred afflictions approach me on every side?

entries.farsi: اى بسا صياد بى‏رحمت مدام / ‏بهر اين پرها نهد هر سوم دام
entries.english: Oh, many a pitiless fowler always lays a trap for me everywhere for the sake of these feathers.

entries.farsi: چند تير انداز بهر بالها / تير سوى من كشد اندر هوا
entries.english: How many an archer, for the sake of my plumage, shoots arrows at me in the air!

entries.farsi: آن به آيد كه شوم زشت و كريه / ‏تا بوم ايمن در اين كهسار و تيه
entries.english: Since I have not strength and self-control from this destiny and this affliction and these tribulations,

entries.farsi: چون ندارم زور و ضبط خويشتن / زين قضا و زين بال و زين فتن
entries.english: It is better I should be ugly and hideous, that I may be safe amidst these mountains and deserts.

entries.farsi: اين سالح عجب من شد اى فتى / عجب آرد معجبان را صد بال
entries.english: These are the weapons of my pride, O noble sir: pride brings a hundred afflictions on the proud.

sections.title_fa: ‏بيان آن كه هنرها و زيركيها و مال دنيا همچون پرهاى طاوس عدوى جان است
sections.title_en: Explaining that accomplishments and intellectual abilities and worldly wealth are enemies to life, like the peacock’s feathers.

entries.farsi: پس هنر آمد هالكت خام را / كز پى دانه نبيند دام را
entries.english: Accomplishments, then, destroy the ignorant man, for in his pursuit of the bait he does not see the trap.

entries.farsi: اختيار آن را نكو باشد كه او / مالك خود باشد اندر اتقوا
entries.english: Free-will is good for him who is master of himself in ‘Fear God.’

entries.farsi: جلوه گاه و اختيارم آن پر است / ‏بر كنم پر را كه در قصد سر است
entries.english: When there is no safeguarding and piety, beware, put far the instrument: drop free-will.

entries.farsi: چون نباشد حفظ و تقوى زينهار / دور كن آلت بينداز اختيار
entries.english: Those feathers are the object of my display and freewill: I will tear out the feathers, for they are in quest of my head.

entries.farsi: نيست انگارد پر خود را صبور / تا پرش در نفگند در شر و شور
entries.english: The self-restrained man deems his feathers to be naught, in order that his feathers may not cast him into calamity and bale.

entries.farsi: پس زيانش نيست پر گو بر مكن / ‏گر رسد تيرى به پيش آرد مجن
entries.english: Therefore his feathers are no harm to him: let him not tear them out, if an arrow come he will present the shield.

entries.farsi: ليك بر من پر زيبا دشمنى است / ‏چون كه از جلوه‏گرى صبريم نيست
entries.english: But to me my beauteous feathers are an enemy, since I cannot restrain myself from making a display.

entries.farsi: گر بدى صبر و حفاظم راهبر / بر فزودى ز اختيارم كر و فر
entries.english: If self-restraint and safeguarding had been my guide, my conquest would have been increased by free-will;

entries.farsi: همچو طفلم يا چو مست اندر فتن / ‏نيست اليق تيغ اندر دست من
entries.english: In the case of temptations I am like a child or a drunk man: the sword is unsuitable in my hand.

entries.farsi: گر مرا عقلى بدى و منزجر / تيغ اندر دست من بودى ظفر
entries.english: Had I possessed an intellect and conscience, the sword in my hand would have been victory.

entries.farsi: عقل بايد نور ده چون آفتاب / ‏تا زند تيغى كه نبود جز صواب
entries.english: An intellect giving light like the sun is needed to wield the sword that never misses the right direction.

entries.farsi: در چه اندازم كنون تيغ و مجن / ‏كاين سالح خصم من خواهد شدن
entries.english: Since I do not possess a resplendent intellect and righteousness, why, then, should not I throw my weapons into the well?

entries.farsi: چون ندارم عقل تابان و صالح / ‏پس چرا در چاه نندازم سالح
entries.english: I now throw my sword and shield into the well; for they will become the weapons of my adversary.

entries.farsi: چون ندارم زور و يارى و سند / تيغم او بستاند و بر من زند
entries.english: Since I do not possess strength and aid and support, he will seize my sword and smite me with it.

entries.farsi: رغم اين نفس و قبيحه خوى را / كه نپوشد رو خراشم روى را
entries.english: In despite of this fleshly soul and evil-natured one who does not veil her face, I will rend my face,

entries.farsi: شود كم اين جمال و اين كمال / ‏چون نماند رو كم افتم در وبال
entries.english: That this beauty and perfection may be impaired. When my face remains no more, I shall not fall into woe.

entries.farsi: چون بدين نيت خراشم بزه نيست / ‏كه به زخم اين روى را پوشيدنى است
entries.english: When I shatter this intention, it is no sin, for this face ought to be covered with wounds.

entries.farsi: گر دلم خوى ستيرى داشتى / ‏روى خوبم جز صفا نفراشتى
entries.english: If my heart had a modest disposition, my handsome face would produce naught but purity.

entries.farsi: چون نديدم زور و فرهنگ و صالح / ‏خصم ديدم زود بشكستم سالح
entries.english: Since I did not see strength and wisdom and righteousness, I saw the adversary and at once broke my weapons,

entries.farsi: تا نگردد تيغ من او را كمال / ‏تا نگردد خنجرم بر من وبال
entries.english: Lest my sword should become useful to him; lest my dagger should become hurtful to me

entries.farsi: مى‏گريزم تا رگم جنبان بود / كى فرار از خويشتن آسان بود
entries.english: I will continue to flee as long as my veins are running, how should it be easy to escape from one’s self?

entries.farsi: آن كه از غيرى بود او را فرار / چون از او ببريد گيرد او قرار
entries.english: He who is in flight from another obtains rest when he has been separated from him.

entries.farsi: من كه خصمم هم منم اندر گريز / تا ابد كار من آمد خيز خيز
entries.english: I, who am the adversary, it is I that am in flight: rising and departing is my occupation forever.

entries.farsi: نه به هند است ايمن و نه در ختن / ‏آن كه خصم اوست سايه‏ى خويشتن
entries.english: He whose adversary is his own shadow is not safe either in India or Khutan.

sections.title_fa: در صفت آن بى‏خودان كه از شر خود و هنر خود ايمن شده‏اند كه فانى‏اند در بقاى حق همچون ستارگان كه فانى‏اند روز در آفتاب و فانى را خوف آفت و خطر نباشد
sections.title_en: Description of the selfless ones who have become safe from their own vices and virtues; for they are negated in the everlastingness of God, like stars which are negated in the Sun
during the daytime; and he who is negated has no fear of ruin and danger.

entries.farsi: فقر فخرى را فنا پيرايه شد / چون زبانه‏ى شمع او بى‏سايه شد
entries.english: When, through poverty, fana graces him, he becomes without shadow like Mohammed.

entries.farsi: چون فناش از فقر پيرايه شود / او محمد وار بى‏سايه شود
entries.english: Fana graced, ‘Poverty is my pride’: he became without shadow like the flame of a candle.

entries.farsi: شمع جمله شد زبانه پا و سر / سايه را نبود به گرد او گذر
entries.english: The candle has become entirely flame from head to foot; the shadow has no passage around it.

entries.farsi: موم از خويش و ز سايه در گريخت / ‏در شعاع از بهر او كه شمع ريخت
entries.english: The wax (candle) fled from itself and from the shadow into the radiance for the sake of Him who moulded the candle.

entries.farsi: گفت او بهر فنايت ريختم / ‏گفت من هم در فنا بگريختم
entries.english: He said, ‘I moulded you for the sake of fana.’ It replied, ‘I accordingly took refuge in fana.’

entries.farsi: اين شعاع باقى آمد مفترض / شمع چون در نار شد كلى فنا
entries.english: This is the necessary everlasting radiance, not the radiance of the perishable accidental candle.

entries.farsi: آتش صورت به مومى پايدار / بر خالف موم شمع جسم كان
entries.english: When the candle is wholly negated in the fire, you will not see any trace of the candle or rays.

entries.farsi: نه اثر بينى ز شمع و نه ضيا / هست اندر دفع ظلمت آشكار
entries.english: Manifestly, in dispelling the darkness, the external flame is maintained by a wax candle;

entries.farsi: ‏تا شود كم گردد افزون نور جان / اين شعاع باقى و آن فانى است
entries.english: The candle the body is contrary to the wax candle, since in proportion as that dwindles, the light of the spirit is increased.

entries.farsi: ‏شمع جان را شعله‏ى ربانى است / اين زبانه‏ى آتشى چون نور بود
entries.english: This is the everlasting radiance, and that is perishable: the candle of the spirit has a Divine flame.

entries.farsi: سايه‏ى فانى شدن زو دور بود / ابر را سايه بيفتد بر زمين
entries.english: Since this tongue of fire was light, it was far from it to become a perishable shadow.

entries.farsi: ‏ماه را سايه نباشد همنشين / بى‏خودى بى‏ابرى است اى نيك خواه
entries.english: The cloud’s shadow falls on the earth: the shadow never consorts with the moon.

entries.farsi: ‏باشى اندر بى‏خودى چون قرص ماه / باز چون ابرى بيايد رانده
entries.english: Selflessness is cloudlessness, O well-disposed one: in selflessness you will be like the orb of the moon.

entries.farsi: ‏رفت نور از مه خيالى مانده / از حجاب ابر نورش شد ضعيف
entries.english: Again, when a cloud comes, driven along, the light goes: of the moon there remains a phantom.

entries.farsi: ‏كم ز ماه نو شد آن بدر شريف / مه خيالى مى‏نمايد ز ابر و گرد
entries.english: Its light is made feeble by the cloud-veil: that noble full-moon becomes less than the new moon.

entries.farsi: ابر تن ما را خيال انديش كرد / لطف مه بنگر كه اين هم لطف اوست
entries.english: The moon is made to appear a phantom by clouds and dust: the cloud, the body, has caused us to conceive phantasies.

entries.farsi: ‏كه بگفت او ابرها ما را عدوست / مه فراغت دارد از ابر و غبار
entries.english: Behold the kindness of the Moon; for this too is His kindness, that He has said, ‘The clouds are enemies to Us.’

entries.farsi: بر فراز چرخ دارد مه مدار / ابر ما را شد عدو و خصم جان
entries.english: The Moon is independent of clouds and dust: the Moon has His orbit aloft in the sky.

entries.farsi: ‏كه كند مه را ز چشم ما نهان / حور را اين پرده زالى مى‏كند
entries.english: The cloud is our mortal enemy and adversary because it hides the Moon from our eyes.

entries.farsi: بدر را كم از هاللى مى‏كند / ماه ما را در كنار عز نشاند
entries.english: This veil makes the houri a hag: it makes the full-moon less than a new moon.

entries.farsi: دشمن ما را عدوى خويش خواند / تاب ابرو آب او خود زين مه است
entries.english: The Moon has seated us in the lap of glory: He has called our foe His enemy.

entries.farsi: ‏روى تاريكش ز مه مبدل شده‏ست / گر چه هم رنگ مه است و دولتى است
entries.english: The splendour and beauty of the cloud is from the Moon, whoever calls the cloud the Moon is much astray.

entries.farsi: ‏هر كه مه خواند ابر را بس گمره است / نور مه برابر چون منزل شده‏ست
entries.english: Since the light of the Moon has been poured down upon the cloud, its dark face has been transfigured by the Moon.

entries.farsi: ‏اندر ابر آن نور مه عاريتى است / در قيامت شمس و مه معزول شد
entries.english: Although it is of the same colour as the Moon and is associated with empire, in the cloud the light of the Moon is borrowed.

entries.farsi: وين رباط فانى از دار القرار / دايه عاريه بود روزى سه چار
entries.english: At the Resurrection the sun and moon are discharged: the eye is occupied in the Source of radiance,

entries.farsi: چشم در اصل ضيا مشغول شد / تا بداند ملك را از مستعار
entries.english: In order that it may know the possession from the loan, and this perishable caravanseray from the everlasting abode.

entries.farsi: مادرا ما را تو گير اندر كنار / پر من ابر است و پرده‏ست و كثيف
entries.english: The nurse is borrowed for three or four days: do you, O Mother, take us into your bosom!

entries.farsi: ز انعكاس لطف حق شد او لطيف / بر كنم پر را و حسنش را ز راه
entries.english: My feathers are the cloud and are a veil and gross: by the reflection of God’s loveliness are they made lovely.

entries.farsi: ‏تا ببينم حسن مه را هم ز ماه / من نخواهم دايه مادر خوشتر است
entries.english: I will pluck my feathers and their beauty from the Way that I may behold the Moon’s beauty from the Moon.

entries.farsi: ‏موسى‏ام من دايه‏ى من مادر است / من نخواهم لطف مه از واسطه
entries.english: I do not want the nurse; Mother is fairer. I am like Moses: Mother is my nurse.

entries.farsi: ‏كه هالك قوم شد اين رابطه / با مگر ابرى بگيرد خوى ماه
entries.english: I do not want the loveliness of the Moon through an intermediary, for this link is perdition to the people;

entries.farsi: ‏تا نگردد او حجاب روى ماه / صورتش بنمايد او در وصف ال
entries.english: Unless a cloud becomes negated in the Way in order that it may not be a veil to the face of the Moon.

entries.farsi: همچو جسم انبيا و اوليا / آن چنان ابرى نباشد پرده بند
entries.english: In the aspect of la it displays the Moon’s form, like the bodies of the prophets and saints.

entries.farsi: پرده در باشد به معنى سودمند / آن چنانك اندر صباح روشنى
entries.english: Such a cloud is not veil-tying; it is in reality veil-tearing salutary.

entries.farsi: گشته ابر از محو هم رنگ سما / بود ابر و رفته از وى خوى ابر
entries.english: It is as when, on a bright morning, drops of rain were falling though there was no cloud above.

entries.farsi: ‏قطره مى‏باريد و بالا ابر نى / معجزه‏ى پيغمبرى بود آن سقا
entries.english: That water-skin was a miracle of the Prophet: from self-effacement the cloud had become of the same colour as the sky.

entries.farsi: اين چنين گردد تن عاشق به صبر / تن بود اما تنى گم گشته زو
entries.english: The cloud was, but the cloud-nature had gone from it: the body of the lover becomes like this by means of renunciation.

entries.farsi: گشته مبدل رفته از وى رنگ و بو / پر پى غير است و سر از بهر من
entries.english: It is body, but materiality has vanished from it: it has been transfigured; colour and perfume have gone from it.

entries.farsi: كفر مطلق دان و نوميدى ز خير / هين مشو چون قند پيش طوطيان
entries.english: Feathers are for the sake of others, while head is for my own sake: the abode of hearing and sight is the pillar of the body.

entries.farsi: ‏خانه‏ى سمع و بصر استون تن / جان فدا كردن براى صيد غير
entries.english: Know that to sacrifice the spirit for the sake of catching others is absolute infidelity and despair of good.

entries.farsi: ‏بلكه زهرى شو شو ايمن از زيان / يا براى شاد باشى در خطاب
entries.english: Beware! Do not be like sugar before parrots; nay, be a poison, be secure from loss;

entries.farsi: ‏خويش چون مردار كن پيش كالب / پس خضر كشتى براى اين شكست
entries.english: Or, for the sake of having a ‘Bravo’ addressed to you, make yourself a carcass in the presence of dogs!

entries.farsi: ‏تا ز طماعان گريزم در غنى / گنجها را در خرابى ز آن نهند
entries.english: Therefore Khadir scuttled the boat for this purpose that the boat might be delivered from him who would have seized it by force.

entries.farsi: ‏تا كه آن كشتى ز غاصب باز رست / فقر فخرى بهر آن آمد سنى
entries.english: ‘Poverty is my pride’ is sublime: for the purpose that I may take refuge from the covetous with Him who is Self-sufficient.

entries.farsi: تا ز حرص اهل عمران وارهند / پر ندانى كند رو خلوت گزين
entries.english: Treasures are deposited in a ruined spot to the end that they may escape the greed of those who dwell in places of cultivation.

entries.farsi: ‏تا نگردى جمله خرج آن و اين / ز انكه تو هم لقمه‏اى هم لقمه خوار
entries.english: You cannot tear out your feathers, go, and adopt solitude, that you may not be entirely squandered by that one and this one;

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكولى اى جان هوش دار
entries.english: For you are both the morsel and the eater of the morsel: you are the devourer and the devoured. Apprehend O soul!

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه ما سوى اللهَّ هر چيزى آكل و مأكول است همچون آن مرغى كه قصد صيد ملخ مى‏كرد ،و به صيد ملخ مشغول مى‏بود و غافل بود از باز گرسنه كه از پس قفاى او قصد صيد او داشت اكنون اى آدمى صياد آكل از صياد آكل خود ايمن مباش، اگر چه نمى‏بينيش به نظر چشم به نظر دليل ‏عبرتش مى‏بين تا چشم سر باز شدن

sections.title_en: Explaining that everything except God is devouring and devoured, like the bird that was in pursuit of a locust and occupied in chasing it and oblivious of the hungry hawk behind its own back, that was about to seize it. Now, O hunting and devouring man; be not secure against your own hunter and devourer. Though with the sight of the eye
you do not see him, see him with the eye of serious consideration till the opening of the eye of the inmost heart.

entries.farsi: مرغكى اندر شكار كرم بود / گربه فرصت يافت او را در ربود
entries.english: A little bird was hunting a worm: a cat found its opportunity and seized it.

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكول بود و بى‏خبر / در شكار خود ز صيادى دگر
entries.english: It was a devourer and a thing devoured, and in its hunting was ignorant of another hunter.

entries.farsi: دزد گر چه در شكار كاله‏اى است / ‏شحنه با خصمانش در دنباله‏اى است
entries.english: Although the thief is in hunting articles of property, the prefect of police along with his enemies is behind him.

entries.farsi: عقل او مشغول رخت و قفل و در / غافل از شحنه ست و از آه سحر
entries.english: His mind is occupied with chattels and lock and door: he is heedless of the prefect and of the outcry at dawn.

entries.farsi: او چنان غرق است در سوداى خود / غافل است از طالب و جوياى خود
entries.english: He is so absorbed in his passion he gives no heed to his seekers and pursuers.

entries.farsi: گر حشيش آب زاللى مى‏خورد / معده‏ى حيوانش در پى مى‏چرد
entries.english: If the herbage is drinking pure water, afterwards an animal’s belly will feed on it.

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكول آمد آن گياه / ‏همچنين هر هستى غير اله
entries.english: That grass is devouring and devoured: even so everything that exists except God.

entries.farsi: و هو يطعمكم و ال يطعم چو اوست / ‏نيست حق مأكول و آكل لحم و پوست
entries.english: Since He is and He feeds you and is not fed, God is not devouring and devoured, flesh and skin.

entries.farsi: آكل و مأكول كى ايمن بود / ز آكلى كاندر كمين ساكن بود
entries.english: How should that which is devouring and devoured be secure from a devourer who dwells in a hiding-place?

entries.farsi: امن مأكوالن جذوب ماتم است / ‏رو بدان درگاه كاو ال يطعم است
entries.english: The security of those who are devoured brings mourning in its train: go to the Portal of Him who is not fed.

entries.farsi: هر خيالى را خيالى مى‏خورد / فكر آن فكر دگر را مى‏چرد
entries.english: Every phantasy is devouring another phantasy: thought feeds on another thought.

entries.farsi: تو نتانى كز خيالى وارهى / ‏يا بخسبى كه از آن بيرون جهى
entries.english: You cannot be delivered from any phantasy or fall asleep so as to escape from it.

entries.farsi: فكر زنبور است و آن خواب تو آب / ‏چون شوى بيدار باز آيد ذباب
entries.english: Thoughts are like hornets, and your sleep is like the water: when you awake, the hornets come back,

entries.farsi: چند زنبور خيالى در پرد / مى‏كشد اين سو و آن سو مى‏برد
entries.english: And many hornet-like phantasies fly in and draw you this way and take you that way.

entries.farsi: كمترين آكالن است اين خيال / ‏و آن دگرها را شناسد ذو الجلال
entries.english: This phantasy is the least of the devourers: the Almighty knows the others.

entries.farsi: هين گريز از جوق آكال غليظ / سوى او كه گفت ماييم‏ات حفيظ
entries.english: Listen; flee from the troop of huge devourers towards Him who has said, ‘We are your protector’;

entries.farsi: دست را مسپار جز در دست پير / حق شده‏ست آن دست او را دستگير
entries.english: Or towards one who has gained that protection, if you cannot hasten towards the Protector.

entries.farsi: يا به سوى آن كه او آن حفظ يافت / ‏گر نتانى سوى آن حافظ شتافت
entries.english: Do not surrender your hand save to the hand of the Pir; God has become the aider of his hand.

entries.farsi: پير عقلت كودكى خو كرده است / ‏از جوار نفس كاندر پرده است
entries.english: The Pir, your intellect, has become childish from being a neighbour to the carnal soul which is in the veil.

entries.farsi: عقل كامل را قرين كن با خرد / تا كه باز آيد خرد ز آن خوى بد
entries.english: Associate the perfect intelligence with your understanding, in order that your understanding may return from that evil disposition.

entries.farsi: چون كه دست خود به دست او نهى / ‏پس ز دست آكالن بيرون جهى
entries.english: When you lay your hand in his, then you will escape from the hand of the devourers,

entries.farsi: دست تو از اهل آن بيعت شود / كه يَدُ اللهَِّ فَوْقَ أَيْدِيهِمْ بود
entries.english: And your hand will become one of the Covenanters above whose hands is the Hand of Allah.

entries.farsi: چون بدادى دست خود در دست پير / پير حكمت كه عليم است و خطير
entries.english: When you have put your hand in the hand of the Pir, the Pir of wisdom who is knowing and eminent,

entries.farsi: در حديبيه شدى حاضر بدين / ‏و آن صحابه‏ى بيعتى را هم قرين
entries.english: Who is the prophet of his own time, O disciple, so that the Light of the Prophet is manifested by him,

entries.farsi: كاو نبى وقت خويش است اى مريد / تا از او نور نبى آيد پديد
entries.english: By this means you have been present at Hudaybiya and have been associated with the Companions who took the Covenant.

entries.farsi: پس زده يار مبشر آمدى / ‏همچو زر ده دهى خالص شدى
entries.english: Therefore you have become one of the ten Friends to whom the glad tidings were given, and have been made pure like sterling gold.

entries.farsi: تا معيت راست آيد ز انكه مرد / با كسى جفت است كاو را دوست كرد
entries.english: To the end that communion may be made perfect; for a man is united with that one whom he has made his friend.

entries.farsi: اين جهان و آن جهان با او بود / وين حديث احمد خوش خو بود
entries.english: He is with him in this world and in that world; and this is the Hadíth of sweet-natured Ahmad,

entries.farsi: گفت المرء مع محبوبه / ‏ال يفك القلب من مطلوبه
entries.english: Said ‘A man is with him whom he loves’: the heart is not severed from its object of desire.

entries.farsi: هر كجا دام است و دانه كم نشين / ‏رو زبون گيرا زبون گيران ببين
entries.english: Do not sit in any place where there is a trap and bait: O you who regard others as weak, go, consider those who regard as weak.

entries.farsi: اى زبون‏گير زبونان اين بدان / ‏دست هم بالاى دست است اى جوان
entries.english: O you who regard the weak as weak, know this, there is a hand above your hand, O youth.

entries.farsi: تو زبونى و زبون‏گير اى عجب / ‏هم تو صيد و صيد گير اندر طلب
entries.english: You are weak and you regard others as weak. Oh, wonderful! You are at once the prey and the pursuing hunter.

entries.farsi: بين ايدى خلفهم سدا مباش / ‏كه نبينى خصم را و آن خصم فاش
entries.english: Be not before and behind them a barrier, so that you cannot see the enemy, though the enemy is manifest.

entries.farsi: حرص صيادى ز صيدى مغفل است / ‏دلبريى مى‏كند او بى‏دل است
entries.english: The greed of hunting makes oblivious of being a prey: he tries to win hearts he has lost his own.

entries.farsi: تو كم از مرغى مباش اندر نشيد / بين ايدى خلف عصفورى بديد
entries.english: Be not you inferior to a bird in seeking: a sparrow sees before and behind.

entries.farsi: چون به نزد دانه آيد پيش و پس / ‏چند گرداند سر و رو آن نفس
entries.english: When it approaches the grain, at that moment it turns its head and face several times to front and rear,

entries.farsi: كاى عجب پيش و پسم صياد هست / ‏تا كشم از بيم او زين لقمه دست
entries.english: Oh, I wonder whether there is a fowler in front of me or behind, so that for fear of him I should abstain from this food.’

entries.farsi: تو ببين پس قصه‏ى فجار را / پيش بنگر مرگ يار و جار را
entries.english: Do you see behind the story of the wicked; see before the death of friend and neighbour,

entries.farsi: كاو هالكت دادشان بى‏آلتى / ‏او قرين تست در هر حالتى
entries.english: Whom He destroyed without any instrument: He is close to you in every circumstance.

entries.farsi: حق شكنجه كرد و گر زو دست نيست / ‏پس بدان بى‏دست حق داور كنى است
entries.english: God inflicted torment, and there is no mace or hand: know, then, that God is one who deals justice without hands.

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏گفتى اگر حق هست كو / در شكنجه‏ى او مقر مى‏شد كه هو
entries.english: He who was saying, ‘If God exists, where is He?’ was confessing on the rack that it is He.

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏گفت اين بعيد است و عجيب / ‏اشك مى‏راند و همى‏گفت اى قريب
entries.english: He who was saying, ‘This is far-fetched and marvellous’ was shedding tears and crying, ‘O You who are near!’

entries.farsi: چون فرار از دام واجب ديده است / ‏دام تو خود بر پرت چسبيده است
entries.english: Since he has deemed it necessary to flee from the trap, the trap for you is in fact stuck fast to your feathers.

entries.farsi: بر كنم من ميخ اين منحوس دام / ‏از پى كامى نباشم تلخ كام
entries.english: I will tear out the pin of this ill-fated trap: I will not suffer bitter grief for the sake of a desire.

entries.farsi: در خور عقل تو گفتم اين جواب / ‏فهم كن وز جستجو رو بر متاب
entries.english: I have given you this answer suitable to your understanding: apprehend and do not avert your face from seeking.

entries.farsi: بگسل اين حبلى كه حرص است و حسد / ياد كن فى جيدها حبل مسد
entries.english: Snap this cord, which is greed and envy: remember on her neck a cord of palm-fibres.”

sections.title_fa: سبب كشتن خليل عليه السلام زاغ را كه آن اشارت به قمع كدام صفت بود از صفات مذمومه‏ى مهلكه در مريد
sections.title_en: The reason why Khalíl, on whom be peace, killed the crow, indicating the subjugation of certain blameworthy and pernicious qualities in the disciple.

entries.farsi: اين سخن را نيست پايان و فراغ / ‏اى خليل حق چرا كشتى تو زاغ
entries.english: There is no end and completion to this discourse. O Friend of God, why didst you kill the crow?

entries.farsi: بهر فرمان حكمت فرمان چه بود / اندكى ز اسرار آن بايد نمود
entries.english: Because of the command. What was the wisdom of the command? A small part of the mysteries thereof must be shown.

entries.farsi: كاغ كاغ و نعره‏ى زاغ سياه / ‏دايما باشد به دنيا عمر خواه
entries.english: The cawing and noisy cry of the black crow is ever asking for life in this world.

entries.farsi: همچو ابليس از خداى پاك فرد / تا قيامت عمر تن درخواست كرد
entries.english: Like Iblís, it besought the holy and incomparable God for bodily life till the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: گفت انظرنى الى يوم الجزا / كاشكى گفتى كه تبنا ربنا
entries.english: He said, “Grant me a respite till the Day of Retribution.” Would that he had said, “We repent, O our Lord.”

entries.farsi: عمر بى‏توبه همه جان كندن است / ‏مرگ حاضر غايب از حق بودن است
entries.english: Life without repentance is all agony of spirit: to be absent from God is present death.

entries.farsi: آن هم از تاثير لعنت بود كاو / در چنان حضرت همى‏شد عمر جو
entries.english: Life and death—both these are sweet with God: without God the Water of Life is fire.

entries.farsi: عمر و مرگ اين هر دو با حق خوش بود / بى‏خدا آب حيات آتش بود
entries.english: Moreover, it was from the effect of the curse that in such a Presence he was requesting life.

entries.farsi: از خدا غير خدا را خواستن / ‏ظن افزونى است و كلى كاستن
entries.english: To crave of God anything other than God is the supposition of gain, and it is entire loss;

entries.farsi: خاصه عمرى غرق در بيگانگى / ‏در حضور شير روبه شانگى
entries.english: Especially a life sunk in estrangement is to behave like a fox in the presence of the lion,

entries.farsi: عمر بيشم ده كه تا پس‏تر روم / ‏مهلم افزون كن كه تا كمتر شوم
entries.english: “Give me longer life that I may go farther back; grant me more time that I may become less.”

entries.farsi: تا كه لعنت را نشانه او بود / بد كسى باشد كه لعنت‏جو بود
entries.english: That he is a mark for the curse: evil is that one who seeks to be accursed.

entries.farsi: عمر خوش در قرب جان پروردن است / ‏عمر زاغ از بهر سرگين خوردن است
entries.english: The goodly life is to nourish the spirit in nearness; the crow’s life is for the sake of eating shit.

entries.farsi: عمر بيشم ده كه تا گه مى‏خورم / ‏دايم اينم ده كه بس بد گوهرم
entries.english: “Give me more life that I may be ever eating shit: give me this always, for I am very evil-natured.”

entries.farsi: گر نه گه خوارست آن گنده دهان / ‏گويدى كز خوى زاغم وارهان
entries.english: Were it not that that foul-mouthed one is a shit-eater, he would say, “Deliver me from the nature of the crow!”

sections.title_fa: ‏مناجات
sections.title_en: Prayer.

entries.farsi: ى مبدل كرده خاكى را به زر / خاك ديگر را بكرده بو البشر
entries.english: O You who have transmuted one clod of earth into gold, and another clod into the Father of mankind,

entries.farsi: كار تو تبديل اعيان و عطا / كار من سهو است و نسيان و خطا
entries.english: Your work is the transmutation of essences and munificence; my work is mistake and forgetfulness and error.

entries.farsi: سهو و نسيان را مبدل كن به علم / ‏من همه خلمم مرا كن صبر و حلم
entries.english: Transmute mistake and forgetfulness into knowledge: I am all choler, make me patience and forbearance.

entries.farsi: اى كه خاك شوره را تو نان كنى / ‏وى كه نان مرده را تو جان كنى
entries.english: O You who make nitrous earth to be bread, and O You who make dead bread to be life,

entries.farsi: اى كه جان خيره را رهبر كنى / ‏وى كه بى‏ره را تو پيغمبر كنى
entries.english: O You who make the distracted soul to be a Guide, and O You who make the wayless wanderer to be a Prophet,

entries.farsi: مى‏كنى جزو زمين را آسمان / ‏مى‏فزايى در زمين از اختران
entries.english: You make a piece of earth to be heaven; You give increase in the earth from the stars.

entries.farsi: هر كه سازد زين جهان آب حيات / ‏زوترش از ديگران آيد ممات
entries.english: Whosoever makes the Water of Life to consist of this world, death comes to him sooner than to the others.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى دل كاو به گردون بنگريست / ‏ديد كاينجا هر دمى ميناگرى است
entries.english: The eye of the heart that contemplated the firmament perceived that here is a continual alchemy.

entries.farsi: قلب اعيان است و اكسيرى محيط / ائتالف خرقه‏ى تن بى‏مخيط
entries.english: The harmonious cohesion of the patched garment, the body, without being stitched, is the transmutation of essences and an all-embracing elixir.

entries.farsi: تو از آن روزى كه در هست آمدى / ‏آتشى يا باد يا خاكى بدى
entries.english: From the day when you came into existence, you were fire or air or earth.

entries.farsi: گر بر آن حالت ترا بودى بقا / كى رسيدى مر ترا اين ارتقا
