entries.english: If you had remained in that condition, how should this height have been reached by you?

entries.farsi: از مبدل هستى اول نماند / هستى بهتر به جاى آن نشاند
entries.english: The Transmuter did not leave you in your first existence: He established a better existence in the place of that;

entries.farsi: از مبدل بين وسايط را بمان / ‏كز وسايط دور گردى ز اصل آن
entries.english: And so on till a hundred thousand states of existence, one after the other, the second better than the beginning.

entries.farsi: همچنين تا صد هزاران هستها / بعد يكديگر دوم به ز ابتدا
entries.english: Regard from the Transmuter; leave the intermediaries, for by the intermediaries you will become far from their Origin.

entries.farsi: واسطه هر جا فزون شد وصل جست / ‏واسطه‏ى كم ذوق وصل افزون‏تر است
entries.english: Wherever the intermediaries increase, union is removed: the intermediaries are less; the delight of union is greater.

entries.farsi: از سبب دانى شود كم حيرتت / ‏حيرت تو ره دهد در حضرتت
entries.english: By knowing the intermediaries your bewilderment is diminished: your bewilderment gives you admission to the Presence.

entries.farsi: اين بقاها از فناها يافتى / ‏از فنايش رو چرا بر تافتى
entries.english: You have gained these lives from deaths: why have you averted your face from dying in Him?

entries.farsi: ز آن فناها چه زيان بودت كه تا / بر بقا چسبيده‏اى اى نافقا
entries.english: What did you lose from those deaths that you have clung to life, O rat?

entries.farsi: چون دوم از اولينت بهتر است / ‏پس فنا جو و مبدل را پرست
entries.english: Since your second is better than your first, therefore seek to die, and worship the Transmuter.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران حشر ديدى اى عنود / تا كنون هر لحظه از بدو وجود
entries.english: O contumacious man, you have experienced a hundred thousand resurrections at every moment from the beginning of your existence until now:

entries.farsi: از جمادى بى‏خبر سوى نما / و ز نما سوى حيات و ابتال
entries.english: From inanimateness unconsciously towards plant life, and from vegetation towards life and tribulation;

entries.farsi: باز سوى عقل و تمييزات خوش / ‏باز سوى خارج اين پنج و شش
entries.english: Again, towards reason and goodly discernments; again, towards outside of these five senses and six directions.

entries.farsi: تا لب بحر اين نشان پايهاست / ‏پس نشان پا درون بحر الست
entries.english: These footprints extend as far as the shore of the Ocean; then the footprints disappear in the Ocean;

entries.farsi: ز انكه منزلهاى خشكى ز احتياط / هست دهها و وطنها و رباط
entries.english: Because, from precaution, the resting-places on the dry land are villages and dwellings and caravanserays,

entries.farsi: باز منزلهاى دريا در وقوف / ‏وقت موج و حبس بى‏عرصه و سقوف
entries.english: On the contrary the resting-places of the Ocean, when its billows swell, have no floor or roof during stay and detention.

entries.farsi: نيست پيدا آن مراحل را سنام / نيست پيدا آن مراحل را سنام
entries.english: These stages have no visible beacon: these resting-places have neither sign nor name.

entries.farsi: هست صد چندان ميان منزلين / ‏آن طرف كه از نما تا روح عين
entries.english: Between every two resting-places Yonder there is a hundred times as much as from the vegetal state to the Essential Spirit.

entries.farsi: در فناها اين بقا را ديده‏اى / ‏بر بقاى جسم چون چفسيده‏اى
entries.english: You have seen this life in deaths: how, are you attached to the life of the body?

entries.farsi: هين بده اى زاغ اين جان باز باش / ‏پيش تبديل خدا جان‏باز باش
entries.english: Come, O crow, give up this soul! Be a falcon; be self-sacrificing in the presence of the Divine transmutation.

entries.farsi: تازه مى‏گير و كهن را مى‏سپار / كه هر امسالت فزون است از سه پار
entries.english: Take the new and surrender the old, for every “this year” of yours is superior to three “last years.”

entries.farsi: گر نباشى نخل وار ايثار كن / ‏كهنه بر كهنه نه و انبار كن
entries.english: If you will not be lavish like the date-palm, pile old rags on old rags and make a heap,

entries.farsi: كهنه و گنديده و پوسيده را / تحفه مى‏بر بهر هر ناديده را
entries.english: And offer the stinking and rotten old rags to every blind man.

entries.farsi: آن كه نو ديد او خريدار تو نيست / ‏صيد حق است او گرفتار تو نيست
entries.english: He that has seen the new is not your customer: he is God’s prey; he is not your captive.

entries.farsi: هر كجا باشند جوق مرغ كور / بر تو جمع آيند اى سيالب شور
entries.english: Wherever is a flock of blind birds, they will gather around you, O brackish flood-water,

entries.farsi: تا فزايد كورى از شورابها / ‏ز انكه آب شور افزايد عمى
entries.english: That blindness may be increased by brackish waters; for brackish water increases blindness.

entries.farsi: اهل دنيا ز آن سبب اعمى دلند / شارب شورابه‏ى آب و گلند
entries.english: Hence the worldly are blind of heart: they are drinkers of the brackish water of clay.

entries.farsi: شور مى‏ده كور مى‏خر در جهان / ‏چون ندارى آب حيوان در نهان
entries.english: Continue to give brackish water and buy the blind in the world, since you have not the Water of Life within you.

entries.farsi: با چنين حالت بقا خواهى و ياد / همچو زنگى در سيه رويى تو شاد
entries.english: In such a state you would happily live and be remembered: in blackness of face, like a Negro, you are rejoicing.

entries.farsi: در سياهى زنگ از آن آسوده است / ‏كو ز زاد و اصل زنگى بوده است
entries.english: The Negro in blackness is pleased, for he has been a negro by birth and nature;

entries.farsi: آن كه روزى شاهد و خوش رو بود / گر سيه گردد تدارك جو بود
entries.english: He that for a day is beloved and beautiful, if he become black, will seek to repair.

entries.farsi: مرغ پرنده چو ماند در زمين / ‏باشد اندر غصه و درد و حنين
entries.english: When the bird that can fly remains on the earth, it is in anguish and grief and lamentation;

entries.farsi: مرغ خانه بر زمين خوش مى‏رود / دانه چين و شاد و شاطر مى‏دود
entries.english: The domestic fowl walks complacently on the earth: it runs about picking grain and happy and bold,

entries.farsi: ز انكه او از اصل بى‏پرواز بود / و آن دگر پرنده و پرواز بود
entries.english: Because by nature it was without flight, while the other was a flier and open-winged.

sections.title_fa: ‏قال النَّبىّ عليه السلام ارحموا ثالثا عزيز قوم ذل و غنى قوم افتقر و عالما يلعب به الجهال
sections.title_en: The Prophet, on whom be peace, said, “Pity three (classes of men): the mighty man of a people who is abased, and the rich man of a people who is impoverished,
and a learned man whom the ignorant make sport of.”

entries.farsi: و الذي كان عزيزا فاحتقر / او صفيا عالما بين المضر
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Take pity on the soul of him who was rich and then became poor,

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه رحم آريد بر / جان من كان غنيا فافتقر
entries.english: And on him who was mighty and became despised, or on one virtuous and learned amongst the Mudar.”

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه با اين سه گروه / ‏رحم آريد ار ز سنگيد و ز كوه
entries.english: The Prophet said, “Show pity to these three classes, if you are of rock and mountain:

entries.farsi: آن كه او بعد از رئيسى خوار شد / و آن توانگر هم كه بى‏دينار شد
entries.english: Him who was made lowly after having been a chief, and the rich man, too, who became impecunious,

entries.farsi: و آن سوم آن عالمى كاندر جهان / ‏مبتال گردد ميان ابلهان
entries.english: And, thirdly, the learned man who in this world becomes afflicted the foolish;

entries.farsi: ز انكه از عزت به خوارى آمدن / ‏همچو قطع عضو باشد از بدن
entries.english: For to come from high to low estate is like the amputation of a limb from the body.”

entries.farsi: عضو گردد مرده كز تن وابريد / نو بريده جنبد اما نى مديد
entries.english: The limb that is cut off from the body becomes dead: newly cut off, it moves, but not for long.

entries.farsi: هر كه از جام أَ لَسْ تُ او خورد پار / هستش امسال آفت رنج و خمار
entries.english: He who drank of the cup of Alast last year, this year he suffers the pain and headache,

entries.farsi: و انكه چون سگ ز اصل كهدانى بود / كى مر او را حرص سلطانى بود
entries.english: While he who, like a dog, is by nature attached to the kennel — how should he have the desire for sovereignty?

entries.farsi: توبه او جويد كه كردست او گناه / ‏آه او گويد كه گم كردست راه
entries.english: He that has sinned seeks to repent; he that has lost the way cries “Alas!”

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى محبوس شدن آن آهو بچه در آخور خران و طعنه‏ى آن خران بر آن غريب گاه به جنگ و گاه به تسخر و مبتال گشتن او به كاه خشك كه غذاى او نيست، و اين صفت بنده‏ى خاص خداست ميان َّهاهل دنيا و اهل هوا و شهوت كه الاسالم بدا غريبا و سيعود غريبا فطوبى للغرباء صدق رسول الل )(ص

sections.title_en: Story of the young gazelle being confined in the donkey-stable, and how the donkey assailed the stranger, now with hostility and now with mockery, and how it was afflicted by dry straw which is not its food. And this is a description of the chosen servant of God amongst human beings and those addicted to passion and sensuality; for “Islam strange appeared, and will become strange again,
and blessed are the strangers.” The Messenger of Allah spoke the truth.

entries.farsi: آهويى را كرد صيادى شكار / اندر آخور كردش آن بى‏زينهار
entries.english: A hunter captured a gazelle: the merciless man put it into a stable.

entries.farsi: آخورى را پر ز گاوان و خران / ‏حبس آهو كرد چون استمگران
entries.english: Like oppressors, he made a stable full of cows and donkeys the prison of the gazelle.

entries.farsi: آهو از وحشت به هر سو مى‏گريخت / ‏او به پيش آن خران شب كاه ريخت
entries.english: The gazelle, wild with terror, was fleeing in every direction: at night he poured straw before the donkeys.

entries.farsi: از مجاعت و اشتها هر گاو و خر / كاه را مى‏خورد خوشتر از شكر
entries.english: By hunger and appetite, every cow and donkey was devouring the straw, sweeter than sugar.

entries.farsi: گاه آهو مى‏رميد از سو به سو / گه ز دود و گرد كه مى‏تافت رو
entries.english: Now the gazelle would run in fright from side to side, now it would turn its face away from the smoke and dust of the straw.

entries.farsi: هر كه را با ضد خود بگذاشتند / آن عقوبت را چو مرگ انگاشتند
entries.english: Whosoever is left with his opposite, they have deemed that punishment as death,

entries.farsi: تا سليمان گفت كان هدهد اگر / عجز را عذرى نگويد معتبر
entries.english: So that Solomon said, “Unless the hoopoe makes a respectable excuse for his absence,

entries.farsi: بكشمش يا خود دهم او را عذاب / ‏يك عذاب سخت بيرون از حساب
entries.english: I will kill him or inflict upon him a torment, a torment severe beyond calculation.”

entries.farsi: هان كدام است آن عذاب اى معتمد / در قفس بودن بغير جنس خود
entries.english: Listen, what is that torment, O trusted? To be in a cage without your same kind.

entries.farsi: زين بدن اندر عذابى اى بشر / مرغ روحت بسته با جنسى دگر
entries.english: O Man, you art in torment on account of this body: the bird, your spirit, is imprisoned with one of another kind.

entries.farsi: روح باز است و طبايع زاغها / دارد از زاغان و جغدان داغها
entries.english: The spirit is a falcon, and the properties are crows: it has painful brands from the crows and owls.

entries.farsi: او بمانده در ميانشان زار زار / همچو بو بكرى به شهر سبزوار
entries.english: It remains amongst them in sore misery, like an Abu Bakr in the city of Sabzawár.

sections.title_fa: حكايت محمد خوارزمشاه كه شهر سبزوار كه همه رافضى باشند به جنگ بگرفت، امان جان خواستند، گفت آن گه امان دهم كه از اين شهر پيش من به هديه ابو بكر نامى بياريد
sections.title_en: Story of Muhammad Khwárizmsháh who took by war the city of Sabzawár, where all are Ráfizís
(extreme Shí‘ites). They begged him to spare their lives, he said, “I will grant security as soon as you produce from this city a man named Abu Bakr and present him to me.”

entries.farsi: شد محمد الپ الغ خوارزمشاه / ‏در قتال سبزوار پر پناه
entries.english: Muhammad Alp Ulugh Khwárizmsháh marched to battle against Sabzawár, full of refuge.

entries.farsi: تنگشان آورد لشكرهاى او / اسپهش افتاد در قتل عدو
entries.english: His troops reduced them to straits; his army fell to killing the foe.

entries.farsi: سجده آوردند پيشش كالامان / ‏حلقه‏مان در گوش كن وابخش جان
entries.english: They prostrated themselves before him, crying, “Mercy! Make us your thralls, spare our lives!

entries.farsi: هر خراج و صلتى كه بايدت / ‏آن ز ما هر موسمى افزايدت
entries.english: Whatever you require of tribute or presents will come to you from us with increase at every fixed time.

entries.farsi: جان ما آن تو است اى شير خو / پيش ما چندى امانت باش گو
entries.english: Our lives are your, O lion-natured: let them be on deposit with us for a while.”

entries.farsi: گفت نرهانيد از من جان خويش / ‏تا نياريدم ابو بكرى به پيش
entries.english: He replied, “You will not save your lives from me unless you bring an Abu Bakr into my presence.

entries.farsi: تا مرا بو بكر نام از شهرتان / ‏هديه ناريد اى رميده امتان
entries.english: Unless you bring to me as a gift from your city one whose name is Abu Bakr, O people who have fled,

entries.farsi: بدروم تان همچو كشت اى قوم دون / ‏نه خراج استانم و نه هم فسون
entries.english: I will mow you down like corn, O vile folk: I will accept neither tribute nor blandishments.”

entries.farsi: بس جوال زر كشيدندش به راه / ‏كز چنين شهرى ابو بكرى مخواه
entries.english: They offered him many sacks of gold, saying, “Do not demand an Abu Bakr from a city like this.

entries.farsi: كى بود بو بكر اندر سبزوار / يا كلوخ خشك اندر جويبار
entries.english: How should there be an Abu Bakr in Sabzawár, or a dry sod in the river?”

entries.farsi: رو بتابيد از زر و گفت اى مغان / ‏تا نياريدم ابو بكر ارمغان
entries.english: He averted his face from the gold and said, “O Magians, unless you bring me an Abu Bakr as an offering,

entries.farsi: هيچ سودى نيست كودك نيستم / ‏تا به زر و سيم حيران بيستم
entries.english: It is of no avail. I am not a child that I should stand dumbfounded by gold and silver.”

entries.farsi: تا نيارى سجده نرهى اى زبون / ‏گر بپيمايى تو مسجد را به كون
entries.english: Unless you prostrate yourself, you will not escape, O wretch, if you traverse the mosque on your séant.

entries.farsi: منهيان انگيختند از چپ و راست / ‏كاندر اين ويرانه بو بكرى كجاست
entries.english: They dispatched emissaries, where in this desolate place an Abu Bakr was.

entries.farsi: بعد سه روز و سه شب كه شتافتند / يك ابو بكرى نزارى يافتند
entries.english: After three days and three nights, during which they made haste, they found an emaciated Abu Bakr.

entries.farsi: رهگذر بود و بمانده از مرض / ‏در يكى گوشه‏ى خرابه پر حرض
entries.english: He was a wayfarer and, on account of sickness, had remained in the corner of a ruin, in utter exhaustion.

entries.farsi: خفته بود او در يكى كنجى خراب / ‏چون بديدندش بگفتندش شتاب
entries.english: He was lying in a ruined nook. When they espied him, they said to him hurriedly,

entries.farsi: خيز كه سلطان ترا طالب شده‏ست / ‏كز تو خواهد شهر ما از قتل رست
entries.english: “Arise! The Sultan has demanded you: by you our city will be saved from slaughter.”

entries.farsi: گفت اگر پايم بدى يا مقدمى / ‏خود به راه خود به مقصد رفتمى
entries.english: He replied, “If I had the foot or any arrival, I myself would have gone by my own road to my destination.

entries.farsi: اندر اين دشمن‏كده كى ماندمى / ‏سوى شهر دوستان مى‏راندمى
entries.english: How should I have remained in this abode of my enemies? I would have pushed on towards the city of my friends.”

entries.farsi: تخته‏ى مرده كشان بفراشتند / بر كتف بو بكر را برداشتند
entries.english: They raised the corpse-bearers’ board and lifted our Abu Bakr.

entries.farsi: سوى خوارزمشاه حمالان كشان / ‏مى‏كشيدندش كه تا بيند نشان
entries.english: The carriers were taking him along to Khwárizmsháh, that he might behold the token.

entries.farsi: سبزوار است اين جهان و مرد حق / ‏اندر اينجا ضايع است و ممتحق
entries.english: Sabzawár is this world, and in this place the man of God is wasted and good for-nothing.

entries.farsi: هست خوارمشاه يزدان جليل / ‏دل همى‏خواهد از اين قوم رذيل
entries.english: Khwárizmsháh is God Almighty: He demands from this wicked folk the heart.

entries.farsi: گفت ال ينظر الى تصويركم / ‏فابتغوا ذا القلب فى تدبيركم
entries.english: The Prophet said, “He does not regard your form: therefore in your devising seek you the owner of the Heart.”

entries.farsi: من ز صاحب دل كنم در تو نظر / نى به نقش سجده و ايثار زر
entries.english: “I regard you through the owner of the Heart, not because of the marks of prostration and the giving away of gold.”

entries.farsi: تو دل خود را چو دل پنداشتى / ‏جستجوى اهل دل بگذاشتى
entries.english: Since you have deemed your heart to be the Heart, you have abandoned the search after those who possess the Heart—

entries.farsi: دل كه گر هفصد چو اين هفت آسمان / ‏اندر او آيد شود ياوه و نهان
entries.english: The Heart into which if seven hundred like these Seven Heavens should enter, they would be lost and hidden.

entries.farsi: اين چنين دل ريزه‏ها را دل مگو / سبزوار اندر ابو بكرى مجو
entries.english: Do not call such fragments of heart as these “the Heart”: do not seek an Abu Bakr in Sabzawár!

entries.farsi: صاحب دل آينه‏ى شش رو شود / حق از او در شش جهت ناظر بود
entries.english: The owner of the Heart becomes a six-faced mirror: through him God looks upon the six directions.

entries.farsi: هر كه اندر شش جهت دارد مقر / نكندش بى‏واسطه‏ى او حق نظر
entries.english: Whosoever has his dwelling-place in six directions God doth not look upon him except through the mediation of him.

entries.farsi: بى‏از او ندهد كسى را حق نوال / ‏شمه‏اى گفتم من از صاحب وصال
entries.english: If He reject, He does it for his sake; and if He accept, he likewise is the authority.

entries.farsi: گر كند رد از براى او كند / ور قبول آرد همو باشد سند
entries.english: Without him God does not bestow bounty on any one. I have told one sample of the possessor of union.

entries.farsi: موهبت را بر كف دستش نهد / و ز كفش آن را به مرحومان دهد
entries.english: He lays His gift on the palm of his hand, and from his palm dispenses it to those who are the objects of His mercy.

entries.farsi: با كفش درياى كل را اتصال / ‏هست بى‏چون و چگونه و بر كمال
entries.english: The unity of the Universal Sea with his palm is unqualified and unconditional and perfect.

entries.farsi: اتصالى كه نگنجد در كالم / ‏گفتنش تكليف باشد و السلام
entries.english: A unity that is not containable in words— to speak of it was a vain task, so farewell.

entries.farsi: صد جوال زر بيارى اى غنى / ‏حق بگويد دل بيار اى منحنى
entries.english: O rich man, you bring a hundred sacks of gold, God will say, “Bring the Heart, O you that are bent.

entries.farsi: گر ز تو راضى است دل من راضى‏ام / ‏ور ز تو معرض بود اعراضى‏ام
entries.english: If the Heart is pleased with you, I am pleased; and if it be averse to you, I am averse.

entries.farsi: ننگرم در تو در آن دل بنگرم / ‏تحفه او را آر اى جان بر درم
entries.english: I do not regard you, I regard that Heart: bring it, O soul, as a gift to My door!

entries.farsi: با تو او چون است هستم من چنان / ‏زير پاى مادران باشد جنان
entries.english: According as it is in relation to you, so am I: Paradise is under the feet of mothers.”

entries.farsi: مادر و بابا و اصل خلق اوست / ‏اى خنك آن كس كه داند دل ز پوست
entries.english: It is the mother and father and origin of the creatures: oh, blest is that one who knows the Heart from the skin.

entries.farsi: تو بگويى نك دل آوردم به تو / گويدت پر است از اين دلها قتو
entries.english: You will say, “Lo, I have brought You a heart”: He will say to you, “Qutú is full of these hearts.

entries.farsi: آن دلى آور كه قطب عالم اوست / ‏جان جان جان جان آدم اوست
entries.english: Bring the Heart that is the Qutb of the world and the soul of the soul of the soul of the soul of Adam.”

entries.farsi: از براى آن دل پر نور و بر / هست آن سلطان دلها منتظر
entries.english: The Sultan of hearts is waiting expectantly for that Heart full of light and goodness.

entries.farsi: تو بگردى روزها در سبزوار / آن چنان دل را نيابى ز اعتبار
entries.english: You may wander days in Sabzawár; you will not find a Heart like that by careful observation.

entries.farsi: پس دل پژمرده‏ى پوسيده جان / ‏بر سر تخته نهى آن سو كشان
entries.english: Then you will lay upon a bier the corrupt heart, whose soul is rotten, to carry Yonder,

entries.farsi: كه دل آوردم ترا اى شهريار / به از اين دل نبود اندر سبزوار
entries.english: And say, “I bring You a heart, O King: there is no better heart than this in Sabzawár.”

entries.farsi: گويدت اين گورخانه است اى جرى / ‏كه دل مرده بدين جا آورى
entries.english: He will answer you, saying, “O audacious man, is this a graveyard that you should bring a dead heart here?

entries.farsi: رو بياور آن دلى كاو شاه خوست / ‏كه امان سبزوار كون از اوست
entries.english: Go, bring the Heart that is kingly, from which is the security of the Sabzawár of existence.”

entries.farsi: گويى آن دل زين جهان پنهان بود / ز انكه ظلمت با ضيا ضدان بود
entries.english: You may say that that Heart is hidden from this world, because darkness and light are opposites.

entries.farsi: ُدشمنى آن دل از روز أَ لَسْ ت / ‏سبزوار طبع را ميراثى است
entries.english: From the Day of Alast there is a hereditary enmity of that Heart to the Sabzawár of the carnal nature;

entries.farsi: ز انكه او باز است و دنيا شهر زاغ / ‏ديدن ناجنس بر ناجنس داغ
entries.english: For it is a falcon, while this world is the city of the crow: the sight of one who is uncongenial inflicts pain upon him who is not his congener;

entries.farsi: مى‏كند آرى نه از بهر نياز / تا كه ناصح كم كند نصح دراز
entries.english: And if he behaves with mildness, he is acting hypocritically: he is seeking an advantage for himself by conciliating.

entries.farsi: ور كند نرمى نفاقى مى‏كند / ز استمالت ارتفاقى مى‏كند
entries.english: He assents, not on account of sincere feeling, in order that the admonisher may curtail his long admonition;

entries.farsi: ز انكه اين زاغ خس مردار جو / صد هزاران مكر دارد تو بتو
entries.english: For the vile carrion-seeking crow has a hundred thousand manifold tricks.

entries.farsi: گر پذيرند آن نفاقش را رهيد / شد نفاقش عين صدق مستفيد
entries.english: If they accept his hypocrisy, he is saved: his hypocrisy becomes identical with the sincerity of him who benefits by instruction,

entries.farsi: ز انكه آن صاحب دل با كر و فر / هست در بازار ما معيوب خر
entries.english: Because the august owner of the Heart is a buyer of damaged goods in our bazaar.

entries.farsi: صاحب دل جو اگر بى‏جان نه‏اى / ‏جنس دل شو گر ضد سلطان نه‏اى
entries.english: Seek the owner of the Heart, if you are not soulless: become a congener of the Heart, if you are not an adversary of the Sultan.

entries.farsi: آن كه زرق او خوش آيد مر ترا / آن ولى تست نه خاص خدا
entries.english: That one whose hypocrisy pleases you, he is your saint, not the elect of God.

entries.farsi: هر كه او بر خو و بر طبع تو زيست / ‏پيش طبع تو ولى است و نبى است
entries.english: Whosoever lives in accordance with your disposition and nature seems to your nature to be a saint and a prophet.

entries.farsi: رو هوا بگذار تا بويت شود / و آن مشام خوش عبر جويت شود
entries.english: Go, renounce sensuality in order that the scent may be yours and that the sweet ambergris-seeking organ of smell may be yours.

entries.farsi: از هوارانى دماغت فاسد است / ‏مشك و عنبر پيش مغزت كاسد است
entries.english: Your nose is corrupted by sensual indulgence: to your sense of smell musk and ambergris are unsalable.

entries.farsi: حد ندارد اين سخن و آهوى ما / مى‏گريزد اندر آخور جا به جا
entries.english: This discourse has no bound, and our gazelle is running to and fro in flight in the stable.

sections.title_fa: ‏بقيه‏ى قصه‏ى آهو و آخور خران
sections.title_en: The remainder of the Story of the gazelle in the donkey-stable.

entries.farsi: روزها آن آهوى خوش ناف نر / در شكنجه بود در اصطبل خر
entries.english: During days the sweet-navelled male gazelle was in torment in the donkey-stable,

entries.farsi: مضطرب در نزع چون ماهى ز خشك / ‏در يكى حقه معذب پشك و مشك
entries.english: Like a fish wriggling in the death-agony from dry ground, dung and musk tortured in the same box.

entries.farsi: يك خرش گفتى كه ها اين بو الوحوش / ‏طبع شاهان دارد و ميران خموش
entries.english: One donkey would say to his neighbour, “Ha! This wild fellow has the nature of kings and princes. Hush!”

entries.farsi: و آن دگر تسخر زدى كز جر و مد / گوهر آورده‏ست كى ارزان دهد
entries.english: And the other would mock, saying, “By ebb and flow he has gained a pearl: how should he sell cheaply?”

entries.farsi: و آن خرى گفتى كه با اين نازكى / ‏بر سرير شاه شو گو متكى
entries.english: And another donkey would say, “With this fastidiousness, let him recline on the imperial throne!”

entries.farsi: آن خرى شد تخمه‏ى وز خوردن بماند / پس به رسم دعوت آهو را بخواند
entries.english: A certain donkey became ill with indigestion and was unable to eat; therefore he gave the gazelle a formal invitation.

entries.farsi: سر چنين كرد او كه نه رو اى فالن / ‏اشتهايم نيست هستم ناتوان
entries.english: He shook his head, “Nay, Begone, O such-and such: I have no appetite, I am unwell.”

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏دانم كه نازى مى‏كنى / ‏يا ز ناموس احترازى مى‏كنى
entries.english: He replied, “I know that you are showing disdain, or holding aloof in regard for your reputation.”

entries.farsi: گفت او با خود كه آن طعمه‏ى تو است / ‏كه از آن اجزاى تو زنده و نو است
entries.english: He said to himself, “That is your food, whereby your limbs are revived and renewed.

entries.farsi: من اليف مرغزارى بوده‏ام / ‏در زالل و روضه‏ها آسوده‏ام
entries.english: I have been familiar with a pasture; I have reposed amongst clear water and meadows.

entries.farsi: گر قضا انداخت ما را در عذاب / ‏كى رود آن خو و طبع مستطاب
entries.english: If Destiny has cast me into torment, how should that goodly disposition and nature depart?

entries.farsi: سنبل و الله و سپر غم نيز هم / ‏با هزاران ناز و نفرت خورده‏ام
entries.english: If I have become a beggar, how should I have the face of a beggar? And if my raiment becomes old, I am new.

entries.farsi: گر گدا گشتم گدا رو كى شوم / ‏ور لباسم كهنه گردد من نوم
entries.english: I have eaten hyacinth and anemone and sweet basil too with a thousand disdains and disgusts.”

entries.farsi: گفت آرى الف مى‏زن الف الف / ‏در غريبى بس توان گفتن گزاف
entries.english: He said, “Yes; boast and boast and boast away! In a strange country one can utter many an idle brag.”

entries.farsi: گفت نافم خود گواهى مى‏دهد / منتى بر عود و عنبر مى‏نهد
entries.english: He replied, “Truly my musk gland bears witness: it confers a favour on aloes-wood and ambergris.

entries.farsi: ليك آن را كه شنود صاحب مشام / ‏بر خر سرگين پرست آن شد حرام
entries.english: But who will listen to that? He that has the sense of smell. It is taboo for the donkey addicted to dung.

entries.farsi: خر گميز خر ببويد بر طريق / ‏مشك چون عرضه كنم با اين فريق
entries.english: The donkey smells donkey’s urine on the road: how should I offer musk to this class?”

entries.farsi: بهر اين گفت آن رسول مستجيب / ‏رمز الاسالم فى الدنيا غريب
entries.english: Hence the Prophet, responsive, spoke, the parable, “Islam is a stranger in this world,”

entries.farsi: ز انكه خويشانش هم از وى مى‏رمند / گر چه با ذاتش ماليك هم دمند
entries.english: Because even his (the true Moslem’s) kinsfolk are fleeing from him, though the angels are in harmony with his essence.

entries.farsi: صورتش را جنس مى‏بينند انام / ‏ليك از وى مى‏نيابند آن مشام
entries.english: The people deem his form homogeneous, but they do not perceive in him that fragrance.

entries.farsi: همچو شيرى در ميان نقش گاو / دور مى‏بينش ولى او را مكاو
entries.english: Like a lion in the shape of a cow: behold him from afar but do not investigate him!

entries.farsi: ور بكاوى ترك گاو تن بگو / كه بدرد گاو را آن شير خو
entries.english: And if you investigate, take leave of the cow, the body; for that lion natured one will tear the cow to pieces.

entries.farsi: طبع گاوى از سرت بيرون كند / خوى حيوانى ز حيوان بر كند
entries.english: He will expel the bovine nature from your head; he will uproot animality from the animal.

entries.farsi: گاو باشى شير گردى نزد او / گر تو با گاوى خوشى شيرى مجو
entries.english: You are a cow, you will become a lion near him; if you are glad to be a cow, do not seek to be a lion.

sections.title_fa: تفسير إِنِّي أَرى‏ سَ بْعَ بَقَراتٍ سِ مانٍ يَأْكُلُهُنَّ سَ بْعٌ عِ جافٌ ، آن گاوان الغر را خدا به صفت شيران گرسنه آفريده بود تا آن هفت گاو فربه را به اشتها مى‏خوردند، اگر چه آن خيالات صور گاوان در آينه‏ى خواب بنمودند تو معنى نگر

sections.title_en: Commentary on “Truly I saw seven fat cows which seven lean cows devoured.” God had created those lean cows with the qualities of hungry lions, to the end that they might devour the seven fat ones with avidity. Although the forms of those cows were shown as phantoms in the mirror of dream, do you regard the reality!

entries.farsi: آن عزيز مصر مى‏ديدى به خواب / ‏چون كه چشم غيب را شد فتح باب
entries.english: The Lord of Egypt saw in dream, when the door of his inward eye was opened,

entries.farsi: هفت گاو فربه بس پرورى / ‏خوردشان آن هفت گاو الغرى
entries.english: Seven fat cows, exceedingly well-nourished: the seven lean cows devoured them.

entries.farsi: در درون شيران بدند آن الغران / ‏ور نه گاوان را نبودندى خوران
entries.english: The lean ones were lions within; else they would not have been devouring the cows.

entries.farsi: مرد را خوش واخورد فردش كند / صاف گردد دردش ار دردش كند
entries.english: The man of works, then, is human in appearance, but in him is concealed a man-eating lion.

entries.farsi: پس بشر آمد به صورت مرد كار / ليك در وى شير پنهان مرد خوار
entries.english: He heartily devours the man and makes him single: his dregs become pure if he inflicts pain upon him.

entries.farsi: ز آن يكى درد او ز جمله‏ى دردها / وارهد پا بر نهد او بر سها
entries.english: By that one pain he is delivered from all dregs: he sets his foot upon Suha.

entries.farsi: چند گويى همچو زاغ پر نحوس / ‏اى خليل از بهر چه كشتى خروس
entries.english: How long will you speak like the ill-omened crow? “O Khalíl, wherefore didst you kill the cock?”

entries.farsi: گفت فرمان حكمت فرمان بگو / تا مسبح گردم آن را مو به مو
entries.english: He replied, “The command.” “Tell the wisdom of the command, that I may glorify that punctiliously.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه كشتن خليل عليه السلام خروس را اشارت به قمع و قهر كدام صفت بود از صفات مذمومات مهلكات در باطن مريد
sections.title_en: Explaining that the killing of the cock by Abraham, on whom be peace, signifies the subdual and subjugation of certain blameworthy and pernicious qualities in the heart of the disciple.

entries.farsi: شهوتى است او و بس شهوت پرست / ‏ز آن شراب زهرناك ژاژ مست
entries.english: He is lustful and much addicted to lust, intoxicated by that poisonous insipid wine.

entries.farsi: گر نه بهر نسل بودى اى وصى / ‏آدم از ننگش بكردى خود خصى
entries.english: Had not lust been for the sake of procreation, O executor, Adam for shame of it would have made himself a eunuch.

entries.farsi: گفت ابليس لعين دادار را / دام زفتى خواهم اين اشكار را
entries.english: The accursed Iblís said to the Dispenser of justice, “I want a powerful snare for this prey.”

entries.farsi: زر و سيم و گله‏ى اسبش نمود / كه بدين تانى خاليق را ربود
entries.english: He showed to him gold and silver and herds of horses, saying, “By means of this you can seduce mankind.”

entries.farsi: پس در و گوهر ز معدنهاى خوش / ‏كرد آن پس مانده را حق پيش كش
entries.english: He cried “Bravo!” but let his lip drop sourly: he became wrinkled and sour like a lemon.

entries.farsi: گفت شاباش و ترش آويخت لنج / ‏شد ترنجيده و ترش همچون ترنج
entries.english: Then God offered to that fallen-one gold and jewels from His goodly mines,

entries.farsi: گير اين دام دگر را اى لعين / ‏گفت زين افزون ده اى نعم المعين
entries.english: Saying, “Take this other snare, O accursed one.” He replied, “Give more than this, O most excellent Helper.”

entries.farsi: چرب و شيرين و شرابات ثمين / ‏دادش و بس جامه‏ى ابريشمين
entries.english: He gave him oily and sweet and costly sherbets and many silken robes.

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب بيش از اين خواهم مدد / تا ببندمشان بحبل من مسد
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, I want more assistance than this, to bind them with a cord of palm-fibre.

entries.farsi: تا كه مستانت كه نر و پر دلند / مردوار آن بندها را بگسلند
entries.english: In order that Your devotees, who are fierce and courageous, may manfully burst those bonds,

entries.farsi: تا بدين دام و رسنهاى هوا / مرد تو گردد ز نامردان جدا
entries.english: And that by means of this snare and cords of sensuality Your man may be separated from the unmanly,

entries.farsi: دام ديگر خواهم اى سلطان تخت / ‏دام مرد انداز و حيلت ساز سخت
entries.english: I want another snare, O Sovereign of the throne— a mighty cunning snare that will lay men low.”

entries.farsi: خمر و چنگ آورد پيش او نهاد / نيم خنده زد بدان شد نيم شاد
entries.english: He brought and placed before him wine and harp: thereat he smiled faintly and was moderately pleased.

entries.farsi: سوى اضالل ازل پيغام كرد / كه بر آر از قعر بحر فتنه گرد
entries.english: He sent a message to the eternal Foreordainment of perdition, saying, “Raise dust from the bottom of the sea of temptation.

entries.farsi: نى يكى از بندگانت موسى است / ‏پرده‏ها در بحر او از گرد بست
entries.english: Is not Moses one of Your servants? He tied veils of dust on the sea.

entries.farsi: آب از هر سو عنان را وا كشيد / از تگ دريا غبارى بر جهيد
entries.english: The water retreated on every side: from the bottom of the sea a dust shot up.”

entries.farsi: چون كه خوبى زنان با او نمود / كه ز عقل و صبر مردان مى‏فزود
entries.english: When He showed unto him the beauty of women that was prevailing over the reason and self-restraint of men,

entries.farsi: پس زد انگشتك به رقص اندر فتاد / كه بده زوتر رسيدم در مراد
entries.english: Then he snapped his fingers and began to dance, crying, “Give me as quickly as possible: I have attained my desire.”

entries.farsi: و آن صفاى عارض آن دلبران / ‏كه بسوزد چون سپند اين دل بر آن
entries.english: When he saw those languorous eyes which make the reason and understanding unquiet,

entries.farsi: چون بديد آن چشمهاى پر خمار / كه كند عقل و خرد را بى‏قرار
entries.english: And the loveliness of that fascinating cheek on which this heart burns like rue-seed,

entries.farsi: رو و خال و ابرو و لب چون عقيق / ‏گوييا حق تافت از پرده‏ى رقيق
entries.english: Face and mole and eyebrow and lip like cornelian, it was as though God shone forth through a subtle veil.

entries.farsi: ديد او آن غنج و بر جست او سبك / ‏چون تجلى حق از پرده‏ى تنك
entries.english: He deemed that coquetry and light springing gait to be like the revelation of Divine glory through a thin veil.

sections.title_fa: ِتفسير خَلَقْنَا الإِْنْسانَ فِي أَحْ سَ نِ تَقْوِيمٍ ثُمَّ رَدَدْناهُ أَسْ فَلَ سافِلِينَ‏و تفسير وَ مَنْ نُعَمِّرْهُ نُنَكِّسْ هُ فِي الْخَلْق
sections.title_en: Commentary on “We created Man in the best proportion, then We reduced him to the lowest of the low”; and on “And to whomsoever We grant long life, We cause him to relapse in constitution.”

entries.farsi: آدم حسن و ملك ساجد شده / ‏همچو آدم باز معزول آمده
entries.english: The beauty personified in Adam, to whom the angels bow down, is afterwards deposed, like Adam.

entries.farsi: گفت آوه بعد هستى نيستى / ‏گفت جرمت اين كه افزون زيستى
entries.english: It cries, “Alas, after existence non-existence!” He says, “Your crime is this, that you have lived too long.”

entries.farsi: جبرئيلش مى‏كشاند موكشان / ‏كه برو زين خلد و از جوق خوشان
entries.english: Gabriel, dragging it by the hair, leads it away, saying, “Leave this Paradise and the company of the fair ones.”

entries.farsi: گفت بعد از عز اين اذالل چيست / ‏گفت آن داد است و اينت داورى است
entries.english: It says, “What is this abasement after exaltation?” He replies, “That is a gift, and this is judgement on you.”

entries.farsi: جبرئيال سجده مى‏كردى به جان / ‏چون كنون مى‏رانيم تو از جنان
entries.english: “O Gabriel, you bowed down with your soul: why are you now driving me from Paradise?

entries.farsi: حله مى‏پرد ز من در امتحان / ‏همچو برگ از نخل در فصل خزان
entries.english: My robes are flying from me in tribulation, like leaves from the date-palm in the season of autumn.”

entries.farsi: آن رخى كه تاب او بد ماه‏وار / شد به پيرى همچو پشت سوسمار
entries.english: The countenance whose splendour was moon-like becomes with old age like the back of the Libyan lizard;

entries.farsi: و آن سرو فرق گش شعشع شده / ‏وقت پيرى ناخوش و اصلع شده
entries.english: And the fair head and crown that once were radiant become ugly and bald at the time of old;

entries.farsi: و آن قد صف در نازان چون سنان / ‏گشته در پيرى دو تا همچون كمان
entries.english: And the tall proud figure, piercing the ranks like a spear-point, in old age is bent double like a bow.

entries.farsi: رنگ الله گشته رنگ زعفران / ‏زور شيرش گشته چون زهره‏ى زنان
entries.english: The colour of red anemone becomes the colour of saffron; his lion-like strength becomes as the courage of women.

entries.farsi: آن كه مردى در بغل كردى به فن / ‏مى‏بگيرندش بغل وقت شدن
entries.english: He that used to grip a man in his arms by skill, they take hold of his arms at the time of departure.

entries.farsi: اين خود آثار غم و پژمردگى است / ‏هر يكى زينها رسول مردگى است
entries.english: Truly these are marks of pain and decay: every one of them is a messenger of death.

sections.title_fa: ٍتفسير أَسْ فَلَ سافِلِينَ إِلاَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَ عَ مِلُوا الصَّ الِحاتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْ رٌ غَ يْرُ مَمْنُون
sections.title_en: Commentary on “The lowest of the low, except those who have believed and wrought good works;
for they shall have a reward that is not cut off.”

entries.farsi: ليك گر باشد طبيبش نور حق / ‏نيست از پيرى و تب نقصان و دق
entries.english: But if his physician be the Light of God, there is no loss or crushing blow from old age and fever.

entries.farsi: سستى او هست چون سستى مست / ‏كاندر آن سستيش رشك رستم است
entries.english: His weakness is like the weakness of the intoxicated, for in his weakness he is the envy of a Rustam.

entries.farsi: گر بميرد استخوانش غرق ذوق / ‏ذره ذره‏ش در شعاع نور شوق
entries.english: If he dies, his bones are drowned in savour; every mote of him is in the beams of the light of love-desire.

entries.farsi: وان كه آنش نيست باغ بى‏ثمر / كه خزانش مى‏كند زير و زبر
entries.english: And he who has not that is an orchard without fruit, which the autumn brings to ruin.

entries.farsi: گل نماند خارها ماند سياه / ‏زرد و بى‏مغز آمده چون تل كاه
entries.english: The roses remain not; the black thorns remain: it becomes pale and spineless like a heap of straw.

entries.farsi: تا چه زلت كرد آن باغ اى خدا / كه از او اين حله‏ها گردد جدا
entries.english: O God, I wonder what fault did that orchard commit, that these robes should be stripped from it.

entries.farsi: خويشتن را ديد و ديد خويشتن / ‏زهر قتال است هين اى ممتحن
entries.english: “It paid regard to itself, and self-regard is a deadly poison. Beware, O you who are put to the trial!”

entries.farsi: شاهدى كز عشق او عالم گريست / ‏عالمش مى‏راند از خود جرم چيست
entries.english: The minion for love of whom the world wept— the world is repulsing him from itself: what is crime?

entries.farsi: جرم آن كه زيور عاريه بست / ‏كرد دعوى كاين حلل ملك من است
entries.english: “The crime is that he put on a borrowed adornment and pretended that these robes were his own property.

entries.farsi: واستانيم آن كه تا داند يقين / ‏خرمن آن ماست خوبان دانه چين
entries.english: We take them back, in order that he may know for sure that the stack is Ours and the fair ones are gleaners;

entries.farsi: تا بداند كان حلل عاريه بود / پرتوى بود آن ز خورشيد وجود
entries.english: That he may know that those robes were a loan: it was a ray from the Sun of Being.”

entries.farsi: آن جمال و قدرت و فضل و هنر / ز آفتاب حسن كرد اين سو سفر
entries.english: That beauty and power and virtue and knowledge have journeyed here from the Sun of Excellence.

entries.farsi: باز مى‏گردند چون استارها / نور آن خورشيد زين ديوارها
entries.english: They, the light of that Sun, turn back again, like the stars, from these walls.

entries.farsi: آن كه كرد او در رخ خوبانت دنگ / ‏نور خورشيد است از شيشه‏ى سه رنگ
entries.english: The Sunbeam has gone home; every wall is left dark and black.

entries.farsi: پرتو خورشيد شد وا جايگاه / ‏ماند هر ديوار تاريك و سياه
entries.english: That which made you amazed at the faces of the fair is the Light of the Sun from the three-coloured glass.

entries.farsi: شيشه‏هاى رنگ رنگ آن نور را / مى‏نمايند اين چنين رنگين به ما
entries.english: The glasses of diverse hue cause that Light to seem coloured like this to us.

entries.farsi: چون نماند شيشه‏هاى رنگ رنگ / ‏نور بى‏رنگت كند آن گاه دنگ
entries.english: When the many-coloured glasses are no more, then the colourless Light makes you amazed.

entries.farsi: خوى كن بى‏شيشه ديدن نور را / ‏تا چو شيشه بشكند نبود عمى
entries.english: Make it your habit to behold the Light without the glass, in order that when the glass is shattered there may not be blindness.

entries.farsi: قانعى با دانش آموخته / ‏در چراغ غير چشم افروخته
entries.english: You are content with knowledge learned: you have lit your eye at another’s lamp.

entries.farsi: او چراغ خويش بربايد كه تا / تو بدانى مستعيرى نى فتا
entries.english: He takes away his lamp, that you may know you are a borrower, not a giver.

entries.farsi: گر تو كردى شكر و سعى مجتهد / غم مخور كه صد چنان بازت دهد
entries.english: If you have rendered thanks and made the utmost exertion, be not grieved, for He will give a hundred such in return;

entries.farsi: ور نكردى شكر اكنون خون‏گرى / ‏كه شده ست آن حسن از كافر برى
entries.english: But if you have not rendered thanks, weep blood now, for that excellence has become quit of the ungrateful.

entries.farsi: أمة الكفران أَضَ لَّ أعمالهم / ‏أمة الايمان أَصْ لَحَ بالهم
entries.english: He causes the works of the unbelieving people to be lost; He makes the state of the believing people to prosper.

entries.farsi: گم شد از بى‏شكر خوبى و هنر / كه دگر هرگز نبيند ز آن اثر
entries.english: From the ungrateful man excellence and knowledge disappear, so that never again does he see a trace of them.

entries.farsi: خويشى و بى‏خويشى و شكر و وداد / رفت ز آن سان كه نياردشان به ياد
entries.english: Affinity and non-affinity and gratitude and affection vanish in such wise that he cannot remember them;

entries.farsi: كه أَضَ لَّ أَعْ مالَهُمْ اى كافران / ‏جستن كام است از هر كامران
entries.english: For, O ingrates, He causes their works to be lost are the flight of object of desire from everyone who has obtained his desire,

entries.farsi: جز ز اهل شكر و اصحاب وفا / كه مر ايشان راست دولت در قفا
entries.english: Excepting the thankful and faithful who are attended by fortune.

entries.farsi: دولت رفته كجا قوت دهد / دولت آينده خاصيت دهد
entries.english: How should the past fortune bestow strength? It is the future fortune that bestows a special virtue.

entries.farsi: قرض ده زين دولت اندر اقرضوا / تا كه صد دولت ببينى پيش رو
entries.english: In “Lend,” make a loan from this fortune, that you may see a hundred fortunes before your face.

entries.farsi: اندكى زين شرب كم كن بهر خويش / ‏تا كه حوض كوثرى يابى به پيش
entries.english: Diminish a little for your own sake this drinking, that you may find in front the basin of Kawthar.

entries.farsi: جرعه بر خاك وفا آن كس كه ريخت / ‏كى تواند صيد دولت زو گريخت
entries.english: He who poured a draught on the earth of faithfulness, how should the prey, fortune, be able to flee from him?

entries.farsi: اى اجل وى ترك غارت ساز ده / ‏هر چه بردى زين شكوران باز ده
entries.english: He gladdens their hearts, for He makes their state to prosper: He restores their entertainment after they have perished.

entries.farsi: خوش كند دلشان كه أَصْ لَحَ بالهم / ‏رد من بعد النوى انزالهم
entries.english: “O Death, O Turcoman who plunders the village, give back whatever you have taken from these thankful ones.”

entries.farsi: وا دهد ايشان بنپذيرند آن / ‏ز انكه منعم گشته‏اند از رخت جان
entries.english: He gives it back; they will not receive it, for they have been endowed with the goods of spiritual life.

entries.farsi: ا عوض ديديم آن گه چون عوض / ‏رفت از ما حاجت و حرص و غرض
entries.english: “We are Sufis and have cast off our mantles: we will not take back after we have gambled away.

entries.farsi: صوفييم و خرقه‏ها انداختيم / ‏باز نستانيم چون درباختيم
entries.english: We have seen the recompense from God—how can there be a worldly recompense then? Want and desire and object are gone from us.

entries.farsi: ز آب شور و مهلكى بيرون شديم / ‏بر رحيق و چشمه‏ى كوثر زديم
entries.english: We have emerged from a briny and destroying water, we have attained to the pure wine and the fountain of Kawthar.

entries.farsi: آن چه كردى اى جهان با ديگران / ‏بى‏وفايى و فن و ناز گران
entries.english: O World, that which you have shown unto others— faithlessness and deceit and grievous pride—

entries.farsi: بر سرت ريزيم ما بهر جزا / كه شهيديم آمده اندر غزا
entries.english: We pour on your head in repayment, for we are martyrs come to war.”

entries.farsi: تا بدانى كه خداى پاك را / ‏بندگان هستند پر حمله و مرى
entries.english: In order that you may know that the Holy God has servants impetuous and combative,

entries.farsi: سبلت تزوير دنيا بر كنند / خيمه را بر باروى نصرت زنند
entries.english: Tear out the moustache of worldly hypocrisy and pitch their tents on the rampart of aid.

entries.farsi: اين شهيدان باز نو غازى شدند / وين اسيران باز بر نصرت زدند
entries.english: These martyrs have become warriors anew, and these captives have gained the victory once more;

entries.farsi: تا بدانى در عدم خورشيدهاست / ‏و انچه اينجا آفتاب آن جا سهاست
entries.english: They have lifted up their heads again from non-existence, saying, “Behold us if you are not blind from birth,”

entries.farsi: سر بر آوردند باز از نيستى / ‏كه ببين ما را گر اكمه نيستى
entries.english: That you may know that in non-existence there are suns, and that which is a sun here is a small star yonder.

entries.farsi: در عدم هستى برادر چون بود / ضد اندر ضد چون مكنون بود
entries.english: How, O brother is existence in non-existence? How is opposite concealed in opposite?

entries.farsi: مرد كارنده كه انبارش تهى است / ‏شاد و خوش نه بر اميد نيستى است
entries.english: He brings forth the living from the dead: know that the hope of worshippers is non-existence.

entries.farsi: يُخْ رِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ بدان / ‏كه عدم آمد اميد عابدان
entries.english: The sower whose barn is empty, is not he joyful and happy in hope of non-existence—

entries.farsi: كه برويد آن ز سوى نيستى / ‏فهم كن گر واقف معنيستى
entries.english: That crop will grow from the quarter of nonexistence? Apprehend if you are aware of reality.

entries.farsi: دم به دم از نيستى تو منتظر / كه بيابى فهم و ذوق آرام و بر
entries.english: Moment by moment you are expecting from non-existence to gain understanding and perception and peace and good.

entries.farsi: نيست دستورى گشاد اين راز را / ور نه بغدادى كنم ابخاز را
entries.english: It is not permitted to divulge this mystery; else I should make Abkhaz into Baghdad.

entries.farsi: پس خزانه‏ى صنع حق باشد عدم / ‏كه بر آرد زو عطاها دم به دم
entries.english: Non-existence, then, is God’s factory from which He continually produces gifts.

entries.farsi: مبدع آمد حق و مبدع آن بود / كه بر آرد فرع بى‏اصل و سند
entries.english: God is the Originator, and an originator is he who produces a branch without root or support.

sections.title_fa: مثال عالم هست نيست نما و عالم نيست هست نما
sections.title_en: Parable of the world existent that appears non-existent and the world non-existent that appears existent.

entries.farsi: نيست را بنمود هست و محتشم / ‏هست را بنمود بر شكل عدم
entries.english: He has caused the non-existent to appear existent and magnificent; He has caused the existent to appear in the form of non-existence.

entries.farsi: بحر را پوشيد و كف كرد آشكار / باد را پوشيد و بنمودت غبار
entries.english: He has concealed the Sea and made the foam visible; He has concealed the Wind and displayed to you the dust.

entries.farsi: چون مناره‏ى خاك پيچان در هوا / خاك از خود چون بر آيد بر عال
entries.english: The dust is whirling in the air, as a minaret: how should the dust rise aloft of itself?

entries.farsi: خاك را بينى به بالا اى عليل / ‏باد را نى جز به تعريف دليل
entries.english: You see the dust on high, O infirm: not the Wind itself, except through knowledge given by induction.

entries.farsi: كف همى‏بينى روانه هر طرف / ‏كف بى‏دريا ندارد متصرف
entries.english: You see the foam moving in every direction: without the Sea the foam has no turning-place.

entries.farsi: كف به حس بينى و دريا از دليل / ‏فكر پنهان آشكارا قال و قيل
entries.english: You see the foam by sense perception and the Sea by induction: thought is hidden, speech manifest.

entries.farsi: نفى را اثبات مى‏پنداشتيم / ‏ديده‏ى معدوم بينى داشتيم
entries.english: We deemed negation to be affirmation: we had an eye that saw the nonexistent.

entries.farsi: ديده‏اى كاندر نعاسى شد پديد / كى تواند جز خيال و نيست ديد
entries.english: The eye that appeared in a state of slumber, how should it be able to see anything but phantasy and non-existence?

entries.farsi: الجرم سر گشته گشتيم از ضالل / ‏چون حقيقت شد نهان پيدا خيال
entries.english: Necessarily we were bewildered by error, since Reality was hidden and Phantasy visible,

entries.farsi: ين عدم را چون نشاند اندر نظر / چون نهان كرد آن حقيقت از بصر
entries.english: Why He set up this non-existence in view and why He caused that Reality to be hidden from sight.

entries.farsi: آفرين اى اوستاد سحر باف / ‏كه نمودى معرضان را درد صاف
entries.english: Praise, O Master-weaver of magic who have made the dregs to seem pure to them that turn away

entries.farsi: ساحران مهتاب پيمايند زود / پيش بازرگان و زر گيرند سود
entries.english: Magicians quickly measure moonbeams in the presence of the merchant and receive gold as profit.

entries.farsi: سيم بربايند زين گون پيچ پيچ / ‏سيم از كف رفته و كرباس هيچ
entries.english: By artful tricks of this sort they take money, the money is gone from his hand, there is no linen.

entries.farsi: اين جهان جادوست ما آن تاجريم / ‏كه از او مهتاب پيموده خريم
entries.english: This world is a sorcerer, and we are the merchants who buy from it the measured moonbeams.

entries.farsi: گز كند كرباس پانصد گز شتاب / ‏ساحرانه او ز نور ماهتاب
entries.english: Magician-like, it hastily measures out by the meter five hundred meters of linen from the light of the moonbeams,

entries.farsi: چون ستد او سيم عمرت اى رهى / ‏سيم شد، كرباس نى، كيسه تهى
entries.english: When it takes the money, your life, O slave, the money is gone, there is no linen, and your purse is empty.

entries.farsi: قل اعوذت خواند بايد كاى احد / هين ز نفاثات افغان وز عقد
entries.english: You must recite Say, I take refuge, crying, “O One, come, save me from the witches and from knots.

entries.farsi: مى‏دمند اندر گره آن ساحرات / ‏الغياث المستغاث از برد و مات
entries.english: These sorceresses are blowing on the knots: help, O You whose help is besought against victory and checkmate.”

entries.farsi: ليك بر خوان از زبان فعل نيز / كه زبان قول سست است اى عزيز
entries.english: But invoke with the tongue of deeds also, for the tongue of words is weak, O honourable man.

entries.farsi: آن يكى ياران و ديگر رخت و مال / ‏و آن سوم وافى است و آن حسن الفعال
entries.english: In the world you have three fellow-travellers: one is faithful and these two are treacherous.

entries.farsi: در زمانه مر ترا سه همرهند / آن يكى وافى و اين دو غدرمند
entries.english: One is friends and the other is goods and chattels; and the third is faithful, and that one is excellence in deeds.

entries.farsi: مال نايد با تو بيرون از قصور / يار آيد ليك آيد تا به گور
entries.english: Wealth will not come with you out of your palaces; friend will come, but he will come as far as your grave.

entries.farsi: چون ترا روز اجل آيد به پيش / ‏يار گويد از زبان حال خويش
entries.english: When your day of doom comes to meet you, your friend will say in the language appropriate to his sentiments,

entries.farsi: تا بدين جا بيش همره نيستم / ‏بر سر گورت زمانى بيستم
entries.english: “As far as here: I accompany you no farther, I will stand a while at your grave.”

entries.farsi: فعل تو وافى است زو كن ملتحد / كه در آيد با تو در قعر لحد
entries.english: Your deeds are faithful: make of them your refuge, for they will come with you into the depths of the tomb.

sections.title_fa: در تفسير قول مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام ال بد من قرين يدفن معك و هو حى و تدفن معه و أنت ميت، ان كان كريما اكرمك و ان كان لئيما اسلمك، و ذلك القرين عملك فاصلحه ما استطعت، صدق )رسول اللهَّ (ص

sections.title_en: Commentary on the saying of Mustafá, on whom be peace, “You must have a familiar who is buried with you, he being alive, and with whom you are buried when you art dead; if he be generous, he will treat you generously, and if he be base, he will forsake you. That familiar is your works, so make them right as far as you are able.”
The Messenger of Allah spoke the truth.

entries.farsi: پس پيمبر گفت بهر اين طريق / ‏با وفاتر از عمل نبود رفيق
entries.english: Therefore the Prophet said, “For the purpose of this Way there is no comrade more faithful than works.

entries.farsi: گر بود نيكو ابد يارت شود / ور بود بد در لحد مارت شود
entries.english: If they be good they will be your friends for ever, and if they be evil they will be a snake in your tomb.”

entries.farsi: اين عمل وين كسب در راه سداد / كى توان كرد اى پدر بى‏اوستاد
entries.english: How, O father, can one do this work and earning in the Way of righteousness without a master?

entries.farsi: دون‏ترين كسبى كه در عالم رود / هيچ بى‏ارشاد استادى بود
entries.english: The meanest earning that goes on in the world, is it ever without the guidance of a master?

entries.farsi: اولش علم است آن گاهى عمل / ‏تا دهد بر بعد مهلت يا اجل
entries.english: Its beginning is knowledge; then action, that it may yield fruit after a time or after death.

entries.farsi: استعينوا فى الحرف يا ذا النهى / من كريم صالح من اهلها
entries.english: Seek help in crafts, O possessor of intelligence, from a generous and righteous craftsman.

entries.farsi: اطلب الدر اخى وسط الصدف / ‏و اطلب الفن من ارباب الحرف
entries.english: Seek the pearl in the oyster-shell, my brother, and seek technical skill from the craftsmen.

entries.farsi: ان رايتم ناصحين انصفوا / بادروا التعليم ال تستنكفوا
entries.english: If you see sincere advisers, deal fairly and be eager to learn: do not show disdain.

entries.farsi: در دباغى گر خلق پوشيد مرد / خواجگى خواجه را آن كم نكرد
entries.english: If the tanner wore a threadbare garment, that did not diminish the master’s mastery;

entries.farsi: وقت دم آهنگر ار پوشيد دلق / ‏احتشام او نشد كم پيش خلق
entries.english: If the ironsmith wore a patched frock when blowing the bellows, his reputation was not impaired in the eyes of the people.

entries.farsi: پس لباس كبر بيرون كن ز تن / ‏ملبس ذل پوش در آموختن
entries.english: Therefore strip the raiment of pride from your body: in learning, put on the garment of humility.

entries.farsi: علم آموزى طريقش قولى است / ‏حرفت آموزى طريقش فعلى است
entries.english: If you wouldst learn knowledge, the way of it is oral; if you wouldst learn a craft, the way of it is practical.

entries.farsi: فقر خواهى آن به صحبت قايم است / ‏نه زبانت كار مى‏آيد نه دست
entries.english: If you desire poverty that depends on companionship: neither your tongue nor your hand avails.

entries.farsi: دانش آن را ستاند جان ز جان / ‏نه ز راه دفتر و نه از زبان
entries.english: Soul receives from soul knowledge, not by way of book or from tongue.

entries.farsi: در دل سالك اگر هست آن رموز / رمز دانى نيست سالك را هنوز
entries.english: If those mysteries are in the traveller’s heart, knowledge of the mystery is not yet possessed by the traveller.

entries.farsi: تا دلش را شرح آن سازد ضيا / پس أَ لَمْ نَشْ رَحْ بفرمايد خدا
entries.english: Until the expansion of his heart shall make it the Light: then God says, “Did not We expand …?

entries.farsi: كه درون سينه شرحت داده‏ايم / ‏شرح اندر سينه‏ات بنهاده‏ايم
entries.english: For We have given you the expansion within your breast, We have put the expansion into your breast.”

entries.farsi: و هنوز از خارج آن را طالبى / ‏محلبى از ديگران چون حالبى
entries.english: You are still seeking illumination from outside; you are a source of milk: how are you a milking others?

entries.farsi: چشمه‏ى شير است در تو بى‏كنار / تو چرا مى شير جويى از تغار
entries.english: There is an unlimited fountain of milk within you: why are you seeking milk from the pail?

entries.farsi: كه أَ لَمْ نَشْ رَحْ نه شرحت هست باز / چون شدى تو شرح جو و كديه ساز
entries.english: O lake, you have a channel to the Sea: be ashamed to seek water from the pool;

entries.farsi: منفذى دارى به بحر اى آبگير / ننگ دار از آب جستن از غدير
entries.english: For did not We expand…? Again, have not you the expansion? How are you become a seeker of the expansion and a mendicant?

entries.farsi: درنگر در شرح دل در اندرون / ‏تا نيايد طعنه‏ى فَال تبصرون
entries.english: Contemplate the expansion of the heart within, lest there come the reproach, Do not you see?

sections.title_fa: ْتفسير وَ هُوَ مَعَكُم
sections.title_en: Commentary on “And He is with you.”

entries.farsi: يك سبد پر نان ترا بر فرق سر / تو همى‏خواهى لب نان در بدر
entries.english: There is a basket full of loaves on the crown of your head, and you are begging a crust of bread from door to door.

entries.farsi: در سر خود پيچ هل خيره سرى / ‏رو در دل زن چرا بر هر درى
entries.english: Attend to your own head, abandon giddy-headedness; go, knock at the door of your heart: why are you at every door?

entries.farsi: تا به زانويى ميان آب جو / غافل از خود ز اين و آن تو آب جو
entries.english: While you are up to the knee in the river-water, you are heedless of yourself and art seeking water from this one and that one.

entries.farsi: اسب زير ران و فارس اسب جو / چيست اين گفت اسب ليكن اسب كو
entries.english: Water in front; and behind, too, an unfailing supply of water; before your eyes is a barrier and behind them a barrier.

entries.farsi: پيش آب و پس هم آب با مدد / چشمها را پيش سد و خلف سد
entries.english: The horse is under the thigh, and the rider is seeking the horse. “What is this?” he says, “A horse, but where is the horse?”

entries.farsi: هى نه اسب است اين به زير تو پديد / گفت آرى ليك خود اسبى كه ديد
entries.english: “Eh, is not this a horse under you, plain to see?” “Yes,” says he, “but who ever saw a horse?”

entries.farsi: مست آب و پيش روى اوست آن / ‏اندر آب و بى‏خبر ز آب روان
entries.english: He is mad with thirst for the water, and it is before his face: he is in the water and unconscious of the running water.

entries.farsi: چون گهر در بحر گويد بحر كو / و ان خيال چون صدف ديوار او
entries.english: Like the pearl in the sea, he says, “Where is the sea?” and that shell-like phantasy is his wall.

entries.farsi: گفتن آن كو حجابش مى‏شود / ابر تاب آفتابش مى‏شود
entries.english: His saying “Where?” becomes for him a screen: it becomes for him a cloud over the radiance of the sun.

entries.farsi: بند چشم اوست هم چشم بدش / ‏عين رفع سد او گشته سدش
entries.english: His bad eye is a bandage on his eye: his very removing the barrier has become a barrier for him.

entries.farsi: بند گوش او شده هم هوش او / هوش با حق دار اى مدهوش او
entries.english: His consciousness has become the plug of his ear: keep your consciousness towards God, O you who are bewildered in Him.

sections.title_fa: در تفسير قول مصطفى عليه الصالة و السلام من جعل الهموم هما واحدا كفاه اللهَّ سائر همومه و من ‏تفرقت به الهموم ال يبالى اللهَّ فى اى واد اهلكه
sections.title_en: Commentary on the saying of Mustafá, on whom be peace, “Whoever shall make his cares one care,
God will relieve him of all his cares; and whoever is distracted by his cares, God will not care in what valley He destroys him.”

entries.farsi: آب هش را مى‏كشد هر بيخ خار / آب هوشت چون رسد سوى ثمار
entries.english: You have distributed your consciousness in directions: those vanities are not worth a cress leaf.

entries.farsi: هوش را توزيع كردى بر جهات / ‏مى‏نيرزد تره‏اى آن ترهات
entries.english: Every thorn-root draws the water of your consciousness: how should the water of your consciousness reach the fruit?

entries.farsi: هين بزن آن شاخ بد را خو كنش / ‏آب ده اين شاخ خوش را نو كنش
entries.english: Listen, smite that evil bough, and cut it off: water this goodly bough, refresh it.

entries.farsi: هر دو سبزند اين زمان آخر نگر / كاين شود باطل از آن رويد ثمر
entries.english: Both are green at this time, look to the end that this one will come to nothing; fruit will grow from that one.

entries.farsi: آب باغ اين را حالل آن را حرام / ‏فرق را آخر ببينى و السلام
entries.english: To this one the water in the orchard is lawful, to that one unlawful. In the end you will see the difference, and farewell.

entries.farsi: عدل وضع نعمتى در موضعش / ‏نه به هر بيخى كه باشد آب كش
entries.english: What is justice? Giving water to trees. What is injustice? To give water to thorns.

entries.farsi: عدل چه بود آب ده اشجار را / ظلم چه بود آب دادن خار را
entries.english: Justice is bestowing a bounty in its proper place, not on every root that will absorb water.

entries.farsi: ظلم چه بود وضع در ناموضعى / ‏كه نباشد جز بال را منبعى
entries.english: What is injustice? To bestow in an improper place that can only be a source of calamity.

entries.farsi: نعمت حق را به جان و عقل ده / ‏نه به طبع پر ز حير پر گره
entries.english: Bestow the bounty of God on the spirit and reason, not on the nature full of disease and complications.

entries.farsi: بار كن بيگار غم را بر تنت / ‏بر دل و جان كم نه آن جان كندنت
entries.english: Load the conflict of cares upon your body: do not lay your anxiety upon the heart and spirit.

entries.farsi: بر سر عيسى نهاده تنگ بار / خر سكيزه مى‏زند در مرغزار
entries.english: The pack is laid upon Jesus’ head; the ass is frisking in the meadow.

entries.farsi: سرمه را در گوش كردن شرط نيست / ‏كار دل را جستن از تن شرط نيست
entries.english: It is not right to put eye salve in the ear: it is not right to demand from the body the work of the heart.

entries.farsi: گر دلى رو ناز كن خوارى مكش / ‏ور تنى شكر منوش و زهر چش
entries.english: If you are a heart, go, scorn, do not suffer insult; and if you are a body, do not eat sugar but taste poison.

entries.farsi: زهر تن را نافع است و قند بد / تن همان بهتر كه باشد بى‏مدد
entries.english: Poison is beneficial to the body, and sugar noxious: it is better that the body should be deprived of supplies.

entries.farsi: هيزم دوزخ تن است و كم كنش / ور برويد هيزمى رو بركنش
entries.english: The body is fuel for Hell, weaken it; and if it produce a growth of fuel, go, destroy it.

entries.farsi: ور نه حمال حطب باشى حطب / ‏در دو عالم همچو جفت بو لهب
entries.english: Else, O firewood, you will be a carrier of firewood in both worlds, like the wife of Bu Lahab.

entries.farsi: از حطب بشناس شاخ سدره را / ‏گر چه هر دو سبز باشند اى فتى
entries.english: Know the bough of the Sidra from the firewood, though both are green, O youth.

entries.farsi: اصل آن شاخ است هفتم آسمان / ‏اصل اين شاخ است از نار و دخان
entries.english: The origin of that bough is the Seventh Heaven; the origin of this bough is from fire and smoke.

entries.farsi: هست مانندا به صورت پيش حس / ‏كه غلط بين است چشم و كيش حس
entries.english: To sense-perception they are similar in appearance, for the eye and habit of sense-perception is seeing falsely;

entries.farsi: هست آن پيدا به پيش چشم دل / ‏جهد كن سوى دل آ جهد المقل
entries.english: That is manifest to the eye of the heart: exert yourself, advance towards the heart with the exertion of one whose means are small.

entries.farsi: ور ندارى پا بجنبان خويش را / تا ببينى هر كم و هر بيش را
entries.english: And if you have no foot, bestir yourself that you may behold every less and more.

sections.title_fa: :در معنى اين بيت ور نيست شوى به هستى‏ات بگرايند گر راه روى راه برت بگشايند
sections.title_en: On the meaning of this verse: “If you fare on the Way, the Way will be revealed to you;
and if you become nonexistent, existence will be conferred on you.”

entries.farsi: گر زليخا بست درها هر طرف / ‏يافت يوسف هم ز جنبش منصرف
entries.english: Though Zalikha shut the doors on every side, still Joseph gained return by bestirring himself.

entries.farsi: باز شد قفل و در و شد ره پديد / چون توكل كرد يوسف بر جهيد
entries.english: Lock and door opened, and the way appeared: when Joseph put trust in God, he escaped.

entries.farsi: گر چه رخته نيست عالم را پديد / خيره يوسف‏وار مى‏بايد دويد
entries.english: Though the world has no visible crevice, one must run recklessly, like Joseph,

entries.farsi: تا گشايد قفل و در پيدا شود / سوى بى‏جايى شما را جا شود
entries.english: In order that the lock may open and the doorway become clear, and the region of non-spatiality become your dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: آمدى اندر جهان اى ممتحن / ‏هيچ مى‏بينى طريق آمدن
entries.english: You came into the world, O afflicted one: do you ever see the way of your coming?

entries.farsi: تو ز جايى آمدى وز موطنى / ‏آمدن را راه دانى هيچ نى
entries.english: You came from a certain place and abode: do you know the way of your coming? Nay.

entries.farsi: گر ندانى تا نگويى راه نيست / ‏زين ره بى‏راهه ما را رفتنى است
entries.english: If you don’t know, beware of saying that there is no way: by this wayless way we shall depart.

entries.farsi: تو ببند آن چشم و خود تسليم كن / ‏خويش را بينى در آن شهر كهن
entries.english: In dreams you wander happily to left and right: have you any knowledge where the way is that leads to that arena?

entries.farsi: مى‏روى در خواب شادان چپ و راست / ‏هيچ دانى راه آن ميدان كجاست
entries.english: Shut that eye and give yourself up: you will find yourself in the ancient City.

entries.farsi: چشم چون بندى كه صد چشم خمار / بند چشم تست اين سو از غرار
entries.english: How should you shut your eye when in this direction a hundred inebriated eyes are a bandage on your eye because of infatuation?

entries.farsi: چار چشمى تو ز عشق مشترى / ‏بر اميد مهترى و سرورى
entries.english: From love of an admirer you are with four eyes in the hope of eminence and chieftainship.

entries.farsi: مشترى خواهى به هر دم پيچ پيچ / ‏تو چه دارى كه فروشى هيچ هيچ
entries.english: And if you fall asleep you see the purchaser in your dreams: how should the ill-omened owl dream of anything but a wilderness?

entries.farsi: ور بخسبى مشترى بينى به خواب / ‏جغد بد كى خواب بيند جز خراب
entries.english: At every moment you want a purchaser cringing: what have you to sell? Nothing, nothing.

entries.farsi: گر دلت را نان بدى يا چاشتى / ‏از خريداران فراغت داشتى
entries.english: If your heart had any bread or breakfast, it would have been empty of purchasers.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى آن شخص كه دعوى پيغامبرى مى‏كرد گفتندش چه خورده‏اى كه گيج شده‏اى و ياوه مى‏گويى گفت اگر چيزى يافتمى كه خوردمى نه گيج شدمى و نه ياوه گفتمى كه هر سخن نيك كه با غير اهلش گويند ياوه گفته باشند اگر چه در آن ياوه گفتن مأمورند

sections.title_en: Story of the person who claimed to be a prophet. They said to him, “What have you eaten that you have become crazy and art talking in vain?” He replied, “If I had found anything to eat, I should not have become crazy and talked in vain”; for whenever they speak good words to people unworthy to hear them, they will have talked in vain, although they are commanded to talk thus in vain.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت من پيغمبرم / ‏از همه پيغمبران فاضلترم
entries.english: A certain man was saying, “I am a prophet: I am superior to all the prophets.”

entries.farsi: خلق بر وى جمع چون مور و ملخ / ‏كه چه مكر است و چه تزوير و چه فخ
entries.english: They bound his neck and took him to the king, saying, “This man says he is a prophet sent by God.”

entries.farsi: گردنش بستند و بردندش به شاه / ‏كاين همى‏گويد رسولم از اله
entries.english: The people gathered round him as ants and locusts, crying, “What deceit and imposture and trap is?

entries.farsi: ما از آن جا آمديم اينجا غريب / ‏تو چرا مخصوص باشى اى اديب
entries.english: If he that comes from non-existence is a prophet, we all are prophets and grand.

entries.farsi: گر رسول آن است كايد از عدم / ‏ما همه پيغمبريم و محتشم
entries.english: We came hither as strangers from that place: why should you be specially endowed, O accomplished one?”

entries.farsi: نه شما چون طفل خفته آمديد / بى‏خبر از راه وز منزل بديد
entries.english: “Did not you come like a sleeping child? You were ignorant of the way and the destination.

entries.farsi: از منازل خفته بگذشتيد و مست / ‏بى‏خبر از راه و از بالا و پست
entries.english: You passed through the stages asleep and intoxicated, unconscious of the way and ups and downs;

entries.farsi: ما به بيدارى روان گشتيم و خوش / ‏از وراى پنج و شش تا پنج و شش
entries.english: We set out in wakefulness and well from beyond the five and the six to the five and six,

entries.farsi: ديده منزلها ز اصل و از اساس / ‏چون قالووزان خبير و ره شناس
entries.english: Having perceived the stages from the source and foundation, possessed of experience and knowing the way like guides.”

entries.farsi: شاه را گفتند اشكنجه‏ش بكن / ‏تا نگويد جنس او هيچ اين سخن
entries.english: They said to the king, “Put him to the rack, that a person of his sort may never speak such words.”

entries.farsi: شاه ديدش بس نزار و بس ضعيف / ‏كه به يك سيلى بميرد آن نحيف
entries.english: The king saw that he was very thin and infirm, so that such an emaciated man would die at a single blow.

entries.farsi: كى توان او را فشردن يا زدن / ‏كه چو شيشه گشته است او را بدن
entries.english: “How is it possible to torture or beat him, since his body has become as a glass?

entries.farsi: ليك با او گويم از راه خوشى / ‏كه چرا دارى تو الف سركشى
entries.english: But I will speak to him kindly and say, ‘Why do you boast of high estate?’

entries.farsi: كز درشتى نايد اينجا هيچ كار / هم به نرمى سر كند از غار مار
entries.english: For here harshness is of no use: it is by gentleness that the snake puts forth its head from the hole.”

entries.farsi: مردمان را دور كرد از گرد وى / ‏شه لطيفى بود و نرمى ورد وى
entries.english: He caused the people to withdraw from around him: the king was a gracious man, and gentleness was his way.

entries.farsi: پس نشاندش باز پرسيدش ز جا / ‏كه كجا دارى معاش و ملتجى
entries.english: Then he bade him be seated, and asked him concerning his dwelling-place, saying, “Where have you your means of livelihood and refuge?”

entries.farsi: نه مرا خانه‏ست و نه يك همنشين / ‏خانه كى كردست ماهى در زمين
entries.english: He replied, “O king, I belong to the Abode of Peace: I have come from the road to this Abode of Blame.

entries.farsi: گفت اى شه هستم از دارُ السلام / ‏آمده از ره در اين دار المالم
entries.english: I have neither home nor any companion: when has a fish made its home on the earth?”

entries.farsi: باز شاه از روى الغش گفت باز / كه چه خوردى و چه دارى چاشت ساز
entries.english: Again the king answered him, saying by way of jest, “What have you eaten and what provision have you made for the morning meal?

entries.farsi: اشتها دارى چه خوردى بامداد / كه چنين سر مستى و پر الف و باد
entries.english: Have you appetite? What did you eat at daybreak that you are so intoxicated and boastful and blustering?”

entries.farsi: دعوى پيغمبرى با اين گروه / ‏همچنان باشد كه دل جستن ز كوه
entries.english: He replied, “If I had bread, dry or moist, how should I lay claim to prophecy?

entries.farsi: گفت اگر نانم بدى خشك و طرى / ‏كى كنيمى دعوى پيغمبرى
entries.english: To claim to be a prophet amongst these people is like seeking a heart from a mountain.

entries.farsi: كس ز كوه و سنگ عقل و دل نجست / ‏فهم و ضبط نكته‏ى مشكل نجست
entries.english: No one sought intellect and heart from mountains and rocks: none sought understanding and apprehension of a difficult point of discourse.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويى باز گويد كه همان / ‏مى‏كند افسوس چون مستهزيان
entries.english: Whatever you say, the mountain replies the same: it makes a mockery like the scoffers.

entries.farsi: از كجا اين قوم و پيغام از كجا / از جمادى جان كه را باشد رجا
entries.english: What relation exists between this folk and the message? Who can hope for life from a soulless thing?

entries.farsi: گر تو پيغام زنى آرى و زر / پيش تو بنهند جمله سيم و سر
entries.english: If you bring a message concerning a woman or gold, they will all lay before you their money and lives.

entries.farsi: كه فالن جا شاهدى مى‏خواندت / ‏عاشق آمد بر تو او مى‏داندت
entries.english: A sweetheart in such and such a place invites you: she is in love with you, she knows you.’

entries.farsi: ور تو پيغام خدا آرى چو شهد / كه بيا سوى خدا اى نيك عهد
entries.english: But if you bring the honey-like message of God, ‘Come to God, O you who have a good covenant;

entries.farsi: از جهان مرگ سوى برگ رو / چون بقا ممكن بود فانى مشو
entries.english: Go from the world of death towards the provision: since everlastingness is possible, do not be perishing’—

entries.farsi: قصد خون تو كنند و قصد سر / نه از براى حميت دين و هنر
entries.english: They will seek your blood and your life, not in zeal for religion and excellence.

sections.title_fa: ‏سبب عداوت عام و بيگانه زيستن ايشان به اولياى خدا كه به حقشان مى‏خوانند و به آب حيات ابدى
sections.title_en: The reason why the vulgar are at enmity with, and live in estrangement from, the saints of God who call them unto God and the Water of Life everlasting.

entries.farsi: بلكه از چسبيدگى بر خان و مان / ‏تلخشان آيد شنيدن اين بيان
entries.english: Nay, but on account of their sticking to house and goods it is bitter to them to hear this exposition.

entries.farsi: خرقه‏اى بر ريش خر چفسيد سخت / ‏چون كه خواهى بر كنى زو لخت لخت
entries.english: A rag is stuck fast upon the donkey’s sore: when you wish to tear it off, bit by bit,

entries.farsi: جفته اندازد يقين آن خر ز درد / حبذا آن كس كز او پرهيز كرد
entries.english: The donkey, because of the pain, will certainly kick: happy the man who abstained from him!—

entries.farsi: خاصه پنجه ريش و هر جا خرقه‏اى / ‏بر سرش چفسيده در نم غرقه‏اى
entries.english: Especially fifty sores, and a soaked rag stuck on the top of them in every case.

entries.farsi: خان و مان چون خرقه و اين حرص ريش / ‏حرص هر گه بيش باشد ريش بيش
entries.english: House and goods are like the rag, and this greed is the sore: the greater the greed, the greater the sore.

entries.farsi: خان و مان جغد ويران است و بس / ‏نشنود اوصاف بغداد و طبس
entries.english: The wilderness alone is the house and goods of the owl: he (the owl) will not listen to descriptions of Baghdad and Tabas.

entries.farsi: گر بيايد باز سلطانى ز راه / ‏صد خبر آرد بدين جغدان ز شاه
entries.english: If a royal falcon come from the road and bring to these owls a hundred reports of the King,

entries.farsi: شرح دار الملك و باغستان و جو / پس بر او افسوس دارد صد عدو
entries.english: A full account of the imperial city and the orchards and the rivers— then a hundred enemies will jeer at him,

entries.farsi: كه چه باز آورد افسانه‏ى كهن / ‏كز گزاف و الف مى‏بافد سخن
entries.english: Saying, ‘What has the falcon brought? An old story. He is weaving words of vanity and idle brag.’

entries.farsi: كهنه ايشانند و پوسيده‏ى ابد / ور نه آن دم كهنه را نو مى‏كند
entries.english: They are old and rotten unto everlasting; otherwise that breath makes the old new.

entries.farsi: دل مدزد از دل رباى روح بخش / ‏كه سوارت مى‏كند بر پشت رخش
entries.english: It gives life to the old dead: it gives the crown of reason and the light of faith.

entries.farsi: مردگان كهنه را جان مى‏دهد / تاج عقل و نور ايمان مى‏دهد
entries.english: Do not steal your heart away from the spirit-bestowing heart-ravisher, for he will mount you on the back of Rakhsh.

entries.farsi: سر مدزد از سر فراز تاج ده / ‏كاو ز پاى دل گشايد صد گره
entries.english: Do not steal your head away from the crown-giving one whose head is exalted, for he will untie a hundred knots from the foot of your heart.

entries.farsi: با كه گويم در همه ده زنده كو / سوى آب زندگى پوينده كو
entries.english: Whom shall I tell? Where in the village is a living one? Where is any one that runs towards the Water of Life?

entries.farsi: تو به يك خوارى گريزانى ز عشق / ‏تو بجز نامى چه مى‏دانى ز عشق
entries.english: You are fleeing from Love because of a single humiliation: what do you know of Love except the name?

entries.farsi: عشق را صد ناز و استكبار هست / ‏عشق با صد ناز مى‏آيد به دست
entries.english: Love has a hundred disdains and prides: Love is gained by means of a hundred blandishments.

entries.farsi: عشق چون وافى است وافى مى‏خرد / در حريف بى‏وفا مى‏ننگرد
entries.english: Since Love is loyal, it purchases him that is loyal: it does not look at a disloyal comrade.

entries.farsi: چون درخت است آدمى و بيخ عهد / بيخ را تيمار مى‏بايد به جهد
entries.english: Man resembles a tree, and the root is the covenant: the root must be cherished with all one’s might.

entries.farsi: عهد فاسد بيخ پوسيده بود / وز ثمار و لطف ببريده بود
entries.english: A corrupt covenant is a rotten root and is cut off of fruit and grace.

entries.farsi: شاخ و برگ نخل گر چه سبز بود / با فساد بيخ سبزى نيست سود
entries.english: Although the boughs and leaves of the date-palm are green, greenness is no benefit with corruption of the root;

entries.farsi: ور ندارد برگ سبز و بيخ هست / ‏عاقبت بيرون كند صد برگ دست
entries.english: And if it has no green leaves, while it has a root, at the last a hundred leaves will put forth their hands.

entries.farsi: تو مشو غره به علمش عهد جو / علم چون قشر است و عهدش مغز او
entries.english: Be not duped by his knowledge; seek the covenant: knowledge is like a husk, and his covenant is its kernel.

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه مرد بد كار چون متمكن شود در بد كارى و اثر دولت نيكو كاران ببيند شيطان شود و مانع خير گردد از حسد همچون شيطان، كه خرمن سوخته همه را خرمن سوخته خواهد أَ رَأَيْتَ الَّذِي يَنْهى‏ عَ بْداً إِذا صَ لَّى
sections.title_en: Explaining that when the evil-doer becomes settled in evil-doing and sees the effect of the fortune

of the doers of righteousness, he from envy becomes a devil and preventer of good, like Satan; for he whose stack is burnt desires that all should have their stacks burnt: have you seen him who forbids a servant when he performs the prayer?’

entries.farsi: وافيان را چون ببينى كرده سود / تو چو شيطانى شوى آن جا حسو
entries.english: When you see that the loyal have profited, thereat you become envious, like a devil.

entries.farsi: هر كه را باشد مزاج و طبع سست / ‏او نخواهد هيچ كس را تندرست
entries.english: Whenever a man’s temperament and constitution is feeble, he does not wish any one to be sound in body.

entries.farsi: چون وفايت نيست بارى دم مزن / ‏كه سخن دعوى است اغلب ما و من
entries.english: If you dislike the jealousy of Iblís, come from the door of pretension to the portal of loyalty.

entries.farsi: گر نخواهى رشك ابليسى بيا / از در دعوى به درگاه وفا
entries.english: When you have not loyalty, at least do not talk, for words are for the most part self-assertion—‘we’ and ‘I.’

entries.farsi: اين سخن در سينه دخل مغزهاست / ‏در خموشى مغز جان را صد نماست
entries.english: These words, in the breast, are an income consisting of kernels: in silence the spiritual kernel grows a hundredfold.

entries.farsi: چون بيامد در زبان شد خرج مغز / خرج كم كن تا بماند مغز نغز
entries.english: When it comes on to the tongue, the kernel is expended: refrain from expending, in order that the goodly kernel may remain.

entries.farsi: مرد كم گوينده را فكر است زفت / ‏قشر گفتن چون فزون شد مغز رفت
entries.english: The man who speaks little has strong thoughts: when the husk, namely speech, becomes excessive, the kernel goes.

entries.farsi: پوست افزون بود الغر بود مغز / پوست الغر شد چو كامل گشت و نغز
entries.english: The rind is excessive, the kernel is thin: the rind becomes thin when it becomes perfect and goodly.

entries.farsi: بنگر اين هر سه ز خامى رسته را / جوز را و لوز را و پسته را
entries.english: Look at these three when they have passed beyond immaturity: the walnut and the almond and the pistachio.

entries.farsi: هر كه او عصيان كند شيطان شود / كه حسود دولت نيكان شود
entries.english: Whoever disobeys becomes a devil, for he becomes envious of the fortune of the righteous.

entries.farsi: چون كه در عهد خدا كردى وفا / از كرم عهدت نگه دارد خدا
