entries.english: When you have acted loyally in your covenant with God, God will graciously keep His covenant with you.

entries.farsi: از وفاى حق تو بسته ديده‏اى / ‏اذكروا اذكركم نشنيده‏اى
entries.english: You have shut your eyes to keeping faith with God, you have not listened to remember Me, I will remember you.

entries.farsi: گوش نه أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي گوش دار / تا كه اوف عهدكم آيد ز يار
entries.english: Give ear, listen to keep My covenant, in order that I will keep your covenant may come from the Friend.

entries.farsi: عهد و قرض ما چه باشد اى حزين / ‏همچو دانه‏ى خشك كشتن در زمين
entries.english: What is our covenant and loan, O sorrowful one? like sowing a dry seed in the earth.

entries.farsi: نه زمين را ز آن فروغ و لمترى / ‏نه خداوند زمين را توانگرى
entries.english: From that neither do glory nor grandeur accrue to the earth,nor riches to the owner of the earth.

entries.farsi: جز اشارت كه از اين مى‏بايدم / ‏كه تو دادى اصل اين را از عدم
entries.english: Except an indication, as though to say,‘I need this kind, the origin of which You created from non-existence.

entries.farsi: خوردم و دانه بياوردم نشان / ‏كه از اين نعمت به سوى ما كشان
entries.english: I ate, and I bring the seed as a token,begging You to send to us such bounty.’

entries.farsi: پس دعاى خشك هل اى نيك بخت / ‏كه فشاند دانه مى‏خواهد درخت
entries.english: Abandon, then, the dry prayer, O fortunate one;for the tree demands the scattering of seed.

entries.farsi: همچو مريم درد بودش دانه نى / ‏سبز كرد آن نخل را صاحب فنى
entries.english: If you have no seed, on account of that prayer God will bestow on you a palm-tree, saying, ‘How well did he labour!’

entries.farsi: گر ندارى دانه ايزد ز آن دعا / ‏بخشدت نخلى كه نعم ما سعى
entries.english: Like Mary: she had pain, but no seed: an artful One made green that palm-tree.

entries.farsi: ز انكه وافى بود آن خاتون راد / بى‏مرادش داد يزدان صد مراد
entries.english: Because that noble Lady was loyal, God gave unto her a hundred desires without desire on her part.

entries.farsi: آن جماعت را كه وافى بوده‏اند / بر همه اصنافشان افزوده‏اند
entries.english: The company who have been loyal are given superiority over all sorts.

entries.farsi: گشت درياها مسخرشان و كوه / ‏چار عنصر نيز بنده‏ى آن گروه
entries.english: Seas and mountains are made subject to them; the four elements also are the slaves of that class.

entries.farsi: اين خود اكرامى است از بهر نشان / ‏تا ببينند اهل انكار آن عيان
entries.english: This is only a favour for a sign, to the end that the disbelievers may see it plainly.

entries.farsi: آن كرامتهاى پنهانشان كه آن / ‏در نيايد در حواس و در بيان
entries.english: Those hidden graces of theirs, which come not into the senses or into description—

entries.farsi: كار آن دارد خود آن باشد ابد / دايما نه منقطع نه مسترد
entries.english: Those are the matter: those are enduring forever; they are neither cut off nor reclaimed.

sections.title_fa: مناجات
sections.title_en: Prayer.

entries.farsi: اى دهنده‏ى قوت و تمكين و ثبات / ‏خلق را زين بى‏ثباتى ده نجات
entries.english: O Giver of nourishment, steadfastness and stability, give Your creatures deliverance from this instability.

entries.farsi: اندر آن كارى كه ثابت بودنى است / ‏قايمى ده نفس را كه منثنى است
entries.english: Grant unto the soul—for it is bent— to stand upright in the work wherein it ought to be stable.

entries.farsi: صبرشان بخش و كفه‏ى ميزان گران / ‏وارهانشان از فن صورتگران
entries.english: Bestow patience upon them and heavy balance-scales: deliver them from the guile of impostors;

entries.farsi: وز حسودى بازشان خر اى كريم / ‏تا نباشند از حسد ديو رجيم
entries.english: And redeem them from envy, O Gracious One, lest from envy they be devils accursed.

entries.farsi: در نعيم فانى مال و جسد / چون همى‏سوزند عامه از حسد
entries.english: How do the vulgar burn with envy for the fleeting happiness of riches and the body!

entries.farsi: پادشاهان بين كه لشكر مى‏كشند / از حسد خويشان خود را مى‏كشند
entries.english: Behold the kings, how they lead armies (to battle) and slay their own kinsmen because of envy.

entries.farsi: عاشقان لعبتان پر قذر / كرده قصد خون و جان همدگر
entries.english: The lovers of filthy dolls have sought each other’s blood and life.

entries.farsi: ويس و رامين خسرو و شيرين بخوان / ‏كه چه كردند از حسد آن ابلهان
entries.english: Read Wís and Rámín and Khusraw and Shírín: what those fools did because of envy.

entries.farsi: كه فنا شد عاشق و معشوق نيز / هم نه چيزند و هواشان هم نه چيز
entries.english: That the lover perished and the beloved too: they are naught and their passion also is naught.

entries.farsi: پاك الهى كه عدم بر هم زند / مر عدم را بر عدم عاشق كند
entries.english: Holy is the god who brings non-existence into collision with itself and makes non-existence to be in love with non-existence.

entries.farsi: در دل نه دل حسدها سر كند / نيست را هست اين چنين مضطر كند
entries.english: Envies arise in the heart that is no heart: thus does Being subject not being to compulsion.

entries.farsi: اين زنانى كز همه مشفق‏ترند / از حسد دو ضره خود را مى‏خورند
entries.english: These women, who are kinder than all two fellow-wives devour each other from envy,

entries.farsi: تا كه مردانى كه خود سنگين دل‏اند / از حسد تا در كدامين منزل‏اند
entries.english: So that you may discern how envious are the men who indeed are stony-hearted.

entries.farsi: گر نكردى شرع افسونى لطيف / ‏بر دريدى هر كسى جسم حريف
entries.english: If the Law had not exercised a gracious spell, everyone would have torn the body of his rival to pieces.

entries.farsi: از گواه و از يمين و از نكول / ‏تا به شيشه در رود ديو فضول
entries.english: The Law makes a plan for repelling evil: it puts the demon into the bottle of proof—

entries.farsi: شرع بهر دفع شر رايى زند / ديو را در شيشه‏ى حجت كند
entries.english: Witness and oath and shrinking— till the insolent demon goes into the bottle.

entries.farsi: شرع چون كيل و ترازو دان يقين / ‏كه بدو خصمان رهند از جنگ و كين
entries.english: Like the balance whereby the two adversaries are surely united in contentment, in jest or earnest.

entries.farsi: مثل ميزانى كه خشنودى دو ضد / جمع مى‏آيد يقين در هزل و جد
entries.english: Know for sure that the Law is like the measure and scales by means of which the litigants are saved from wrangling and enmity.

entries.farsi: گر ترازو نبود آن خصم از جدال / ‏كى رهد از وهم حيف و احتيال
entries.english: If there is no pair of scales, how shall the litigant escape from disputing when he suspects fraud and deceit?

entries.farsi: پس در اين مردار زشت بى‏وفا / اين همه رشك است و خصم است و جفا
entries.english: Then, there is all this jealousy and litigation and injustice in respect of this foul faithless carcass,

entries.farsi: پس در آن اقبال و دولت چون بود / چون شود جنى و انسى در حسد
entries.english: How, then, must it be when genies and men become envious in respect of that fortune and felicity?

entries.farsi: آن شياطين خود حسود كهنه‏اند / يك زمان از ره زنى خالى نه‏اند
entries.english: Truly those devils are envious of old: never for a moment do they cease from waylaying;

entries.farsi: و آن بنى آدم كه عصيان كشته‏اند / از حسودى نيز شيطان گشته‏اند
entries.english: And the sons of Adam who have sown disobedience— they too have become devils from enviousness.

entries.farsi: از نبى بر خوان كه شيطانان انس / ‏گشته‏اند از مسخ حق با ديو جنس
entries.english: Read in the Qur’an how by Divine transformation the devils of mankind have become homogeneous with the Devil.

entries.farsi: ديو چون عاجز شود در افتتان / ‏استعانت جويد او زين انسيان
entries.english: When the Devil fails to tempt, he seeks aid from these human.

entries.farsi: كه شما ياريد با ما ياريى / ‏جانب ماييد جانب داريى
entries.english: Saying, ‘you are my friends: perform an act of friendship towards me; you are on my side: an act of partiality.’

entries.farsi: گر كسى را ره زنند اندر جهان / هر دو گون شيطان بر آيد شادمان
entries.english: If they waylay anyone in the world, both kinds of devils come off rejoicing;

entries.farsi: ور كسى جان برد و شد در دين بلند / نوحه مى‏دارند آن دو رشك‏مند
entries.english: And if anyone has saved his soul and become eminent in religion, those two jealous keep up lamentation.

entries.farsi: هر دو مى‏خايند دندان حسد / بر كسى كه داد اديب او را خرد
entries.english: Both gnash their teeth in envy at any one upon whom the Teacher has bestowed wisdom.”

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن پادشاه از آن مدعى نبوت كه آن كه رسول راستين باشد و ثابت شود با او چه باشد كه كسى را بخشد يا به صحبت و خدمت او چه بخشش يابند غير نصيحت كه به زبان مى‏گويد
sections.title_en: How the king asked the man who claimed to be a prophet, saying, “The person who is a true Messenger and becomes established—what has he to give to any one, or what gifts will people obtain by consorting
with him and serving him, except the counsel which he utters with his tongue?”

entries.farsi: شاه پرسيدش كه بارى وحى چيست / يا چه حاصل دارد آن كس كاو نبى است‏
entries.english: The king questioned him, saying, “After all, what is inspiration, or what has he got who is a prophet?”

entries.farsi: گفت خود آن چيست كش حاصل نشد / يا چه دولت ماند كاو واصل نشد
entries.english: He replied, “What is there indeed that he has not got, or what fortune is left whereunto he has not attained?

entries.farsi: گيرم اين وحى نبى گنجور نيست / ‏هم كم از وحى دل زنبور نيست
entries.english: I will suppose that this prophetic inspiration is not a treasurer; still, it is not inferior to the inspiration in the heart of the bee.

entries.farsi: چونك اوحى الرب الى النحل آمده‏ست / ‏خانه‏ى وحيش پر از حلوا شده‏ست
entries.english: Since God has inspired the bee have come, the dwelling-place of its inspiration has been filled with sweets.

entries.farsi: او به نور وحى حق عز و جل / ‏كرد عالم را پر از شمع و عسل
entries.english: Through the light of the inspiration of God the Almighty and Glorious, it filled the world with wax and honey.

entries.farsi: اين كه كرمناست و بالا مى‏رود / وحيش از زنبور كمتر كى بو
entries.english: This one who is We have honoured and is ever going upward— how should his inspiration be inferior to the bee?”

entries.farsi: نه تو اعطيناك كوثر خوانده‏اى / ‏پس چرا خشكى و تشنه مانده‏اى
entries.english: Have not you read We have given you Kawthar? Why, then, are you dry and why have you remained thirsty?

entries.farsi: يا مگر فرعونى و كوثر چو نيل / ‏بر تو خون گشته است و ناخوش اى عليل
entries.english: Or perchance you are Pharaoh, and for you Kawthar, like the Nile, has turned to blood and impure, O sick man.

entries.farsi: توبه كن بيزار شو از هر عدو / كاو ندارد آب كوثر در كدو
entries.english: Repent; renounce every enemy who has not the water of Kawthar in his cup.

entries.farsi: هر كه را ديدى ز كوثر سرخ رو / او محمد خوست با او گير خو
entries.english: Whomsoever you see flushed by Kawthar, he has the nature of Mohammed: consort with him,

entries.farsi: تا احب هلل آيى در حساب / ‏كز درخت احمدى با اوست سيب
entries.english: That at the Reckoning you may become love for God’s sake; for with him are apples from the tree of Ahmad.

entries.farsi: هر كه را ديدى ز كوثر خشك لب / ‏دشمنش مى‏دار همچون مرگ و تب
entries.english: Whomever you see with lips unmoistened by Kawthar, always deem him an enemy like death and fever,

entries.farsi: گر چه باباى تو است و مام تو / كاو حقيقت هست خون آشام تو
entries.english: Though it is your father or your mother; for in truth he is a drinker of your blood.

entries.farsi: از خليل حق بياموز اين سير / كه شد او بيزار اول از پدر
entries.english: Learn these ways of acting from the Friend of God, who first renounced his father,

entries.farsi: تا كه ابغض هلل آيى پيش حق / ‏تا نگيرد بر تو رشك عشق دق
entries.english: That in the presence of God you may become hate for God’s sake, lest the jealousy of Love take offence at you.

entries.farsi: تا نخوانى ال و الا اهلل را / درنيابى منهج اين راه را
entries.english: Until you recite “There is not any god” and “except Allah,” you will not find the plain track of this Way.

sections.title_fa: ‏داستان آن عاشق كه با معشوق خود بر مى‏شمرد خدمتها و وفاهاى خود را و شبهاى دراز تَتَجافى جُنُوبُهُمْ عَ نِ الْمَضاجِعِ‏را و بى‏نوايى و جگر تشنگى روزهاى دراز را و مى‏گفت كه من جز اين خدمت نمى‏دانم اگر خدمت ديگر هست مرا ارشاد كن كه هر چه فرمايى منقادم اگر در آتش رفتن است چون خليل عليه السلام و اگر در دهان نهنگ دريا فتادن است چون يونس عليه السلام و اگر هفتاد بار كشته شدن است چون جرجيس عليه السلام و اگر از گريه نابينا شدن است چون شعيب عليه السلام
و وفا و جان بازى انبيا را عليه السلام شمار نيست، و جواب گفتن معشوق او را
sections.title_en: Story of the lover who was recounting to his beloved his acts of service and loyalty and the long nights their sides heave up from their beds and the long days of want and parching thirst; and he was saying,

“I know not any service besides these: if there is any other service, direct me, for I submit to whatever you may command, whether to enter the fire, like Khalíl, on whom be peace, or fall into the mouth of the leviathan of the sea, like Jonah, on whom be peace, or be killed seventy times, like Jirjís, on whom be peace, or be made blind by weeping, like Shu‘ayb, on whom be peace; and the loyalty and self-sacrifice of the prophets cannot be reckoned”; and how the beloved answered him.

entries.farsi: آن يكى عاشق به پيش يار خود / مى‏شمرد از خدمت و از كار خود
entries.english: A certain lover in the presence of his beloved was recounting his services and works,

entries.farsi: كز براى تو چنين كردم چنان / ‏تيرها خوردم درين رزم و سنان
entries.english: Saying, “For your sake I did such and such, in this war I suffered arrows and spears.

entries.farsi: مال رفت و زور رفت و نام رفت / هيچ صبحم خفته يا خندان نيافت
entries.english: Wealth is gone and strength is gone and fame is gone: on account of my love for you many a misfortune has befallen me.

entries.farsi: ‏هيچ شامم با سر و سامان نيافت / آن چه او نوشيده بود از تلخ و درد
entries.english: No dawn found me asleep or laughing; no eve found me with capital and means.”

entries.farsi: او به تفصيلش يكايك مى‏شمرد / نه از براى منتى بل مى‏نمود
entries.english: What he had tasted of bitters and dregs he was recounting to her in detail, point by point,

entries.farsi: بر درستى محبت صد شهود / عاقالن را يك اشارت بس بود
entries.english: Not for the sake of reproach; nay, he was displaying a hundred testimonies of the trueness of his love.

entries.farsi: عاشقان را تشنگى ز آن كى رود / مى‏كند تكرار گفتن بى‏مالل
entries.english: For men of reason a single indication is enough, how should the thirst of lovers be removed thereby?

entries.farsi: ‏كى ز اشارت بس كند حوت از زالل / صد سخن مى‏گفت ز آن درد كهن
entries.english: He ceaselessly repeats his tale: how should a fish be satisfied with indication from the limpid water?

entries.farsi: ‏در شكايت كه نگفتم يك سخن / آتشى بودش نمى‏دانست چيست
entries.english: He, from that ancient grief, was speaking a hundred words in complaint, saying, “I have not spoken a word.”

entries.farsi: ‏ليك چون شمع از تف آن مى‏گريست / گفت معشوق اين همه كردى و ليك
entries.english: There was a fire in him: he did not know what it was, but on account of its heat he was weeping like a candle.

entries.farsi: ‏گوش بگشا پهن و اندر ياب نيك / كانچه اصل اصل عشق است و والست
entries.english: The beloved said, “You have done all this, yet open your ear wide and apprehend well;

entries.farsi: ‏آن نكردى اين چه كردى فرعهاست / گفتش آن عاشق بگو كان اصل چيست
entries.english: For you have not done what is the root of the root of love and fealty: this that you have done is the branches.”

entries.farsi: ‏هين بمير ار يار جان با زنده‏اى / هم در آن دم شد دراز و جان بداد
entries.english: The lover said to her, “Tell me, what is that root?” She said, “The root thereof is to die and be naught.

entries.farsi: ‏گفت اصلش مردن است و نيستى است / تو همه كردى نمردى زنده‏اى
entries.english: You have done all, you have not died, you are living. Listen; die, if you are a self-sacrificing friend!”

entries.farsi: همچو گل در باخت سر خندان و شاد / ماند آن خنده بر او وقف ابد
entries.english: Instantly he laid himself at full length and gave up the ghost: like the rose, he played away his head, laughing and rejoicing.

entries.farsi: همچو جان و عقل عارف بى‏كبد / نور مه آلوده كى گردد ابد
entries.english: That laughter remained with him as an endowment unto everlasting, like the untroubled spirit and reason of the gnostic.

entries.farsi: گر زند آن نور بر هر نيك و بد / او ز جمله پاك وا گردد به ماه
entries.english: How should the light of the moon ever become defiled, though its light strikes on everything good and evil?

entries.farsi: ‏همچو نور عقل و جان سوى اله / وصف پاكى وقف بر نور مه است
entries.english: Pure of all it returns to the moon, even as the light of the spirit and reason unto God.

entries.farsi: ‏تابشش گر بر نجاسات ره است / ز ان نجاسات ره و آلودگى
entries.english: The quality of purity is an endowment on the light of the moon, though its radiance is on the defilements of the way.

entries.farsi: ‏نور را حاصل نگردد بد رگى / ارْجِعِي بشنود نور آفتاب
entries.english: Malignity does not accrue to the light of the moon from those defilements of the way or from pollution.

entries.farsi: ‏سوى اصل خويش باز آمد شتاب / نه ز گلخنها بر او ننگى بماند
entries.english: The light of the sun heard Return, and came back in haste to its source.

entries.farsi: نه ز گلشنها بر او رنگى بماند / نور ديده و نور ديده باز گشت
entries.english: No disgrace remained with it from the ash pits, no colour remained with it from the rose-gardens.

entries.farsi: ‏ماند در سوداى او صحرا و دشت
entries.english: The light of the eye and the seer of the light returned: the desert and plain were left in passionate desire thereof.

sections.title_fa: يكى پرسيد از عالمى عارفى كه اگر در نماز كسى بگريد به آواز و آه كند و نوحه كند نمازش باطل شود جواب گفت كه نام آن آب ديده است تا آن گرينده چه ديده است، اگر شوق خدا ديده است و مى‏گريد يا پشيمانى گناهى نمازش تباه نشود بلكه كمال گيرد كه ال صالة الا بحضور القلب، و اگر او رنجورى تن يا فراق فرزند ديده است نمازش تباه شود كه اصل نماز ترك تن است و ترك فرزند ابراهيم‏وار كه فرزند را قربان مى‏كرد از بهر تكميل نماز و تن را به آتش نمرود مى‏سپرد، و امر آمد
مصطفى را صلى اللهَّ عليه و آله بدين خصال كه وَ اتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ‏و قَدْ كانَتْ لَكُمْ أُسْ وَةٌ حَسَ نَةٌ فِي َإِبْراهِيم
sections.title_en: A certain man asked a mystic theologian, “If any one weeps loudly during the ritual prayer and moan and lament, is his prayer rendered void?” He replied, “The name of those is ‘water of the eye’: consider what that weeper has seen: if he has seen longing for God or repentance for a sin and weeps, his prayer is not spoilt; nay, it attains perfection, for ‘there is no prayer without presence of the heart’;

but if he has seen bodily sickness or the loss of a son, his prayer is spoilt, for the foundation of prayer is the abandonment of the body and the abandonment of sons, like Abraham, who was offering his son as a sacrifice in order to perfect his prayer and giving up his body to Nimrod’s fire; and Mustafá, on whom be peace, was commanded to act after these manners: “follow the religion of Abraham.” “Truly you have had a good example in Abraham.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى پرسيد از مفتى به راز / گر كسى گريد به نوحه در نماز
entries.english: A certain man asked a mufti in private, “If any one weeps lamentably during the ritual prayer,

entries.farsi: آن نماز او عجب باطل شود / يا نمازش جايز و كامل بود
entries.english: I wonder, will his prayer be rendered void, or will his prayer be licit and perfect?”

entries.farsi: گفت آب ديده نامش بهر چيست / ‏بنگرى تا كه چه ديد او و گريست
entries.english: He replied, “Why is it named ‘the water of the eye’? You should consider what it saw and wept.

entries.farsi: آب ديده تا چه ديد او از نهان / ‏تا بدان شد او ز چشمه‏ى خود روان
entries.english: Consider what the water of the eye saw in secret, so that on that account it began to flow from its spring.

entries.farsi: آن جهان گر ديده است آن پر نياز / رونقى يابد ز نوحه آن نماز
entries.english: If the supplicant has seen yonder world, that prayer gains a lustre from lamentation;

entries.farsi: ور ز رنج تن بدان گريه و ز سوك / ‏ريسمان بگسست و هم بشكست دوك
entries.english: But if that weeping was caused by bodily pain or by mourning, the thread is snapped and the spindle too is broken.”

sections.title_fa: مريدى در آمد به خدمت شيخ و از اين شيخ پير سن نمى‏خواهم بلكه پير عقل و معرفت و اگر چه عيسى است عليه السلام در گهواره و يحيى است عليه السلام در مكتب كودكان، مريد شيخ را گريان ديد او نيز موافقت كرد و گريست، چون فارغ شد و به در آمد مريدى ديگر كه از حال شيخ واقف‏تر بود از سر غيرت در عقب او تيز بيرون آمد گفتش اى برادر من ترا گفته باشم اللهَّ اللهَّ تا نينديشى و

نگويى كه شيخ مى‏گريست و من نيز مى‏گريستم كه سى سال رياضت بى‏ريا بايد كرد و از عقبات و ،درياهاى پر نهنگ و كوههاى بلند پر شير و پلنگ مى‏بايد گذشت تا بدان گريه‏ى شيخ رسى يا نرسى اگر رسى شكر زويت لى الارض گويى بسيار
sections.title_en: A disciple came in to pay his respects to the Shaykh—and by this “Shaykh” I do not mean one old in years, but one old in understanding and knowledge, even if he is Jesus, on whom be peace, in the cradle, or Yahya, on whom be peace, in the children’s school. The disciple saw the Shaykh weeping; he too acted

in conformity and wept. When he had finished and gone forth, another disciple, who was more cognisant of the Shaykh’s spiritual state, impelled by jealousy, went out quickly after him and said to him, “O brother, I shall have told you: for God’s sake, for God’s sake, beware of thinking or saying that the Shaykh wept and you wept likewise; you must practise self-discipline without hypocrisy for thirty years, and you must traverse ravines and seas full of leviathans, and lofty mountains full of lions and leopards, that you may attain to that weeping of the Shaykh or not attain.
If you attain, you will often utter thanksgiving, ‘The earth was gathered together for me.’”

entries.farsi: شيخ را چون ديد گريان آن مريد / گشت گريان آب از چشمش دويد
entries.english: A disciple came into the presence of the Pír: the Pír was in weeping and lamentation.

entries.farsi: يك مريدى اندر آمد پيش پير / پير اندر گريه بود و در نفير
entries.english: When the disciple saw the Shaykh weeping, he began to weep: the tears ran from his eyes.

entries.farsi: گوشور يك بار خندد كر دو بار / چون كه الغ امال كند يارى به يار
entries.english: The man possessed of an ear laughs once, when a friend repeats a joke to a friend; the deaf man twice:

entries.farsi: بار اول از ره تقليد و سوم / ‏كه همى‏بيند كه مى‏خندند قوم
entries.english: The first time by way of conformity and affectation, because he sees the company laughing

entries.farsi: كر بخندد همچو ايشان آن زمان / ‏بى‏خبر از حالت خندندگان
entries.english: The deaf man laughs then like them, without knowing the state of the laughers.

entries.farsi: باز وا پرسد كه خنده بر چه بود / پس دوم كرت بخندد چون شنود
entries.english: Afterwards he inquires what the laughter was about, and then, having heard, he laughs a second time.

entries.farsi: پس مقلد نيز مانند كر است / ‏اندر آن شادى كه او را در سر است
entries.english: Hence the mere imitator, too, resembles the deaf man in respect of the joy that is in his head.

entries.farsi: پرتو شيخ آمد و منهل ز شيخ / ‏فيض شادى نه از مريدان بل ز شيخ
entries.english: It is the Shaykh’s reflection, and its source is in the Shaykh: the overflow of joy is not from the disciples; nay, it is from the Shaykh.

entries.farsi: چون جدا گردد ز جو داند عنود / كاندر او آن آب خوش از جوى بود
entries.english: Like a basket in water or a light on glass: if they think it from themselves, it is defect

entries.farsi: چون سبد در آب و نورى بر زجاج / ‏گر ز خود دانند آن باشد خداج
entries.english: When it is separated from the river, that perverse one will recognise that the sweet water within it was from the river;

entries.farsi: آبگينه هم بداند از غروب / ‏كان لمع بود از مه تابان خوب
entries.english: The glass also will recognise, at the setting that those beams were from the beauteous shining moon.

entries.farsi: چون كه چشمش را گشايد امر قم / ‏پس بخندد چون سحر بار دوم
entries.english: When the command “Arise!” opens his eye, then he will laugh, like the dawn, a second time.

entries.farsi: خنده‏ش آيد هم بر آن خنده‏ى خودش / ‏كه در آن تقليد بر مى‏آمدش
entries.english: He will even laugh at his own laughter which was produced in him in that imitation,

entries.farsi: گويد از چندين ره دور و دراز / كاين حقيقت بود و اين اسرار و راز
entries.english: And will say, “By all these far and long ways, and thinking that this was the Reality and that this was the Mystery and Secret,

entries.farsi: من در آن وادى چگونه خود ز دور / شاديى مى‏كردم از عميا و شور
entries.english: How in truth, in that valley, did I rejoice from afar through blindness and confusion?

entries.farsi: من چه مى‏بستم خيال و آن چه بود / درك سستم سست نقشى مى‏نمود
entries.english: What was I fancying, and what was it? My weak perception was showing a weak image.”

entries.farsi: طفل ره را فكرت مردان كجاست / ‏كو خيال او و كو تحقيق راست
entries.english: Where is the thought of the men in relation to the child of the Way? Where is his fancy in comparison with true realization?

entries.farsi: فكر طفالن دايه باشد يا كه شير / يا مويز و جوز يا گريه و نفير
entries.english: The thought of children is the nurse or milk or raisins and walnuts or weeping and crying.

entries.farsi: آن مقلد هست چون طفل عليل / ‏گر چه دارد بحث باريك و دليل
entries.english: The imitator is like a sick child, although he may have subtle argumentation and proofs.

entries.farsi: آن تعمق در دليل و در شكيل / ‏از بصيرت مى‏كند او را گسيل
entries.english: That profundity in proofs and difficult problems is severing him from insight.

entries.farsi: مايه‏اى كاو سرمه‏ى سر وى است / ‏برد و در اشكال گفتن كار بست
entries.english: It took away the stock, which is the eye salve of his inmost consciousness, and applied itself to the discussion of problems.

entries.farsi: اى مقلد از بخارا باز گرد / رو به خوارى تا شوى تو شير مرد
entries.english: O imitator, turn back from Bukhara: go to self-abasement (ba-khwárí) that you may become a hero,

entries.farsi: تا بخاراى دگر بينى درون / ‏صف در آن در محفلش ال يفقهون
entries.english: And that you may behold within another Bukhara, in the assembly place where the champions are unlearned.

entries.farsi: پيك اگر چه در زمين چابك تگى است / ‏چون به دريا رفت بگسسته رگى است
entries.english: Although the courier is a swift runner on land, when he goes to sea his sinews are broken.

entries.farsi: او حملناهم بود فى البر و بس / ‏آن كه محمول است در بحر اوست كس
entries.english: He is only We have borne them on the land; that one who is borne on the sea—he is somebody.

entries.farsi: بخشش بسيار دارد شه بدو / اى شده در وهم و تصويرى گرو
entries.english: The King has great bounty: run, O you who have become in pawn to an imagination and fancy.

entries.farsi: آن مريد ساده از تقليد نيز / گريه‏اى مى‏كرد وفق آن عزيز
entries.english: From conformity that simple disciple, too, was weeping in concert with the venerable;

entries.farsi: او مقلدوار همچون مرد كر / گريه مى‏ديد و ز موجب بى‏خبر
entries.english: Like the deaf man, he regarded the Shaykh’s weeping in the manner of a conformist and was unaware of the cause.

entries.farsi: چون بسى بگريست خدمت كرد و رفت / ‏از پى‏اش آمد مريد خاص تفت
entries.english: When he had wept a long while, he paid his respects and departed: the favourite disciple came quickly after him,

entries.farsi: گفت اى گريان چو ابر بى‏خبر / بر وفاق گريه‏ى شيخ نظر
entries.english: And said, “O you who art weeping like a witless cloud in concert with the weeping of the Shaykh of insight,

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اللهَّ اللهَّ اى وافى مريد / گر چه در تقليد هستى مستفيد
entries.english: For God’s sake, for God’s sake, for God’s sake, O loyal disciple, although in conformity you are seeking profit,

entries.farsi: تا نگويى ديدم آن شه مى‏گريست / ‏من چو او بگريستم كان منكرى است
entries.english: Take heed not to say, ‘I saw that king weeping, and I wept like him’; for that is denial.”

entries.farsi: گريه پر جهل و پر تقليد و ظن / ‏نيست همچون گريه‏ى آن موتمن
entries.english: A weeping full of ignorance and conformity and opinion is not like the weeping of that trusted one.

entries.farsi: تو قياس گريه بر گريه مساز / هست زين گريه بدان راه دراز
entries.english: Do not judge weeping by the analogy of another weeping: it is a long way from this weeping to that weeping.

entries.farsi: هست آن از بعد سى ساله جهاد / عقل آن جا هيچ نتواند فتاد
entries.english: That weeping is after thirty years warfare: the intellect can never get there.

entries.farsi: هست ز آن سوى خرد صد مرحله / ‏عقل را واقف مدان ز آن قافله
entries.english: Beyond reason there are a hundred stages: deem not the intellect to be acquainted with that caravan.

entries.farsi: گريه‏ى او خنده‏ى او آن سرى است / ‏ز انچه وهم عقل باشد آن برى است
entries.english: His weeping is neither from sorrow nor from joy: the spirit knows the weeping of the fountain of beauties.

entries.farsi: گريه‏ى او نه از غم است و نى فرح / ‏روح داند گريه‏ى عين الملح
entries.english: His weeping, his laughter— both are of Yonder and transcend all that the intellect may conceive.

entries.farsi: آب ديده‏ى او چو ديده‏ى او بود / ديده‏ى ناديده ديده كى شود
entries.english: His tears are like his eye: how should the sightless eye become a eye?

entries.farsi: آن چه او بيند نتان كردن مساس / ‏نه از قياس عقل و نز راه حواس
entries.english: That which he sees cannot be touched either by the analogical judgement of the intellect or by way of the senses.

entries.farsi: شب گريزد چون كه نور آيد ز دور / پس چه داند ظلمت شب حال نور
entries.english: Night flees when Light comes from afar: what, then, should the darkness of Night know concerning Light?

entries.farsi: پشه بگريزد ز باد بادها / پس چه داند پشه ذوق بادها
entries.english: The gnat flees from the keen wind: what, then, should the gnat know of the savour of the winds?

entries.farsi: بر حدث چون زد قدم دنگش كند / چون كه كردش نيست هم رنگش كند
entries.english: When the Eternal comes, the temporal is made vain: what, then, should the temporal know of Eternity?

entries.farsi: چون قديم آيد حدث گردد عبث / ‏پس كجا داند قديمى را حدث
entries.english: When Eternity comes in contact with the temporal, it strikes it dumb; when it has negated it, it makes it homogeneous.

entries.farsi: اين الم و حم اين حروف / ‏چون عصاى موسى آمد در وقوف
entries.english: You can find a hundred parallels if you wish, but I do not care, O dervish.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهى تو بيابى صد نظير ليك من / پروا ندارم اى فقير
entries.english: This Alif-Lám-Mím and Há-Mím— these Letters become, on comprehension, like the rod of Moses.

entries.farsi: حرفها ماند بدين حرف از برون / ‏ليك باشد در صفات اين زبون
entries.english: The letters resemble these Letters outwardly but are subject in respect of the attributes of the latter.

entries.farsi: هر كه گيرد او عصايى ز امتحان / ‏كى بود چون آن عصا وقت بيان
entries.english: A staff that any one takes on trial— how should it be described as being like that staff?

entries.farsi: عيسوى است اين دم نه هر باد و دمى / ‏كه بر آيد از فرح يا از غمى
entries.english: This Breath is of Jesus; it is not any wind and breath that arises from joy or sorrow.

entries.farsi: اين الم و حم اى پدر / آمده‏ست از حضرت مولى البشر
entries.english: This Alif-Lám-Mím and Há-Mím, O father, have come from the presence of the Lord of Mankind.

entries.farsi: هر الف المى چه مى‏ماند بدين / ‏گر تو جان دارى بدين چشمش مبين
entries.english: What resemblance has any alif-lám to these? Do not regard them with this eye, if you have a soul.

entries.farsi: هست تركيب محمد لحم و پوست / ‏گر چه در تركيب هر تن جنس اوست
entries.english: Although they are composed of letters, O sire, and resemble the composition of the common folk,.

entries.farsi: گر چه تركيبش حروف است اى همام / ‏مى‏بماند هم به تركيب عوام
entries.english: Mohammed is composed of flesh and skin; although everybody is homogeneous with him in its composition.

entries.farsi: گوشت دارد پوست دارد استخوان / ‏هيچ اين تركيب را باشد همان
entries.english: It has flesh, it has skin and bone; has this constitution the same qualities as his?

entries.farsi: كاندر آن تركيب آمد معجزات / ‏كه همه تركيبها گشتند مات
entries.english: No; for in that constitution there appeared miracles by which all constitutions were vanquished.

entries.farsi: همچنان تركيب حم كتاب / ‏هست بس بالا و ديگرها نشيب
entries.english: Likewise, the composition of the Há-Mím in the Book is exceedingly lofty, while the others are low,

entries.farsi: ز انكه زين تركيب آيد زندگى / ‏همچو نفخ صور در درماندگى
entries.english: Because from this composition comes life, like the blast of the trumpet, in helplessness.

entries.farsi: اژدها گردد شكافد بحر را / چون عصا حم از داد خدا
entries.english: By the dispensation of God Há-Mím becomes a dragon and cleaves the sea like the Moses’ staff.

entries.farsi: ظاهرش ماند به ظاهرها و ليك / ‏قرص نان از قرص مه دور است نيك
entries.english: Its external appearance resembles appearances, but the disc of bread is very far from the disc of the moon.

entries.farsi: گريه‏ى او خنده‏ى او نطق او / نيست از وى هست محض خلق هو
entries.english: His weeping, his laughter, and his speech are not from him: they are the pure nature of Hú.

entries.farsi: چون كه ظاهرها گرفتند احمقان / ‏و آن دقايق شد از ايشان بس نهان
entries.english: Since the foolish took the external appearances, and the subtleties were very much hidden from them,

entries.farsi: الجرم محجوب گشتند از غرض / ‏كه دقيقه فوت شد در معترض
entries.english: Necessarily they were debarred from the object; for the subtlety escaped on the occasion when it presented itself.

sections.title_fa: داستان آن كنيزك كه با خر خاتون شهوت مى‏راند و او را چون بز و خرس آموخته بود شهوت راندن آدميانه و كدويى در قضيب خر مى‏كرد تا از اندازه نگذرد، خاتون بر آن وقوف يافت ليكن دقيقه‏ى كدو را نديد كنيزك را به بهانه به راه كرد جايى دور و با خر جمع شد بى‏كدو هالك شد به فضيحت، كنيزك بى‏گاه باز آمد و نوحه كرد كه اى جانم و اى چشم روشنم كير ديدى كدو نديدى ذكر ديدى آن دگر ،نديدى، كل ناقص ملعون يعنى كل نظر و فهم ناقص ملعون و گر نه ناقصان چشم ظاهر مرحومند
ملعون نه‏اند، بر خوان لَيْسَ عَ لَى الأَْعْ مى‏ حَرَجٌ ، نفى حرج و نفى لعنت و نفى عتاب و غضب كرد
sections.title_en: Story of the maidservant who had trained a donkey to perform the functions of a man. Her mistress discovered it but did not perceive the device of the gourd; making a pretext, she sent the maid away to a distant place and had intercourse with the donkey and perished shamefully. The maid came back late and lamented, crying, “O my soul and O light of my eyes,.

The maid came back late and lamented, crying, “O my soul and O light of my eyes, you saw the cock but you did not see the gourd; you saw the penis but you did not see that other thing.”. Every deficient one is accursed, i.e. every deficient insight and understanding is accursed; for those deficient in respect of the outward eye are objects of mercy and are not accursed. Recite, It is no crime in the blind. He (God) has removed the crime, He has removed the curse,
and He has removed the reproach and the wrath.

entries.farsi: يك كنيزك يك خرى بر خود فگند / از وفور شهوت و فرط گزند
entries.english: There was a passionate fun loving maidservant.

entries.farsi: آن خر نر را به گان خو كرده بود / خر جماع آدمى پى برده بود
entries.english: Who had taught a donkey to perform the services of a man.

entries.farsi: يك كدويى بود حيلت سازه را / در نرش كردى پى اندازه را
entries.english: She had carved a protective flange from a gourd.

entries.farsi: در ذكر كردى كدو را آن عجوز / تا رود نيم ذكر وقت سپوز
entries.english: To fit on the donkey’s penis to prevent his going in too far.

entries.farsi: گر همه كير خر اندر وى رود / آن رحم و آن روده‏ها ويران شود
entries.english: Had the whole thing entered her, it would have destroyed her womb and intestines.

entries.farsi: خر همى‏شد الغر و خاتون او / مانده عاجز كز چه شد اين خر چو مو
entries.english: The ass was becoming lean, and his mistress remained helpless, saying, “Why has this ass become as thin as a hair?”

entries.farsi: نعل بندان را نمود آن خر كه چيست / ‏علت او كه نتيجه‏اش الغرى است
entries.english: She showed the ass to the shoeing-smiths and asked, “Why is the ass becoming so lean?”

entries.farsi: هيچ علت اندر او ظاهر نشد / هيچ كس از سر آن مخبر نشد
entries.english: No ailment was discerned in him, no one gave information concerning the secret cause.

entries.farsi: جد را بايد كه جان بنده بود / ز انكه جد جوينده يابنده بود
entries.english: She began to investigate in earnest: she became prepared to investigate at every moment.

entries.farsi: در تفحص اندر افتاد او به جد / شد تفحص را دمادم مستعد
entries.english: The soul must be devoted to earnest endeavour, for the earnest seeker will be a finder.

entries.farsi: چون تفحص كرد از حال اشك / ‏ديد خفته زير خر آن نرگسك
entries.english: She saw the maidservant sleeping under the donkey

entries.farsi: از شكاف در بديد آن حال را / بس عجب آمد از آن آن زال را
entries.english: Through a crack in the door she saw what was going on: the old woman marvelled greatly at it.

entries.farsi: خر همى‏گايد كنيزك را چنان / ‏كه به عقل و رسم مردان با زنان
entries.english: The ass was treating the maidservant exactly as a man takes a woman.

entries.farsi: در حسد شد گفت چون اين ممكن است / ‏پس من اوليتر كه خر ملك من است
entries.english: She became envious and said, “Since this is possible, then I have the best right, for the ass is my property.

entries.farsi: خر مهذب گشته و آموخته / ‏خوان نهادست و چراغ افروخته
entries.english: The ass has been perfectly trained and instructed: the table is laid and the lamp is lighted.”

entries.farsi: كرد ناديده و در خانه بكوفت / ‏كاى كنيزك چند خواهى خانه روفت
entries.english: Feigning to have seen nothing, she knocked at the door of the room, saying, “How long will you be sweeping the room, O maid?”

entries.farsi: از پى رو پوش مى‏گفت اين سخن / ‏كاى كنيزك آمدم در باز كن
entries.english: She spoke these words as a blind, “I have come, O maid: open the door.”

entries.farsi: كرد خاموش و كنيزك را نگفت / ‏راز را از بهر طمع خود نهفت
entries.english: She became silent and said no more to the maid: she concealed the secret for the sake of her own desire.

entries.farsi: پس كنيزك جمله آالت فساد / كرد پنهان پيش شد در را گشاد
entries.english: Thereupon the maid hid all the apparatus of iniquity and came forward and opened the door.

entries.farsi: رو ترش كرد و دو ديده پر ز نم / ‏لب فرو ماليد يعنى صايمم
entries.english: She made her face sour and her eyes full of moisture and rubbed her lips, meaning to say, “I am fasting.”

entries.farsi: در كف او نرمه جارويى كه من / ‏خانه را مى‏روفتم بهر عطن
entries.english: In her hand was a soft broom, as though to say, “I was sweeping the room in order to clean it.”

entries.farsi: چون كه با جاروب در را واگشاد / گفت خاتون زير لب كاى اوستاد
entries.english: When, with the broom, she opened the door, the mistress said under her breath, “O crafty one,

entries.farsi: رو ترش كردى و جارويى به كف / ‏چيست آن خر بر گسسته از علف
entries.english: You have made your face sour and a broom in your hand; what is the ass having turned away from his fodder?

entries.farsi: نيم كاره و خشمگين جنبان ذكر / ز انتظار تو دو چشمش سوى در
entries.english: Half-finished and angry, its penis still moving, the ass’s two eyes toward the door expecting you.”

entries.farsi: زير لب گفت اين نهان كرد از كنيز / داشتش آن دم چو بى‏جرمان عزيز
entries.english: This she said under her breath and concealed from the maid: at that moment she treated her, like innocent persons, honourably.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش كه چادر نه به سر / رو فالن خانه ز من پيغام بر
entries.english: Afterwards she said to her, “Put the chadar on your head, go and take a message from me to such and such a house.

entries.farsi: اين چنين گو وين چنين كن و آن چنان / ‏مختصر كردم من افسانه‏ى زنان
entries.english: Say so-and-so and do so-and-so.” I abridge the talk of the women.

entries.farsi: آن چه مقصود است مغز آن بگير / چون به راهش كرد آن زال ستير
entries.english: Take the gist of what is to the purpose. When the discreet old woman had sent her away,

entries.farsi: بود از مستى شهوت شادمان / ‏در فرو بست و همى‏گفت آن زمان
entries.english: She was happy in anticipation of the pleasurable passion: she shut the door, saying meanwhile,

entries.farsi: يافتم خلوت زنم از شكر بانگ / ‏رسته‏ام از چار دانگ و از دو دانگ
entries.english: “I have secured privacy, I will shout in thanksgiving: I am delivered from the four dángs and the two dángs.”

entries.farsi: از طرب گشته بز آن زن هزار / در شرار شهوت خر بى‏قرار
entries.english: From ecstatic pleasure her vagina was singing like a nightingale.

entries.farsi: چه بز آن كان شهوت او را بز گرفت / ‏بز گرفتن گيج را نبود شگفت
entries.english: She was impatient for the flame of passion. Having reached the heights of excitement, it was no wonder she was feeling dizzy.

entries.farsi: ميل شهوت كرد كند دل را و كور / تا نمايد خر چو يوسف نار نور
entries.english: Lustful desire makes the heart deaf and blind, so that an ass seems like Joseph, fire (like) light.

entries.farsi: اى بسا سر مست نار و نار جو / خويشتن را نور مطلق داند او
entries.english: Oh, many a one intoxicated with fire and seeking fire deems himself absolute light.

entries.farsi: جز مگر بنده‏ى خدا يا جذب حق / ‏با رهش آرد بگرداند ورق
entries.english: Unless a servant of God, or the pull of God, lead him into the way and turn over his leaf,

entries.farsi: تا بداند كان خيال ناريه / ‏در طريقت نيست الا عاريه
entries.english: So that he may know that the fiery phantom in the Path is but a loan.

entries.farsi: زشتها را خوب بنمايد شره / ‏نيست چون شهوت بتر ز آفات ره
entries.english: Greed causes foul things to appear fair: among the challenges of the Way there is none like lust, none worse.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران نام خوش را كرد ننگ / ‏صد هزاران زيركان را كرد دنگ
entries.english: It has disgraced a hundred thousand good names; it has stupefied a hundred thousand clever men.

entries.farsi: چون خرى را يوسف مصرى نمود / يوسفى را چون نمايد آن جهود
entries.english: Since it caused an ass to appear Joseph of Egypt, how will that Jew cause a Joseph to appear?

entries.farsi: بر تو سرگين را فسونش شهد كرد / شهد را خود چون كند وقت نبرد
entries.english: Its spell made dung seem honey to you: what, fortruth, will it make honey seem at the time of contest?

entries.farsi: شهوت از خوردن بود كم كن ز خور / يا نكاحى كن گريزان شو ز شر
entries.english: Lust arises from eating and drinking: diminish your food, or marry and flee from wickedness.

entries.farsi: چون بخوردى مى‏كشد سوى حرم / ‏دخل را خرجى ببايد الجرم
entries.english: When you have eaten and drunk, it leads to things forbidden: there must necessarily be some outgoing of income.

entries.farsi: پس نكاح آمد چو الحول و ال / تا كه ديوت نفگند اندر بال
entries.english: Marriage, then, is like, “There is neither power nor strength except in God,” lest the Devil cast you into temptation.

entries.farsi: بار سنگى بر خرى كه مى‏جهد / زود بر نه پيش از آن كاو بر نهد
entries.english: Since you are fond of eating and drinking, ask a woman at once; else the cat comes and carries off the fat sheep’s tail.

entries.farsi: چون حريص خوردنى زن خواه زود / ور نه آمد گربه و دنبه ربود
entries.english: Quickly put a heavy load on the shying ass before he puts down.

entries.farsi: فعل آتش را نمى‏دانى تو برد / گرد آتش با چنين دانش مگرد
entries.english: You do not know the effect of fire, hold aloof: do not approach the fire with such knowledge.

entries.farsi: علم ديگ و آتش ار نبود ترا / از شرر نه ديگ ماند نه ابا
entries.english: If you have no knowledge of the cooking-pot and the fire, neither the pot nor the soup will be spared by the flames.

entries.farsi: آب حاضر بايد و فرهنگ نيز / تا پزد آن ديگ سالم در ازيز
entries.english: Water must be there and skill too, in order that the pot may be safely cooked in boiling.

entries.farsi: چون ندانى دانش آهنگرى / ‏ريش و مو سوزد چو آن جا بگذرى
entries.english: If you are ignorant of the science of the ironsmith; your beard and hair will be burned when you pass by the forge

entries.farsi: در فرو بست آن زن و خر را كشيد / شادمانه الجرم كيفر چشيد
entries.english: The woman closed the door and dragged the ass into the house and undoubtedly enjoying herself.

entries.farsi: در ميان خانه آوردش كشان / ‏خفت اندر زير آن نر خر ستان
entries.english: She slowly pulled him into the house and lay beneath him.

entries.farsi: هم بر آن كرسى كه ديد او از كنيز / تا رسد در كام خود آن قحبه نيز
entries.english: She stood on the same chair she had seen the maid servant use.

entries.farsi: پا بر آورد و خر اندر وى سپوخت / ‏آتشى از كير خود در وى فروخت
entries.english: She raised her legs and the ass penetrated her. His penis set her on fire

entries.farsi: خر مودب گشته در خاتون فشرد / تا به خايه در زمان خاتون بمرد
entries.english: The donkey politely penetrated her up to his testicles until she died.

entries.farsi: بر دريد از زخم كير خر جگر / روده‏ها بگسسته شد از همدگر
entries.english: The donkey’s member burst her liver and tore apart her intestines.

entries.farsi: دم نزد در حال آن زن جان بداد / كرسى از يك سو زن از يك سو فتاد
entries.english: She did not utter a word as she lay down her life. The chair went one way and the woman the other.

entries.farsi: صحن خانه پر ز خون شد زن نگون / ‏مرد او و برد جان ريب المنون
entries.english: The courtyard was smeared with blood, and the woman lay prostrate. Calamity had arrived.

entries.farsi: تو عَذابَ الْخِزْيِ بشنو از نبى / ‏در چنين ننگى مكن جان را فدى
entries.english: Such a bad end, O reader; have you ever seen a martyr to a donkey’s penis?

entries.farsi: مرگ بد با صد فضيحت اى پدر / تو شهيدى ديده‏اى از كير خر
entries.english: Hear from the Qur’an the torment of disgrace: do not sacrifice your life in such a shameful cause.

entries.farsi: دان كه اين نفس بهيمى نر خر است / ‏زير او بودن از آن ننگين‏تر است
entries.english: Know that the male ass is this bestial soul: to be under it is more shameful than that.

entries.farsi: در ره نفس ار بميرى در منى / ‏تو حقيقت دان كه مثل آن زنى
entries.english: If you die in egoism in the way of the fleshly soul, know for certain that you are like that woman.

entries.farsi: نفس ما را صورت خر بدهد او / ز انكه صورتها كند بر وفق خو
entries.english: He will give our fleshly soul the form of an ass, because He makes the forms to be in accordance with the nature.

entries.farsi: اين بود اظهار سر در رستخيز / اللهَّ اللهَّ از تن چون خر گريز
entries.english: This is the manifestation of the secret at the Resurrection: by God, by God, flee from the ass-like body!

entries.farsi: كافران را بيم كرد ايزد ز نار / كافران گفتند نار اولى ز عار
entries.english: God terrified the unbelievers with the Fire: the unbelievers said, “Better the Fire than shame.”

entries.farsi: گفت نى آن نار اصل عارهاست / ‏همچو اين نارى كه اين زن را بكاست
entries.english: He said, “Nay, that Fire is the source of all shame”— like the fire that destroyed this woman.

entries.farsi: لقمه اندازه نخورد از حرص خود / در گلو بگرفت لقمه‏ى مرگ بد
entries.english: In her greed she ate immoderately: the mouthful of an infamous death stuck in her throat.

entries.farsi: لقمه اندازه خور اى مرد حريص / ‏گر چه باشد لقمه حلوا و خبيص
entries.english: Eat in moderation, O greedy man, though it is a mouthful of halwá or khabís.

entries.farsi: حق تعالى داد ميزان را زبان / ‏هين ز قرآن سوره‏ى رحمان بخوان
entries.english: The high God has given the balance a tongue: listen, recite the Súratu’ l-Rahmán in the Qur’an.

entries.farsi: هين ز حرص خويش ميزان را مهل / ‏آز و حرص آمد ترا خصم مضل
entries.english: Beware; do not in your greed let the balance go: cupidity and greed are enemies that lead you to perdition.

entries.farsi: حرص جويد كل بر آيد او ز كل / ‏حرص مپرست اى فجل ابن الفجل
entries.english: Greed craves all and loses all: do not serve greed, O ignoble son of the ignoble.

entries.farsi: آن كنيزك مى‏شد و مى‏گفت آه / ‏كردى اى خاتون تو استارا به راه
entries.english: The maid, while she went, was saying, “Ah, mistress, you have sent away the expert.

entries.farsi: اى ز من دزديده علمى ناتمام / ‏ننگت آمد كه بپرسى حال دام
entries.english: You will set to work without the expert and will foolishly hazard your life.

entries.farsi: كار بى‏استاد خواهى ساختن / ‏جاهالنه جان بخواهى باختن
entries.english: O you who have stolen from me an imperfect knowledge, you were ashamed to ask about the trap.”

entries.farsi: هم بچيدى دانه مرغ از خرمنش / ‏هم نيفتادى رسن در گردنش
entries.english: The bird had picked the grain from its stack; the cord would not have fallen on its neck.

entries.farsi: دانه كمتر خور مكن چندين رفو / چون كُلُوا خواندى بخوان ال تسرفوا
entries.english: Eat less of the grain, do not patch so much: after having recited, eat, recite do not exceed,

entries.farsi: تا خورى دانه نيفتى تو بدام / ‏اين كند علم و قناعت و السلام
entries.english: So that you may eat the grain and not fall into the trap. Knowledge and contentment affect this. And now farewell.

entries.farsi: نعمت از دنيا خورد عاقل نه غم / ‏جاهالن محروم مانده در ندم
entries.english: The wise man gets happiness from the present life, not sorrow, the ignorant are left in disappointment and regret.

entries.farsi: چون در افتد در گلوشان حبل دام / ‏دانه خوردن گشت بر جمله حرام
entries.english: When the trap-cord falls on their throats, it becomes unlawful to them all to eat the grain.

entries.farsi: مرغ اندر دام دانه كى خورد / دانه چون زهر است در دام ار چرد
entries.english: How should the bird in the trap eat the grain? The grain in the trap is like poison, if he feed.

entries.farsi: مرغ غافل مى‏خورد دانه ز دام / ‏همچو اندر دام دنيا اين عوام
entries.english: The heedless bird will eat grain from the trap, as these common folk do in the trap of the present world.

entries.farsi: باز مرغان خبير هوشمند / كرده‏اند از دانه خود را خشك بند
entries.english: Again, the knowing and prudent birds have debarred themselves from the grain;

entries.farsi: كاندرون دام دانه زهرباست / ‏كور آن مرغى كه در فخ دانه خواست
entries.english: For the grain in the trap is poisonous food: blind is the bird that desires the grain in the trap.

entries.farsi: صاحب دام ابلهان را سر بريد / و ان ظريفان را به مجلسها كشيد
entries.english: The Owner of the trap cut off the heads of the foolish ones, and conducted the clever ones to the assembly-places;

entries.farsi: كه از آنها گوشت‏مى‏آيد بكار / و ز ظريفان بانگ و ناله‏ى زير و زار
entries.english: For in the former the flesh is serviceable, but in the clever ones song and warble soft and low

entries.farsi: پس كنيزك آمد از اشكاف در / ديد خاتون را بمرده زير خر
entries.english: The maidservant returned from her duties and saw the lady lying dead below the donkey.

entries.farsi: گفت اى خاتون احمق اين چه بود / گر ترا استاد خوش نقشى نمود
entries.english: “O stupid woman!’ she said, what is this? Did your teacher not provide you with the full picture?

entries.farsi: ظاهرش ديدى سرش از تو نهان / ‏اوستا ناگشته بگشادى دكان
entries.english: You saw only the appearance while the secret remained hidden from you. You opened a shop without mastering the tricks of the trade.

entries.farsi: ير ديدى همچو شهد و چون خبيص / ‏آن كدو را چون نديدى اى حريص
entries.english: You saw only the penis which appeared so sweet to you.

entries.farsi: يا چو مستغرق شدى در عشق خر / آن كدو پنهان بماندت از نظر
entries.english: In your greed you neglected to see the gourd.

entries.farsi: اى بسا زراق گول بى‏وقوف / ‏از ره مردان نديده غير صوف
entries.english: Or were you so in love with the ass, that the pumpkin remained hidden from your sight.

entries.farsi: اى بسا شوخان ز اندك احتراف / ‏از شهان ناموخته جز گفت و الف
entries.english: Oh, there is many a stupid ignorant hypocrite who has seen nothing of the Way of the men except the woollen mantle (súf).

entries.farsi: ظاهر صنعت بديدى ز اوستاد / اوستادى بر گرفتى شاد شاد
entries.english: Oh, there are many impudent fellows who, with little practice, have learned from the kings nothing but talk and brag.

entries.farsi: هر يكى در كف عصا كه موسى‏ام / ‏مى‏دمد بر ابلهان كه عيسى‏ام
entries.english: Every one, staff in hand, says, “I am Moses,” and breathes upon the foolish folk, saying, “I am Jesus.”

entries.farsi: آه از آن روزى كه صدق صادقان / ‏باز خواهد از تو سنگ امتحان
entries.english: Alas the Day when the touchstone will demand from you the sincerity of the sincere!

entries.farsi: آخر از استاد باقى را بپرس / ‏اين حريصان جمله كورانند و خرس
entries.english: Come, inquire of the Master the remainder; or are the greedy ones all blind and deaf?

entries.farsi: جمله جستى باز ماندى از همه / ‏صيد گرگانند اين ابله رمه
entries.english: You craved all and you lost all: this foolish flock is the prey of wolves.

entries.farsi: صورتى بشنيده گشتى ترجمان / ‏بى‏خبر از گفت خود چون طوطيان
entries.english: Having heard a form, you have become its expounder, ignorant of your words— like parrots.

sections.title_fa: تمثيل تلقين شيخ مريدان را و پيغامبر امت را كه ايشان طاقت تلقين حق ندارند و با حق الفت ندارند چنان كه طوطى با صورت آدمى الفت ندارد كه از او تلقين تواند گرفت حق تعالى شيخ را چون آينه ،‏اى پيش مريد همچو طوطى دارد و از پس آينه تلقين مى‏كند ال تُحَرِّكْ بِهِ لِسانَكَ إِنْ هُوَ إِلاَّ وَحْ يٌ يُوحى اين است ابتداى مسئله‏ى بى‏منتهى چنان كه منقار جنبانيدن طوطى اندرون آينه كه خيالش مى‏خوانى بى‏اختيار و تصرف اوست عكس خواندن طوطى برونى كه متعلم است نه عكس آن معلم كه پس آينه
است و ليكن خواندن طوطى برونى تصرف آن معلم است پس اين مثال آمد نه مثل
sections.title_en: The instruction given by a Shaykh to disciples, or by a prophet to a people, who are unable to receive the Divine lesson and have no familiar acquaintance with God, may be compared with the case of a parrot which has no such acquaintance with the form of a man, so that it should

be able to receive instruction from him. God most High holds the Shaykh in front of the disciple, as the mirror in front of the parrot, while He dictates from behind the mirror, saying, “Do not move your tongue to hasten it; it is naught but an inspiration that is inspired.” This is the beginning of an endless problem. When the parrot, which you call the image,

moves its beak in the mirror, the movement is not by its own volition and power: it is the reflection of the articulation by the parrot outside, which is the learner; not the reflection of the Teacher behind the mirror; but the external parrot’s articulation is controlled by the Teacher. This, then, is a comparison, not a similitude.

entries.farsi: طوطيى در آينه مى‏بيند او / عكس خود را پيش او آورده رو
entries.english: A parrot sees its reflection facing it in the mirror.

entries.farsi: در پس آيينه آن استا نهان / ‏حرف مى‏گويد اديب خوش زبان
entries.english: The teacher is concealed behind the mirror: that sweet-tongued well-instructed man is talking.

entries.farsi: طوطيك پنداشته كين گفت پست / ‏گفتن طوطى است كاندر آينه است
entries.english: The little parrot thinks that these words uttered in low tones are spoken by the parrot in the mirror.

entries.farsi: پس ز جنس خويش آموزد سخن / ‏بى‏خبر از مكر آن گرگ كهن
entries.english: Therefore it learns speech from one of its own kind, being unaware of the cunning of that old wolf.

entries.farsi: از پس آيينه مى‏آموزدش / ‏ور نه ناموزد جز از جنس خودش
entries.english: He is teaching it behind the mirror; otherwise it does not learn except from its congeners.

entries.farsi: از بشر بگرفت منطق يك به يك / ‏از بشر جز اين چه داند طوطيك
entries.english: It learned to talk from that accomplished man, but it is ignorant of his meaning and mystery.

entries.farsi: گفت را آموخت ز آن مرد هنر / ليك از معنى و سرش بى‏خبر
entries.english: It received speech, word by word, from Man; what should the little parrot know of Man except this?

entries.farsi: همچنان در آينه‏ى جسم ولى / ‏خويش را بيند مريد ممتلى
entries.english: Similarly, the disciple full sees himself in the mirror of the Shaykh’s body.

entries.farsi: از پس آيينه عقل كل را / كى ببيند وقت گفت و ماجرا
entries.english: How should he see Universal Reason behind the mirror at the time of speech and discourse?

entries.farsi: او گمان دارد كه مى‏گويد بشر / و آن دگر سر است و او ز آن بى‏خبر
entries.english: He supposes that a man is speaking; and the other is a mystery of which he is ignorant.

entries.farsi: حرف آموزد ولى سر قديم / ‏او نداند طوطى است او نى نديم
entries.english: He learns the words, but the eternal mystery he cannot know, for he is a parrot, not a boon-companion.

entries.farsi: هم صفير مرغ آموزند خلق / ‏كاين سخن كار دهان افتاد و حلق
entries.english: Likewise, people learn the note of birds, for this speech is an affair of the mouth and throat;

entries.farsi: ليك از معنى مرغان بى‏خبر / جز سليمان قرانى خوش نظر
entries.english: But ignorant of the birds’ meaning, except an august Solomon of goodly insight

entries.farsi: حرف درويشان بس آموختند / منبر و محفل بدان افروختند
entries.english: Many learned the language of dervishes and gave added lustre to the pulpit and assembly-place.

entries.farsi: يا بجز آن حرفشان روزى نبود / يا در آخر رحمت آمد ره نمود
entries.english: Either nothing was bestowed upon them except those expressions, or at last mercy came and revealed the way.

sections.title_fa: صاحب دلى ديد سگى حامله در شكم آن سگ بچگان بانگ مى‏كردند در تعجب ماند كه حكمت بانگ سگ پاسبانى است بانگ در اندرون شكم مادر پاسبانى نيست و نيز بانگ جهت يارى خواستن و شير خواستن باشد و غيره و اينجا هيچ از اين فايده‏ها نيست، چون به خويش آمد با حضرت مناجات كرد وَ ما يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُ إِلاَّ اللهَُّ‏جواب آمد كه آن صورت حال قومى است از حجاب بيرون نيامده و چشم دل باز ناشده دعوى بصيرت كنند و مقالات گويند، از آن نه ايشان را قوتى و ياريى رسد و نه
مستمعان را هدايتى و رشدى
sections.title_en: A mystic saw a bitch big with young, in whose womb the young were barking. He remained in amazement, saying, “The reason of a dog’s barking is to keep watch: to bark in the mother’s womb is not keeping watch; and, again, barking may be a call for help, or its cause may be a desire

for milk, etc.; and there is no such purpose in this case.” When he came to himself, he made supplication to God—and no one knows the interpretation thereof except Allah. Answer came: “It represents the state of a party who pretend to insight and utter sayings without having come forth from the veil and before the eyes of their hearts have been opened.
Therefore neither to themselves do strength and support accrue, nor to their hearers any guidance and right direction.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏ديد خواب اندر چله / در رهى ماده سگى بد حامله
entries.english: During a chila (forty days’ religious seclusion), a certain man dreamed that he saw a bitch big with young on a road.

entries.farsi: بس عجب آمد و را آن بانگها / سگ بچه اندر شكم چون زد ندا
entries.english: Suddenly he heard the cry of puppies: the puppies were in the womb, invisible.

entries.farsi: ناگهان آواز سگ بچگان شنيد / سگ بچه اندر شكم بد ناپديد
entries.english: The yelps astonished him exceedingly: how the puppies called out in the womb.

entries.farsi: سگ بچه اندر شكم ناله كنان / هيچ كس ديده‏ست اين اندر جهان
entries.english: Puppies howling in the womb—“has any one,”, “ever seen this in the world?”

entries.farsi: چون بجست از واقعه آمد به خويش / ‏حيرت او دم‏به‏دم مى‏گشت بيش
entries.english: When he sprang up from his dream and came to himself, his perplexity was increasing at every moment.

entries.farsi: در چله كس نى كه گردد عقده حل / ‏جز كه درگاه خدا عز و جل
entries.english: During the chila there was none by whom the knot should be untied except the Presence of God Almighty and Glorious.

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب زين شكال و گفت و گو / در چله وامانده‏ام از ذكر تو
entries.english: He said, “O Lord, on account of this difficulty and debate I am deprived of recollection (dhikr) of You during the chila.

entries.farsi: پر من بگشاى تا پران شوم / ‏در حديقه‏ى ذكر و سيبستان شوم
entries.english: Loose my wings, that I may soar and enter the garden of recollection and the apple-orchard.”

entries.farsi: آمدش آواز هاتف در زمان / ‏كان مثالى دان ز الف جاهالن
entries.english: At once there came to him a mysterious voice, saying, “Know that it is an emblem of the idle talk of the ignorant,

entries.farsi: بانگ سگ اندر شكم باشد زيان / ‏نه شكار انگيز و نه شب پاسبان
entries.english: Who, without having come forth from the veil and curtain, blindfold have begun to speak in vain.”

entries.farsi: كز حجاب و پرده بيرون نامده / ‏چشم بسته بى‏هده گويان شده
entries.english: The yelp of the dog in the womb is loss: he is neither a starter of hunted animals nor a keeper of watch by night.

entries.farsi: گرگ ناديده كه منع او بود / دزد ناديده كه دفع او شود
entries.english: He has not seen the wolf, so as to prevent him; he has not seen the robber, so as to repel him.

entries.farsi: از حريصى وز هواى سرورى / ‏در نظر كند و به الفيدن جرى
entries.english: He, because of covetousness and desire for eminence, is dull as regards vision and bold in prating.

entries.farsi: از هواى مشترى و گرم دار / بى‏بصيرت پا نهاده در فشار
entries.english: From desire for the purchaser and warm admirer, devoid of insight, he begins to talk nonsense.

entries.farsi: ماه ناديده نشانها مى‏دهد / روستايى را بدان كژ مى‏نهد
entries.english: Without having seen the Moon, he gives indications: thereby he perverts the countryman.

entries.farsi: از براى مشترى در وصف ماه / ‏صد نشان ناديده گويد بهر جاه
entries.english: On account of the purchaser he gives a hundred indications describing the Moon which he has never seen, for the sake of power.

entries.farsi: مشترى كاو سود دارد خود يكى است / ‏ليك ايشان را در او ريب و شكى است
entries.english: There is in truth one Purchaser who is profitable, but concerning Him they have a suspicion and doubt.

entries.farsi: از هواى مشترى بى‏شكوه / ‏مشترى را باد دادند اين گروه
entries.english: In their desire for the inglorious purchaser, these people have thrown the Purchaser to the winds.

entries.farsi: مشترى ماست اللهَّ اشترى / از غم هر مشترى هين برتر آ
entries.english: He is our Purchaser—God has purchased: listen, rise above anxiety for any purchaser.

entries.farsi: مشتريى جو كه جويان تو است / ‏عالم آغاز و پايان تو است
entries.english: Seek the Purchaser who is seeking you, One who knows your beginning and end.

entries.farsi: هين مكش هر مشترى را تو به دست / ‏عشق بازى با دو معشوقه بد است
entries.english: Beware, do not try to win every purchaser: it is bad to make love to two sweethearts.

entries.farsi: زو نيابى سود و مايه گر خرد / نبودش خود قيمت عقل و خرد
entries.english: You will not get interest or capital from him, if he purchases you: in truth he has not the price for reason and intellect.

entries.farsi: يست او را خود بهاى نيم نعل / ‏تو بر او عرضه كنى ياقوت و لعل
entries.english: He has not even the price of half a horseshoe, and you are offering him minerals and rubies.

entries.farsi: حرص كورت كرد و محرومت كند / ديو همچون خويش مرجومت كند
entries.english: Greed has blinded you and will deprive you: the Devil will make you accursed like himself.

entries.farsi: همچنانك اصحاب فيل و قوم لوط / كردشان مرجوم چون خود آن سخوط
entries.english: Just as that wrathful made accursed like himself the Fellows of the Elephant and the people of Lot.

entries.farsi: مشترى را صابران دريافتند / چون سوى هر مشترى نشتافتند
entries.english: The patient have gained the Purchaser, since they did not hasten towards every purchaser.

entries.farsi: آن گه گردانيد رو ز آن مشترى / ‏بخت و اقبال و بقا شد رو برى
entries.english: He that averted his face from that Purchaser— fortune and felicity and everlasting life are quit of him.

entries.farsi: ماند حسرت بر حريصان تا ابد / همچو حال اهل ضروان در حسد
entries.english: Grief remains for ever on the covetous, as happened to the people of Zarwán in their envy.

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى اهل ضروان و حسد ايشان بر درويشان كه پدر ما از سليمى اغلب دخل باغ را به مسكينان مى‏داد چون انگور بودى عشر دادى و چون مويز و دوشاب شدى عشر دادى و چون حلوا و پالوده كردى عشر دادى و از قصيل عشر دادى و چون در خرمن كوفتى از كفه‏ى آميخته عشر دادى و چون گندم از كاه جدا شدى عشر دادى و چون آرد كردى عشر دادى و چون خمير كردى عشر دادى

و چون نان كردى عشر دادى الجرم حق تعالى در آن باغ و كشت بركتى نهاده بود كه همه اصحاب باغها محتاج او بدندى هم به ميوه و هم به سيم و او محتاج هيچ كس نى از ايشان، فرزندانشان خرج عشر مى‏ديدند مكرر و آن بركت را نمى‏ديدند همچون آن زن بد بخت كه آلت خر را ديد و كدو را نديد

sections.title_en: Story of the people of Zarwán and their envy of the poor. “Our father,” they said, “from simplicity used to give to the poor the most part of the produce of his orchard.” When it was grapes, he would give a tithe; and when they were turned into raisins or syrup, he would give a tithe; and whenever he made halwá or pálúda, he would give a tithe; and he would give a tithe of the corn-sheaves, and when he threshed, he would give a tithe of the unthreshed ears mixed; and when he separated

the wheat from the straw, he would give a tithe; and when he made flour, he would give a tithe; and when he leavened the dough, he would give a tithe; and when he made bread, he would give a tithe. Consequently God most High had laid such a blessing on his orchard and crops that all the owners of orchards were in need of him, both for fruit and money, while he needed nothing from any of them. His sons saw the repeated payment of tithes, and did not see the blessing, velut
illa femina infelix quae veretrum asini vidit, cucurbitam non vidit.

entries.farsi: بود مردى صالحى ربانيى / ‏عقل كامل داشت و پايان دانيى
entries.english: There was a righteous godly man: he had perfect intelligence and a (great) foresight as to the end.

entries.farsi: در ده ضروان به نزديك يمن / ‏شهره اندر صدقه و خلق حسن
entries.english: In the village of Zarwán, near Yemen, renowned for almsgiving and good disposition

entries.farsi: كعبه‏ى درويش بودى كوى او / آمدندى مستمندان سوى او
entries.english: His abode was the Ka‘ba of the poor: the distressed were coming to him.

entries.farsi: هم ز خوشه عشر دادى بى‏ريا / هم ز گندم چون شدى از كه جدا
entries.english: He would give, unostentatiously, a tithe both of the ears of corn and of the wheat when it was separated from the chaff.

entries.farsi: آرد گشتى عشر دادى هم از آن / ‏نان شدى عشر دگر دادى ز نان
entries.english: It was made into flour, he would give a tithe of that too; if it was made into bread, he would give another tithe of the bread.

entries.farsi: عشر هر دخلى فرو نگذاشتى / ‏چار باره دادى ز آن چه كاشتى
entries.english: He would never omit the tithe of any produce: he would give four times on that which he planted.

entries.farsi: بس وصيتها بگفتى هر زمان / ‏جمع فرزندان خود را آن جوان
entries.english: That young man was continually giving many injunctions to all his sons,

entries.farsi: اللهَّ اللهَّ قسم مسكين بعد من / ‏وا مگيريدش ز حرص خويشتن
entries.english: Saying, “For God’s sake, for God’s sake, after I am gone, do not on account of your covetousness withhold the portion of the poor,

entries.farsi: تا بماند بر شما كشت و ثمار / در پناه طاعت حق پايدار
entries.english: So that the crops and fruit may remain permanent on you under the safeguard of your obedience to God”

entries.farsi: دخلها و ميوه‏ها جمله ز غيب / ‏حق فرستاده‏ست بى‏تخمين و ريب
entries.english: Without surmise or doubt, God has sent all produce and fruits from the Unseen.

entries.farsi: در محل دخل اگر خرجى كنى / ‏درگه سود است سودى بر زنى
entries.english: If you expend something in the place where the produce comes, it is the gateway to profit: you will obtain a profit.

entries.farsi: ترك اغلب دخل را در كشتزار / باز كارد كه وى است اصل ثمار
entries.english: The Turk sows the major part of the produce again in the field, because it is the source of the fruits.

entries.farsi: بيشتر كارد خورد ز آن اندكى / ‏كه ندارد در بروييدن شكى
entries.english: He sows most of it and consumes a little, for he has no doubt of its growing.

entries.farsi: ز ان بيفشاند به كشتن ترك دست / ‏كان غله‏ش هم ز آن زمين حاصل شده است
entries.english: The Turk shakes his hand in sowing, because that crop of his has been produced from the same soil.

entries.farsi: كفشگر هم آن چه افزايد ز نان / ‏مى‏خرد چرم و اديم و سختيان
entries.english: Likewise the shoemaker buys hide and leather and morocco the surplus left over from bread,

entries.farsi: كه اصول دخلم اينها بوده‏اند / هم ز اينها مى‏گشايد رزق بند
entries.english: Saying, “These have been the sources of my income: from these, accordingly, my means of livelihood are flowing.”

entries.farsi: دخل از آن جا آمده‏ستش الجرم / ‏هم در آن جا مى‏كند داد و كرم
entries.english: His income has come from that place: consequently he bestows in the same place with liberality and generosity.

entries.farsi: اين زمين و سختيان پرده‏ست و بس / ‏اصل روزى از خدا دان هر نفس
entries.english: This soil that produces crops and morocco are only a veil: know that at every moment the source of livelihood is in God.

entries.farsi: چون بكارى در زمين اصل كار / تا برويد هر يكى را صد هزار
entries.english: When you sow, sow in the soil of the Origin, that for every single a hundred thousand may grow.

entries.farsi: گيرم اكنون تخم را گر كاشتى / ‏در زمينى كه سبب پنداشتى
entries.english: If just now you have sown seed, I will suppose, in a soil which you thought a means.

entries.farsi: چون دو سه سال آن نرويد چون كنى / ‏جز كه در البه و دعا كف در زنى
entries.english: When it does not grow for two or three years, how can you do anything but put your hand in supplication and prayer?

entries.farsi: دست بر سر مى‏زنى پيش اله / ‏دست و سر بر دادن رزقش گواه
entries.english: You will beat your hand on your head in the presence of God: hand and head bear witness to His giving sustenance;

entries.farsi: تا بدانى اصل اصل رزق اوست / ‏تا همو را جويد آن كه رزق جوست
entries.english: So that you may know that He is the Source of the source of sustenance, and that the seeker of sustenance may seek only Him.

entries.farsi: رزق از وى جو مجو از زيد و عمر / مستى از وى جو مجو از بنگ و خمر
entries.english: Seek sustenance from Him, do not seek it from Zayd and Amr: seek intoxication from Him, do not seek it from hashish and wine.

entries.farsi: توانگرى زو خواه نه از گنج و مال / ‏نصرت از وى خواه نه از عم و خال
entries.english: Desire wealth from Him, not from treasure and possessions: desire aid from Him, not from paternal and maternal uncles.

entries.farsi: عاقبت زينها بخواهى ماندن / ‏هين كه را خواهى در آن دم خواندن
entries.english: At the last you will be left without these things: listen, unto whom will you call then?

entries.farsi: اين دم او را خوان و باقى را بمان / ‏تا تو باشى وارث ملك جهان
entries.english: Call unto Him now, and leave the rest, that you may inherit the kingdom of the world.

entries.farsi: چون يَفِرُّ الْمَرْءُ آيد مِنْ أخيه / ‏يهرب المولود يوما من ابيه
entries.english: When comes a man shall flee from his brother and on such a Day the son will flee from his father,

entries.farsi: ز آن شود هر دوست آن ساعت عدو / كه بت تو بود و از ره مانع او
entries.english: In that hour every friend will become your foe, because he was your idol and one who hindered from the Way.

entries.farsi: روى از نقاش رو مى‏تافتى / ‏چون ز نقشى انس دل مى‏يافتى
entries.english: You were averting your face from the Painter of the face, since you were gaining heart’s delight from a picture.

entries.farsi: اين دم ار يارانت با تو ضد شوند / وز تو بر گردند و در خصمى روند
entries.english: If at this time your friends become hostile to you and turn aside from you and quarrel,

entries.farsi: هين بگو نك روز من پيروز شد / آن چه فردا خواست شد امروز شد
entries.english: Take heed and say, “Lo, my fortune is triumphant: that which would have happened to-morrow has happened to-day.

entries.farsi: ضد من گشتند اهل اين سرا / تا قيامت عين شد پيشين مرا
entries.english: The people of this caravanseray have become my enemies, in order that the Resurrection might be made clearly visible to me beforehand,

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كه روزگار خود برم / ‏عمر با ايشان به پايان آورم
entries.english: Before I should lose my time and associate with them to the end of my life.

entries.farsi: كاله‏ى معيوب بخريده بدم / ‏شكر كز عيبش پگه واقف شدم
entries.english: I had bought defective goods: thanks to God that I have become aware of their defectiveness in time,

entries.farsi: مال رفته عمر رفته اى نسيب / ‏مال و جان داده پى كاله‏ى معيب
entries.english: Before the stock-in-trade should go out of my hands and finally come forth as defective.

entries.farsi: پيش از آن كز دست سرمايه شدى / ‏عاقبت معيوب بيرون آمدى
entries.english: My wealth was gone; my life was gone, O man of noble lineage: I had given away my wealth and life for damaged goods.

entries.farsi: رخت دادم زر قلبى بستدم / ‏شاد شادان سوى خانه مى‏شدم
entries.english: I sold my merchandise, I received base gold: I was going home in great jubilation.

entries.farsi: شكر كاين زر قلب پيدا شد كنون / ‏پيش از آن كه عمر بگذشتى فزون
entries.english: Thanks that this gold was shown to be base now, before too much of my life had passed.

entries.farsi: قلب ماندى تا ابد در گردنم / ‏حيف بودى عمر ضايع كردنم
entries.english: The base coin would have remained on my neck for ever: to waste my life would have been an iniquity.

entries.farsi: چون پگه تر قلبى او رو نمود / پاى خود زو واكشم من زود زود
entries.english: Since its baseness has been revealed earlier, I will step back from it very quickly.”

entries.farsi: يار تو چون دشمنى پيدا كند / گر حقد و رشك او بيرون زند
entries.english: When your friend displays enmity the itch of his hatred and jealousy shoots forth,

entries.farsi: تو از آن اعراض او افغان مكن / ‏خويشتن را ابله و نادان مكن
entries.english: Do not bewail his aversion, do not make yourself a fool and ignoramus;

entries.farsi: از جوالش زود بيرون آمدى / ‏تا بجويى يار صدق سرمدى
entries.english: Nay, thank God and give bread, that you have not become old in his sack,

entries.farsi: بلكه شكر حق كن و نان بخش كن / ‏كه نگشتى در جوال او كهن
entries.english: You have quickly come out of his sack to seek the true Eternal Friend,

entries.farsi: نازنين يارى كه بعد از مرگ تو / رشته‏ى يارى او گردد سه تو
entries.english: The delectable Friend whose friendship’s cord becomes threefold after your death.

entries.farsi: آن مگر سلطان بود شاه رفيع / ‏يا بود مقبول سلطان و شفيع
entries.english: That friend, in truth, may be the Sultan and exalted King, or he may be one accepted of the Sultan and one who intercedes.

entries.farsi: رستى از قالب و سالوس و دغل / ‏غر او ديدى عيان پيش از اجل
entries.english: You are delivered from the false coiner and hypocrisy and fraud: you have seen his tumour plainly before death.

entries.farsi: اين جفاى خلق با تو در جهان / ‏گر بدانى گنج زر آمد نهان
entries.english: If you understood this injustice shown towards you by the people in the world, it is a hidden treasure of gold.

entries.farsi: خلق را با تو چنين بد خو كنند / تا ترا ناچار رو آن سو كنند
entries.english: The people are made to be thus evil-natured towards you, that your face may inevitably be turned Yonder.

entries.farsi: اين يقين دان كه در آخر جمله‏شان / ‏خصم گردند و عدو و سركشان
entries.english: Know this for sure that in the end all of them will become adversaries and foes and rebels.

entries.farsi: تو بمانى با فغان اندر لحد / ال تذرنى فرد خواهان از احد
entries.english: You will be left in the tomb, lamenting and beseeching the One, “Do not leave me alone!

entries.farsi: اى جفايت به ز عهد وافيان / ‏هم ز داد تست شهد وافيان
entries.english: O You whose harshness is better than the troth of the faithful, the honey of the faithful is also from Your bounty.”

entries.farsi: بشنو از عقل خود اى انبار دار / گندم خود را به ارض اللهَّ سپار
entries.english: Listen to your own reason, O possessor of a granary, and commit your wheat to the earth of Allah,

entries.farsi: تا شود ايمن ز دزد و از شپش / ‏ديو را با ديوچه زوتر بكش
entries.english: That it may be safe from thieves and weevils. Kill the Devil with the wood-fretter as quickly as possible;

entries.farsi: كاو همى‏ترساندت هر دم ز فقر / همچو كبكش صيد كن اى نره صقر
entries.english: For he is always frightening you with poverty: make him your prey like a partridge, O valiant hawk.

entries.farsi: باز سلطان عزيز كاميار / ننگ باشد كه كند كبكش شكار
entries.english: It would be a shame for the falcon of the mighty and fortunate Sultan to be made a prey by the partridge.

entries.farsi: بس وصيت كرد و تخم وعظ كاشت / ‏چون زمينشان شوره بد سودى نداشت
entries.english: He gave many injunctions and sowed the seed of exhortation, as their soil was nitrous, it was of no avail.

entries.farsi: گر چه ناصح را بود صد داعيه / ‏پند را اذنى ببايد واعيه
entries.english: Although the admonisher has a hundred appeals, counsel demands a retentive ear.

entries.farsi: تو به صد تلطيف پندش مى‏دهى / ‏او ز پندت مى‏كند پهلو تهى
entries.english: You counsel him with a hundred courtesies, and he turns aside from your counsel.

entries.farsi: ز انبيا ناصح‏تر و خوش لهجه‏تر / كى بود كه گرفت دمشان در حجر
entries.english: A single person who obstinately refuses to listen will baffle a hundred speakers.

entries.farsi: يك كس نامستمع ز استيز و رد / صد كس گوينده را عاجز كند
entries.english: Who should be more persuasive in counselling and sweeter-tongued than the prophets, whose words made an impression on stones?

entries.farsi: ز انچه كوه و سنگ در كار آمدند / مى‏نشد بد بخت را بگشاده بند
entries.english: The bonds of the ill-fated were not being loosed by that whereby mountain and stone were moved.

entries.farsi: آن چنان دلها كه بدشان ما و من / نعتشان شد بل أَشَدُّ قسوة
entries.english: Such hearts as had egoism were described; nay, harder.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه عطاى حق و قدرت موقوف قابليت نيست همچون داد خلقان كه آن را قابليت بايد زيرا عطا قديم است و قابليت حادث، عطا صفت حق است و قابليت صفت مخلوق، و قديم موقوف حادث نباشد و اگر نه حدوث محال باشد

sections.title_en: Explaining that the bounty of God and of the Omnipotence is not dependent on receptivity, as human bounty is; for in the latter case receptivity is necessary. (In the former case it is not) because bounty is eternal, whereas receptivity is temporal. Bounty is an attribute of the Creator, while receptivity is an attribute of the creature; and the eternal cannot depend on the temporal,
otherwise temporality would be absurd.

entries.farsi: چاره‏ى آن دل عطاى مبدلى است / ‏داد او را قابليت شرط نيست
entries.english: The remedy for such a heart is the gift bestowed by a Transmuter: receptivity is not a necessary condition for His bounty.

entries.farsi: اينكه موسى را عصا ثعبان شود / همچو خورشيدى كفش رخشان شود
entries.english: Nay, His bounty is the necessary condition for receptivity: Bounty is the kernel, and receptivity the husk.

entries.farsi: بلكه شرط قابليت داد اوست / داد لب و قابليت هست پوست
entries.english: The change of Moses’ rod into a serpent and the shining of his hand like a sun,

entries.farsi: صد هزاران معجزات انبيا / كان نگنجد در ضمير و عقل ما
entries.english: And a hundred thousand miracles of the prophets which are not comprehended by our mind and understanding—

entries.farsi: قابلى گر شرط فعل حق بدى / ‏هيچ معدومى به هستى نامدى
entries.english: These are not derived from secondary causes but are under the control of God: how can receptivity belong to non-existent things?

entries.farsi: نيست از اسباب تصريف خداست / ‏نيستها را قابليت از كجاست
entries.english: If receptivity were a necessary condition for God’s action, no non-existent thing would come into existence.

entries.farsi: سنتى بنهاد و اسباب و طرق / طالبان را زير اين ازرق تتق
entries.english: He has established a law and causes and means for the sake of those who seek under this blue veil.

entries.farsi: بيشتر احوال بر سنت رود / گاه قدرت خارق سنت شود
entries.english: Most happenings come to pass according to the law; sometimes the Power breaks the law.

entries.farsi: سنت و عادت نهاده با مزه / ‏باز كرده خرق عادت معجزه
entries.english: He has established a goodly law and custom; then He has made the miracle a breach of the custom.

entries.farsi: بى‏سبب گر عز به ما موصول نيست / ‏قدرت از عزل سبب معزول نيست
entries.english: If honour does not reach us without a cause, the Power is not remote from the removal of the cause.

entries.farsi: اى گرفتار سبب بيرون مپر / ليك عزل آن مسبب ظن مبر
entries.english: O you, who are caught by the cause, do not fly outside; but do not suppose the removal of the Causer.

entries.farsi: هر چه خواهد آن مسبب آورد / قدرت مطلق سببها بر درد
