entries.english: The Causer brings whatsoever He will: the Absolute Power tears up the causes;

entries.farsi: ليك اغلب بر سبب راند نفاذ / تا بداند طالبى جستن مراد
entries.english: But, for the most part, He lets the execution follow the course of causation, in order that a seeker may be able to pursue the object of his desire.

entries.farsi: اين سببها بر نظرها پرده‏هاست / ‏كه نه هر ديدار صنعش را سزاست
entries.english: When there is no cause, what way should the seeker pursue? Therefore he must have a visible cause in the way.

entries.farsi: چون سبب نبود چه ره جويد مريد / پس سبب در راه مى‏بايد پديد
entries.english: These causes are veils on the eyes, for not every eye is worthy of His work.

entries.farsi: ديده‏اى بايد سبب سوراخ كن / ‏تا حجب را بر كند از بيخ و بن
entries.english: An eye that can penetrate the cause is needed to extirpate veils from root and bottom,

entries.farsi: تا مسبب بيند اندر المكان / ‏هرزه داند جهد و اكساب و دكان
entries.english: So that it may behold the Causer in non-spatiality and regard exertion and earnings and shops as nonsense.

entries.farsi: از مسبب مى‏رسد هر خير و شر / نيست اسباب و وسايط اى پدر
entries.english: Everything good or evil comes from the Causer: causes and means, O father, are naught

entries.farsi: جز خيالى منعقد بر شاه راه / ‏تا بماند دور غفلت چند گاه
entries.english: But a phantom that has materialized on the King’s highway in order that the period of heedlessness may endure for some time.

sections.title_fa: در ابتداى خلقت جسم آدم عليه السلام كه جبرئيل را عليه السلام اشارت كرد كه برو از اين زمين مشتى خاك برگير و به روايتى از هر نواحى مشت مشت برگير
sections.title_en: On the beginning of the creation of the body of Adam, on whom be peace, when He commanded Gabriel, on whom be peace, saying, “Go, take a handful of clay from this Earth,”
or according to another relation, “Take a handful from every region.”

entries.farsi: چون كه صانع خواست ايجاد بشر / از براى ابتالى خير و شر
entries.english: When the Maker willed to bring Man into existence for the purpose of probation with good and evil,

entries.farsi: جبرئيل صدق را فرمود رو / مشت خاكى از زمين بستان گرو
entries.english: He commanded Gabriel the true, saying, “Go, and take a handful of clay from the Earth as a pledge.”

entries.farsi: او ميان بست و بيامد تا زمين / ‏تا گزارد امر رب العالمين
entries.english: He girt his loins and came to the Earth, that he might execute the command of the Lord of created beings.

entries.farsi: دست سوى خاك برد آن موتمر / خاك خود را در كشيد و شد حذر
entries.english: That obedient one moved his hand towards the Earth: the Earth withdrew herself and was afraid.

entries.farsi: پس زبان بگشاد خاك و البه كرد / كز براى حرمت خالق فرد
entries.english: Then the Earth loosed her tongue and made supplication, saying, “For the sake of the reverence due to the unique Creator,

entries.farsi: ترك من گو و برو جانم ببخش / ‏رو بتاب از من عنان خنگ رخش
entries.english: Take leave of me and go! Spare my life! Go; turn aside from me the reins of your white steed!

entries.farsi: در كشاكشهاى تكليف و خطر / بهر اللهَّ هل مرا اندر مبر
entries.english: For God’s sake, leave me and do not plunge me into the troubles of obligation and danger.

entries.farsi: بهر آن لطفى كه حقت بر گزيد / كرد بر تو علم لوح كل پديد
entries.english: For the sake of the favour by which God chose you out and revealed to you the knowledge in the Universal Tablet,

entries.farsi: تا ماليك را معلم آمدى / ‏دايما با حق مكلم آمدى
entries.english: So that you have become the teacher of the Angels and art conversing with God continually;

entries.farsi: كه سفير انبيا خواهى بدن / ‏تو حيات جان وحيى نى بدن
entries.english: For you will be the messenger sent to the prophets: you are the life of the inspired spirit, not (the life) of the body.

entries.farsi: بر سر افيلت فضيلت بود از آن / ‏كاو حيات تن بود تو آن جان
entries.english: You had superiority over Seraphiel because he is the body’s life, you are the spirit’s.

entries.farsi: بانگ صورش نشات تنها بود / نفخ تو نشو دل يكتا بود
entries.english: The blast of his trumpet is the growth of bodies; your breath is the growth of the single heart.

entries.farsi: جان جان تن حيات دل بود / پس ز دادش داد تو فاضل بود
entries.english: The life of the heart is the soul of the soul of the body: therefore your gift is superior to his.

entries.farsi: باز ميكاييل رزق تن دهد / سعى تو رزق دل روشن دهد
entries.english: Again, Michael gives the sustenance for the body; your labour gives the sustenance for the illumined heart.

entries.farsi: او به داد كيل پر كردست ذيل / ‏داد رزق تو نمى‏گنجد به كيل
entries.english: He has filled his skirt with gifts by measure; your gifts of sustenance are immeasurable.

entries.farsi: هم ز عزراييل با قهر و عطب / ‏تو بهى چون سبق رحمت بر غضب
entries.english: Moreover, you are better than Azrael the tyrannous and enraged, even as Mercy is prior to Wrath.

entries.farsi: حامل عرش اين چهارند و تو شاه / ‏بهترين هر چهارى ز انتباه
entries.english: These four are the bearers of the Throne, and you king: you are the best of all of the four from being awake.

entries.farsi: روز محشر هشت بينى حامالنش / ‏هم تو باشى افضل هشت آن زمانش
entries.english: On the Day of the Congregation you will see that its bearers are eight: at that time also you will be the most excellent of its eight.”

entries.farsi: همچنين بر مى‏شمرد و مى‏گريست / ‏بوى مى‏برد او كز اين مقصود چيست
entries.english: Thus was she enumerating and weeping: she guessed what the object of this was.

entries.farsi: معدن شرم و حيا بد جبرئيل / ‏بست آن سوگندها بر وى سبيل
entries.english: Gabriel was a mine of reverence and respect: those adjurations barred the way against him.

entries.farsi: بس كه البه كردش و سوگند داد / باز گشت و گفت يا رب العباد
entries.english: Inasmuch as she entreated and appealed to him, he returned and said, “O Lord of Your servants,

entries.farsi: كه نبودم من به كارت سرسرى / ‏ليك ز انچه رفت تو داناترى
entries.english: I have not been remiss in Your affair, but You know what happened better than I.

entries.farsi: گفت نامى كه ز هولش اى بصير / هفت گردون باز ماند از مسير
entries.english: She pronounced the Name from awe of which, O All-seeing One, the Seven Heavens would cease from their course.

entries.farsi: شرمم آمد گشتم از نامت خجل / ‏ور نه آسان است نقل مشت گل
entries.english: Shame came over me, I was abashed by Your Name; else, it is easy to convey a handful of earth,

entries.farsi: كه تو زورى داده‏اى امالك را / كه بدرانند اين افلاک را
entries.english: For You have bestowed such a strength upon the Angels that they can tear these celestial spheres to shreds.”

sections.title_fa: فرستادن ميكاييل را عليه السلام به قبض حفنه‏ى خاك از زمين جهت تركيب جسم مبارك ابو البشر ‏خليفه الحق مسجود الملك و معلمهم آدم عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The sending of Michael, on whom be peace, to take a handful of clay from the Earth for putting together the frame of the blessed body of the Father of Mankind, the Vicegerent of God,
Adam, on whom be peace, the Adored of the Angels and their Teacher.

entries.farsi: گفت ميكاييل را تو رو به زير / مشت خاكى در ربا از وى چو شير
entries.english: He said to Michael, “Do you go down and seize, like a lion, a handful of clay from her.”

entries.farsi: چون كه ميكاييل شد تا خاكدان / ‏دست كرد او تا كه بربايد از آن
entries.english: When Michael reached the Earth, he put forth his hand to seize from her.

entries.farsi: خاك لرزيد و در آمد در گريز / گفت او البه كنان و اشك ريز
entries.english: The Earth trembled and began to flee: she became suppliant and shed tears.

entries.farsi: سينه سوزان البه كرد و اجتهاد / با سرشك پر ز خون سوگند داد
entries.english: Her breast burning, she made supplication and earnest entreaty: she appealed with bloody tears,

entries.farsi: كه به يزدان لطيف بى‏نديد / كه بكردت حامل عرش مجيد
entries.english: Saying, “by the gracious incomparable God who has made you the bearer of the majestic Throne.

entries.farsi: كيل ارزاق جهان را مشرفى / ‏تشنگان فضل را تو مغرفى
entries.english: You are the overseer for measuring the world’s means of sustenance: you are the ladler to them that thirst for the bounty”—

entries.farsi: ز انكه ميكاييل از كيل اشتقاق / ‏دارد و كيال شد در ارتزاق
entries.english: Because Michael is derived from kayl (measure), and he has become the measurer (kayyál) in dispensation of the means of subsistence

entries.farsi: كه امانم ده مرا آزاد كن / بين كه خون آلود مى‏گويم سخن
entries.english: “Give me quarter, set me free! See how I am uttering words stained with blood.”

entries.farsi: معدن رحم اله آمد ملك / ‏گفت چون ريزم بر آن ريش اين نمك
entries.english: The Angel is a mine of God’s mercy: Michael said, “How should I sprinkle this salt on that wound?”—

entries.farsi: مچنان كه معدن قهر است ديو / كه بر آورد از بنى آدم غريو
entries.english: Just as the Devil is a mine of wrath, for he has raised up a roar from the sons of Adam.

entries.farsi: سبق رحمت بر غضب هست اى فتا / لطف غالب بود در وصف خدا
entries.english: The precedence of Mercy over Wrath exists, O youth: clemency was predominant in the nature of God.

entries.farsi: بندگان دارند ال بد خوى او / مشكهاشان پر ز آب جوى او
entries.english: His servants necessarily possess His disposition: their water-skins are filled from the water of His stream.

entries.farsi: آن رسول حق قالووز سلوك / ‏گفت الناس على دين الملوك
entries.english: The Messenger of God and the Guide on the journey said that men follow the usage of their kings.

entries.farsi: رفت ميكاييل سوى رب دين / ‏خالى از مقصود دست و آستين
entries.english: Michael went to the Lord of the Judgement, with hand and sleeve empty of the object of his quest.

entries.farsi: گفت اى داناى سر و شاه فرد / خاكم از زارى و گريه بسته كرد
entries.english: He said, “O Knower of the secret, O peerless King, the Earth bound me by lamenting and weeping.

entries.farsi: آب ديده پيش تو با قدر بود / من نتانستم كه آرم ناشنود
entries.english: Tears were precious with You: I could not feign not to have heard.

entries.farsi: آه و زارى پيش تو بس قدر داشت / ‏من نتانستم حقوق آن گذاشت
entries.english: Moaning and wailing had great value with You: I could not leave their rights unheeded.

entries.farsi: پيش تو بس قدر دارد چشم تر / من چگونه گشتمى استيزه‏گر
entries.english: With You the moist eye is much prized: how should I have become quarrelsome in resisting?”

entries.farsi: دعوت زارى است روزى پنج بار / بنده را كه در نماز آ و بزار
entries.english: There is a summons to the servant to lamentation five times a day— “come to the ritual prayer, and lament.”

entries.farsi: نعره‏ى موذن كه حى على الفالح / ‏و آن فالح اين زارى است و اقتراح
entries.english: The muezzin’s cry is “hasten to welfare,” and that welfare is this lamentation and petitioning.

entries.farsi: آن كه خواهى كز غمش خسته كنى / ‏راه زارى بر دلش بسته كنى
entries.english: He whom You wish to make sorrow-stricken— You bar against his heart the way to lamentation,

entries.farsi: تا فرو آيد بال بى‏دافعى / ‏چون نباشد از تضرع شافعى
entries.english: In order that affliction may descend without anything to repel it, when there is no intercessor of humble entreaty;

entries.farsi: وان كه خواهى كز باليش واخرى / ‏جان او را در تضرع آورى
entries.english: And You lead to humble entreaty the spirit of him whom You wish to redeem from affliction.

entries.farsi: گفته اى اندر نبى كان امتان / ‏كه بر ايشان آمد آن قهر گران
entries.english: You have said in the Qur’an that those peoples on which that heavy vengeance fell,

entries.farsi: چون تضرع مى‏نكردند آن نفس / ‏تا بال زيشان بگشتى باز پس
entries.english: It was because at that moment they would not make humble entreaty that the affliction might be averted from them;

entries.farsi: ليك دلهاشان چو قاسى گشته بود / آن گنه‏هاشان عبادت مى‏نمود
entries.english: But since their hearts had been hardened, their sins appeared as obedient service.

entries.farsi: تا نداند خويش را مجرم عنيد / آب از چشمش كجا داند دويد
entries.english: Until the sinner deems himself rebellious, how can tears run from his eye?

sections.title_fa: ،قصه‏ى قوم يونس عليه السلام بيان و برهان آن است كه تضرع و زارى دافع بالى آسمانى است و حق تعالى فاعل مختار است پس تضرع و تعظيم پيش او مفيد باشد، و فالسفه گويند فاعل به طبع است و به علت نه مختار پس تضرع طبع را نگرداند

sections.title_en: The Story of the people of Yúnus, on whom be peace, is a demonstration and manifest proof that humble entreaty and lamentation avert affliction sent from Heaven. And God most High acts by free choice: therefore humble entreaty and reverence avail with Him. The philosophers, however, say that He acts by nature and as a cause, not by free choice:
therefore humble entreaty cannot alter nature.

entries.farsi: برق مى‏انداخت مى‏سوزيد سنگ / ‏ابر مى‏غريد و رخ مى‏ريخت رنگ
entries.english: When the affliction became visible to the people of Yúnus, a cloud full of fire departed from heaven.

entries.farsi: قوم يونس را چو پيدا شد بال / ابر پر آتش جدا شد از سما
entries.english: It was shooting lightning, the rocks were burning; the cloud was roaring, cheeks were shedding colour.

entries.farsi: جملگان از بامها زير آمدند / سر برهنه جانب صحرا شدند
entries.english: All were on the roofs at night, when that woe came into view from on high.

entries.farsi: جملگان بر بامها بودند شب / ‏كه پديد آمد ز بالا آن كرب
entries.english: All came down from the roofs and went bare-headed towards the open country.

entries.farsi: مادران بچگان برون انداختند / تا همه ناله و نفير افراختند
entries.english: Mothers cast out their children, that all might raise wailing and distressful cries.

entries.farsi: از نماز شام تا وقت سحر / خاك مى‏كردند بر سر آن نفر
entries.english: From the evening prayer till the hour of dawn, those folk were throwing dust on their heads.

entries.farsi: جملگى آوازها بگرفته شد / رحم آمد بر سر آن قوم لد
entries.english: All voices were hushed: Mercy came upon that perverse people.

entries.farsi: بعد نوميدى و آه ناشكفت / ‏اندك اندك ابر واگشتن گرفت
entries.english: After despair and unrestrained lamentation, little by little the cloud began to turn back.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى يونس دراز است و عريض / ‏وقت خاك است و حديث مستفيض
entries.english: The story of Yúnus is long and broad: it is time of the Earth and the far-spread tale.

entries.farsi: چون تضرع را بر حق قدرهاست / ‏و آن بها كانجاست زارى را كجاست
entries.english: Since humble entreaty has value with God— and where has lamentation the price that it has there?—

entries.farsi: هين اميد اكنون ميان را چست بند / خيز اى گرينده و دايم بخند
entries.english: Oh, hope! Now gird your loins tight! Arise, O weeper, and laugh continually,

entries.farsi: كه برابر مى‏نهد شاه مجيد / اشك را در فضل با خون شهيد
entries.english: For the glorious King is ranking tears as equal in merit to the blood of the martyr.

sections.title_fa: ‏فرستادن اسرافيل را عليه السلام به خاك كه حفنه اى برگير از خاك بهر تركيب جسم آدم عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The sending of Seraphiel, on whom be peace, to the Earth with orders to take a handful of clay for
moulding the body of Adam, on whom be peace.

entries.farsi: گفت اسرافيل را يزدان ما / كه برو ز آن خاك پر كن كف بيا
entries.english: Our God said to Seraphiel, “Go, fill your hand with that clay and come.”

entries.farsi: آمد اسرافيل هم سوى زمين / ‏باز آغازيد خاكستان حنين
entries.english: Seraphiel, likewise, came to the Earth: again the Earth began to moan,

entries.farsi: كاى فرشته‏ى صور و اى بحر حيات / ‏كه ز دمهاى تو جان يابد موات
entries.english: Saying, “O Angel of the Resurrection and O Sea of life, by whose breaths the dead are revived,

entries.farsi: در دمى از صور يك بانگ عظيم / ‏پر شود محشر خاليق از رميم
entries.english: You blow one terrible blast from the trumpet, and the place of Judgement becomes full of people from rotten bones.

entries.farsi: اى هالكت ديده‏گان از تيغ مرگ / ‏بر زنيد از خاك سر چون شاخ و برگ
entries.english: You blow on the trumpet and cry, ‘Listen, spring up, O you slain of Karbalá!

entries.farsi: در دمى در صور گويى الصال / بر جهيد اى كشتگان كربال
entries.english: O you, who have perished by the sword of Death, put forth your heads from the earth, like bough and leaf!’

entries.farsi: رحمت تو و آن دم گيراى تو / پر شود اين عالم از احياى تو
entries.english: From your bringing the dead to life this world is filled with your mercy and with that potent breath of yours.

entries.farsi: تو فرشته‏ى رحمتى رحمت نما / حامل عرشى و قبله‏ى دادها
entries.english: You are the Angel of mercy: show mercy! You are the bearer of the Throne and the qibla of gifts.”

entries.farsi: عرش معدن گاه داد و معدلت / ‏چارجو در زير او پر مغفرت
entries.english: The Throne is the mine of justice and equity: beneath it are four rivers filled with forgiveness:

entries.farsi: جوى شير و جوى شهد جاودان / ‏جوى خمر و دجله‏ى آب روان
entries.english: A river of milk and a river of honey everlasting; a river of wine and a river of running water

entries.farsi: پس ز عرش اندر بهشتستان رود / در جهان هم چيزكى ظاهر شود
entries.english: Then from the Throne they flow into Paradise; some little thing appears in this world too,

entries.farsi: گر چه آلوده‏ست اينجا آن چهار / از چه از زهر فنا و ناگوار
entries.english: Although here those four are defiled—by what? By the poison of mortality and indigestion.

entries.farsi: تا بجويند اصل آن را اين خسان / ‏خود بر اين قانع شدند اين ناكسان
entries.english: From those four a draught has been poured on the dark Earth and a temptation has been offered,

entries.farsi: جرعه اى بر خاك تيره ريختند / ز آن چهار و فتنه‏اى انگيختند
entries.english: In order that these vile wretches may seek the source of it; these worthless folk are content with this.

entries.farsi: شير داد و پرورش اطفال را / چشمه كرده سينه‏ى هر زال را
entries.english: He has given milk and nourishment for babes: He has made the breast of every wife a fountain.

entries.farsi: خمر دفع غصه و انديشه را / چشمه كرده از عنب در اجترا
entries.english: He has given wine to drive away grief and care: He has made of the grape a fountain to inspire courage.

entries.farsi: انگبين داروى تن رنجور را / چشمه كرده باطن زنبور را
entries.english: He has given honey as a remedy for the sick body: He has made the inward part of the bee a fountain.

entries.farsi: آب دادى عام اصل و فرع را / از براى طهر و بهر كرع را
entries.english: He gave water universally to high and low for cleanliness and for drinking.

entries.farsi: تا از اينها پى برى سوى اصول / ‏تو بر اين قانع شدى اى بو الفضول
entries.english: That you may follow the track from these towards the origins; but you are content with this, O trifler.

entries.farsi: بشنو اكنون ماجراى خاك را / كه چه مى‏گويد فسون محراك را
entries.english: Now hear the story of the Earth and what she is saying to enchant the disturber (of her peace).

entries.farsi: پيش اسرافيل گشته او عبوس / ‏مى‏كند صد گونه شكل و چاپلوس
entries.english: With frowning looks in the presence of Seraphiel, she is practicing a hundred sorts of coquetry and blandishment,

entries.farsi: كه به حق ذات پاك ذو الجلال / ‏كه مدار اين قهر را بر من حالل
entries.english: Saying, “By the truth of the holy essence of the Almighty, do not regard this violence to me as lawful!

entries.farsi: من از اين تقليب بويى مى‏برم / ‏بد گمانى مى‏دود اندر سرم
entries.english: I have a presentiment of this change: suspicious thoughts are running in my head.

entries.farsi: اى شفا و رحمت اصحاب درد / تو همان كن كان دو نيكو كار كرد
entries.english: You are the Angel of mercy: show mercy, for the huma will not harm any bird.

entries.farsi: تو فرشته‏ى رحمتى رحمت نما / ز انكه مرغى را نيازارد هما
entries.english: O healing and mercy to the sorrowful; do the same as those two benefactors did.”

entries.farsi: زود اسرافيل باز آمد به شاه / ‏گفت عذر و ماجرا نزد اله
entries.english: At once Seraphiel returned to the King: in God’s presence he excused himself and told what had passed,

entries.farsi: امر كردى در گرفتن سوى گوش / ‏نهى كردى از قساوت سوى هوش
entries.english: Saying, “Outwardly You gave the command to take; You inspired my conscience to do the opposite of that.

entries.farsi: كز برون فرمان بدادى كه بگير / عكس آن الهام دادى در ضمير
entries.english: The command to take You did address to my ear, the prohibition against hardheartedness You did address to my understanding.

entries.farsi: سبق رحمت گشت غالب بر غضب / ‏اى بديع افعال و نيكو كار رب
entries.english: Mercy, being prior, prevailed over wrath, O Lord whose actions are incomparable and whose dealings are gracious.”

sections.title_fa: فرستادن عزراييل ملك العزم و الحزم را عليه السلام به بر گرفتن حفنه‏ى خاك تا شود جسم آدم چالاك ‏عليه السلام
sections.title_en: The sending of Azrael, the Angel of firm resolution and strong mind, on whom be peace, to seize a handful of clay in order that the body of Adam, on whom be peace, might be quickened.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان زود عزراييل را / كه ببين آن خاك پر تخييل را
entries.english: Straightway God said to Azrael, “Behold the Earth full of vain imagination!

entries.farsi: آن ضعيف زال ظالم را بياب / ‏مشت خاكى هين بياور با شتاب
entries.english: Find that feeble unjust old crone: listen, fetch a handful of clay and make haste!”

entries.farsi: خاك بر قانون نفير آغاز كرد / داد سوگندش بسى سوگند خورد
entries.english: Azrael, the captain of the Decree, went off towards the terrestrial globe for the purpose of requisition.

entries.farsi: رفت عزراييل سرهنگ قضا / سوى كره‏ى خاك بهر اقتضا
entries.english: The Earth, according to rule, began lamenting loudly: she begged him; she swore many an oath,

entries.farsi: كاى غالم خاص و اى حمال عرش / ‏اى مطاع الامر اندر عرش و فرش
entries.english: Crying, “O favourite youth (page-of-honour), O bearer of the Throne, O you whose command is obeyed in heaven and earth,

entries.farsi: رو به حق رحمت رحمان فرد / رو به حق آن كه با تو لطف كرد
entries.english: Depart, for the sake of the mercy of the Merciful! Depart, for the sake of Him who has shown kindness unto you!

entries.farsi: حق شاهى كه جز او معبود نيست / ‏پيش او زارى كس مردود نيست
entries.english: For the sake of that King who alone is worshipped and with whom no one’s lamentation is rejected!”

entries.farsi: گفت آخر امر فرمود او به حلم / ‏هر دو امرند آن بگير از راه علم
entries.english: He replied, “These conjurations cannot move me to avert my face from the Giver of commands secret or manifest.”

entries.farsi: گفت نتوانم بدين افسون كه من / ‏رو بتابم ز آمر سر و علن
entries.english: She said, “After all, He has commanded forbearance: both are commanded: take the latter on the ground of knowledge.”

entries.farsi: گفت آن تاويل باشد يا قياس / ‏در صريح امر كم جو التباس
entries.english: He replied, “That would be an interpretation or an inference: do not seek to confuse the plain meaning of the command.

entries.farsi: فكر خود را گر كنى تاويل به / ‏كه كنى تاويل اين نامشتبه
entries.english: If you interpret your own thought, it is better than that you should interpret this unequivocal command.

entries.farsi: دل همى‏سوزد مرا بر البه‏ات / ‏سينه‏ام پر خون شد از شورآبه‏ات
entries.english: My heart is burning at your supplication; my bosom is filled with blood on account of your salty tears.

entries.farsi: نيستم بى‏رحم بل ز آن هر سه پاك / ‏رحم بيش استم ز درد دردناك
entries.english: I am not pitiless; nay, I have greater pity than those three holy ones for the sorrow of the sorrowful.

entries.farsi: گر طپانچه مى‏زنم من بر يتيم / ‏ور دهد حلوا به دستش آن حليم
entries.english: If I am slapping an orphan, while a mild-natured person may put halwá in his hand,

entries.farsi: اين طپانچه خوشتر از حلواى او / ور شود غره به حلوا واى او
entries.english: Those slaps are better than the other’s halwá; and if he be beguiled by the halwá, woe to him!

entries.farsi: بر نفير تو جگر مى‏سوزدم / ‏ليك حق لطفى همى‏آموزدم
entries.english: My heart is burning at your lamentable cry, but God is teaching me a kindness—

entries.farsi: قهر حق بهتر ز صد حلم من است / ‏منع كردن جان ز حق جان كندن است
entries.english: The kindness concealed amidst cruelties, the priceless cornelian hidden in filth.

entries.farsi: لطف مخفى در ميان قهرها / در حدث پنهان عقيق بى‏بها
entries.english: The cruelty done by God is better than a hundred clemencies of mine: to withhold the soul from God is agony to the soul.

entries.farsi: بدترين قهرش به از حلم دو كون / ‏نعم رب العالمين و نعم عون
entries.english: His worst cruelty is better than the clemency of both worlds: how excellent is the Lord of created beings and how excellent help!

entries.farsi: لطفهاى مضمر اندر قهر او / جان سپردن جان فزايد بهر او
entries.english: In His cruelty there are secret kindnesses: to surrender the soul for His sake increases the soul.

entries.farsi: هين رها كن بد گمانى و ضالل / ‏سر قدم كن چون كه فرمودت تعال
entries.english: Listen, dismiss suspicion and error: make your head a foot since He has bidden you come.

entries.farsi: آن تعال او تعاليها دهد / مستى و جفت و نهاليها دهد
entries.english: His ‘Come’ will give exaltations; it will give intoxication and brides and couches.

entries.farsi: بارى آن امر سنى را هيچ هيچ / ‏من نيارم كرد وهن و پيچ پيچ
entries.english: In short, never, never can I weaken that sublime command and complicate it.”

entries.farsi: اين همه بشنيد آن خاك نژند / ز آن گمان بد بدش در گوش بند
entries.english: The wretched Earth heard all this, in her ear was a plug arising from that evil suspicion.

entries.farsi: باز از نوع دگر آن خاك پست / ‏البه و سجده همى‏كرد او چو مست
entries.english: Once more in another fashion the lowly Earth made entreaty and prostrated herself, like a drunken man.

entries.farsi: گفت نه برخيز نبود زين زيان / ‏من سر و جان مى‏نهم رهن و ضمان
entries.english: He said, “Nay, arise! There is no loss from this, I lay my head and life as a pledge and guarantee.

entries.farsi: البه منديش و مكن البه دگر / جز بدان شاه رحيم دادگر
entries.english: Do not think of entreating, do not make further entreaty except to that merciful and justice-dealing King.

entries.farsi: بنده فرمانم نيارم ترك كرد / امر او كز بحر انگيزيد گرد
entries.english: I am a slave to command, I dare not neglect His command which raised dust from the sea.

entries.farsi: جز از آن خالق گوش و چشم و سر / نشنوم از جان خود هم خير و شر
entries.english: Save from the Creator of ear and eye and head I will hear neither good nor evil—not even from my own soul.

entries.farsi: گوش من از غير گفت او كر است / ‏او مرا از جان شيرين جان‏تر است
entries.english: My ear is deaf to all words but His: He is dearer to me than my sweet soul.

entries.farsi: جان از او آمد نيامد او ز جان / ‏صد هزاران جان دهد او رايگان
entries.english: The soul came from Him, not He from the soul: He bestows a hundred thousand souls free of cost.

entries.farsi: جان كه باشد كش گزينم بر كريم / ‏كيك چه بود كه بسوزم زو گليم
entries.english: Who is the soul that I should prefer her to the Gracious? What is a flea that I should burn the blanket on account of it?

entries.farsi: من ندانم خير الا خير او / صم و بكم و عمى من از غير او
entries.english: I know no good but His good: I am deaf and dumb and blind to all but Him.

entries.farsi: گوش من كر است از زارى كنان / ‏كه منم در كف او همچون سنان
entries.english: My ear is deaf to those who make lamentation, for I am as the spear in His hand.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه مخلوقى كه ترا از او ظلمى رسد به حقيقت او همچون آلتى است، عارف آن بود كه به حق رجوع كند نه به آلت و اگر به آلت رجوع كند به ظاهر نه از جهل كند بلكه براى مصلحتى چنان كه بايزيد قدس اللهَّ سره گفت كه چندين سال است كه من با مخلوق سخن نگفته‏ام و از مخلوق سخن نشنيده‏ام و ليكن خلق چنين پندارند كه با ايشان سخن مى‏گويم و از ايشان مى‏شنوم زيرا ايشان مخاطب اكبر را نمى‏بينند كه ايشان چون صدايند او را نسبت به حال من، التفات مستمع عاقل به صدا
‏نباشد چنان كه مثل است معروف: قال الجدار للوتد لم تشقنى قال الوتد انظر الى من يدقنى
sections.title_en: Explaining that when injury befalls you from a creature of God, he in reality is like an instrument. The gnostic is he that refers (all action) to God, not to the instrument; and if he refer it to the instru­

ment formally, he does so not in ignorance but for a purpose. Thus Abu Yazíd, may God sanctify his spirit, said, “During all these years I have never spoken to any creature or heard any creature speak to me; but people fancy that I am speaking and listening to them, because they do not see the Most Great Speaker, of whom they in relation to me are the echo.” The intelligent hearer pays no heed to the echo.
There is a well-known proverb to this effect,, “The wall said to the nail, ‘Why are you splitting me?’ The nail replied, ‘Look at him who is hitting me.’”

entries.farsi: احمقانه از سنان رحمت مجو / ز آن شهى جو كان بود در دست او
entries.english: Do not foolishly beg the spear for mercy: beg mercy of the King in whose hand is the spear.

entries.farsi: با سنان و تيغ البه چون كنى / ‏او اسير آمد به دست آن سنى
entries.english: How should you supplicate the spear and sword which are captives in the hand of that Exalted One?

entries.farsi: او به صنعت آزر است و من صنم / ‏آلتى كاو سازدم من آن شوم
entries.english: He is Ázar in craftsmanship, and I am the idol: whatever instrument He may make of me, I become that.

entries.farsi: گر مرا ساغر كند ساغر شوم / ‏ور مرا خنجر كند خنجر شوم
entries.english: If He makes me a cup, I become a cup; and if He makes me a dagger, I become a dagger.

entries.farsi: گر مرا چشمه كند آبى دهم / ‏ور مرا آتش كند تابى دهم
entries.english: If He makes me a fountain, I give water; and if He makes me fire, I give heat.

entries.farsi: گر مرا باران كند خرمن دهم / ‏ور مرا ناوك كند در تن جهم
entries.english: If He makes rain of me, I give a wheat stack; and if He makes an arrow of me, I dart into the body.

entries.farsi: من چو كلكم در ميان اصبعين / ‏نيستم در صف طاعت بين بين
entries.english: If He make me a snake (márí), I emit venom; and if He make me a friend (yárí), I do service.

entries.farsi: گر مرا مارى كند زهر افكنم / ‏ور مرا يارى كند خدمت كنم
entries.english: I am as a pen between His two fingers: I do not waver in the ranks of obedience.”

entries.farsi: خاك را مشغول كرد او در سخن / ‏يك كفى بربود از آن خاك كهن
entries.english: He engaged the Earth in discourse; he snatched from the old Earth a handful.

entries.farsi: ساحرانه در ربود از خاكدان / ‏خاك مشغول سخن چون بى‏خودان
entries.english: Like a magician he snatched it from the Earth, the Earth was absorbed, like those beside themselves, in listening to his words.

entries.farsi: رد تا حق تربت بى‏راى را / تا به مكتب آن گريزان پاى را
entries.english: He brought the inconsiderate clay to God: the runaway to school.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان كه به علم روشنم / ‏كه ترا جالد اين خلقان كنم
entries.english: God said, “By My resplendent knowledge, I will make you the executioner of these creatures.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب دشمنم گيرند خلق / ‏چون فشارم خلق را در مرگ حلق
entries.english: He replied, “O Lord, Your creatures will regard me as their enemy when I strangle them at death.

entries.farsi: تو روا دارى خداوند سنى / ‏كه مرا مبغوض و دشمن رو كنى
entries.english: Deem it right, O exalted Lord, to make me hated and like a foe in appearance?”

entries.farsi: گفت اسبابى پديد آرم عيان / ‏از تب و قولنج و سرسام و سنان
entries.english: He said, “I will bring into clear view certain causes, fever, dysentery, inflammation and spear wounds;

entries.farsi: كه بگردانم نظرشان را ز تو / در مرضها و سببهاى سه تو
entries.english: For I will turn their attention from you to the diseases and threefold causes”

entries.farsi: چشمشان باشد گذاره از سبب / ‏در گذشته از حجب از فضل رب
entries.english: Azrael replied, “O Lord, there are also servants who shatter causes, O Almighty.”

entries.farsi: گفت يا رب بندگان هستند نيز / كه سببها را بدرند اى عزيز
entries.english: Their eye pierces through the cause: by the grace of the Lord, it has passed beyond veils.

entries.farsi: سرمه‏ى توحيد از كحال حال / ‏يافته رسته ز علت و اعتالل
entries.english: It has obtained the eye salve of Unity from the oculist of ecstasy and has been delivered from ailment and infirmity.

entries.farsi: ننگرند اندر تب و قولنج و سل / ‏راه ندهند اين سببها را به دل
entries.english: They do not look at fever and dysentery and consumption: they do not admit these causes into their heart;

entries.farsi: ز انكه هر يك زين مرضها را دواست / ‏چون دوا نپذيرد آن فعل قضاست
entries.english: For every one of these diseases has its cure: when it becomes incurable, that is the act of the Decree.

entries.farsi: هر مرض دارد دوا مى‏دان يقين / ‏چون دواى رنج سرما پوستين
entries.english: Know for certain that every disease has its cure, as a fur is the cure for the pain of cold;

entries.farsi: چون خدا خواهد كه مردى بفسرد / سردى از صد پوستين هم بگذرد
entries.english: When God wills that a man shall be frozen, the cold penetrates even a hundred furs

entries.farsi: در وجودش لرزه‏اى بنهد كه آن / ‏نه به جامه به شود نه از آشيان
entries.english: And puts into his body a tremor that will not be made better by clothes or by the house

entries.farsi: چون قضا آيد طبيب ابله شود / و آن دوا در نفع هم گمره شود
entries.english: When the Decree comes, the physician is made foolish, and the medicine too loses its beneficial effect.

entries.farsi: اصل بيند ديده چون اكمل بود / فرع بيند چون كه مرد احول بود
entries.english: How should the perception of the (mystic) seer be veiled by these causes, which are a veil to catch the dolt?

entries.farsi: كى شود محجوب ادراك بصير / زين سببهاى حجاب گول گير
entries.english: When the eye is quite perfect, it sees the root; when a man is cross eyed, it sees the branch.

sections.title_fa: جواب آمدن كه آن كه نظر او بر اسباب و مرض و زخم تيغ نيايد بر كار تو عزراييل هم نيايد كه تو ِهم سببى اگر چه مخفى‏ترى از آن سببها، و بود كه بر آن رنجور مخفى نباشد كه و هو أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْه َمِنْكُمْ وَ لكِنْ ال تُبْصِ رُون

sections.title_en: The answer, “One who does not regard causes and diseases and sword-wounds will likewise pay no regard to your action, O Azrael, for you too are a cause, although you are more concealed than those causes.” And maybe it is not concealed from the sick man, for He is nearer to him than you are, but you do not see.

entries.farsi: گفت يزدان آن كه باشد اصل دان / ‏پس ترا كى بيند او اندر ميان
entries.english: God said, “He who perceives the origin: how, then, should he be conscious of your intervention?

entries.farsi: دان كه ايشان را شكر باشد اجل / ‏چون نظرشان مست باشد در دول
entries.english: Although you have concealed yourself from the vulgar, still to the clear-eyed you are a veil.”

entries.farsi: گر چه خويش از عامه پنهان كرده‏اى / ‏پيش روشن ديده‏گان هم پرده‏اى
entries.english: And those to whom death is as sugar— how should their sight be intoxicated with the fortunes?

entries.farsi: تلخ نبود پيش ايشان مرگ تن / ‏چون روند از چاه و زندان در چمن
entries.english: Bodily death is not bitter to them, since they go from a dungeon and prison into a garden.

entries.farsi: وا رهيدند از جهان پيچ پيچ / ‏كس نگريد بر فوات هيچ هيچ
entries.english: They have been delivered from the world of torment: none weeps for the loss of nothing, nothing.

entries.farsi: برج زندان را شكست اركانيى / ‏هيچ از او رنجد دل زندانيى
entries.english: If an elemental spirit breaks the bastion of a prison, will the heart of any prisoner be angry with him?

entries.farsi: كاى دريغ اين سنگ مرمر را شكست / ‏تا روان و جان ما از حبس رست
entries.english: “Alas, he has broken this marble stone, so that our spirits and souls have escaped from confinement.

entries.farsi: آن رخام خوب و آن سنگ شريف / ‏برج زندان را بهى بود و اليف
entries.english: The beautiful marble and the noble stone of the prison-bastion were pleasing and agreeable.

entries.farsi: چون شكستش تا كه زندانى برست / ‏دست او در جرم اين بايد شكست
entries.english: Why did he break them, so that the prisoners escaped? His hand must be broken as a penalty for this.”

entries.farsi: هيچ زندانى نگويد اين فشار / جز كسى كز حبس آرندش به دار
entries.english: No prisoner will talk such nonsense except that one who is brought from prison to the gallows.

entries.farsi: تلخ كى باشد كسى را كش برند / از ميان زهر ماران سوى قند
entries.english: How should it be bitter to one whom they take from amidst snake-poison towards sugar?

entries.farsi: جان مجرد گشته از غوغاى تن / ‏مى‏پرد با پر دل بى‏پاى تن
entries.english: The soul, freed from the turmoil of the body, is soaring on the wings of the heart without bodily feet,

entries.farsi: همچو زندانى چه كاندر شبان / ‏خسبد و بيند به خواب او گلستان
entries.english: Like the prisoner in a dungeon who falls asleep at night and dreams of a rose garden,

entries.farsi: گويد اى يزدان مرا در تن مبر / تا در اين گلشن كنم من كر و فر
entries.english: And says, “O God, do not bring me to my body, in order that I may walk as a prince in this garden.”

entries.farsi: گويدش يزدان دعا شد مستجاب / اين چنين خوابى ببين چون خوش بود
entries.english: God says to him, “Your prayer is granted: go not back”—and God best knows the right course.

entries.farsi: مرگ ناديده به جنت در رود / هيچ او حسرت خورد بر انتباه
entries.english: Consider how delightful such a dream is! Without having seen death, he goes into Paradise.

entries.farsi: ‏بر تن با سلسله در قعر چاه / مومنى آخر در آ در صف رزم
entries.english: Does he feel any regret for wakefulness and for the body in chains at the bottom of the dungeon?

entries.farsi: ‏كه ترا بر آسمان بوده ست بزم / بر اميد راه بالا كن قيام
entries.english: If you are a true believer, come now, enter the ranks of battle, for a feast has been or you in Heaven.

entries.farsi: ‏همچو شمعى پيش محراب اى غالم / اشك مى‏بار و همى‏سوز از طلب
entries.english: In the hope of journeying upwards, take your stand before the mihrab, like a candle, O youth!

entries.farsi: ‏همچو شمع سر بريده جمله شب / لب فرو بند از طعام و از شراب
entries.english: Let your tears fall like rain, and burn in search all night long, like the candle beheaded (by the flame).

entries.farsi: ‏سوى خوان آسمانى كن شتاب / دم‏به‏دم بر آسمان مى‏دار اميد
entries.english: Close your lips against food and drink: hasten towards the Heavenly table.

entries.farsi: در هواى آسمان رقصان چو بيد / دم‏به‏دم از آسمان مى‏آيدت
entries.english: Continually keep your hope on Heaven, dancing like the willow in desire for Heaven.

entries.farsi: ‏آب و آتش رزق مى‏افزايدت / گر ترا آن جا برد نبود عجب
entries.english: Continually from Heaven water and fire will be coming to you and increasing your provision.

entries.farsi: ‏منگر اندر عجز و بنگر در طلب / كاين طلب در تو گروگان خداست
entries.english: If your aspiration bears you there, it is no wonder: do not regard your weakness, regard your search;

entries.farsi: ‏ز انكه هر طالب به مطلوبى سزاست / هد كن تا اين طلب افزون شود
entries.english: For this search is God’s pledge within you, because every seeker deserves something sought.

entries.farsi: تا دلت زين چاه تن بيرون شود / خلق گويد مرد مسكين آن فالن
entries.english: Strive that this search may increase, so that your heart may escape from this bodily dungeon.

entries.farsi: ‏تو بگويى زنده‏ام اى غافالن / گر تن من همچو تنها خفته است
entries.english: People will say, “Poor so-and-so is dead,” you will say, “I am living, O ye heedless ones!

entries.farsi: ‏هشت جنت در دلم بشكفته است / جان چو خفته در گل و نسرين بود
entries.english: Though my body, like bodies, is laid to rest, the Eight Paradises have blossomed in my heart.”

entries.farsi: چه غم است ار تن در آن سرگين بود / جان خفته چه خبر دارد ز تن
entries.english: When the spirit is lying at rest amidst roses and eglantines, what does it matter if the body is in that dung?

entries.farsi: ‏كاو به گلشن خفت يا در گولخن / مى‏زند جان در جهان آبگون
entries.english: What should the spirit laid asleep know of the body, whether it is in a rose-garden or an ash pit?

entries.farsi: ‏نعره‏ى يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يعلمون / گر نخواهد زيست جان بى‏اين بدن
entries.english: In the bright world the spirit is crying, “Oh, would that my people knew!”

entries.farsi: ‏پس فلك ايوان كى خواهد بدن / گر نخواهد بى‏بدن جان تو زيست
entries.english: If the spirit shall not live without this body, then for whom shall Heaven be the palace?

entries.farsi: ‏فِي السَّماءِ رِزْقُكُمْ روزى كيست / وارهى زين روزى ريزه‏ى كثيف
entries.english: If your spirit shall not live without the body, for whom is the blessing in Heaven is your provision?

sections.title_fa: در بيان وخامت چرب و شيرين دنيا و مانع شدن او از طعام اللهَّ چنان كه فرمود الجوع طعام اللهَّ يحيى به َابدان الصديقين اى فى الجوع طعام اللهَّ و قوله ابيت عند ربى يطعمنى و يسقينى و قوله يُرْزَقُونَ فَرِحِين
sections.title_en: Explaining the ruinous cause of the fat and sweet things of the World and how they hinder one

from the Food of God, as he has said—“Hunger is the Food of God with which He revives the bodies of the true,” i.e. in hunger the Food of God is; and he has said, “I pass the night with my Lord and He gives me food and drink”; and God has said, “being provided for, rejoicing.”

entries.farsi: ‏در فتى در لوت و در قوت شريف / گر هزاران رطل لوتش مى‏خورى
entries.english: If you are delivered from this provision of gross scraps, you will fall to dainty viands and noble food.

entries.farsi: ‏مى‏روى پاك و سبك همچون پرى / كه نه حبس باد و قولنجت كند
entries.english: If you are eating a hundred pounds’ weight of His viands, you will depart pure and light as a peri;

entries.farsi: چار ميخ معده آهنجت كند / گر خورى كم گرسنه مانى چو زاغ
entries.english: For they will not make you a prisoner of wind and dysentery and crucify you with gripes.

entries.farsi: ‏ور خورى پر گيرد آروغت دماغ / كم خورى خوى بد و خشكى و دق
entries.english: If you eat little, you will remain hungry like the crow; and if you eat your fill, you will suffer from belching.

entries.farsi: ‏پر خورى شد تخمه را تن مستحق / از طعام اللهَّ و قوت خوش گوار
entries.english: If you eat little, ill-temper and anemia and consumption; if you eat your fill, your body will incur indigestion.

entries.farsi: بر چنان دريا چو كشتى شو سوار / باش در روزه شكيبا و مصر
entries.english: Through the Food of God and the easily digested nutriment, ride like a ship on such a ocean.

entries.farsi: دم‏به‏دم قوت خدا را منتظر / كان خداى خوب كار بردبار
entries.english: Be patient and persistent in fasting: always expecting the Food of God;

entries.farsi: كه سبك آيد وظيفه يا كه دير / بى‏نوا هر دم همى‏گويد كه كو
entries.english: For God, who acts with goodness and is long-suffering, bestows gifts in expectation.

entries.farsi: هديه‏ها را مى‏دهد در انتظار / انتظار نان ندارد مرد سير
entries.english: The full-fed man does not wait expectantly for bread, whether his allowance will come soon or late;

entries.farsi: در مجاعت منتظر در جستجو / چون نباشى منتظر نايد به تو
entries.english: The foodless man is always asking, “Where is it?” and expecting it hungrily and seeking and searching.

entries.farsi: آن نواله‏ى دولت هفتاد تو / اى پدر الانتظار الانتظار
entries.english: Unless you are expectant, that bounty of manifold felicity will not come to you.

entries.farsi: از براى خوان بالا مردوار / هر گرسنه عاقبت قوتى بيافت
entries.english: Expectation, O father, expectation, like a man, for the sake of the dishes from above.

entries.farsi: ‏آفتاب دولتى بر وى بتافت / ضيف با همت چو آشى كم خورد
entries.english: Every hungry man obtained some food at last: the sun of fortune shone upon him.

entries.farsi: صاحب خوان آش بهتر آورد / جز كه صاحب خوان درويشى لئيم
entries.english: When a magnanimous guest will not eat some food, the host brings better food,
Unless he is a poor host and a mean one. Do not think ill of the generous Provider!

entries.farsi: سر بر آور همچو كوهى اى سند / تا نخستين نور خور بر تو زند
entries.english: Lift up your head like a mountain, O man of authority, in order that the first rays of the Sun may strike upon you;

entries.farsi: كان سر كوه بلند مستقر / هست خورشيد سحر را منتظر
entries.english: For the lofty firm-based mountain-peak is expecting the sun of dawn.

sections.title_fa: جواب آن مغفل كه گفته است كه خوش بودى اين جهان اگر مرگ نبودى و خوش بودى ملك دنيا اگر ‏زوالش نبودى و على هذه الوتيرة من الفشارات
sections.title_en: Reply to the simpleton who has said that this world would be delightful if there were no death
and that the possessions of the present life would be delightful if they were not fleeting and other absurdities in the same style.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت خوش بودى جهان / ‏گر نبودى پاى مرگ اندر ميان
entries.english: A certain man was saying, “The world would be delightful, were it not for the intervention of death.”

entries.farsi: آن دگر گفت ار نبودى مرگ هيچ / ‏كه نيرزيدى جهان پيچ پيچ
entries.english: The other said, “If there were no death, the tangled world would not be worth a straw.

entries.farsi: خرمنى بودى به دشت افراشته / ‏مهمل و ناكوفته بگذاشته
entries.english: It would be a stack heaped up in the field and neglected and left unthreshed.

entries.farsi: مرگ را تو زندگى پنداشتى / ‏تخم را در شوره خاكى كاشتى
entries.english: You have supposed death to be life: you have sown your seed in a barren soil.

entries.farsi: عقل كاذب هست خود معكوس بين / ‏زندگى را مرگ بيند اى غبين
entries.english: The false reason, indeed, sees the reverse: it sees life as death, O man of weak judgement.”

entries.farsi: اى خدا بنماى تو هر چيز را / آن چنان كه هست در خدعه سرا
entries.english: Do You, O God, show us everything as it really is in this house of illusion.

entries.farsi: هيچ مرده نيست پر حسرت ز مرگ / ‏حسرتش آن است كش كم بود برگ
entries.english: None that has died is filled with grief on account of death; his grief is caused by having too little provision;

entries.farsi: ور نه از چاهى به صحرا اوفتاد / در ميان دولت و عيش و گشاد
entries.english: Otherwise he has come from a dungeon into the open country amidst fortune and pleasure and delight;

entries.farsi: زين مقام ماتم و تنگين مناخ / ‏نقل افتادش به صحراى فراخ
entries.english: From this place of mourning and narrow vale he has been transported to the spacious plain.

entries.farsi: مقعد صدقى نه ايوان دروغ / ‏باده‏ى خاصى نه مستيى ز دوغ
entries.english: It is a seat of truth, not a palace of falsehood; a choice wine, not an intoxication with buttermilk.

entries.farsi: مقعد صدق و جليس حق شده / ‏رسته زين آب و گل آتشكده
entries.english: It is the seat of truth, and God is beside him: he is delivered from this water and earth of the fire-temple.

entries.farsi: ور نكردى زندگانى منير / يك دو دم مانده است مردانه بمير
entries.english: And if you have not led the illuminative life, one or two moments remain: die like a man!

sections.title_fa: ،فيما يرجى من رحمه اللهَّ تعالى معطى النعم قبل استحقاقها وَ هُوَ الَّذِي يُنَزِّلُ الْغَيْثَ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما قَنَطُوا و رب بعد يورث قربا و رب معصية ميمونة و رب سعاده تاتى من حيث يرجى النقم ليعلم ان اللهَّ يبدل سيئاتهم حسنات

sections.title_en: Concerning what may be hoped for from the mercy of God most High, who bestows His favours before they have been deserved— and He it is who sends down the rain after they have despaired. And many an estrangement produces intimacy, and there is many a blessed sin, and many a happiness that comes in a case where penalties are expected, in order that it may be known that God changes their evil deeds to good.

entries.farsi: در حديث آمد كه روز رستخيز / امر آيد هر يكى تن را كه خيز
entries.english: In the Traditions it is related that on the Day of Resurrection every single body will be commanded to arise.

entries.farsi: نفخ صور امر است از يزدان پاك / ‏كه بر آريد اى ذراير سر ز خاك
entries.english: The blast of the trumpet is the command from the Holy God, namely, “O children, lift up your heads from the grave.”

entries.farsi: باز آيد جان هر يك در بدن / ‏همچو وقت صبح هوش آيد به تن
entries.english: Every one’s soul will return to its body, just as consciousness returns to the body at dawn.

entries.farsi: جان تن خود را شناسد وقت روز / در خراب خود در آيد چون كنوز
entries.english: At daybreak the soul recognises its own body and re-enters its own ruin, like treasures.

entries.farsi: جسم خود بشناسد و در وى رود / جان زرگر سوى درزى كى رود
entries.english: It recognises its own body and goes into it: how should the soul of the goldsmith go to the tailor?

entries.farsi: جان عالم سوى عالم مى‏دود / روح ظالم سوى ظالم مى‏دود
entries.english: The soul of the scholar runs to the scholar, the spirit of the tyrant runs to the tyrant;

entries.farsi: كه شناسا كردشان علم اله / ‏همچو بره و ميش وقت صبحگاه
entries.english: For the Divine Knowledge has made them cognisant, as the lamb and the ewe, at the hour of dawn.

entries.farsi: پاى كفش خود شناسد در ظلم / ‏چون نداند جان تن خود اى صنم
entries.english: The foot knows its own shoe in the dark: how should not the soul know its own body, O worshipful one?

entries.farsi: صبح حشر كوچك است اى مستجير / حشر اكبر را قياس از وى بگير
entries.english: Dawn is the little resurrection: O seeker of refuge, judge from it what the greater resurrection will be like.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه جان بپرد سوى طين / ‏نامه پرد تا يسار و تا يمين
entries.english: Even as the soul flies towards the clay, the scroll will fly into the left hand or the right.

entries.farsi: در كفش بنهند نامه‏ى بخل و جود / فسق و تقوى آن چه دى خو كرده بود
entries.english: Into his hand will be put the scroll of avarice and liberality, impiety and piety, and all the dispositions that he had formed yesterday

entries.farsi: چون شود بيدار از خواب او سحر / باز آيد سوى او آن خير و شر
entries.english: At dawn when he wakes from slumber, that good and evil will come back to him.

entries.farsi: گر رياضت داده باشد خوى خويش / ‏وقت بيدارى همان آيد به پيش
entries.english: If he has disciplined his moral nature, the same nature will present itself to him when he wakes;

entries.farsi: ور بد او دى خام و زشت و در ضالل / ‏چون عزا نامه سيه يابد شمال
entries.english: And if yesterday he was ignorant and wicked and misguided, he will find his left hand black as a letter of mourning;

entries.farsi: ور بد او دى پاك و با تقوى و دين / ‏وقت بيدارى برد در ثمين
entries.english: But if yesterday he was clean and pious and religious, when he wakes he will gain the precious pearl.

entries.farsi: حشر اصغر حشر اكبر را نمود / مرگ اصغر مرگ اكبر را زدود
entries.english: Our sleep and waking are two witnesses which attest to us the significance of death and resurrection.

entries.farsi: هست ما را خواب و بيدارى ما / بر نشان مرگ و محشر دو گوا
entries.english: The lesser resurrection has shown forth the greater resurrection; the lesser death has illumined the greater death.

entries.farsi: ليك اين نامه خيال است و نهان / ‏و آن شود در حشر اكبر بس عيان
entries.english: But this scroll is a fancy and hidden, though at the greater resurrection it will be very clearly seen.

entries.farsi: اين خيال اينجا نهان پيدا اثر / زين خيال آن جا بروياند صور
entries.english: Here this fancy is hidden, the traces are visible; but there He from this fancy will produce forms.

entries.farsi: در مهندس بين خيال خانه‏اى / ‏در دلش چون در زمينى دانه‏اى
entries.english: Behold in the architect the fancy of a house, in his mind like a seed in a piece of earth.

entries.farsi: آن خيال از اندرون آيد برون / ‏چون زمين كه زايد از تخم درون
entries.english: That fancy comes forth from within, as the earth bears from the seed within.

entries.farsi: هر خيالى كاو كند در دل وطن / ‏روز محشر صورتى خواهد شدن
entries.english: Every fancy that makes its abode in the mind will become a form on the Day of Resurrection,

entries.farsi: چون خيال آن مهندس در ضمير / چون نبات اندر زمين دانه گير
entries.english: Like the architect’s fancy in his thought; like the plant in the earth that takes the seed.

entries.farsi: مخلصم زين هر دو محشر قصه‏اى است / ‏مومنان را در بيانش حصه‏اى است
entries.english: My object in both these resurrections is a story; in its exposition there is a moral for the true believers.

entries.farsi: چون بر آيد آفتاب رستخيز / بر جهند از خاك زشت و خوب تيز
entries.english: When the sun of the Resurrection rises, foul and fair will leap up hastily from the grave

entries.farsi: سوى ديوان قضا پويان شوند / نقد نيك و بد به كوره مى‏روند
entries.english: They will be running to the Díwán of the Decree: the good and bad coin will go into the crucible—

entries.farsi: نقد نيكو شادمان و ناز ناز / نقد قلب اندر زحير و در گداز
entries.english: The good coin joyously and with great delight; the false coin in anguish and melting.

entries.farsi: لحظه لحظه امتحانها مى‏رسد / سر دلها مى‏نمايد در جسد
entries.english: At every moment the probations will be arriving: the thoughts concealed in the heart will be appearing in the body,

entries.farsi: چون ز قنديل آب و روغن گشته فاش / ‏يا چو خاكى كه برويد سرهاش
entries.english: As when the water and oil in a lamp are exposed to view, or like a piece of earth from which grow up the deposited within.

entries.farsi: آن يكى سر سبز نحن المتقون / ‏و آن دگر همچون بنفشه سر نگون
entries.english: From onion, leek, and poppy the hand of Spring reveals the secret of Winter—

entries.farsi: از پياز و گندنا و كوكنار / سر دى پيدا كند دست بهار
entries.english: One fresh and green, saying, “We are the devout”; and the other drooping their heads like the violet,

entries.farsi: چشمها بيرون جهيده از خطر / گشته ده چشمه ز بيم مستقر
entries.english: Their eyes starting out from the danger, and streaming like ten fountains from fear of the appointed end;

entries.farsi: باز مانده ديده‏ها در انتظار / تا كه نامه نايد از سوى يسار
entries.english: Their eyes remaining in expectation, lest the scroll come from the left side;

entries.farsi: چشم گردان سوى راست و سوى چپ / ‏ز انكه نبود بخت نامه‏ى راست زپ
entries.english: Their eyes rolling to right and left, because the fortune of the scroll from the right is not easy.

entries.farsi: نامه‏اى آيد به دست بنده‏اى / ‏سر سيه از جرم و فسق آگنده‏اى
entries.english: There comes into the hand of a servant a scroll headed with black and cram-full of crime and wickedness;

entries.farsi: اندر او يك خير و يك توفيق نه / ‏جز كه آزار دل صديق نه
entries.english: Containing not a single good deed or act of saving grace— nothing but wounds inflicted on the hearts of the saintly;

entries.farsi: پر ز سر تا پاى زشتى و گناه / ‏تسخر و خنبك زدن بر اهل راه
entries.english: Filled from top to bottom with foulness and sin, with mockery and jeering at the followers of the Way,

entries.farsi: آن دغل كارى و دزديهاى او / و آن چو فرعونان انا و اناى او
entries.english: His rascal behaviour, thieving and Pharaoh-like expressions of self-glorification.

entries.farsi: چون بخواند نامه‏ى خود آن ثقيل / ‏داند او كه سوى زندان شد رحيل
entries.english: When that odious man reads his scroll, he knows that he is on the road to prison.

entries.farsi: آن هزاران حجت و گفتار بد / بر دهانش گشته چون مسمار بد
entries.english: Then he sets out, like robbers going to the gallows; his crime manifest, and the way of excusing himself barred.

entries.farsi: پس روان گردد چو دزدان سوى دار / جرم پيدا بسته راه اعتذار
entries.english: The thousands of bad pleas and speeches have become like an evil nail on his mouth.

entries.farsi: رخت دزدى بر تن و در خانه‏اش / ‏گشته پيدا گم شده افسانه‏اش
entries.english: The stolen property has been discovered on his person and in his house: his story has vanished.

entries.farsi: پس روان گردد به زندان سعير / كه نباشد خار را ز آتش گزير
entries.english: He sets out, therefore, to the prison of Hell; for thorns have no means of escape from the fire.

entries.farsi: چون موكل آن ماليك پيش و پس / ‏بوده پنهان گشته پيدا چون عسس
entries.english: The angels that were hidden, as custodians before and behind have become visible like policemen.

entries.farsi: مى‏برندش مى‏سپوزندش به نيش / ‏كه برو اى سگ به كهدانهاى خويش
entries.english: They take him along, prodding him with the goad and saying, “Begone, O dog, to your own kennels!”

entries.farsi: مى‏كشد پا بر سر هر راه / تا بود كه بر جهد ز آن چاه او
entries.english: He drags his feet on every road that perchance he may escape from the pit.

entries.farsi: منتظر مى‏ايستد تن مى‏زند / در اميدى روى واپس مى‏كند
entries.english: He stands expectantly, keeping silence and turning his face backward in a hope,

entries.farsi: اشك مى‏بارد چو باران خزان / ‏خشك اوميدى چه دارد او جز آن
entries.english: Pouring tears like autumn rain. A mere hope—what has he except that?

entries.farsi: هر زمانى روى واپس مى‏كند / رو به درگاه مقدس مى‏كند
entries.english: At every moment he is looking back and turning his face to the Holy Court.

entries.farsi: پس ز حق امر آيد از اقليم نور / كه بگوييدش كه اى بطال عور
entries.english: Then from God in the realm of light comes the command— “Say to him, ‘O never-do-well destitute,

entries.farsi: انتظار چيستى اى كان شر / رو چه واپس مى‏كنى اى خيره‏سر
entries.english: What are you expecting, O mine of mischief? Why are you looking back, O giddy-headed man?

entries.farsi: نامه‏ات آن است كت آمد به دست / ‏اى خدا آزار و اى شيطان‏پرست
entries.english: Your scroll is that which came into your hand, O offender against God and worshipper of the Devil.

entries.farsi: چون بديدى نامه‏ى كردار خويش / ‏چه نگرى پس بين جزاى كار خويش
entries.english: Since you have seen the scroll of your deeds, why do you look back? Behold the reward of your works!

entries.farsi: بى‏هده چه مول مولى مى‏زنى / ‏در چنين چه كو اميد روشنى
entries.english: Why are you tarrying in vain? Where is hope of light in such a pit as this?

entries.farsi: نه ترا از روى ظاهر طاعتى / ‏نه ترا در سر و باطن نيتى
entries.english: Neither outwardly have you any act of piety, nor inwardly and in your heart an intention;

entries.farsi: نه ترا شبها مناجات و قيام / ‏نه ترا در روز پرهيز و صيام
entries.english: No nightly prayers and vigils, no abstinence and fasting in the daytime;

entries.farsi: نه ترا حفظ زبان ز آزار كس / ‏نه نظر كردن به عبرت پيش و پس
entries.english: No holding your tongue to avoid hurting anyone, no looking earnestly forward and backward.

entries.farsi: پيش چه بود ياد مرگ و نزع خويش / ‏پس چه باشد مردن ياران ز پيش
entries.english: What is forward? To think of your own death and last agony. What is backward? To remember the earlier deaths of your friends.

entries.farsi: نه ترا بر ظلم توبه‏ى پر خروش / ‏اى دغا گندم نماى جو فروش
entries.english: You have no wailful penitence for your injustice, O rogue who show wheat and sell barley.

entries.farsi: چون ترازوى تو كژ بود و دغا / راست چون جويى ترازوى جزا
entries.english: Since your scales were wrong and false, how should you require the scales of your retribution to be right?

entries.farsi: چون كه پاى چپ بدى در غدر و كاست / ‏نامه چون آيد ترا در دست راست
entries.english: Since you were a left foot in fraud and dishonesty, how should the scroll come into your right hand?

entries.farsi: چون جزا سايه‏ست اى قد تو خم / ‏سايه‏ى تو كژ فتد در پيش هم
entries.english: Since retribution is the shadow, accordingly your shadow, O man of bent figure, falls crookedly before you.’”

entries.farsi: زين قبل آيد خطابات درشت / ‏كه شود كه را از آن هم گوژ پشت
entries.english: From this quarter comes harsh words of rebuke that even the back of a mountain would be bowed by them.

entries.farsi: بنده گويد آن چه فرمودى بيان / ‏صد چنانم صد چنانم صد چنان
entries.english: The servant answers: “I am a hundred, hundred, hundred times as much as that which You have declared.

entries.farsi: خود تو پوشيدى بترها را به حلم / ‏ور نه مى‏دانى فضيحت‏ها به علم
entries.english: Truly, in Your patience You have thrown a veil over worse things; otherwise You know with Your knowledge shameful deeds;

entries.farsi: ليك بيرون از جهاد و فعل خويش / ‏از وراى خير و شر و كفر و كيش
entries.english: But, outside of my own exertion and action, beyond good and evil and religion and infidelity,

entries.farsi: وز نياز عاجزانه‏ى خويشتن / ‏وز خيال و وهم من يا صد چو من
entries.english: And beyond my feeble supplication and the fancy and imagination of myself or a hundred like me,

entries.farsi: بودم اوميدى به محض لطف تو / از وراى راست باشى يا عتو
entries.english: Beyond living righteously or behaving disobediently— I had a hope in Your pure lovingkindness.

entries.farsi: بخشش محضى ز لطف بى‏عوض / ‏بودم اوميد اى كريم بى‏غرض
entries.english: I had hope in the pure bounty from Your spontaneous loving kindness, O Gracious Disinterested One.

entries.farsi: رو سپس كردم بدان محض كرم / ‏سوى فعل خويشتن مى‏ننگرم
entries.english: I turn my face back to that pure grace: I am not looking towards my own actions.

entries.farsi: سوى آن اوميد كردم روى خويش / ‏كه وجودم داده‏اى از پيش پيش
entries.english: I turn my face towards that hope, for You have given me existence older than of old.

entries.farsi: خلعت هستى بدادى رايگان / ‏من هميشه معتمد بودم بر آن
entries.english: You gave existence, free of cost, as a robe of honour: I have always relied upon that.”

entries.farsi: چون شمارد جرم خود را و خطا / محض بخشايش در آيد در عطا
entries.english: When he recounts his sins and trespasses, the Pure Bounty begins to show munificence,

entries.farsi: كاى ماليك باز آريدش به ما / كه بده‏ستش چشم دل سوى رجا
entries.english: Saying, “O angels, bring him back to Us, for his inward eye has been towards hope.

entries.farsi: الابالى‏وار آزادش كنيم / ‏و آن خطاها را همه خط بر زنيم
entries.english: Like one who cares about nothing, We will set him free and cancel all his trespasses.

entries.farsi: الابالى مر كسى را شد مباح / ‏كش زبان نبود ز غدر و از صالح
entries.english: I don’t care is permitted to that One who loses nothing by perfidy and by probity.

entries.farsi: آتشى خوش بر فروزيم از كرم / ‏تا نماند جرم و زلت بيش و كم
entries.english: We will kindle up a goodly fire of grace, in order that no sin and fault, great or small, may endure—

entries.farsi: آتشى كز شعله‏اش كمتر شرار / مى‏بسوزد جرم و جبر و اختيار
entries.english: Such a fire that the least spark of the flame thereof is consuming sin and necessity and free-will.

entries.farsi: شعله در بنگاه انسانى زنيم / ‏خار را گلزار روحانى كنيم
entries.english: We will set fire to the tenement of Man and make the thorns a spiritual garden of roses.

entries.farsi: ما فرستاديم از چرخ نهم / ‏كيميا يُصْ لِحْ لَكُمْ أعمالكم
entries.english: We have sent from the Ninth Sphere the elixir; He will rectify for you your actions.”

entries.farsi: خود چه باشد پيش نور مستقر / كر و فر اختيار بو البشر
entries.english: What in truth is Adam’s sovereignty and power of choice beside the Light of the Everlasting Abode?

entries.farsi: مسمع او آن دو پاره استخوان / ‏مدركش دو قطره خون يعنى جنان
entries.english: His speaking organ is a piece of flesh; the seat of his vision is a piece of fat;

entries.farsi: گوشت پاره آلت گوياى او / پيه پاره منظر بيناى او
entries.english: The seat of his hearing consists of two pieces of bone; the seat of his perception is two drops of blood, that is to say, the heart.

entries.farsi: كرمكى و از قذر آگنده‏اى / ‏طمطراقى در جهان افكنده‏اى
entries.english: You are a little worm and art stuffed with filth; you have made a display of pomp in the world.

entries.farsi: از منى بودى منى را واگذار / اى اياز آن پوستين را ياد دار
entries.english: You were of seed: relinquish egoism! O Ayaz, keep in mind that sheepskin jacket!

sections.title_fa: قصه‏ى اياز و حجره داشتن او جهت چارق و پوستين و گمان آمدن خواجه‏تاشانش را كه او را در آن ‏حجره دفينه است به سبب محكمى در و گرانى قفل
sections.title_en: The Story of Ayaz and his having a chamber for his rustic shoes and sheepskin jacket;
and how his fellow-servants thought he had a buried treasure in that room, because the door was so strong and the lock so heavy.

entries.farsi: آن اياز از زيركى انگيخته / ‏پوستين و چارقش آويخته
entries.english: Impelled by sagacity, Ayaz hung up his sheepskin jacket and rustic shoes.

entries.farsi: شاه را گفتند او را حجره‏اى است / ‏اندر آن جا زر و سيم و خمره‏اى است
entries.english: Every day he would go into the private chamber, “These are your shoes: do not regard your eminence.”

entries.farsi: مى‏رود هر روز در حجره‏ى خال / چارقت اين است منگر در عال
entries.english: They said to the King, “He has a chamber, and in it there is gold and silver and a jar of treasure.

entries.farsi: راه مى‏ندهد كسى را اندر او / بسته مى‏دارد هميشه آن در او
entries.english: He admits no one into it: he always keeps the door locked.”

entries.farsi: شاه فرمود اى عجب آن بنده را / چيست خود پنهان و پوشيده ز ما
entries.english: The King said, “Oh, I wonder what in truth that servant has that is hidden and concealed from me.”

entries.farsi: پس اشارت كرد ميرى را كه رو / نيم شب بگشاى و اندر حجره شو
entries.english: Then he gave orders to a certain Amír, saying, “Go at midnight and open and enter the room.

entries.farsi: هر چه يابى مر ترا يغماش كن / ‏سر او را بر نديمان فاش كن
entries.english: Whatever you find is yours: plunder him, expose his secret to the courtiers.

entries.farsi: با چنين اكرام و لطف بى‏عدد / از لئيمى سيم و زر پنهان كند
entries.english: Notwithstanding such innumerable kindnesses and favours, does he meanly hide silver and gold?

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد او وفا و عشق و جوش / ‏وانگه او گندم‏نماى جو فروش
entries.english: He professes loyalty and love and enthusiasm— and then he is one who shows wheat and sells barley!

entries.farsi: نيم شب آن مير با سى معتمد / در گشاد حجره‏ى او راى زد
entries.english: To anyone who finds life in love, aught but service would seem infidelity.”

entries.farsi: هر كه اندر عشق يابد زندگى / ‏كفر باشد پيش او جز بندگى
entries.english: At midnight the Amír with thirty trusted set out to open his chamber,

entries.farsi: مشعله بر كرده چندين پهلوان / ‏جانب حجره روانه شادمان
entries.english: And all these valiant men, carrying torches, moved joyfully in that direction,

entries.farsi: كه امر سلطان است بر حجره زنيم / ‏هر يكى هميان زر در كش كنيم
entries.english: Saying, “’It is the Sultan’s command: let us raid the room and each of us pocket a purse of gold.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏گفت هى چه جاى زر / از عقيق و لعل گوى و از گهر
entries.english: “Hey!” cried one of them, “why trouble about gold?” Talk of carnelians and rubies and jewels.

entries.farsi: خاص خاص مخزن سلطان وى است / ‏بلكه اكنون شاه را خود جان وى است
entries.english: He is the most privileged of the Sultan’s treasury: nay, he is now to the King life itself.”

entries.farsi: چه محل دارد به پيش اين عشيق / ‏لعل و ياقوت و زمرد يا عقيق
entries.english: What worth should rubies and corundums and emeralds or carnelians possess in the eyes of this man beloved?

entries.farsi: شاه را بر وى نبودى بد گمان / ‏تسخرى مى‏كرد بهر امتحان
entries.english: The King had no evil thoughts of him: he was making a mock by way of trial.

entries.farsi: كه مبادا كاين بود خسته شود / من نخواهم كه بر او خجلت رود
entries.english: He knew him to be free from all deceitfulness and guile; again his heart was shaken with misgiving,

entries.farsi: پاك مى‏دانستش از هر غش و غل / ‏باز از وهمش همى‏لرزيد دل
entries.english: Lest this might be and he should be wounded. “I do not wish,” “that shame should come over him.

entries.farsi: اين نكرده‏ست او و گر كرد او رواست / ‏هر چه خواهد گو بكن محبوب ماست
entries.english: He has not done this thing; and if he has, it is right: let him do whatever he will, he is my beloved.

entries.farsi: باز گفتى دور از آن خو و خصال / ‏اين چنين تخليط ژاژ است و خيال
entries.english: Whatever my beloved may do, it is I have done. I am he, he is I: what though I am in the veil?”

entries.farsi: هر چه محبوبم كند من كرده‏ام / ‏او منم من او چه گر در پرده‏ام
entries.english: Again he would say, “He is far removed from this disposition and qualities: such wild accusations are drivel and fancy.

entries.farsi: از اياز اين خود محال است و بعيد / كاو يكى درياست قعرش ناپديد
entries.english: This from Ayaz is absurd and incredible, for he is an ocean whereof none can see the bottom.”

entries.farsi: هفت دريا اندر او يك قطره‏اى / ‏جمله‏ى هستى ز موجش چكره‏اى
entries.english: The Seven Seas are a drop in it: the whole of existence is a driblet of its waves.

entries.farsi: جمله پاكيها از آن دريا برند / قطره‏هايش يك به يك ميناگرند
entries.english: All purities are fetched from that ocean: its drops, every one, are alchemists.

entries.farsi: شاه شاهان است بلكه شاه ساز / وز براى چشم بد نامش اياز
entries.english: He is the King of kings; nay, he is the King-maker, though on account of the evil eye his name is “Ayaz.”

entries.farsi: چشمهاى نيك هم بر وى بد است / ‏از ره غيرت كه حسنش بى‏حد است
entries.english: Even the good eyes are evil to him in respect of jealousy, for his beauty is infinite.

entries.farsi: يك دهان خواهم به پهناى فلك / ‏تا بگويم وصف آن رشك ملك
entries.english: I want a mouth as broad as heaven to describe the qualities of him who is envied by the angels;

entries.farsi: اين قدر گر هم نگويم اى سند / شيشه‏ى دل از ضعيفى بشكند
entries.english: And if I should get a mouth like this and a hundred times as this, it would be too narrow for this longing’s distressful cry.

entries.farsi: ور دهان يابم چنين و صد چنين / ‏تنگ آيد در فغان اين حنين
entries.english: If I should not utter even this amount, O trusted, the phial, my heart, would burst from weakness.

entries.farsi: شيشه‏ى دل را چو نازك ديده / ‏بهر تسكين بس قبا بدريده
entries.english: Since I have seen my heart’s phial fragile, I have rent many a mantle in order to allay my pain.

entries.farsi: من سر هر ماه سه روز اى صنم / ‏بى‏گمان بايد كه ديوانه شوم
entries.english: Beyond doubt, O worshipful one, I must become mad for three days at the beginning of every month.

entries.farsi: هين كه امروز اول سه روزه است / ‏روز پيروز است نه پيروزه است
entries.english: Listen, to-day is the first of the prayer days: it is the day of triumph (pírúz), not the turquoise (pírúza).

entries.farsi: هر دلى كاندر غم شه مى‏بود / دم به دم او را سر مه مى‏بود
entries.english: Every heart that is in love with the King, for it is always the beginning of the month.

entries.farsi: قصه‏ى محمود و اوصاف اياز / چون شدم ديوانه رفت اكنون ز ساز
entries.english: Since I have become mad, the story of Mahmud and the description of Ayaz are now out of order.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه آن چه بيان كرده مى‏شود صورت قصه است و آن كه آن صورتى است كه در خورد اين صورت گيران است و در خورد آينه‏ى تصوير ايشان و از قدوسيتى كه حقيقت اين قصه راست نطق ‏را از اين تنزيل شرم مى‏آيد و از خجالت سر و ريش و قلم گم مى‏كند و العاقل يكفيه الاشاره

sections.title_en: Explaining that what is related is the outward form of the Story, and that it is a form befitting these who apprehend the external form and suitable to the mirror of their imagination, whereas the real essence of the Story is so transcendent that speech is ashamed to reveal it, and from confusion loses head, beard, and pen. And a hint is enough for the wise.

entries.farsi: ز انكه پيلم ديد هندستان بخواب / ‏از خراج اوميد بر ده شد خراب
entries.english: Forasmuch as my elephant has dreamed of Hindustan, abandon hope of the tax: the village is ruined.

entries.farsi: كيف ياتى النظم لى و القافيه / ‏بعد ما ضاعت اصول العافيه
entries.english: How should poesy and rhyme come to me after the foundations of sanity are destroyed?

entries.farsi: ما جنون واحد لى فى الشجون / ‏بل جنون فى جنون فى جنون
entries.english: It is not (merely) one madness I have amidst the sorrows of love; nay, but madness on madness on madness.

entries.farsi: ذاب جسمى من اشارات الكنى / منذ عاينت البقاء فى الفنا
entries.english: My body is wasted away by secret indications of the mysteries, ever since I beheld eternal life (baqá) in dying to self (faná).

entries.farsi: اى اياز از عشق تو گشتم چو موى / ‏ماندم از قصه تو قصه‏ى من بگوى
entries.english: O Ayaz, from love of you I have become thin as a hair: I am unable to tell story, do you tell my story.

entries.farsi: بس فسانه‏ى عشق تو خواندم به جان / ‏تو مرا كافسانه گشتستم بخوان
entries.english: I have recited many a tale of your love with my soul: that I have become a tale, do you recite mine.

entries.farsi: خود تو مى‏خوانى نه من اى مقتدى / من كه طورم تو موسى وين صدا
entries.english: Truly you are reciting, O model, not I: I am Mount Sinai, you art Moses, and this is the echo.

entries.farsi: كوه بى‏چاره چه داند گفت چيست / ‏ز انكه موسى مى‏بداند كه تهى است
entries.english: How should the helpless mountain know what the words are? The mountain is empty of that which Moses knows.

entries.farsi: كوه مى‏داند به قدر خويشتن / ‏اندكى دارد ز لطف روح تن
entries.english: The mountain knows according to its own measure: the body has a little of the grace of the spirit.

entries.farsi: آن منجم چون نباشد چشم تيز / شرط باشد مرد اسطرالب ريز
entries.english: The body is like the astrolabe in respect of calculation: it is a sign of the sun-like spirit.

entries.farsi: تن چو اسطرالب باشد ز احتساب / ‏آيتى از روح همچون آفتاب
entries.english: When the astronomer is not keen-sighted, an astrolabe-moulder is required,

entries.farsi: تا سطرالبى كند از بهر او / تا برد از حالت خورشيد بو
entries.english: To make an astrolabe for him in order that he may gain some knowledge concerning the state of the sun.

entries.farsi: جان كز اسطرالب جويد او صواب / ‏چه قدر داند ز چرخ و آفتاب
entries.english: The soul that seeks the truth from the astrolabe— how much should it know of the sky and sun?

entries.farsi: تو كه ز اسطرالب ديده بنگرى / ‏در جهان ديدن يقين بس قاصرى
entries.english: You who observe with the astrolabe of the eye are certainly very far short in your view of the world.

entries.farsi: تو جهان را قدر ديده ديده‏اى / ‏كو جهان سبلت چرا ماليده‏اى
entries.english: You have seen the world according to the measure of your eye, where is the world? Why, have you twisted your moustache?

entries.farsi: عارفان را سرمه‏اى هست آن بجوى / ‏تا كه دريا گردد اين چشم چو جوى
entries.english: The gnostics possess a eye salve: seek it, in order that this eye which resembles a river may become an ocean.

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى از عقل و هوش ار با من است / ‏اين چه سودا و پريشان گفتن است
entries.english: If a single mote of reason and consciousness is with me, what is this melancholy madness and distracted speech?

entries.farsi: چون كه مغز من ز عقل و هش تهى است / ‏پس گناه من در اين تخليط چيست
entries.english: Since my brain is empty of reason and consciousness, how then am I at fault in this raving?

entries.farsi: نه گناه او راست كه عقلم ببرد / عقل جمله‏ى عاقالن پيشش بمرد
entries.english: No; the fault is his, for he robbed me of my reason: in his presence the reason of all rational beings is dead.

entries.farsi: ما اشتهيت العقل مذ جننتنى / ‏ما حسدت الحسن مذ زينتنى
entries.english: O you who cause the reason to wander and the understanding to go astray, intelligences have no object of hope but you.

entries.farsi: يا مجير العقل فتان الحجى / ‏ما سواك للعقول مرتجى
entries.english: I have never desired reason since you made me mad: I have never envied beauty since you did adorn me.

entries.farsi: هل جنونى فى هواك مستطاب / ‏قل بلى و اللهَّ يجزيك الثواب
entries.english: Is my madness for love of you approved? Say “Yes and God will reward you.

entries.farsi: گر به تازى گويد او ور پارسى / ‏گوش و هوشى كو كه در فهمش رسى
entries.english: Whether he speaks Arabic or Persian, where is the ear and mind by means of which you should attain to the apprehension of it?

entries.farsi: باده‏ى او در خور هر هوش نيست / ‏حلقه‏ى او سخره‏ى هر گوش نيست
entries.english: His wine is not suitable to every mind; his ring is not subject to every ear.

entries.farsi: بار ديگر آمدم ديوانه‏وار / رو رو اى جان زود زنجيرى بيار
entries.english: Once again I have become mad-like: go, go, my soul, quickly fetch a chain;

entries.farsi: غير آن زنجير زلف دلبرم / ‏گر دو صد زنجير آرى بر درم
entries.english: Except the chain of my beloved’s curl— though you bring two hundred chains, I will burst them.

sections.title_fa: َحكمت نظر كردن در چارق و پوستين كه فَلْيَنْظُرِ الإِْنْسانُ مِمَّ خُلِق
sections.title_en: The wise purpose in looking at his rustic shoes and sheepskin jacket— then let Man consider from what he was created.

entries.farsi: باز گردان قصه‏ى عشق اياز / كان يكى گنجى است مالامال راز
entries.english: Bring back the story of Ayaz’s love; for it is a treasure full of mystery.

entries.farsi: مى‏رود هر روز در حجره‏ى برين / ز انكه هستى سخت مستى آورد
entries.english: Every day he is going into the uppermost chamber to see his rustic shoes and sheepskin jacket,

entries.farsi: عقل از سر شرم از دل مى‏برد / صد هزاران قرن پيشين را همين
entries.english: Because self-existence produces grievous intoxication: it removes intelligence from the head and reverence from the heart.

entries.farsi: ‏مستى هستى بزد ره زين كمين / شد عزازيلى از اين مستى بليس
entries.english: From this ambush this same intoxication of self-existence waylaid a hundred thousand generations of old.

entries.farsi: ‏كه چرا آدم شود بر من رئيس / خواجه‏ام من نيز و خواجه زاده‏ام
entries.english: By this self-existence an Azázíl was made to be Iblís, saying, “Why should Adam become lord over me?

entries.farsi: ‏صد هنر را قابل و آماده‏ام / در هنر من از كسى كم نيستم
entries.english: I too am noble and nobly-born: I am capable of receiving and ready for a hundred excellences.

entries.farsi: ‏تا بخدمت پيش دشمن بيستم / من ز آتش زاده‏ام او از وحل
entries.english: In excellence I am inferior to none that I should stand before my enemy to do him service.

entries.farsi: ‏پيش آتش مر وحل را چه محل / او كجا بود اندر آن دورى كه من
entries.english: I am born of fire, he of mud: what is the position of mud compared with fire?

entries.farsi: ‏صدر عالم بودم و فخر زمن
entries.english: Where was he in the period when I was the Prince of the World and the glory of Time?”

sections.title_fa: َخَلَقَ الْجَانَّ مِنْ مارِجٍ مِنْ نارٍو قوله تعالى فى حق ابليس انه كانَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَفَسَ ق
sections.title_en: “He created the Jinn from smokeless fire,” and His words concerning Iblís:
“truly he was one of the Jinn, and he transgressed.”

entries.farsi: شعله مى‏زد آتش جان سفيه / ‏كاتشى بود الولد سر ابيه
entries.english: The fire was aflame in the soul of the fool, because he was of fire: the son is the inward nature of his father.

entries.farsi: نه غلط گفتم كه بد قهر خدا / علتى را پيش آوردن چرا
entries.english: No; I have spoken in error; it was the compelling might of God: why, adduce any cause?

entries.farsi: كار بى‏علت مبرا از علل / ‏مستمر و مستقر است از ازل
entries.english: The causeless action is quit of causes: it is lasting and firmly established from eternity.

entries.farsi: در كمال صنع پاك مستحث / ‏علت حادث چه گنجد يا حدث
entries.english: In the perfection of the holy work sped on what room is there for temporal cause or temporal thing?

entries.farsi: سر اب چه بود اب ما صنع اوست / ‏صنع مغز است و اب صورت چو پوست
entries.english: What is “the inward nature of father”? His work is our father: work is the kernel, and the formal father is the skin.

entries.farsi: عشق دان اى فندق تن دوستت / ‏جانت جويد مغز و كوبد پوستت
entries.english: O nut-like body, know that Love is your friend: your soul will seek your kernel and batter your shell.

entries.farsi: دوزخى كه پوست باشد دوستش / ‏داد بدلنا جلودا پوستش
entries.english: The man doomed to Hell whose skin is his friend “We will give them skins in exchange” bestows a skin upon him.

entries.farsi: معنى و مغزت بر آتش حاكم است / ‏ليك آتش را قشورت هيزم است
entries.english: Your spiritual principle and kernel is dominant over the Fire, but your skins are fuel for the Fire.

entries.farsi: كوزه‏ى چوبين كه در وى آب جوست / ‏قدرت آتش همه بر ظرف اوست
entries.english: A wooden pot in which river-water is, the power of fire is entirely against the vessel containing it.

entries.farsi: معنى انسان بر آتش مالك است / ‏مالك دوزخ در او كى هالك است
entries.english: Man’s spiritual principle is a ruler over the Fire: when is Malik of Hell destroyed therein?

entries.farsi: پس ميفزا تو بدن معنى فزا / تا چو مالك باشى آتش را كيا
entries.english: Do not, then, increase your body; increase your spiritual principle, in order that you may be the Fire’s sovereign, like Malik.

entries.farsi: پوستها بر پوست مى‏افزوده‏اى / ‏الجرم چون پوست اندر دوده‏اى
entries.english: You have ever been adding skins to your skin: necessarily you are as a skin in soot.

entries.farsi: ز انكه آتش را علف جز پوست نيست / ‏قهر حق آن كبر را پوستين كنى است
entries.english: Since the Fire has no fodder except the skin, the vengeance of God will tear the skin off that pride.

entries.farsi: اين تكبر از نتيجه‏ى پوست است / ‏جاه و مال آن كبر را ز آن دوست است
entries.english: This arrogance is a product of the skin; hence power and riches are friends to that pride.

entries.farsi: اين تكبر چيست غفلت از لباب / ‏منجمد چون غفلت يخ ز آفتاب
