entries.english: Praises be unto You, O God! You did suddenly put me afar from sorrow.

entries.farsi: گر سر هر موى من يابد زبان / ‏شكرهاى تو نيايد در بيان
entries.english: If the tip of every hair of me should gain a tongue, the thanks due to You are inexpressible.

entries.farsi: مى‏زنم نعره در اين روضه و عيون / ‏خلق را يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يعلمون
entries.english: Amidst these gardens and fountains I am crying to the people, ‘Oh, would that my folk did but know!’ ”

sections.title_fa: باز خواندن شه زاده نصوح را از بهر دالكى بعد از استحكام توبه و قبول ‏ توبه و بهانه كردن او و دفع گفتن
sections.title_en: How the princess again invited Nasúh to shampoo her, after his repentance had taken firm hold
and was accepted, and how he made an excuse and refused to comply.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن آمد كسى كز مرحمت / ‏دختر سلطان ما مى‏خواندت
entries.english: Afterwards someone came to Nasúh, saying, “The daughter of our sovereign graciously invites you.

entries.farsi: دختر شاهت همى‏خواند بيا / تا سرش شويى كنون اى پارسا
entries.english: The King’s daughter invites you: come and wash her head now, O devout one.

entries.farsi: جز تو دالكى نمى‏خواهد دلش / ‏كه بمالد يا بشويد با گلش
entries.english: Her heart desires no shampooer except you to massage her or wash her with clay.”

entries.farsi: گفت رو رو دست من بى‏كار شد / وين نصوح تو كنون بيمار شد
entries.english: He answered, “Begone, begone! My hand is not in practice, and your Nasúh is now fallen sick.

entries.farsi: با دل خود گفت كز حد رفت جرم / ‏از دل من كى رود آن ترس و گرم
entries.english: Go; look for someone else hastily and speedily, for by God my hand has gone out of business.”

entries.farsi: رو كسى ديگر بجو اشتاب و تفت / ‏كه مرا و اهلل دست از كار رفت
entries.english: He said to himself, “My sin passed beyond bounds: how should that terror and anguish go from my mind?

entries.farsi: من بمردم يك ره و باز آمدم / ‏من چشيدم تلخى مرگ و عدم
entries.english: I died once, and I came back: I tasted the bitterness of death and non-existence.

entries.farsi: توبه‏اى كردم حقيقت با خدا / نشكنم تا جان شدن از تن جدا
entries.english: I have turned to God with real repentance: I will not break till my soul shall be parted from my body.

entries.farsi: بعد آن محنت كه را بار دگر / پا رود سوى خطر الا كه خر
entries.english: After such a tribulation, whose foot should move towards danger a second time, unless it be an ass?”

sections.title_fa: حكايت در بيان آن كه كسى توبه كند و پشيمان شود و باز آن پشيمانيها را فراموش كند و آزموده را باز آزمايد در خسارت ابد افتد، چون توبه‏ى او را ثباتى و قوتى و حالوتى و قبولى مدد نرسد چون ‏درخت بى‏بيخ هر روز زردتر و خشك‏تر بود، نعوذ باهلل

sections.title_en: Story demonstrating that when a person repents and feels remorse and then forgets his feelings of remorse and tries again what he has tried, he falls into everlasting perdition. Unless his repentance is reinforced by a firmness and strength and by a sweetness and acceptance, it is like a rootless tree, more faded and withered every day. We take refuge with God.

entries.farsi: گازرى بود و مر او را يك خرى / ‏پشت ريش اشكم تهى و الغرى
entries.english: There was a washer man, who had an ass with a sore on its back and empty-bellied and lean.

entries.farsi: بهر خوردن جز كه آب آن جا نبود / روز و شب بد خر در آن كور و كبود
entries.english: In ground covered with stones, where no grass grew: from morning till night it went without food and shelter.

entries.farsi: در ميان سنگ‏الخ بى‏گياه / ‏روز تا شب بى‏نوا و بى‏پناه
entries.english: Except water, there was nothing for it to eat or drink: the ass was in that miserable state by day and by night.

entries.farsi: آن حوالى نيستان و بيشه بود / شير بود آن جا كه صيدش پيشه بود
entries.english: In the neighbourhood was a reed-bed and a jungle, where a lion lived whose occupation was hunting.

entries.farsi: شير را با پيل نر جنگ اوفتاد / خسته شد آن شير و ماند از اصطياد
entries.english: A battle took place between the lion and a fierce elephant: the lion was wounded and disabled from going to hunt.

entries.farsi: مدتى واماند ز آن ضعف از شكار / بى‏نوا ماندند دد از چاشت خوار
entries.english: On account of his weakness he was unable to hunt for some time, and the wild animals were deprived of their morning-meal;

entries.farsi: ز انكه باقى خوار شير ايشان بدند / شير چون رنجور شد تنگ آمدند
entries.english: For they used to eat the lion’s leavings: when the lion became ill they suffered distress.

entries.farsi: شير يك روباه را فرمود رو / مر خرى را بهر من صياد شو
entries.english: The lion gave orders to a fox, saying, “Go and hunt an ass for me.

entries.farsi: گر خرى يابى به گرد مرغزار / رو فسونش خوان فريبانش بيار
entries.english: If you find an ass round about the meadow, go, charm him with specious talk, beguile him, and bring him.

entries.farsi: چون بيابم قوتى از گوشت خر / پس بگيرم بعد از آن صيدى دگر
entries.english: As soon as I gain some strength from (eating) the flesh of the ass, then afterwards I will seize another victim.

entries.farsi: اندكى من مى خورم باقى شما / من سبب باشم شما را در نوا
entries.english: I will eat a little, you the rest: I am the means for you as regards food.

entries.farsi: يا خرى يا گاو بهر من بجوى / ‏ز آن فسون‏هايى كه مى‏دانى بگوى
entries.english: Procure for me either an ass or an ox: address some of the charming words that you know.

entries.farsi: از فسون و از سخنهاى خوشش / ‏از رهش بيرون كن و اينجا كشش
entries.english: Deprive him of his wits by flatteries and fair words and bring him here.”

sections.title_fa: تشبيه كردن قطب كه عارف واصل است در اجرى دادن خلق از قوت مغفرت و رحمت بر مراتبى كه حقش الهام دهد و تمثيل به شير كه دد اجرى خوار و باقى خوار ويند بر مراتب قرب ايشان به شير ‏نه قرب مكانى بلكه قرب صفتى، و تفاصيل اين بسيار است و اللهَّ الهادى

sections.title_en: Parable of the Qutb, who is the gnostic united with God, in respect of his dispensing to the people their rations of forgiveness and mercy in the order and degree which God inspires him to observe; and a comparison of him with the lion, for the wild animals partake of the lion’s rations and eat his leavings in proportion to their nearness to him—not nearness in space
but nearness in quality. The details of this are many, and God is the Guide.

entries.farsi: قطب شير و صيد كردن كار او / باقيان اين خلق باقى خوار او
entries.english: The Qutb is the lion, and it is his business to hunt: the rest, these people, eat his leavings.

entries.farsi: تا توانى در رضاى قطب كوش / ‏تا قوى گردد كند صيد وحوش
entries.english: So far as you can, endeavour to satisfy the Qutb, so that he may gain strength and hunt the wild beasts.

entries.farsi: چون برنجد بى‏نوا مانند خلق / ‏كز كف عقل است جمله‏ى رزق حلق
entries.english: When he is ailing, the people remain unfed, for all food provided for the gullet comes from the hand of reason,

entries.farsi: ز انكه وجد خلق باقى خورد اوست / ‏اين نگه دار ار دل تو صيد جوست
entries.english: Since the ecstasies of the people are his leavings. Keep this in mind, if your heart desires the prey.

entries.farsi: او چو عقل و خلق چون اعضاى تن / بسته‏ى عقل است تدبير بدن
entries.english: He is like the reason, and the people are like the members of the body: the management of the body depends on the reason.

entries.farsi: ضعف قطب از تن بود از روح نى / ‏ضعف در كشتى بود در نوح نى
entries.english: The weakness of the Qutb is bodily, not spiritual: the weakness lies in the Ship, not in Noah.

entries.farsi: قطب آن باشد كه گرد خود تند / گردش افلاک گرد او بود
entries.english: The Qutb is he who turns round himself, round him is the revolution of the celestial spheres.

entries.farsi: ياريى ده در مرمه‏ى كشتى‏اش / ‏گر غالم خاص و بنده گشتى‏اش
entries.english: Lend some assistance in repairing his ship, if you have become his favourite slave and devoted servant.

entries.farsi: همچو روبه صيد گير و كن فداش / ‏تا عوض گيرى هزاران صيد بيش
entries.english: Your assistance is advantageous to you, not to him: God has said, “If you help God, you will be helped.”

entries.farsi: يارى‏ات در تو فزايد نه در او / گفت حق ان تنصروا اللهَّ تنصروا
entries.english: Hunt like the fox and sacrifice your prey to him, that you may gain in return a thousand preys and more.

entries.farsi: رو بهانه باشد آن صيد مريد / مرده گيرد صيد كفتار مريد
entries.english: The prey caught by the disciple is after the manner of the fox, the disobedient hyena catches prey dead.

entries.farsi: مرده پيش او كشى زنده شود / چرك در پاليز روينده شود
entries.english: If you present the dead to the Qutb, it will become living: filth in the orchard will produce.

entries.farsi: حيله و افسونگرى كار من است / ‏كار من دستان و از ره بردن است
entries.english: The fox said to the lion, “I will serve you: I will contrive expedients and rob him of his wits.

entries.farsi: گفت روبه شير را خدمت كنم / ‏حيله‏ها سازم ز عقلش بر كنم
entries.english: Cunning and enchantment is my business: it is my business to beguile and lead astray.”

entries.farsi: از سر كه جانب جو مى‏شتافت / ‏آن خر مسكين الغر را بيافت
entries.english: Hastening from the mountain-top towards the river, he found that miserable emaciated ass.

entries.farsi: پس سالم گرم كرد و پيش رفت / ‏پيش آن ساده دل درويش رفت
entries.english: Then he saluted him cordially and advanced: he advanced to meet that poor simpleton,

entries.farsi: گفت چونى اندر اين صحراى خشك / ‏در ميان سنگ‏الخ و جاى خشك
entries.english: And said, “How are you in this arid desert amidst stones and on sterile ground?”

entries.farsi: گفت خر گر در غمم گر در ارم / ‏قسمتم حق كرد من ز آن شاكرم
entries.english: The ass replied, “Whether I am in pain or in Iram, God has made it my portion, and I am grateful for it.

entries.farsi: شكر گويم دوست را در خير و شر / ز انكه هست اندر قضا از بد بتر
entries.english: I give thanks to the Friend in good and evil estate, because in destiny there is worse than ill.

entries.farsi: غير حق جمله عدويند اوست دوست / ‏با عدو از دوست شكوت كى نكوست
entries.english: Since He is the Dispenser of portions, complaint is infidelity. Patience is needful: patience is the key to the gift.

entries.farsi: چون كه قسام اوست كفر آمد گله / ‏صبر بايد صبر مفتاح الصله
entries.english: All except God are enemies: He is the Friend: how is it good to complain of a friend to an enemy?

entries.farsi: تا دهد دوغم نخواهم انگبين / ‏ز انكه هر نعمت غمى دارد قرين
entries.english: So long as He gives me buttermilk I will not desire honey, for every pleasure has a pain joined with it.”

sections.title_fa: حكايت ديدن خر سقايى با نوايى اسبان تازى بر آخور خاص و تمنا بردن آن دولت را، در موعظه‏ى آن كه تمنا نبايد بردن الا مغفرت و عنايت كه اگر در صد لون رنجى چون لذت مغفرت بود همه شيرين شود، باقى هر دولتى كه آن را ناآزموده تمنى مى‏برى با آن رنجى قرين است كه آن را نمى‏بينى، چنان كه از هر دامى دانه پيدا بود و فخ پنهان، تو در اين يك دام مانده اى تمنى مى‏برى كه
‏كاشكى با آن دانه‏ها رفتمى، پندارى كه آن دانه‏ها بى‏دام است
sections.title_en: Story of an ass belonging to a seller of firewood, which saw some well-fed Arab horses in the royal stable and wished for the same fortune. This story conveys the lesson that one ought not to wish for anything

but forgiveness and favour; for though you are in a hundred kinds of pain, they all become sweet when you feel the delight of being forgiven; and for the rest, every fortune that you wish for before you have experienced it is accompanied by a pain which you do not perceive; as in every trap the bait is visible while the snare is concealed. You have been caught in this one trap are wishing,
“Would that I had gone after those baits!” You fancy that those baits are without a trap.

entries.farsi: بود سقايى مر او را يك خرى / ‏گشته از محنت دو تا چون چنبرى
entries.english: There was a water-carrier who owned an ass that had been bent double like a hoop by affliction.

entries.farsi: پشتش از بار گران صد جاى ريش / ‏عاشق و جويان روز مرگ خويش
entries.english: Its back was galled by the heavy load in a hundred places: it was passionately desiring the day of its death.

entries.farsi: جو كجا از كاه خشك او سير نى / ‏در عقب زخمى و سيخى آهنى
entries.english: What of barley? It never got its fill of dry straw: at its heels a blow and an iron goad.

entries.farsi: مير آخور ديد او را رحم كرد / كاشناى صاحب خر بود مرد
entries.english: The Master of the stable saw it and took pity— for the man was acquainted with the owner of the ass—

entries.farsi: پس سالمش كرد و پرسيدش ز حال / ‏كز چه اين خر گشت دو تا همچو دال
entries.english: So he saluted him and asked him what had happened, saying, “What is the cause of this ass being bent double like a dál?”

entries.farsi: گفت از درويشى و تقصير من / ‏كه نمى‏يابد خود اين بسته دهن
entries.english: He replied, “On account of my poverty and destitution this dumb animal is not getting even straw.”

entries.farsi: گفت بسپارش به من تو روز چند / تا شود در آخور شه زورمند
entries.english: “Hand him over to me,” said the other, “for a few days, that in the King’s stable he may grow strong.”

entries.farsi: خر بدو بسپرد و آن رحمت پرست / ‏در ميان آخور سلطانش بست
entries.english: He handed the ass over to him, and that merciful man tethered him in the Sultan’s stable.

entries.farsi: خر ز هر سو مركب تازى بديد / با نوا و فربه و خوب و جديد
entries.english: The ass saw on every side Arab horses, well-fed and fat and handsome and glossy;

entries.farsi: زير پاشان روفته آبى زده / ‏كه به وقت و جو به هنگام آمده
entries.english: He saw the ground swept under their feet and sprinkled with water; the straw coming at the time, and the barley at the hour.

entries.farsi: خارش و مالش مر اسبان را بديد / پوز بالا كرد كاى رب مجيد
entries.english: He saw the horses curry-combed and rubbed down. He lifted up his muzzle, crying, “O glorious Lord,

entries.farsi: نه كه مخلوق توام گيرم خرم / ‏از چه زار و پشت ريش و الغرم
entries.english: Am not I Your creature? I grant that I am an ass, wherefore am I wretched, with sores on my back, and lean?

entries.farsi: شب ز درد پشت و از جوع شكم / ‏آرزومندم به مردن دم‏به‏دم
entries.english: At night, because of the pain in my back and the hunger in my belly, I am always wishing to die.

entries.farsi: حال اين اسبان چنين خوش با نوا / من چه مخصوصم به تعذيب و بال
entries.english: These horses are so happy and prosperous: why am I singled out for torment and tribulation?”

entries.farsi: ناگهان آوازه‏ى پيكار شد / تازيان را وقت زين و كار شد
entries.english: Suddenly came the rumour of war: it was the time for the Arab horses to be saddled and brought into action.

entries.farsi: زخمهاى تير خوردند از عدو / رفت پيكانها در ايشان سو به سو
entries.english: They were wounded with arrows by the foe: the barbs entered them on every side.

entries.farsi: از غزا باز آمدند آن تازيان / ‏اندر آخور جمله افتاده ستان
entries.english: Those Arab horses returned from the campaign, they all fell down and lay on their backs in the stable.

entries.farsi: مى‏شكافيدند تنهاشان به نيش / ‏تا برون آرند پيكانها ز ريش
entries.english: Their legs were tightly bandaged with canvas: the blacksmiths were standing in file,

entries.farsi: پايهاشان بسته محكم با نوار / نعل بندان ايستاده بر قطار
entries.english: Piercing their bodies with the scalpel in order to extract the barbs from their wounds.

entries.farsi: آن خر آن را ديد و مى‏گفت اى خدا / من به فقر و عافيت دادم رضا
entries.english: The ass saw that, and was saying, “O God, I am satisfied with poverty and health.

entries.farsi: ز ان نوا بى‏زارم و ز ان زخم زشت / ‏هر كه خواهد عافيت دنيا بهشت
entries.english: I have no taste for that food and those hideous wounds.” Everyone who desires health abandons the world.

sections.title_fa: ‏ناپسنديدن روباه گفتن خر را كه من راضيم به قسمت
sections.title_en: How the fox disapproved of the saying of the ass, “I am satisfied with my lot.”

entries.farsi: گفته روبه جستن رزق حالل / ‏فرض باشد از براى امتثال
entries.english: The fox said, “It is an obligation to seek lawful provision in obedience.

entries.farsi: عالم اسباب و چيزى بى‏سبب / ‏مى‏نيايد پس مهم باشد طلب
entries.english: This is the world of means: nothing is obtained without a means: therefore it is important to seek.

entries.farsi: وَ ابْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْ لِ اللهَِّ است امر / تا نبايد غصب كردن همچو نمر
entries.english: ‘And seek ye of the bounty of God’ is command, lest they should seize by violence, like the leopard.

entries.farsi: گفت پيغمبر كه بر رزق اى فتا / در فرو بسته ست و بر در قفلها
entries.english: The Prophet has said, the door is shut against provision, O youth; and on the door there are locks.’

entries.farsi: جنبش و آمد شد ما و اكتساب / ‏هست مفتاحى بر آن قفل و حجاب
entries.english: Our movement and our going to and fro and our acquisition is a key to that lock and barrier.

entries.farsi: بى‏كليد اين در گشادن راه نيست / ‏بى‏طلب نان سنت اهلل نيست
entries.english: Without the key there is no way to open the door: bread without endeavour is not God’s law.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن خر روباه را
sections.title_en: How the ass answered the fox.

entries.farsi: گفت از ضعف توكل باشد آن / ‏ور نه بدهد نان كسى كه داد جان
entries.english: He replied, “That is weak faith; else He who gave life gives (us) bread.

entries.farsi: هر كه جويد پادشاهى و ظفر / كم نيايد لقمه‏ى نان اى پسر
entries.english: Whoever seeks sovereignty and victory, a mouthful of bread will not fail, O son.

entries.farsi: جمله را رزاق روزى مى‏دهد / قسمت هر يك به پيشش مى‏نهد
entries.english: All wild animals, both the herbivorous and the predatory, are devourers of the provision: they neither go in quest of work nor do they support providing.

entries.farsi: دام و دد جمله همه اكال رزق / ‏نه پى كسب‏اند نه حمال رزق
entries.english: The Provider gives their daily bread to all: He lays before each one the portion allotted to him.

entries.farsi: رزق آيد پيش هر كاو صبر جست / ‏رنج كوششها ز بى‏صبرى تست
entries.english: The provision comes to everyone who seeks patience: the trouble of making efforts arises from your want of patience.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن روباه خر را
sections.title_en: How the fox answered the ass.

entries.farsi: گفت روبه آن توكل نادر است / ‏كم كسى اندر توكل ماهر است
entries.english: The fox replied, “Such trust in God is exceptional: few are proficient in trust in God.

entries.farsi: گرد نادر گشتن از نادانى است / ‏هر كسى را كى ره سلطانى است
entries.english: It is ignorance to concern one’s self with the exceptional: how is the King’s highway for every one?

entries.farsi: چون قناعت را پيمبر گنج گفت / ‏هر كسى را كى رسد گنج نهفت
entries.english: Since the Prophet has said that contentment is a treasure, how should the hidden treasure be gained by every one?

entries.farsi: حد خود بشناس و بر بالا مپر / تا نيفتى در نشيب شور و شر
entries.english: Recognise your limit and do not fly aloft, lest you fall into the abyss of woe and bane.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن خر روباه را
sections.title_en: How the ass answered the fox.

entries.farsi: گفت اين معكوس مى‏گويى بدان / ‏شور و شر از طمع آيد سوى جان
entries.english: The ass replied, “Know that you are speaking the reverse, woe and misery comes to the soul from greed.

entries.farsi: از قناعت هيچ كس بى‏جان نشد / از حريصى هيچ كس سلطان نشد
entries.english: No one was deprived of life by contentment; no one was made a king by covetousness.

entries.farsi: نان ز خوكان و سگان نبود دريغ / ‏كسب مردم نيست اين باران و ميغ
entries.english: Bread is not withheld from pigs and dogs: this rain and clouds are not earned by Man.

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه عاشقى بر رزق زار / هست عاشق رزق هم بر رزق خوار
entries.english: Just as you are pitiably enamoured of the daily bread, so the daily bread is enamoured of its consumer.

sections.title_fa: در تقرير معنى توكل حكايت آن زاهد كه توكل را امتحان مى‏كرد از ميان اسباب و شهر بيرون آمد و از قوارع و رهگذر خلق دور شد و به بن كوهى مهجورى مفقودى در غايت گرسنگى سر بر سر سنگى نهاد و خفت و با خود گفت توكل كردم بر سبب سازى و رزاقى تو و از اسباب منقطع شدم تا ببينم سببيت توكل را

sections.title_en: Exposition of the meaning of trust in God, the Story of the ascetic who, making trial of his trust in God, abandoned his property and town and went far away from the beaten tracks and thoroughfares of men to the foot of a remote and inaccessible mountain, in extreme hunger he laid his head upon a stone and fell asleep, saying to himself, ‘I put trust in Your providing the means and daily bread; and I cut myself off from means in order that I may experience the causation of trust in God.’

entries.farsi: آن يكى زاهد شنود از مصطفى / كه يقين آيد به جان رزق از خدا
entries.english: A certain ascetic had heard the saying of Mustafá that the daily bread surely comes from God to the spirit,

entries.farsi: از براى امتحان آن مرد رفت / ‏در بيابان نزد كوهى خفت تفت
entries.english: Whether you will or no, your daily bread comes running to you because it is fond of you.

entries.farsi: گر بخواهى ور نخواهى رزق تو / پيش تو آيد دوان از عشق تو
entries.english: By way of trial that man went into the desert and immediately lay down near a mountain,

entries.farsi: كه ببينم رزق مى‏آيد به من / ‏تا قوى گردد مرا در رزق ظن
entries.english: Saying, ‘I will see whether the daily bread will come to me: that my belief in the daily bread may become firm.’

entries.farsi: كاروانى راه گم كرد و كشيد / سوى كوه آن ممتحن را خفته ديد
entries.english: A caravan lost its way and marched towards the mountain: the travellers saw him lying there who was making the trial.

entries.farsi: گفت اين مرد اين طرف چون است عور / در بيابان از ره و از شهر دور
entries.english: How is this man destitute here in the wilderness, far from road and town?

entries.farsi: اى عجب مرده است يا زنده كه او / مى‏نترسد هيچ از گرگ و عدو
entries.english: Oh, I wonder, is he dead or alive? He has no fear of wolves or enemies.’

entries.farsi: آمدند و دست بر وى مى‏زدند / قاصدا چيزى نگفت آن ارجمند
entries.english: They came on and touched him with their hands: that venerable man deliberately said nothing.

entries.farsi: هم نجنبيد و نجنبانيد سر / وانكرد از امتحان هم او بصر
entries.english: He did not stir; he did not even move his head or open his eyes, because he was making a trial.

entries.farsi: پس بگفتند اين ضعيف بى‏مراد / از مجاعت سكته اندر اوفتاد
entries.english: Then they said, ‘This poor disappointed man has had a stroke of apoplexy caused by hunger.’

entries.farsi: نان بياوردند و در ديگى طعام / ‏تا بريزندش به حلقوم و به كام
entries.english: They fetched bread and food in a kettle that they might pour it into his mouth and his throat.

entries.farsi: پس به قاصد مرد دندان سخت كرد / تا ببيند صدق آن ميعاد مرد
entries.english: Thereupon the man purposely clenched his teeth, in order to see the truth of that promise.

entries.farsi: رحمشان آمد كه اين بس بى‏نواست / ‏وز مجاعت هالك مرگ و فناست
entries.english: They felt pity for him and said, ‘This man is starving and perishing with hunger and at the point of death’;

entries.farsi: كارد آوردند قوم اشتافتند / بسته دندانهاش را بشكافتند
entries.english: They brought a knife and hastily made a rift in his closed teeth.

entries.farsi: ريختند اندر دهانش شوربا / مى‏فشردند اندر او نان پاره‏ها
entries.english: They poured soup into his mouth and forced into it fragments of bread.

entries.farsi: گفت اى دل گر چه خود تن مى‏زنى / ‏راز مى‏دانى و نازى مى‏كنى
entries.english: He said to himself, ‘O my heart, even though you are keeping silence, you know the secret and are showing disdain.’

entries.farsi: امتحان زين بيشتر خود چون بود / رزق سوى صابران خوش مى‏رود
entries.english: His heart replied, ‘I know and am purposely behaving: God is the provider for my soul and body.’

entries.farsi: گفت دل دانم و قاصد مى‏كنم / ‏رازق اللهَّ است بر جان و تنم
entries.english: How should there be a trial more than this? The daily bread comes with joy to those who have patience.”

sections.title_fa: ‏جواب گفتن روباه خر را و تحريض كردن او خر را بر كسب
sections.title_en: How the fox answered the ass and urged him to seek a livelihood.

entries.farsi: گفت روبه اين حكايتها بهل / ‏دستها بر كسب زن جهد المقل
entries.english: The fox said, “Leave these stories and apply all your poor efforts to earning a livelihood.

entries.farsi: دست داده‏ستت خدا كارى بكن / ‏مكسبى كن يارى يارى بكن
entries.english: God has given you hands: do some work, earn something, and help a friend.

entries.farsi: هر كسى در مكسبى پا مى‏نهد / يارى ياران ديگر مى‏كند
entries.english: Everyone takes steps to earn something and helps other friends,

entries.farsi: ز انكه جمله‏ى كسب نايد از يكى / ‏هم دروگر هم سقا هم حايكى
entries.english: Because all the earning is not done by one: a carpenter and also a water-carrier and a weaver.

entries.farsi: اين به هنبازى است عالم برقرار / هر كسى كارى گزيند ز افتقار
entries.english: By means of this partnership the world is maintained: every one, by want, chooses some work.

entries.farsi: طبل خوارى در ميانه شرط نيست / ‏راه سنت كار و مكسب كردنى است
entries.english: It is not right to be an idle parasite in the midst: the way of the Sunna is to work and earn.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن خر روباه را كه توكل بهترين كسبهاست كه هر كسى محتاجست به توكل كه اى خدا اين كار مرا راست آر و دعا متضمن توكل است و توكل كسبى است كه به هيچ كسبى ‏ ديگر محتاج نيست الى آخره

sections.title_en: How the ass answered the fox, saying, “Trust in God is the best way of earning a livelihood, for everyone needs to trust in God and cry, ‘O God, bring this work of mine to success’; and prayer involves trust in God, and trust in God is the means of livelihood that is independent of any other means, etc.”

entries.farsi: بخششان بسيار شد اندر خطاب / ‏مانده گشتند از سؤال و از جواب
entries.english: He said, “In the two worlds I do not know any means of livelihood superior to trust in my Lord.

entries.farsi: كسب شكرش را نمى‏دانم نديد / تا كشد شكر خدا رزق و مزيد
entries.english: I know nothing to be compared with the acquisition of thanksgiving to Him, in order that thanksgiving to God may bring the daily bread and the increase.”

entries.farsi: گفت من به از توكل بر ربى / ‏مى‏ندانم در دو عالم مكسبى
entries.english: Their dispute was prolonged in mutual altercation they became incapable of questioning and answering.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن گفتش بدان در مملكه / ‏نهى ال تلقوا بايدى تهلكه
entries.english: Afterwards he said to him, “Mark in the kingdom the prohibition, and ‘Do not cast yourselves into destruction.’

entries.farsi: صبر در صحراى خشك و سنگ‏الخ / احمقى باشد جهان حق فراخ
entries.english: In a barren desert covered with stones self-denial is folly: God’s world is wide.

entries.farsi: نقل كن ز ينجا به سوى مرغزار / مى‏چر آن جا سبزه گرد جويبار
entries.english: Move from this place towards the meadow, and browse there on the verdure round about the river—

entries.farsi: مرغزارى سبز مانند جنان / ‏سبزه رسته اندر آن جا تا ميان
entries.english: A meadow as green as Paradise, where the plants grows up to the waist

entries.farsi: خرم آن حيوان كه او آن جا شود / اشتر اندر سبزه ناپيدا شود
entries.english: Happy the animal that goes there: amidst such lush plant life a camel would become invisible.

entries.farsi: هر طرف در وى يكى چشمه روان / ‏اندر او حيوان مرفه در امان
entries.english: There, on every side, is a running fountain; there the animals are in comfort and security.”

entries.farsi: از خرى او را نمى‏گفت اى لعين / كو نشاط و فربهى و فر تو
entries.english: From asininity he did not say to him, “O accursed one, you are from there: how are you so wretched?

entries.farsi: چيست اين الغر تن مضطر تو / شرح روضه گر دروغ و زور نيست
entries.english: Where are your gaiety, fatness and comeliness? What is this lean starved body of yours?

entries.farsi: ‏پس چرا چشمت از او مخمور نيست / اين گدا چشمى و اين ناديدگى
entries.english: If your description of the meadow is not falsehood and fiction, then why is yours eye not intoxicated by it?

entries.farsi: از گدايى تست نز بگلربگى / چون ز چشمه آمدى چو نى تو خشك
entries.english: These greedy looks and this blindness are the result of your beggarliness, not of sovereignty.

entries.farsi: ‏ور تو ناف آهويى كو بوى مشك / ز ان كه مى‏گويى و شرحش مى‏كنى
entries.english: Since you have come from the fountain, how are you dry? And if you are the gland of the musk-deer, where is the fragrance of musk?

entries.farsi: ‏چون نشانى در تو نامد اى سنى
entries.english: How is there no trace in you of that which you say and describe, O exalted one?”

sections.title_fa: مثل آوردن اشتر در بيان آن كه در مخبر دولتى فر و اثر آن چون نبينى جاى متهم داشتن ‏ باشد كه او مقلد است در آن
sections.title_en: Parable of the camel, explaining that when someone tells of his good fortune and you do not perceive in him any appearance or sign of welfare, there is reason to suspect
that he is an imitator of those who have really attained to spiritual felicity.

entries.farsi: آن يكى پرسيد اشتر را كه هى / ‏از كجا مى‏آيى اى اقبال پى
entries.english: A certain man asked a camel, “Hey, where do you come from, O you whom fortune attends?

entries.farsi: گفت از حمام گرم كوى تو / گفت خود پيداست از زانوى تو
entries.english: He replied, “From the hot-bath in your street.” Said the other, “Truly, it is manifest in your knees!”

entries.farsi: مار موسى ديد فرعون عنود / مهلتى مى‏خواست نرمى مى‏نمود
entries.english: Pharaoh, the obstinate rebel, saw Moses’ snake, he begged for a respite and showed meekness.

entries.farsi: زيركان گفتند بايستى كه اين / ‏تندتر گشتى چو هست او رب دين
entries.english: The men of intelligence said, “This man ought to have been fiercer, since he is the Lord of the Judgement.

entries.farsi: رب اعلى گر وى است اندر جلوس / ‏بهر يك كرمى چى است اين چاپلوس
entries.english: Whether the miracle was a dragon or a snake, what has become of the pride and wrath proper to his divinity?

entries.farsi: معجزه گر اژدها گر مار بد / نخوت و خشم خدايى‏اش چه شد
entries.english: If he is the Supreme Lord seated on the throne, what is this blandishment on account of a single worm?”

entries.farsi: نفس تو تا مست نقل است و نبيد / دان كه روحت خوشه‏ى غيبى نديد
entries.english: So long as your nafs is intoxicated with the dessert and date-wine, know that your spirit has not beheld the cluster belonging to the World Unseen,

entries.farsi: كه عالمات است ز آن ديدار نور / التجافى منك عن دار الغرور
entries.english: For the signs of that vision of the Light is your withdrawal from the abode of delusion.

entries.farsi: مرغ چون بر آب شورى مى‏تند / آب شيرين را نديده ست او مدد
entries.english: Since the bird is frequenting briny water, it has not seen help in the sweet water;

entries.farsi: بلكه تقليد است آن ايمان او / روى ايمان را نديده جان او
entries.english: No, its faith is imitation: its spirit has never seen the face of faith.

entries.farsi: پس خطر باشد مقلد را عظيم / ‏از ره و ره زن ز شيطان رجيم
entries.english: Hence, because of the accursed Devil, the imitator is in great danger from the road and the brigand;

entries.farsi: چون ببيند نور حق ايمن شود / ز اضطرابات شك او ساكن شود
entries.english: When he beholds the Light of God, he becomes safe: he is at rest from the agitations of doubt.

entries.farsi: تا كف دريا نيايد سوى خاك / ‏كاصل او آمد بود در اصطكاك
entries.english: The sea-foam is in collision till it comes to the earth which is its origin.

entries.farsi: خاكى است آن كف غريب است اندر آب / ‏در غريبى چاره نبود ز اضطراب
entries.english: The scum is earthly: it is an exile in the water: in exile agitation is inevitable.

entries.farsi: ون كه چشمش باز شد و آن نقش خواند / ديو را بر وى دگر دستى نماند
entries.english: When his eye is opened and he reads those characters, the Devil has no power over him anymore.

entries.farsi: گر چه با روباه خر اسرار گفت / ‏سرسرى گفت و مقلدوار گفت
entries.english: Although the ass spoke of mysteries to the fox, he spoke superficially and like an imitator.

entries.farsi: آب را بستود و او تايق نبود / رخ دريد و جامه او عاشق نبود
entries.english: He praised the water, but he had no longing; he tore his face and raiment, but he was no lover.

entries.farsi: از منافق عذر رد آمد نه خوب / ‏ز انكه در لب بود آن نه در قلوب
entries.english: The excuse made by the hypocrite was bad, not good, because it was on their lips, not in their hearts.

entries.farsi: بوى سيبش هست جزو سيب نيست / ‏بو در او جز از پى آسيب نيست
entries.english: He has the smell of the apple, but no part of the apple; and in him the smell is only for the purpose of contact.

entries.farsi: حمله‏ى زن در ميان كارزار / نشكند صف بلكه گردد كار زار
entries.english: The charge of a woman in battle does not break the ranks; no, her plight becomes pitiable.

entries.farsi: گر چه مى‏بينى چو شير اندر صفش / ‏تيغ بگرفته همى‏لرزد كفش
entries.english: Though you see her take the sword like a lion amidst the ranks, her hand trembles.

entries.farsi: الجرم مغلوب باشد عقل او / جز سوى خسران نباشد نقل او
entries.english: Alas for him whose reason is female, while his wicked fleshly soul is male and ready!

entries.farsi: واى آن كه عقل او ماده بود / نفس زشتش نر و آماده بود
entries.english: Of necessity, his reason is vanquished: his movement is towards naught but perdition.

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن كس كه عقلش نر بود / نفس زشتش ماده و مضطر بود
entries.english: Oh, blest is that one whose reason is male, while his wicked fleshly soul is female and helpless;

entries.farsi: قل جزوى‏اش نر و غالب بود / نفس انثى را خرد سالب بود
entries.english: Whose particular reason is male and dominant, his intellect deprives the female fleshly soul of power to do mischief.

entries.farsi: حمله‏ى ماده به صورت هم جرى است / ‏آفت او همچو آن خر از خرى است
entries.english: The attack of the female, too, is bold in appearance; her defect, as that ass, arises from asininity.

entries.farsi: وصف حيوانى بود بر زن فزون / ‏ز انكه سوى رنگ و بو دارد ركون
entries.english: The animal nature prevails in woman, because she has an inclination towards colour and scent.

entries.farsi: رنگ و بوى سبزه‏زار آن خر شنيد / جمله حجتها ز طبع او رميد
entries.english: The ass heard of the colour and scent of the meadow, all arguments disgusted him.

entries.farsi: تشنه محتاج مطر شد و ابر نه / ‏نفس را جوع البقر بد صبر نه
entries.english: The thirsty man wanted rain, and there was no cloud; the fleshly soul was ravenously hungry, and there was no self-restraint.

entries.farsi: اسپر آهن بود صبر اى پدر / حق نبشته بر سپر جاء الظفر
entries.english: Self-restraint is an iron shield, O father: upon the shield God has written, “Victory will come.”

entries.farsi: صد دليل آرد مقلد در بيان / ‏از قياسى گويد آن را نه از عيان
entries.english: The imitator brings forward a hundred proofs in his exposition; he speaks intellectually, not from immediate experience.

entries.farsi: مشك آلود است الا مشك نيست / ‏بوى مشك استش ولى جز پشك نيست
entries.english: He is tinctured with musk, but he is not musk: he has the scent of musk, but he is only shit.

entries.farsi: تا كه پشكى مشك گردد اى مريد / سالها بايد در آن روضه چريد
entries.english: In order that a piece of shit may become musk, O disciple, one must browse for years in that garden.

entries.farsi: جز قرنفل ياسمن يا گل مچر / رو به صحراى ختن با آن نفر
entries.english: One must not eat straw and barley, like asses: browse on arghawán, like the musk-deer in Khutan.

entries.farsi: كه نبايد خورد و جو همچون خران / ‏آهوانه در ختن چر ارغوان
entries.english: Do not browse on aught but clove, jasmine, or roses: go to the plain of Khutan in company with those personages.

entries.farsi: معده را خو كن بدان ريحان و گل / ‏تا بيابى حكمت و قوت رسل
entries.english: Accustom your belly to the sweet basil and the rose, that you may gain the wisdom and food of the prophets.

entries.farsi: خوى معده زين كه و جو باز كن / ‏خوردن ريحان و گل آغاز كن
entries.english: Break your belly of its habit of this straw and barley: begin to eat the sweet basil and the rose.

entries.farsi: معده‏ى تن سوى كهدان مى‏كشد / معده‏ى دل سوى ريحان مى‏كشد
entries.english: The corporeal belly leads to the straw-barn; the spiritual belly leads to the sweet basil.

entries.farsi: هر كه كاه و جو خورد قربان شود / هر كه نور حق خورد قرآن شود
entries.english: Whoever feeds on straw and barley becomes a sacrifice (qurbán); whoever feeds on the Light of God becomes the Qur’án.

entries.farsi: نيم تو مشك است و نيمى پشك هين / ‏هين ميفزا پشك افزا مشك چين
entries.english: Beware! Half of you is musk and half is shit. Beware! Do not increase the shit, increase the Chinese musk.

entries.farsi: آن مقلد صد دليل و صد بيان / ‏در زبان آرد ندارد هيچ جان
entries.english: The imitator brings on to his tongue a hundred proofs and explanations, but he has no soul.

entries.farsi: چون كه گوينده ندارد جان و فر / گفت او را كى بود برگ و ثمر
entries.english: When the speaker has no soul and glory, how should his speech have leaves and fruit?

entries.farsi: مى‏كند گستاخ مردم را به راه / ‏او به جان لرزان‏تر است از برگ كاه
entries.english: He boldly directs people in the Way; he is more tremulous in soul than a blade of straw.

entries.farsi: پس حديثش گر چه بس با فر بود / در حديثش لرزه هم مضمر بود
entries.english: Therefore, though his discourse may be splendid, tremor is also latent in his discourse.

sections.title_fa: ‏فرق ميان دعوت شيخ كامل واصل و ميان سخن ناقصان فاضل فضل تحصيلى بر بسته
sections.title_en: The difference between the call of the perfect Shaykh who is united with God and the words of imperfect men whose virtues are acquired and artificial

entries.farsi: شيخ نورانى ز ره آگه كند / با سخن هم نور را همره كند
entries.english: The illumined Shaykh makes cognisant of the Way; moreover, he causes the light to accompany his words.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا مست و نورانى شوى / ‏تا حديثت را شود نورش روى
entries.english: Strive to become intoxicated and illumined, in order that his light may be the rhyme-letter to your discourse.

entries.farsi: هر چه در دوشاب جوشيده شود / در عقيده طعم دوشابش بود
entries.english: Whatever is boiled in grape-juice, the flavour of grape-juice will be in its syrup.

entries.farsi: از جزر و ز سيب و به و ز گردكان / ‏لذت دوشاب يابى تو از آن
entries.english: Whether it is syrup of carrots or apples or quinces or walnuts, you will taste in it the delicious flavour of grape-juice.

entries.farsi: علم اندر نور چون فر غرده شده / پس ز علمت نور يابد قوم لد
entries.english: When your knowledge is steeped in the light, then the rebellious folk derive light from your knowledge.

entries.farsi: هر چه گويى باشد آن هم نورناك / ‏كاسمان هرگز نبارد غير پاك
entries.english: Whatever you say, too, will be luminous, for the sky never rains aught but pure.

entries.farsi: آسمان شو ابر شو باران ببار / ناودان بارش كند نبود بكار
entries.english: Become the sky, become the cloud and shed rain: the spout rains, it is not at work.

entries.farsi: آب اندر ناودان عاريتى است / ‏آب اندر ابر و دريا فطرتى است
entries.english: The water in the spout is borrowed; the water in the cloud and in the sea is original.

entries.farsi: فكر و انديشه‏ست مثل ناودان / ‏وحى و مكشوف است ابر و آسمان
entries.english: Your thought and cogitation resemble the spout; inspiration and revelation are the cloud and the sky.

entries.farsi: آب باران باغ صد رنگ آورد / ناودان همسايه در جنگ آورد
entries.english: The rain-water produces a many-coloured garden; the spout causes your neighbour to quarrel.

entries.farsi: خر دو سه حمله به روبه بحث كرد / چون مقلد بد فريب او بخورد
entries.english: The ass disputed twice or thrice with the fox, since he was an imitator he was beguiled by him.

entries.farsi: طنطنه‏ى ادراك بينايى نداشت / ‏دمدمه‏ى روبه بر او سكته گماشت
entries.english: He had not the glorious power of perception possessed by a seer: the fox’s chattering brought upon him apoplexy.

entries.farsi: حرص خوردن آن چنان كردش ذليل / ‏كه زبونش گشت با پانصد دليل
entries.english: Greedy desire to eat and drink made him so despicable that he submitted to him notwithstanding five hundred arguments.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن مخنث و پرسيدن لوطى از او در حالت لواطه كه اين خنجر از بهر چيست گفت از براى آن كه هر كه با من بد انديشد اشكمش بشكافم، لوطى بر سر او آمد و شد مى‏كرد و مى‏گفت الحمد هلل كه من بد نمى‏انديشم با تو

‏هزل من هزل نيست تعليم است بيت من بيت نيست اقليم است إِنَّ اللهََّ ال يَسْ تَحْ يِي أَنْ يَضْ رِبَ مَثَلاً ما بَعُوضَ ةً فَما فَوْقَها، اى فما فوقها فى تغيير النفوس بالانكار، ما ذا أَرادَ اللهَُّ بِهذا مَثَلاً ، و آن گه جواب مى‏فرمايد كه اين خواستم يُضِ لُّ بِهِ كَثِيراً وَ يَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيراً، كه هر فتنه اى همچون ميزان است بسياران از او سرخ رو شوند و بسياران بى‏مراد شوند، و لو تاملت فيه
قليال وجدت من نتايجه الشريفة كثيرا
sections.title_en: The story of the sodomite and the sodomizer’s asking him during the occasion of sodomy, “For what is this dagger?” “So that, anyone who thinks evil toward me, I may rip open his belly. The sodomizer was coming and going (moving back and forth in the act of sodomy) and was saying,

“God be praised, since I am not thinking evil toward you. “My tent is not a tent, it is a continent; my jest is not a jest, it is a lesson.” Verily, God is not ashamed to set forth as a parable a gnat or what exceeds it, i.e. “what exceeds it in respect of the corruption of souls by disbelief”; “What is it that Allah means by using this as a parable?” and then He answers, “I mean this:

He lets many be led astray thereby and He lets many be guided aright thereby.” Every temptation is like a pair of scales: many come off with honour and many with disgrace; and if you were to meditate on it a little, you would feel many of its excellent effects.

entries.farsi: كنده‏اى را لوطيى در خانه برد / سر نگون افكندش و در وى فشرد
entries.english: A sodomizer brought a beardless youth (catamite) into a house, threw him head downwards, and pressed into him (in the act of sodomy).

entries.farsi: بر ميانش خنجرى ديد آن لعين / ‏پس بگفتش بر ميانت چيست اين
entries.english: The accursed wretch saw a dagger on his waist, so he said to him, “What is this on your waist?”

entries.farsi: گفت آن كه با من ار يك بدمنش / ‏بد بينديشد بدرم اشكمش
entries.english: He replied, “’It is in order that, if any evil-minded person should think of committing evil against me, I may rip his belly.”

entries.farsi: گفت لوطى حمد هلل را كه من / ‏بد نينديشيده‏ام با تو به فن
entries.english: The lútí said, “God be praised that I have not thought of plotting evil against you.”

entries.farsi: چون كه مردى نيست خنجرها چه سود / چون نباشد دل ندارد سود خود
entries.english: When there is no manliness, of what use are daggers? When there is no heart, the helmet avails not.

entries.farsi: از على ميراث دارى ذو الفقار / بازوى شير خدا هستت بيار
entries.english: You may have Dhu ’l-faqár as a heritage from ‘Alí, have you the arm of the Lion of God? Produce it!

entries.farsi: گر فسونى ياد دارى از مسيح / ‏كو لب و دندان عيسى اى وقيح
entries.english: Though you may remember an incantation derived from the Messiah, where are the lips and teeth of Jesus, O abominable man?

entries.farsi: كشتيى سازى ز توزيع و فتوح / ‏كو يكى مالح كشتى همچو نوح
entries.english: You may build a ship with money collected or freely given, where is a captain of the ship like Noah?

entries.farsi: بت شكستى گيرم ابراهيم‏وار / كو بت تن را فدى كردن به نار
entries.english: I grant you have broken the idol, like Abraham, what of devoting the idol, your body, to the fire?

entries.farsi: گر دليلت هست اندر فعل آر / تيغ چوبين را بدان كن ذو الفقار
entries.english: If you have the proof, put it into practice: by means of that make your wooden sword as Dhu ’l-faqár.

entries.farsi: آن دليلى كه ترا مانع شود / از عمل آن نقمت صانع بود
entries.english: The proof that hinders you from the practice is the vengeance of the Maker.

entries.farsi: خايفان راه را كردى دلير / از همه لرزان‏ترى تو زير زير
entries.english: You have emboldened those who are afraid of the Way, underneath you are more tremulous than all.

entries.farsi: بر همه درس توكل مى‏كنى / ‏در هوا تو پشه را رگ مى‏زنى
entries.english: You lecture to them all on trust in God; you are slitting the vein of the gnat in the air.

entries.farsi: اى مخنث پيش رفته از سپاه / ‏بر دروغ ريش تو كيرت گواه
entries.english: O hermaphrodite, going ahead of the army, your penis is witness to the falsehood of your beard (your false boasting).

entries.farsi: چون ز نامردى دل آگنده بود / ريش و سبلت موجب خنده بود
entries.english: When the heart is filled with unmanliness, the beard and moustache are a cause of laughter.

entries.farsi: توبه‏اى كن اشك باران چون مطر / ريش و سبلت را ز خنده باز خر
entries.english: Repent, shed tears like rain, and redeem your beard and moustache from laughter.

entries.farsi: داروى مردى بخور اندر عمل / ‏تا شوى خورشيد گرم اندر حمل
entries.english: Restore your manliness in works that you may become the hot sun in Aries.

entries.farsi: معده را بگذار و سوى دل خرام / ‏تا كه بى‏پرده ز حق آيد سالم
entries.english: Leave the belly and stride towards the heart, in order that the salutation may come to you from God without veil.

entries.farsi: يك دو گامى رو تكلف ساز خوش / ‏عشق گيرد گوش تو آن گاه كش
entries.english: Advance one or two paces, make a good endeavour: Love will lay hold of your ear and then draw.

sections.title_fa: ‏غالب شدن حيله‏ى روباه بر استعصام و تعفف خر و كشيدن روبه خر را سوى شير به بيشه
sections.title_en: How the cunning of the fox prevailed over the desire of the ass to preserve and restrain himself, and how the fox led the ass to the lion in the jungle.

entries.farsi: روبه اندر حيله پاى خود فشرد / ريش خر بگرفت و آن خر را ببرد
entries.english: The fox embarked on the plot: he seized the ass’s beard and led him away.

entries.farsi: مطرب آن خانقه كو تا كه تفت / ‏دف زند كه خر برفت و خر برفت
entries.english: Where is the musician of that Sufi monastery, that he may quickly play the tambourine, “The ass is gone, the ass is gone”?

entries.farsi: چون كه خرگوشى برد شيرى به چاه / ‏چون نيارد روبهى خر تا گياه
entries.english: Since a hare brings a lion to the well, how should not a fox lead an ass to the grass?

entries.farsi: گوش را بر بند و افسونها مخور / جز فسون آن ولى دادگر
entries.english: Shut your ear and do not swallow spells: but the spell of the righteous saint—

entries.farsi: آن فسون خوشتر از حلواى او / آن كه صد حلواست خاك پاى او
entries.english: That spell of his, sweeter than halwá, that of whose feet a hundred halva’s are the dust.

entries.farsi: خنبهاى خسروانى پر ز مى / ‏مايه برده از مى لبهاى وى
entries.english: The imperial jars full of the wine have drawn their stock from the wine of his lips.

entries.farsi: آب شيرين چون نبيند مرغ كور / چون نگردد گرد چشمه‏ى آب شور
entries.english: That alien soul which has never seen the wine of his ruby lips is a lover of the wine.

entries.farsi: عاشق مى باشد آن جان بعيد / كاو مى لبهاى لعلش را نديد
entries.english: Since the blind bird does not see the sweet water, how should not it circle round the brackish water?

entries.farsi: خسرو شيرين جان نوبت زده‏ست / ‏الجرم در شهر قند ارزان شده‏ست
entries.english: The spiritual Moses makes the breast a Sinai: he makes the blind parrots able to see.

entries.farsi: موسى جان سينه را سينا كند / طوطيان كور را بينا كند
entries.english: The Khusraw of the spiritual Shírín has beaten the drum; consequently sugar has become cheap in the city.

entries.farsi: اشتران مصر را رو سوى ما / بشنويد اى طوطيان بانگ درا
entries.english: The Josephs of the unseen world are marching: they are bringing bales of candy and sugar.

entries.farsi: يوسفان غيب لشكر مى‏كشند / تنگهاى قند و شكر مى‏كشند
entries.english: The faces of the camels of Egypt are towards us: listen, O parrots, to the sound of the camel-bell.

entries.farsi: شهر ما فردا پر از شكر شود / شكر ارزان است ارزان‏تر شود
entries.english: To-morrow our city will be filled with sugar; sugar is cheap: it will be cheaper.

entries.farsi: در شكر غلطيد اى حلواييان / ‏همچو طوطى كورى صفراييان
entries.english: O confectioners, wallow in sugar, like the parrot, in despite of the bilious ones.

entries.farsi: نيشكر كوبيد كار اين است و بس / ‏جان بر افشانيد يار اين است و بس
entries.english: Pound the sugar-cane: this is the only work; lavish your souls: this is the only Beloved.

entries.farsi: يك ترش در شهر ما اينك نماند / چون كه شيرين خسروان را بر نشاند
entries.english: Now not a single sour one is left in our city, since Shírín has seated the Khusraws on the throne.

entries.farsi: سركه‏ى نه ساله شيرين مى‏شود / سنگ و مرمر لعل و زرين مى‏شود
entries.english: It is dessert on dessert and wine on wine! Ho, go up on the minaret and proclaim that all are welcome.

entries.farsi: نقل بر نقل است و مى بر مى هال / بر مناره رو بزن بانگ صال
entries.english: The nine years old vinegar is becoming sweet; the stone and marble are becoming ruby like and golden.

entries.farsi: آفتاب اندر فلك دستك زنان / ‏ذره‏ها چون عاشقان بازى‏كنان
entries.english: The sun in heaven is clapping his hands: the motes are dancing like lovers.

entries.farsi: چشم دولت سحر مطلق مى‏كند / روح شد منصور انا الحق مى‏زند
entries.english: Eyes are intoxicated with the orchard abounding in greenery; the blossoms are budding on the boughs.

entries.farsi: چشمها مخمور شد از سبزه‏زار / گل شكوفه مى‏كند بر شاخسار
entries.english: The eye of blessedness works absolute magic: the spirit is made victorious (mansur), crying “I am God.”

entries.farsi: گر خرى را مى‏برد روبه ز سر / گو ببر تو خر مباش و غم مخور
entries.english: If the fox is seducing an ass, let him seduce! Do not you be an ass, and be not troubled.

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن شخص كه از ترس خويشتن را در خانه اى انداخت رخها زرد چون زعفران لبها كبود چون نيل دست لرزان چون برگ درخت، خداوند خانه پرسيد كه خير است چه واقعه است، گفت بيرون خر مى‏گيرند به سخره، گفت مبارك خر مى‏گيرند تو خر نيستى چه مى‏ترسى، گفت سخت به جد مى‏گيرند تمييز برخاسته است امروز ترسم كه مرا خر گيرند

sections.title_en: Story of the person who rushed into a house in terror, with cheeks yellow as saffron, lips blue as indigo, and hands trembling like the leaves of a tree. The master of the house asked, “Is all well? What is the matter?” He replied, “Outside they are taking asses by force.” “Bless you!” cried the other; “they are taking asses, you are not an ass: what are you afraid of?” He said, “They are taking
in a great hurry: discrimination has ceased. To-day I am afraid they will take me for an ass.”

entries.farsi: آن يكى در خانه‏اى در مى‏گريخت / ‏زرد رو و لب كبود و رنگ ريخت
entries.english: A certain man took refuge in a house: his face was yellow, his lips blue, and his colour had ebbed away.

entries.farsi: صاحب خانه بگفتش خير هست / ‏كه همى‏لرزد ترا چون پير دست
entries.english: The master of the house said to him, “Are you well? For your hand is trembling like an old man.

entries.farsi: واقعه چون است چون بگريختى / ‏رنگ رخساره چنين چون ريختى
entries.english: What has happened? Why have you taken refuge? How have you lost the colour of your face?

entries.farsi: گفت بهر سخره‏ى شاه حرون / ‏خر همى‏گيرند امروز از برون
entries.english: “To-day,” said he, “they are seizing asses outside to do forced labour for the tyrannical king.”

entries.farsi: گفت مى‏گيرند گو خر جان عم / ‏چون نه‏اى خر رو ترا زين چيست غم
entries.english: He replied, “O beloved of your uncle, they are taking it because it is an ass: since you are not an ass, go: why are you troubled at this?”

entries.farsi: گفت بس جدند و گرم اندر گرفت / ‏گر خرم گيرند هم نبود شگفت
entries.english: He answered, “They are very urgent and furious in taking: it will be no wonder if they take me too for an ass.

entries.farsi: بهر خر گيرى بر آوردند دست / ‏جد جد تمييز هم برخاسته‏ست
entries.english: They have put their hands with all their might to taking asses: accordingly discrimination has ceased.”

entries.farsi: چون كه بى‏تمييزيان‏مان سرورند / صاحب خر را به جاى خر برند
entries.english: Since undiscriminating persons are our rulers, they carry off the owner of the ass instead of the ass.

entries.farsi: نيست شاه شهر ما بى‏هوده گير / هست تمييزش سميع است و بصير
entries.english: The King of our city is not one who takes at random: He has discrimination, He is hearing and seeing.

entries.farsi: آدمى باش و ز خر گيران مترس / ‏خر نه‏اى اى عيسى دوران مترس
entries.english: Be a man and do not be afraid of those who take the asses: you are not an ass: be not afraid, O Jesus of the Time.

entries.farsi: تو ز چرخ و اختران هم برترى / ‏گر چه بهر مصلحت در آخورى
entries.english: The Fourth Heaven, moreover, is filled with your light: God forbid that the Stable is your abode.

entries.farsi: مير آخور ديگر و خر ديگر است / ‏نه هر آن كه اندر آخور شد خر است
entries.english: You are higher even than the sky and the stars; though for a good reason you are in the Stable.

entries.farsi: چرخ چارم هم ز نور تو پر است / ‏حاش هلل كه مقامت آخور است
entries.english: The Master of the Stable is one thing and the ass another: not everyone who has entered the Stable is an ass.

entries.farsi: چه در افتاديم در دنبال خر / از گلستان گوى و از گلهاى تر
entries.english: Why have we fallen in behind the ass? Tell of the Rose-garden and the fresh roses,

entries.farsi: از انار و از ترنج و شاخ سيب / ‏وز شراب و شاهدان بى‏حساب
entries.english: And of the pomegranate and the citron and the apple-bough, and of the wine and the fair youths innumerable,

entries.farsi: يا از آن دريا كه موجش گوهر است / ‏گوهرش گوينده و بيناور است
entries.english: Or of the Sea whose waves are pearls and whose pearls are speaking and seeing,

entries.farsi: يا از آن مرغان كه گل چين مى‏كنند / بيضه‏ها زرين و سيمين مى‏كنند
entries.english: Or of the Birds which pick roses and lay eggs of silver and gold,

entries.farsi: يا از آن بازان كه كبكان پرورند / هم نگون اشكم هم استان مى‏پرند
entries.english: Or of the Falcons which foster the partridges and fly both with their bellies turned downward and also on their backs.

entries.farsi: نردبانهايى است پنهان در جهان / ‏پايه پايه تا عنان آسمان
entries.english: In the world there are invisible ladders, step by step up to the summit of heaven.

entries.farsi: هر گره را نردبانى ديگر است / ‏هر روش را آسمانى ديگر است
entries.english: There is a different ladder for every class; there is a different heaven for every traveller’s way.

entries.farsi: هر يكى از حال ديگر بى‏خبر / ملك با پهنا و بى‏پايان و سر
entries.english: Everyone is ignorant of another’s condition the kingdom wide and without end or beginning.

entries.farsi: اين در آن حيران كه او از چيست خوش / ‏و آن در اين خيره كه حيرت چيستش
entries.english: This one is amazed at that one and asks wherefore he is happy, while that one is astounded at this one and asks why he is amazed.

entries.farsi: صحن ارض اللهَّ واسع آمده / ‏هر درختى از زمينى سر زده
entries.english: The area of God’s earth is spacious: every tree springs up from a certain soil.

entries.farsi: بر درختان شكر گويان برگ و شاخ / ‏كه زهى ملك و زهى عرصه‏ى فراخ
entries.english: The leaves and boughs on the trees are giving thanks, crying, “Oh, what a fine kingdom! Oh, what a broad expanse!”

entries.farsi: بلبالن گرد شكوفه پر گره / ‏كه از آن چه مى‏خورى ما را بده
entries.english: The nightingales are round the knobby blossom, saying, “Give us some of what you drink.”

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد كن رجوع / ‏سوى آن روباه و شير و سقم و جوع
entries.english: This discourse has no end: return to the fox and the lion and the sickness and hunger.

sections.title_fa: بردن روباه آن خر را پيش شير و جستن خر از شير و عتاب كردن روباه با شير كه هنوز خر دور ‏بود تعجيل كردى، و عذر گفتن شير و البه كردن روبه را شير كه برو بار ديگرش بفريب
sections.title_en: How the fox brought the ass to the lion, and how the ass jumped away from the lion, and how the fox reproached the lion, saying, “The ass was still far off: you were too hasty”;
and how the lion made excuses and entreated the fox to go and trick him a second time.

entries.farsi: چون كه بر كوهش بسوى مرج برد / تا كند شيرش به حمله خرد و مرد
entries.english: When he brought him up the hill towards the meadow, in order that the lion might pulverise him with a charge,

entries.farsi: دور بود از شير و آن شير از نبرد / گنبدى كرد از بلندى شير هول
entries.english: He was far from the lion, but the lion would not wait for him to come near before attacking.

entries.farsi: ‏خود نبودش قوت و امكان حول / خر ز دورش ديد و برگشت و گريز
entries.english: The terrible lion made a spring from an eminence, indeed he had not the strength and power to move.

entries.farsi: تا به زير كوه تازان نعل ريز / گفت روبه شير را اى شاه ما
entries.english: The ass saw him from afar and turned and fled to the bottom of the hill, dropping his shoes as he ran.

entries.farsi: چون نكردى صبر در وقت وغا / تا به نزديك تو آيد آن غوى
entries.english: “O king of us,” said the fox to the lion, “why did not you restrain yourself in the hour of battle,

entries.farsi: ‏تا به اندك حمله اى غالب شوى / مكر شيطان است تعجيل و شتاب
entries.english: In order that that misguided might come near you and that you might vanquish him with a small attack?

entries.farsi: ‏لطف رحمان است صبر و احتساب / دور بود و حمله را ديد و گريخت
entries.english: Precipitation and haste is the Devil’s wile; patience and calculation is God’s grace.

entries.farsi: ضعف تو ظاهر شد و آب تو ريخت / گفت من پنداشتم بر جاست زور
entries.english: He was far off and saw the attack and fled: your weakness is made manifest and your prestige is destroyed.”

entries.farsi: تا بدين حد مى‏ندانستم فتور / نيز جوع و حاجتم از حد گذشت
entries.english: He replied, “I thought my strength was restored: I did not know my feebleness was so great.

entries.farsi: ‏صبر و عقلم از تجوع ياوه گشت / گر توانى بار ديگر از خرد
entries.english: Moreover, my hunger and need had passed beyond bounds: through starvation my patience and understanding had been lost.

entries.farsi: باز آوردن مر او را مسترد / منت بسيار دارم از تو من
entries.english: If by your wits you can reclaim him and bring him back once more,

entries.farsi: بر دل او از عمى مهرى نهد / پس فراموشش شود هولى كه ديد
entries.english: I shall be much obliged to you: try hard, maybe you will fetch him by cunning.”

entries.farsi: ‏جهد كن باشد بيارى‏اش به فن / گفت آرى گر خدا يارى دهد
entries.english: “Yes,” said the fox, “if God should help me and set a seal of blindness on his heart

entries.farsi: از خرى او نباشد اين بعيد / ليك چون آرم من او را بر متاز
entries.english: Then he will forget the terror which he felt on seeing: this will not be alien to his asininity.

entries.farsi: تا به بادش ندهى از تعجيل باز / گفت آرى تجربه كردم كه من
entries.english: But when I bring him, do not you rush, lest you lose him again by overhaste.”

entries.farsi: ‏سخت رنجورم مخلخل گشته تن / تا به نزديكم نيايد خر تمام
entries.english: “Yes,” replied the lion; “I have found by experience that I am very ill and that my body has become shaky.

entries.farsi: ‏من نجنبم خفته باشم در قوام / رفت روبه گفت اى شه همتى
entries.english: Until the ass comes quite near to me, I will not move, I will be sound asleep.”

entries.farsi: ‏تا بپوشد عقل او را غفلتى / توبه‏ها كرده است خر با كردگار
entries.english: The fox departed, saying, “O king, a prayer that heedlessness may muffle his reason.

entries.farsi: كه نگردد غره‏ى هر نابكار / توبه‏هايش را به فن بر هم زنيم
entries.english: The ass has made vows of repentance to the Creator that he will not be duped by any ne’er-do-well.

entries.farsi: ‏ما عدوى عقل و عهد روشنيم / كله‏ى خر گوى فرزندان ماست
entries.english: We by cunning will cause his vows to collapse; we are the enemy of reason and of the splendid covenant.

entries.farsi: ‏فكرتش بازيچه‏ى دستان ماست / عقل كان باشد ز دوران زحل
entries.english: The ass’s head is a ball for our children: his thought is a plaything for our guile.”

entries.farsi: ‏پيش عقل كل ندارد آن محل / از عطارد وز زحل دانا شد او
entries.english: The reason that belongs to the revolution of Saturn has no position in the sight of Universal Reason.

entries.farsi: ما ز داد كردگار لطف خو / عَلَّمَ الإِْنْسانَ خم طغراى ماست
entries.english: It is made knowing by Mercury and Saturn; we by the bounty of the gracious Creator.

entries.farsi: ‏علم عند اللهَّ مقصدهاى ماست / تربيه آن آفتاب روشنيم
entries.english: The twisted script of our sign-manual is He taught Man: our aims are the knowledge is with God.

entries.farsi: در رسد شومى اشكستش در او
entries.english: We are the nurture of that resplendent Sun: on that account we are crying, “Glory to my Lord the Supreme!”

entries.farsi: ‏بشكند صد تجربه زين دمدمه / بو كه توبه بشكند آن سست خو
entries.english: “If he possesses experience, nevertheless a hundred experiences will be shattered by my deceitful idle talk.

entries.farsi: ‏ربى الاعلى از آن رو مى‏زنيم / تجربه گر دارد او با اين همه
entries.english: Maybe that weak-natured one will break his repentance, and the bad luck of his breaking it will overtake him.”

sections.title_fa: در بيان آن كه نقض عهد و توبه موجب بال بود بلكه موجب مسخ است چنان كه در حق اصحاب سبت و در حق اصحاب مايده‏ى عيسى كه وَ جَعَلَ مِنْهُمُ الْقِرَدَةَ وَ الْخَنازِيرَ، و اندر اين امت مسخ دل باشد و به قيامت تن را صورت دل دهند

sections.title_en: Explaining that the violation of a covenant and repentance is the cause of affliction; no, it is the cause of metamorphosis, as in the case of the “Fellows of the Sabbath” and in the case of the “Fellows who disbelieved in the miracle of the Table of Jesus,” for, “And He turned them into apes and swine.” And in this community there is metamorphosis
of the spirit, but at the Resurrection the form of the spirit will be given to the body.

entries.farsi: نقض ميثاق و شكست توبه‏ها / موجب لعنت شود در انتها
entries.english: To violate a pact and break vows of repentance becomes the cause of accursedness in the end.

entries.farsi: نقض توبه و عهد آن اصحاب سبت / ‏موجب مسخ آمد و اهالك و مقت
entries.english: The violation of vows of repentance by the “Fellows of the Sabbath” became the cause of their metamorphosis and destruction and abomination.

entries.farsi: پس خدا آن قوم را بوزينه كرد / چون كه عهد حق شكستند از نبرد
entries.english: Therefore God turned those people into apes, since they rebelliously broke their covenant with God.

entries.farsi: اندر اين امت نبد مسخ بدن / ‏ليك مسخ دل بود اى ذو الفطن
entries.english: In this community there has never been metamorphosis of the body, but there is metamorphosis of the spirit, O man endowed with perception.

entries.farsi: چون دل بوزينه گردد آن دلش / ‏از دل بوزينه شد خوار آن گلش
entries.english: When his spirit becomes the ape-spirit, his clay is debased by the ape-spirit.

entries.farsi: گر هنر بودى دلش را ز اختبار / خوار كى بودى ز صورت آن حمار
entries.english: How should the ass be debased by his form, if his spirit had possessed the virtue from experience?

entries.farsi: آن سگ اصحاب خوش بد سيرتش / ‏هيچ بودش منقصت ز آن صورتش
entries.english: The dog of the Companions had a goodly character: was he worse on account of his form?

entries.farsi: از ره سر صد هزاران دگر / گشته از توبه شكستن خوك و خر
entries.english: The “Fellows of the Sabbath” suffered outward metamorphosis, in order that the people might behold outwardly their ignominious fall.

entries.farsi: مسخ ظاهر بود اهل سبت را / تا ببيند خلق ظاهر كبت را
entries.english: Through breaking repentance a hundred thousand others have become hogs and asses inwardly.

sections.title_fa: ‏دوم بار آمدن روبه بر آن خر گريخته تا باز بفريبدش
sections.title_en: How the fox approached the runaway ass a second time in order to beguile him once more.

entries.farsi: پس بيامد زود روبه سوى خر / گفت خر از چون تو يارى الحذر
entries.english: Then the fox came quickly towards the ass: the ass said, “One must beware of a friend like you.

entries.farsi: ناجوانمردا چه كردم من ترا / كه به پيش اژدها بردى مرا
entries.english: Ignoble creature, what did I do to you that you brought me into the presence of a dragon?

entries.farsi: موجب كين تو با جانم چه بود / غير خبث جوهر تو اى عنود
entries.english: What but the malignity of your nature was the cause of your enmity to my life, O perverse one?”—

entries.farsi: همچو كژدم كاو گزد پاى فتى / ‏نارسيده از وى او را زحمتى
entries.english: Like the scorpion, which bites a man’s foot though no harm has come to it from him?

entries.farsi: يا چو ديوى كاو عدوى جان ماست / ‏نارسيده زحمتش از ما و كاست
entries.english: Or like the Devil who is the enemy of our souls, though no inconvenience or injury has befallen him from us;

entries.farsi: بلكه طبعا خصم جان آدمى است / ‏از هالك آدمى در خرمى است
entries.english: No, but he is naturally the adversary of the human soul and rejoices at the destruction of Man;

entries.farsi: از پى هر آدمى او نسكلد / خو و طبع زشت خود او كى هلد
entries.english: He never breaks off his pursuit of any human being: how should he abandon his wicked disposition and nature?

entries.farsi: ز انكه خبث ذات او بى‏موجبى / ‏هست سوى ظلم و عدوان جاذبى
entries.english: For, without any cause, his essential malignity pulls him on to injustice and tyranny.

entries.farsi: هر زمان خواند ترا تا خرگهى / كه در اندازد ترا اندر چهى
entries.english: He continually invites you to a spacious tent in order that he may cast you into a pit,

entries.farsi: كه فالن جا حوض آب است و عيون / ‏تا در اندازد به حوضت سر نگون
entries.english: Saying, “In such and such a place there is a tank of water and fountains,” that he may cast you headlong into the tank.

entries.farsi: آدمى را با همه وحى و نظر / اندر افكند آن لعين در شور و شر
entries.english: That accursed one caused an Adam, notwithstanding all his inspiration and insight, to fall into woe and bane,

entries.farsi: بى‏گناهى بى‏گزند سابقى / ‏كه رسد او را ز آدم ناحقى
entries.english: Without any sin and without any previous harm having been wrongfully done to him by Adam.

entries.farsi: گفت روبه آن طلسم سحر بود / كه ترا در چشم آن شيرى نمود
entries.english: The fox replied, “It was a spell of magic that appeared in your eyes as a lion;

entries.farsi: ور نه من از تو به تن مسكين‏ترم / ‏كه شب و روز اندر آن جا مى‏چرم
entries.english: Else I am punier in body than you, and I always feed there by night and day.

entries.farsi: گر نه ز آن گونه طلسمى ساختى / ‏هر شكم خوارى بدان جا تاختى
entries.english: If he had not wrought a spell of that kind, every famishing would have run there.

entries.farsi: يك جهان بى‏نوا پر پيل و ارج / ‏بى‏طلسمى كى بماندى سبز مرج
entries.english: In a foodless world full of elephants and rhinoceroses how should the meadow have remained verdant without the protection of a spell?

entries.farsi: من ترا خود خواستم گفتن به درس / ‏كه چنان هولى اگر بينى مترس
entries.english: Truly, I meant to tell you, by way of instruction, not to be afraid if you should see a terrible thing like that;

entries.farsi: ليك رفت از ياد علم آموزى‏ات / ‏كه بدم مستغرق دل سوزى‏ات
entries.english: But I forgot to impart knowledge to you, because I was overwhelmed with grief and pity on your account.

entries.farsi: ديدمت در جوع كلب و بى‏نوا / مى‏شتابيدم كه آيى تا دوا
entries.english: I saw you were ravenously hungry and without food, I was making haste so that you might attain to the remedy;

entries.farsi: ور نه با تو گفتمى شرح طلسم / ‏كان خيالى مى‏نمايد نيست جسم
entries.english: Otherwise I would have explained the spell to you: it presents itself as an apparition, it is not a body.”

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن خر روباه را
sections.title_en: The reply of the ass to the fox.

entries.farsi: گفت رو رو هين ز پيشم اى عدو / تا نبينم روى تو اى زشت رو
entries.english: “Listen,” cried the ass, “go, go from my presence, O enemy, that I may not see your face, O ugly one!

entries.farsi: آن خدايى كه ترا بد بخت كرد / روى زشتت را كريه و سخت كرد
entries.english: That God who made you ill-fated has made your ugly face detestable and impudent.

entries.farsi: با كدامين روى مى‏آيى به من / ‏اين چنين سغرى ندارد كرگدن
entries.english: With what face do you come to me? The rhinoceros has not such a hard skin.

entries.farsi: رفته‏اى در خون جانم آشكار / كه ترا من ره برم تا مرغزار
entries.english: You manifestly attempted to shed my life-blood, saying, ‘I will guide you to the meadow,’

entries.farsi: تا بديدم روى عزراييل را / باز آوردى فن و تسويل را
entries.english: So that I beheld the face of Azrael; again you have brought cunning and plausible suggestion.

entries.farsi: گر چه من ننگ خرانم يا خرم / ‏جانورم جان دارم اين را كى خرم
entries.english: Though I am a disgrace to the asses or an ass, I am possessed of life, I have a vital spirit: how should I purchase this?

entries.farsi: آن چه من ديدم ز هول بى‏امان / ‏طفل ديدى پير گشتى در زمان
entries.english: If a child had seen the pitiless horror that I saw, it would instantly have become old.

entries.farsi: بى‏دل و جان از نهيب آن شكوه / ‏سر نگون خود را در افگندم ز كوه
entries.english: Deprived of heart and soul by dread of that awful object, I threw myself headlong from the mountain.

entries.farsi: بسته شد پايم در آن دم از نهيب / ‏چون بديدم آن عذاب بى‏حجاب
entries.english: My legs were tied by terror as soon as I perceived that torment without barrier.

entries.farsi: عهد كردم با خدا كاى ذو المنن / ‏بر گشا زين بستگى تو پاى من
entries.english: I made a promise to God, crying, ‘O gracious One, do You free my legs from this bondage,

entries.farsi: تا ننوشم وسوسه‏ى كس بعد از اين / ‏عهد كردم نذر كردم اى معين
entries.english: So that henceforth I may not listen to any one’s temptation: I promise, I vow, O Helper!’

entries.farsi: حق گشاده كرد آن دم پاى من / ‏ز آن دعا و زارى و ايماى من
entries.english: Thereupon God loosed my legs because of my prayer and humble entreaty and indication;

entries.farsi: ور نه اندر من رسيدى شير نر / چون بدى در زير پنجه‏ى شير خر
entries.english: Else the fierce lion would have overtaken me: how would an ass have fared in the grip of a lion?

entries.farsi: باز بفرستادت آن شير عرين / ‏سوى من از مكر اى بئس القرين
entries.english: Now the lion of the jungle has sent you to me again for the purpose of deceit, O evil companion that you are!”

entries.farsi: حق ذات پاك اللهَُّ الصمد / كه بود به مار بد از يار بد
entries.english: I swear by the truth of the Holy Person of Allah, the Lord, that a malign snake is better than a malign friend.

entries.farsi: مار بد جانى ستاند از سليم / ‏يار بد آرد سوى نار مقيم
entries.english: The malign snake takes a soul from the man it has bitten; the malign friend leads him into the everlasting Fire.

entries.farsi: از قرين بى‏قول و گفت و گوى او / خو بدزدد دل نهان از خوى او
entries.english: Your heart secretly steals its disposition from the disposition of your companion, without speech and talk on his part.

entries.farsi: چون كه او افكند بر تو سايه را / دزدد آن بى‏مايه از تو مايه را
entries.english: When he casts his shadow over you, that unprincipled one steals away your principles from you.

entries.farsi: عقل تو گر اژدهايى گشت مست / ‏يار بد او را زمرد دان كه هست
entries.english: If your reason has become a furious dragon, know that the evil companion is an emerald to it.

entries.farsi: ديده‏ى عقلت بدو بيرون جهد / طعن اوت اندر كف طاعون نهد
entries.english: Through him the eye of your reason starts out: his thrusts deliver you into the hands of pestilence.

sections.title_fa: جواب گفتن روباه خر را
sections.title_en: The answer of the fox to the ass.

entries.farsi: گفت روبه صاف ما را درد نيست / ‏ليك تخييالت وهمى خرد نيست
entries.english: The fox said, “There are no dregs in my pure liquor, but the illusions of imagination are not small.

entries.farsi: اين همه وهم تو است اى ساده دل / ‏ور نه بر تو نه غشى دارم نه غل
entries.english: All this is your imagination, O simpleton, for I bear no malice and rancour against you.

entries.farsi: از خيال زشت خود منگر به من / ‏بر محبان از چه دارى سوء ظن
entries.english: Do not regard me from your evil fancy: wherefore do you cherish ill thoughts against your lovers?

entries.farsi: ظن نيكو بر بر اخوان صفا / گر چه آيد ظاهر از ايشان جفا
entries.english: Think well of the sincere, even though unkindness come from them in appearance.

entries.farsi: اين خيال و وهم بد چون شد پديد / صد هزاران يار را از هم بريد
entries.english: When this evil fancy and imagination is manifested, it severs a hundred thousand friends from one another.

entries.farsi: مشفقى گر كرد جور و امتحان / ‏عقل بايد كه نباشد بد گمان
entries.english: If an affectionate has behaved unjustly and made a trial, understanding is needed to prevent one from thinking ill.

entries.farsi: خاصه من بد رگ نبودم زشت اسم / ‏آن كه ديدى بد نبد بود آن طلسم
entries.english: In particular, I, who have a bad name, was not evil-natured: what you saw was nothing evil; it was a magic spell;

entries.farsi: ور بدى بد آن سگالش قد را / عفو فرمايند ياران ز آن خطا
entries.english: And if, hypothetically, that purpose had been evil, friends pardon such a fault.”

entries.farsi: عالم وهم و خيال طمع و بيم / ‏هست رهرو را يكى سدى عظيم
entries.english: The world of imagination and the phantom of hope and fear is a great obstacle to the traveller.

entries.farsi: قشهاى اين خيال نقش بند / چون خليلى را كه كه بد شد گزند
entries.english: The pictures of this picture-making phantasy were harmful to one like Abraham, who was a mountain.

entries.farsi: گفت هذا رَبِّي ابراهيم راد / چون كه اندر عالم وهم اوفتاد
entries.english: The noble Abraham said, “This is my Lord,” when he fell into the world of imagination.

entries.farsi: ذكر كوكب را چنين تاويل گفت / ‏آن كسى كه گوهر تاويل سفت
entries.english: That person who bored the pearl of interpretation, interpreted the mention of the star thus—

entries.farsi: عالم وهم و خيال چشم بند / آن چنان كه را ز جاى خويش كند
entries.english: The world of imagination and blinding phantasy uprooted such a mountain from its foundation,

entries.farsi: تا كه هذا رَبِّي آمد قال او / خربط و خر را چه باشد حال او
entries.english: So that the words, “This is my Lord,” were uttered by him: what, must be the case with a goose or an ass?

entries.farsi: غرق گشته عقلهاى چون جبال / ‏در بحار وهم و گرداب خيال
entries.english: Understandings as mountains have been submerged in the seas of imagination and the whirlpools of phantasy.

entries.farsi: كوهها را هست زين طوفان فضوح / ‏كو امانى جز كه در كشتى نوح
entries.english: Mountains are put to shame by this Flood: where is any safety but in Noah’s Ark?

entries.farsi: زين خيال ره زن راه يقين / ‏گشت هفتاد و دو ملت اهل دين
entries.english: By this phantasy, which infests the road of Faith like a brigand, the followers of the Religion have become two and seventy sects.

entries.farsi: مرد ايقان رست از وهم و خيال / ‏موى ابرو را نمى‏گويد هالل
entries.english: The man of sure faith is delivered from imagination and phantasy: he does not call a hair of the eyebrow the new moon,

entries.farsi: و آنكه نور عمرش نبود سند / موى ابروى كژى راهش زند
entries.english: While he that has not the light of Umar as his support is waylaid by a crooked hair of the eyebrow.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران كشتى با هول و سهم / ‏تخته تخته گشته در درياى وهم
entries.english: A hundred thousand awful and terrible ships have been shattered to pieces in the sea of imagination.

entries.farsi: كمترين فرعون چيست فيلسوف / ‏ماه او در برج وهمى در خسوف
entries.english: The least the energetic and ingenious Pharaoh: his moon was eclipsed in the mansion of imagination.

entries.farsi: كس نداند روسپى زن كيست آن / ‏وان كه داند نيستش بر خود گمان
entries.english: Nobody knows who the cuckold is, and he that knows has no doubt concerning himself.

entries.farsi: چون ترا وهم تو دارد خيره سر / از چه گردى گرد وهم آن دگر
entries.english: Since yours own imagination keeps you giddy-headed, wherefore should you revolve round the imagination of another?

entries.farsi: عاجزم من از منى خويشتن / ‏چه نشستى پر منى تو پيش من
entries.english: I am helpless against my own egoism: why have you, full of egoism, sat down beside me?

entries.farsi: بى‏من و مايى همى‏جويم به جان / ‏تا شوم من گوى آن خوش صولجان
entries.english: I am seeking with my soul one who is free from egoism, that I may become the ball of that goodly bat.

entries.farsi: هر كه بى‏من شد همه منها خود اوست / ‏دوست جمله شد چو خود را نيست دوست
entries.english: In truth anyone who has become without ego is all egos: when he is not loved by himself he becomes loved by all.

entries.farsi: آينه بى‏نقش شد يابد بها / ز انكه شد حاكى جمله‏ى نقشها
entries.english: When a mirror becomes devoid of images, it gains splendour because it is the reporter of all images.

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت شيخ محمد سر رزى غزنوى قدس اللهَّ سره
sections.title_en: Story of Shaykh Muhammad Sar-razí of Ghazna, May God sanctify his spirit!

entries.farsi: زاهدى در غزنى از دانش مزى / ‏بد محمد نام و كنيت سر رزى
entries.english: In Ghazna there was an ascetic, abounding in knowledge: his name was Muhammad and his title Sar-razí.

entries.farsi: بود افطارش سر رز هر شبى / ‏هفت سال او دايم اندر مطلبى
entries.english: Every night he would break his fast with vine-tendrils (sar-i raz): during seven years he was continually in one quest.

entries.farsi: بس عجايب ديد از شاه وجود / ليك مقصودش جمال شاه بود
entries.english: He experienced many marvellous things from the King of existence, but his object was the beauty of the King.

entries.farsi: بر سر كه رفت آن از خويش سير / گفت بنما يا فتادم من به زير
entries.english: That man who was surfeited with himself went to the top of a mountain and said, “Appear, or I will fall to the bottom.”

entries.farsi: گفت نامد مهلت آن مكرمت / ‏ور فرو افتى نميرى نكشمت
entries.english: He said, “The time for that favour is not come, and if you fall down, you will not die: I will not kill you.”

entries.farsi: او فرو افكند خود را از وداد / در ميان عمق آبى اوفتاد
entries.english: He, from love, threw himself down: he fell into the depths of a body of water.

entries.farsi: چون نمرد از نكس آن جان سير مرد / از فراق مرگ بر خود نوحه كرد
entries.english: When he was not dead, on account of the shock that man who was sick of life made lament over himself for having been parted from death;

entries.farsi: كاين حيات او را چو مرگى مى‏نمود / كار پيشش باژگونه گشته بود
entries.english: For this life seemed to him like a death: in his view the thing had become reversed.

entries.farsi: موت را از غيب مى‏كرد او كدى / ‏ان فى موتى حياتى مى‏زدى
entries.english: He was begging death from the Unseen, he was crying, “Verily, my life is in my death.”

entries.farsi: موت را چون زندگى قابل شده / ‏با هالك جان خود يكدل شده
entries.english: He had embraced death as life, he had become in full accord with the destruction of his life.

entries.farsi: سيف و خنجر چون على ريحان او / نرگس و نسرين عدوى جان او
entries.english: As Alí, the sword and dagger were his sweet basil, the narcissus and eglantine were his soul’s enemies.

entries.farsi: بانگ آمد رو ز صحرا سوى شهر / بانگ طرفه از وراى سر و جهر
entries.english: A Voice came, “Go from the desert to the city”— a wondrous Voice transcending the occult and the manifest.

entries.farsi: گفت اى داناى رازم مو به مو / چه كنم در شهر از خدمت بگو
entries.english: He cried, “O You that know my secret, hair by hair, tell me, what service am I to do in the city?”

entries.farsi: گفت خدمت آن كه بهر ذل نفس / ‏خويش را سازى تو چون عباس دبس
entries.english: The Voice said, “The service is this, that for the purpose of self abasement you should make yourself Abbas (the seller) of date-syrup.

entries.farsi: مدتى از اغنيا زر مى‏ستان / ‏پس به درويشان مسكين مى‏رسان
entries.english: For a while take money from the rich and then deliver it to the lowly poor.

entries.farsi: خدمتت اين است تا يك چند گاه / ‏گفت سمعا طاعه اى جان پناه
entries.english: This is the service you must do for some time.” He replied, “To hear is to obey, O You who art my soul’s refuge.”

entries.farsi: بس سؤال و بس جواب و ماجرا / ‏بد ميان زاهد و رب الورى
entries.english: Many questions and answers and much conversation passed between the ascetic and the Lord of mankind,

entries.farsi: كه زمين و آسمان پر نور شد / در مقالات آن همه مذكور شد
entries.english: Whereby earth and heaven were filled with light: all that is recorded in the Maqálát;

entries.farsi: ليك كوته كردم آن گفتار را / تا ننوشد هر خسى اسرار را
entries.english: But I will cut short that dialogue, in order that every worthless person may not hear mysteries.

sections.title_fa: آمدن شيخ بعد از چندين سال از بيابان به شهر غزنين و زنبيل گردانيدن به اشارت غيبى و تفرقه كردن آن چه جمع آيد بر فقرا ‏هر كه را جان عز لبيك است نامه بر نامه پيك بر پيك است چنان كه روزن خانه باز باشد آفتاب و ماهتاب و باران و نامه و غيره منقطع نباشد
