entries.english: If he seizes the stone, it is because of his anger against you; for you are far off and he has no means of getting at you.

entries.farsi: ‏كه تو دورى و ندارد بر تو دست / عقل حيوانى چو دانست اختيار
entries.english: Since the animal intelligence is conscious of the power of choice, do not, O human intelligence, hold this Necessitarian doctrine. Be ashamed!

entries.farsi: آن خورنده چشم مى‏بندد ز نور / چون كه كلى ميل او نان خوردنى است
entries.english: This power of choice is manifest, but in his desire for the meal taken before dawn that eater shuts his eyes to the light.

entries.farsi: ‏رو به تاريكى نهد كه روز نيست / حرص چون خورشيد را پنهان كند
entries.english: Since all his desire is for eating bread, he sets his face towards the darkness, saying, it is not day.’

entries.farsi: چه عجب گر پشت بر برهان كند
entries.english: Inasmuch as greed causes the sun to be hidden, what wonder if he turns his back on the convincing proof?

sections.title_fa: ‏حكايت هم در بيان تقرير اختيار خلق و بيان آن كه تقدير و قضا سلب كننده‏ى اختيار نيست
sections.title_en: A Story illustrating and confirming the view that mankind have the power of choice, and showing that Pre-ordination and Predestination do not annul the power of choice.

entries.farsi: گفت دزدى شحنه را كاى پادشاه / ‏آن چه كردم بود آن حكم اله
entries.english: A thief said to the magistrate, ‘O king that which I have done was decreed by God.’

entries.farsi: گفت شحنه آن چه من هم مى‏كنم / ‏حكم حق است اى دو چشم روشنم
entries.english: The magistrate replied, ‘That which I am doing is also decreed by God, O light of my eyes.’

entries.farsi: از دكانى گر كسى تربى برد / كاين ز حكم ايزد است اى با خرد
entries.english: If anyone takes a radish from a shop, saying, ‘This is decreed by God, O man of understanding,’

entries.farsi: بر سرش كوبى دو سه مشت اى كره / ‏حكم حق است اين كه اينجا باز نه
entries.english: The greengrocer will give him two or three blows on the head with your fist, ‘O detestable man, this beating is God’s decree that you put the radish back.’

entries.farsi: در يكى تره چو اين عذر اى فضول / ‏مى‏نيايد پيش بقالى قبول
entries.english: Since this excuse, O trifler, is not accepted by a greengrocer in the case of a single vegetable,

entries.farsi: چون بر اين عذر اعتمادى مى‏كنى / ‏بر حوالى اژدهايى مى‏تنى
entries.english: How are you placing a reliance on this excuse and frequenting the neighbourhood of a dragon?

entries.farsi: از چنين عذر اى سليم نانبيل / ‏خون و مال و زن همه كردى سبيل
entries.english: By an excuse like this, O ignoble simpleton, you sacrifice all— your life, your property, and your wife;

entries.farsi: هر كسى پس سبلت تو بر كند / عذر آرد خويش را مضطر كند
entries.english: Afterwards everyone will pluck your moustache and offer excuse and make himself out to be acting under compulsion.

entries.farsi: حكم حق گر عذر مى‏شايد ترا / پس بياموز و بده فتوى مرا
entries.english: If ‘the decree of God’ seems to you a proper excuse, then instruct me and give me a canonical decision;

entries.farsi: كه مرا صد آرزو و شهوت است / ‏دست من بسته ز بيم و هيبت است
entries.english: For I have a hundred desires and lusts, my hand is tied by fear and awe.

entries.farsi: پس كرم كن عذر را تعليم ده / ‏برگشا از دست و پاى من گره
entries.english: Do me a favour, then: teach me the excuse, untie the knots from my hands and feet!

entries.farsi: اختيارى كرده‏اى تو پيشه‏اى / ‏كاختيارى دارم و انديشه‏اى
entries.english: You have chosen a handicraft, saying, ‘I have a choice and a thought.’

entries.farsi: ور نه چون بگزيده‏اى آن پيشه را / از ميان پيشه‏ها اى كدخدا
entries.english: Otherwise, how have you chosen that handicraft out of all the rest, O master of the house?

entries.farsi: چون كه آيد نوبت نفس و هوا / بيست مرده اختيار آيد ترا
entries.english: When the hour comes for the flesh and the passions, there comes to you as great a power of choice as is possessed by twenty men;

entries.farsi: چون برد يك حبه از تو يار سود / اختيار جنگ در جانت گشود
entries.english: When your friend deprives you of a penny of profit, the power to pick a quarrel is developed in your soul;

entries.farsi: چون بيايد نوبت شكر نعم / ‏اختيارت نيست وز سنگى تو كم
entries.english: When the hour comes for thanksgiving on account of benefactions, you have no power of choice and are inferior to a stone.

entries.farsi: دوزخت را عذر اين باشد يقين / ‏كاندر اين سوزش مرا معذور بين
entries.english: Assuredly this will be the excuse of your Hell, ‘Consider me excused for this burning!’

entries.farsi: كس بدين حجت چو معذورت نداشت / ‏وز كف جالد اين دورت نداشت
entries.english: Since no one holds you excusable on this plea, and this plea does not keep you out of the hands of the executioner,

entries.farsi: پس بدين داور جهان منظوم شد / حال آن عالم همت معلوم شد
entries.english: Then, the world is arranged according to this rule, and the state of things in yonder world too is made known to you.

sections.title_fa: حكايت هم در جواب جبرى و اثبات اختيار و صحت امر و نهى و بيان آن كه عذر جبرى در هيچ ملتى و در هيچ دينى مقبول نيست و موجب خالص نيست از سزاى آن كار كه كرده است چنان كه خالص نيافت ابليس جبرى بدان كه گفت بِما أَغْ وَيْتَنِي، و القليل يدل على الكثير

sections.title_en: Another Story in answer to the Necessitarian, confirming Man’s power of choice and the validity of the commands and prohibitions, and showing that the Necessitarian’s excuse is not accepted in any religious sect or in any religion and that it does not save him from being duly punished for the actions which he has committed, just as the Necessitarian Iblís was not saved by saying,
‘Because You have made me to err.’ And the little indicates the much.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مى‏رفت بالاى درخت / ‏مى‏فشاند آن ميوه را دزدانه سخت
entries.english: A certain man was climbing up a tree and vigorously scattering the fruit in the pounder of thieves.

entries.farsi: صاحب باغ آمد و گفت اى دنى / ‏از خدا شرميت كو چه مى‏كنى
entries.english: The owner of the orchard came along and said, ‘O rascal, where is your reverence for God? What are you doing?’

entries.farsi: گفت از باغ خدا بنده‏ى خدا / گر خورد خرما كه حق كردش عطا
entries.english: He replied, ‘If a servant of God eats from God’s orchard the dates which God has bestowed upon him as a gift,

entries.farsi: عاميانه چه مالمت مى‏كنى / ‏بخل بر خوان خداوند غنى
entries.english: Why do you vulgarly blame? Stinginess at the table of the all-Rich Lord!’

entries.farsi: گفت اى ايبك بياور آن رسن / ‏تا بگويم من جواب بو الحسن
entries.english: ‘O Aybak,’ said he, ‘fetch that rope, that I may give my answer to Bu ’l-Hasan.’

entries.farsi: پس ببستش سخت آن دم بر درخت / ‏مى‏زد او بر پشت و ساقش چوب سخت
entries.english: Then at once he bound him tightly to the tree and thrashed him hard on the back and legs with a cudgel.

entries.farsi: گفت آخر از خدا شرمى بدار / مى‏كشى اين بى‏گنه را زار زار
entries.english: He cried, ‘Pray, have some reverence for God! You are killing me miserably who am innocent.’

entries.farsi: گفت از چوب خدا اين بنده‏اش / ‏مى‏زند بر پشت ديگر بنده خوش
entries.english: He answered, ‘With God’s cudgel this servant of His is soundly beating the back of another servant.

entries.farsi: چوب حق و پشت و پهلو آن او / من غالم و آلت فرمان او
entries.english: It is God’s cudgel, and the back and sides belong to Him: I am the slave and instrument of His command.’

entries.farsi: گفت توبه كردم از جبر اى عيار / اختيار است اختيار است اختيار
entries.english: He said, ‘O cunning knave, I make a recantation of Necessitarianism: there is free-will, there is free-will- free-will!’

entries.farsi: اختيارات اختيارش هست كرد / اختيارش چون سوارى زير گرد
entries.english: His power of choice brought powers of choice into existence: His power of choice is like a rider beneath the dust.

entries.farsi: اختيارش اختيار ما كند / امر شد بر اختيارى مستند
entries.english: His power of choice makes our power of choice; His command is based on a power of choice.

entries.farsi: حاكمى بر صورت بى‏اختيار / هست هر مخلوق را در اقتدار
entries.english: Every created being has it in his power to exercise authority over the form without free-will,

entries.farsi: تا كشد بى‏اختيارى صيد را / تا برد بگرفته گوش او زيد را
entries.english: So that he drags the prey devoid of will, so that having seized Zayd by the ear, he leads him away.

entries.farsi: اختيارش زيد را قيدش كند / بى‏سگ و بى‏دام حق صيدش كند
entries.english: But the action of the Lord, without any instrument, makes his free-will a noose for him.

entries.farsi: ليك بى‏هيچ آلتى صنع صمد / اختيارش را كمند او كند
entries.english: His free-will makes him a fetter for Zayd: God makes him His prey without dog or snare.

entries.farsi: آن دروگر حاكم چوبى بود / و آن مصور حاكم خوبى بود
entries.english: The carpenter has authority over a piece of wood, and the artist has authority over a beauty;

entries.farsi: هست آهنگر بر آهن قيمى / ‏هست بنا هم بر آلت حاكمى
entries.english: The ironsmith is a superintendent of iron; the builder also is a ruler over his tools.

entries.farsi: قدرت تو بر جمادات از نبرد / كى جمادى را از آنها نفى كرد
entries.english: This is extraordinary; for all this free-will is bowing low, like a slave, in His free-will.

entries.farsi: نادر اين باشد كه چندين اختيار / ساجد اندر اختيارش بنده‏وار
entries.english: When did the power forcibly exercised by you over inanimate objects deprive them of inanimateness?

entries.farsi: قدرتش بر اختيارات آن چنان / ‏نفى نكند اختيارى را از آن
entries.english: Similarly, His power over acts of free-will does not deprive any act of free-will of that.

entries.farsi: خواستش مى‏گوى بر وجه كمال / ‏كه نباشد نسبت جبر و ضالل
entries.english: Declare that His will is in a complete pounder, without there being the attribution of compulsion (jabr) and responsibility for error.

entries.farsi: چون كه گفتى كفر من خواست وى است / ‏خواست خود را نيز هم مى‏دان كه هست
entries.english: Since you have said, ‘My unbelief is willed by Him,’ know that it is also willed by yourself;

entries.farsi: ز انكه بى‏خواه تو خود كفر تو نيست / ‏كفر بى‏خواهش تناقض گفتنى است
entries.english: For without your will your unbelief does not exist at all: involuntary unbelief is a self-contradiction.

entries.farsi: امر عاجز را قبيح است و ذميم / ‏خشم بدتر خاصه از رب رحيم
entries.english: It is abominable and blameworthy to lay a command on one incapable; and anger is worse, especially from the Merciful Lord.

entries.farsi: گاو چون معذور نبود در فضول / ‏صاحب گاو از چه معذور است و دول
entries.english: An ox is beaten if he refuses the yoke: is an ox reduced to misery because he will not fly?

entries.farsi: گاو گر يوغى نگيرد مى‏زنند / هيچ گاوى كه نپرد شد نژند
entries.english: Since the ox is not excused for stubbornness, wherefore is the owner of the ox excusable and infirm?

entries.farsi: چون نه‏اى رنجور سر را بر مبند / اختيارت هست برسبلت مخند
entries.english: Since you are not ill, don’t bandage your head: you have freewill, don’t laugh at your moustache.

entries.farsi: جهد كن كز جام حق يابى نوى / ‏بى‏خود و بى‏اختيار آن گه شوى
entries.english: Endeavour to gain freshness from God’s cup: then you will become selfless and volitionless.

entries.farsi: آن گه آن مى را بود كل اختيار / تو شوى معذور مطلق مست‏وار
entries.english: Then all volition will belong to that Wine, and you will be absolutely excusable, like a drunk man.

entries.farsi: هر چه كوبى كفته‏ى مى‏باشد آن / ‏هر چه روبى رفته‏ى مى‏باشد آن
entries.english: Whatever you beat will be beaten by the Wine; whatever you sweep away will be swept away by the Wine.

entries.farsi: كى كند آن مست جز عدل و صواب / ‏كه ز جام حق كشيده است او شراب
entries.english: The drunk man who has quaffed wine from God’s cup— how should he do aught but justice and right?

entries.farsi: جادوان فرعون را گفتند بيست / ‏مست را پرواى دست و پاى نيست
entries.english: The magicians said to Pharaoh, ‘Stop! He that is drunk has no care for his hands and feet.

entries.farsi: دست و پاى ما مى آن واحد است / ‏دست ظاهر سايه است و كاسد است
entries.english: The wine of the One is our hands and feet; the apparent hand is a shadow and worthless.’

sections.title_fa: معنى ما شاء اللهَّ كان يعنى خواست خواست او و رضا رضاى اوست، رضاى او جوييد و از خشم ديگران و رد ديگران دل تنگ مباشيد، آن كان اگر چه لفظ ماضى است ليكن در فعل خدا ماضى و مستقبل نباشد كه ليس عند اهلل صباح و ال مساء

sections.title_en: The meaning of ‘whatever God willed came to be,’ i.e. ‘the will is His will and pleasure. Seek His pleasure; be not distressed by the anger of others and the disapproval of others.’ Although the word ‘kána’ (came to be) denotes the past, yet there is no past or future in the action of God, for with God there is neither morning nor evening.

entries.farsi: قول بنده ايش شاء اللهَّ كان / ‏بهر آن نبود كه تنبل كن در آن
entries.english: The saying of servant, ‘whatever God wills comes to pass’ does not signify ‘be lazy in that’;

entries.farsi: بلكه تحريض است بر اخالص و جد / كه در آن خدمت فزون شو مستعد
entries.english: No, it is an incitement to entire self-devotion and exertion, meaning, ‘Make yourself exceedingly ready to perform that service.’

entries.farsi: گر بگويند آن چه مى‏خواهى تو راد / كار كار تست بر حسب مراد
entries.english: If you are told, O sage, that what you wish you have full power to act according to your desire,

entries.farsi: آن گهان تنبل كنى جايز بود / كانچه خواهى و آن چه گويى آن شود
entries.english: Then, if you are neglectful, it is permissible; for what you wish and say will come to pass.

entries.farsi: چون بگويند ايش شاء اللهَّ كان / ‏حكم حكم اوست مطلق جاودان
entries.english: When you are told that whatever God wills shall come to pass, and that to Him belongs the authority absolute and everlasting,

entries.farsi: پس چرا صد مرده اندر ورد او / بر نگردى بندگانه گرد او
entries.english: Why, then, should not you move round Him like a slave, with the will of a hundred men to perform the devotions due to Him?

entries.farsi: گر بگويند آن چه مى‏خواهد وزير / خواست آن اوست اندر دار و گير
entries.english: If you are told that what the vizier wishes his will is paramount in the exercise of authority,

entries.farsi: گرد او گردان شوى صد مرده زود / تا بريزد بر سرت احسان و جود
entries.english: Will you at once move round him with the zeal of a hundred men, that he may pour kindness and munificence on your head?

entries.farsi: يا گريزى از وزير و قصر او / اين نباشد جست و جوى نصر او
entries.english: Or will you flee from the vizier and his palace? This is not the way to seek his help.

entries.farsi: امر امر آن فالن خواجه‏ست هين / ‏چيست يعنى با جز او كمتر نشين
entries.english: You, inversely, have been made remiss by this saying: you have been turned upside down in your apprehension and thought.

entries.farsi: باژگونه زين سخن كاهل شدى / ‏منعكس ادراك و خاطر آمدى
entries.english: The command is vested in such and such a lord. Listen, what does this mean? It means, ‘Do not sit with anyone except him.

entries.farsi: گرد خواجه گرد چون امر آن اوست / ‏كاو كشد دشمن رهاند جان دوست
entries.english: Move round the lord, since the command belongs to him; for he slays his enemy and saves the life of his friend.

entries.farsi: هر چه او خواهد همان يابى يقين / ‏ياوه كم رو خدمت او بر گزين
entries.english: Whatever he wills, that same thing you will certainly obtain: do not go astray, prefer his service.’

entries.farsi: نى چو حاكم اوست گرد او مگرد / تا شوى نامه سياه و روى زرد
entries.english: Not, ‘Since he is possessed of authority, do not move round him, so that you may fall into his black books and be disgraced.’

entries.farsi: حق بود تاويل كان گرمت كند / پر اميد و چست و با شرمت كند
entries.english: The interpretation that makes you ardent and hopeful and active and reverent is the true one;

entries.farsi: ور كند سستت حقيقت اين بدان / ‏هست تبديل و نه تاويل است آن
entries.english: And if it makes you slack, know the real truth to be this, that it is an alteration, not an interpretation.

entries.farsi: اين براى گرم كردن آمده‏ست / ‏تا بگيرد نااميدان را دو دست
entries.english: This has come in order to make ardent, that He may take the hands of those who have lost hope.

entries.farsi: معنى قرآن ز قرآن پرس و بس / ‏وز كسى كاتش زده‏ست اندر هوس
entries.english: Ask the meaning of the Qur’án from the Qur’án alone, and from that one who has set fire to his idle fancy,

entries.farsi: پيش قرآن گشت قربانى و پست / ‏تا كه عين روح او قرآن شده‏ست
entries.english: And has become a sacrifice to the Qur’án and is low, so that the Qur’án has become the essence of his spirit.

entries.farsi: روغنى كاو شد فداى گل بكل / ‏خواه روغن بوى كن خواهى تو گل
entries.english: The oil that has wholly devoted itself to the rose— smell either the oil or the rose as you please.

sections.title_fa: و همچنين قد جف القلم يعنى جف القلم و كتب ال يستوى الطاعة و المعصية ال يستوى الامانة و َالسرقة، جف القلم ان ال يستوى الشكر و الكفران، جف القلم إِنَّ اللهََّ ال يُضِ يعُ أَجْ رَ الْمُحْ سِ نِين
sections.title_en: And similarly, ‘the Pen has dried’ means that the Pen has dried after writing, ‘Obedience and disobedience are not on the same level, and honesty and stealing are not on the same level.’ The Pen has dried that thanksgiving and ingratitude are not on the same level.
The Pen has dried that God does not let the reward of the righteous be lost.

entries.farsi: همچنين تاويل قد جف القلم / ‏بهر تحريض است بر شغل اهم
entries.english: Likewise the interpretation of ‘the Pen has dried’ it is for the purpose of inciting to the most important work.

entries.farsi: پس قلم بنوشت كه هر كار را / اليق آن هست تاثير و جزا
entries.english: Therefore the Pen wrote that every action has the effect and consequence appropriate to it.

entries.farsi: كژ روى جف القلم كژ آيدت / ‏راستى آرى سعادت زايدت
entries.english: The Pen has dried that if you do wrong you will suffer wrong, and that if you act rightly the result will be your felicity.

entries.farsi: ظلم آرى مدبرى جف القلم / ‏عدل آرى بر خورى جف القلم
entries.english: You behave unjustly, you are damned: the Pen has dried. If you show justice, you eat the fruit: the Pen has dried.

entries.farsi: چون بدزدد دست شد جف القلم / ‏خورد باده مست شد جف القلم
entries.english: When he steals, his hand goes: the Pen has dried. He drinks wine, he becomes intoxicated: the Pen has dried.

entries.farsi: تو روا دارى روا باشد كه حق / ‏همچو معزول آيد از حكم سبق
entries.english: Do you deem it allowable, can it be allowable, that on account of the prior decree God should come, like a person dismissed from office,

entries.farsi: كه ز دست من برون رفته‏ست كار / پيش من چندين ميا چندين مزار
entries.english: Saying, ‘the affair has gone out of My hands: do not approach Me so often, do not entreat so much’?

entries.farsi: بلكه معنى آن بود جف القلم / ‏نيست يكسان پيش من عدل و ستم
entries.english: No, the meaning is: ‘the Pen has dried justice and injustice is not equal in My sight.

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى گر در تو افزونى ادب / ‏باشد از يارت بداند فضل رب
entries.english: I have laid down a distinction between good and evil; I have also laid down a distinction between the bad and the worse.’

entries.farsi: فرق بنهادم ميان خير و شر / فرق بنهادم ز بد هم از بتر
entries.english: If there be in you a single mote of self-discipline in excess of your companion, the grace of God will know,

entries.farsi: قدر آن ذره ترا افزون دهد / ذره چون كوهى قدم بيرون نهد
entries.english: And will bestow on you that mote’s amount of superiority: the mote will step forth as a mountain.

entries.farsi: پادشاهى كه به پيش تخت او / فرق نبود از امين و ظلم جو
entries.english: A king before whose throne there is no distinction between the faithful and the seeker of iniquity—

entries.farsi: آن كه مى‏لرزد ز بيم رد او / وان كه طعنه مى‏زند در جد او
entries.english: Between him who trembles in fear of his disapproval and him who intrigues against his fortune.

entries.farsi: فرق نبود هر دو يك باشد برش / ‏شاه نبود خاك تيره بر سرش
entries.english: There is no difference, but both of them are one to him: he is not a king, may dark earth be on his head!

entries.farsi: ذره‏اى گر جهد تو افزون بود / در ترازوى خدا موزون بود
entries.english: If your labour exceeds another’s by a single mote, it will be weighed in God’s balance.

entries.farsi: پيش اين شاهان هماره جان كنى / ‏بى‏خبر ايشان ز غدر و روشنى
entries.english: You continually work yourself to death in the service of these kings; they are ignorant of treachery and honesty.

entries.farsi: گفت غمازى كه بد گويد ترا / ضايع آرد خدمتت را سالها
entries.english: The words of a tale-bearer who speaks ill of you will cause your years of service to be wasted;

entries.farsi: پيش شاهى كه سميع است و بصير / گفت غمازان نباشد جاى گير
entries.english: The words of tale-bearers do not take their abode in the presence of the King who is hearing and seeing.

entries.farsi: جمله غمازان از او آيس شوند / سوى ما آيند و افزايند بند
entries.english: All the tale-bearers are reduced to despair by Him: they come to us and increase bondage.

entries.farsi: بس جفا گويند شه را پيش ما / كه برو جف القلم كم كن وفا
entries.english: They speak much abuse of the King before us, saying, ‘Go! The Pen has dried. Do not keep faith.’

entries.farsi: بل جفا را هم جفا جف القلم / ‏و آن وفا را هم وفا جف القلم
entries.english: How should the meaning of ‘the Pen has dried’ be, that acts of treacherous and acts of faithfulness are alike?

entries.farsi: معنى جف القلم كى آن بود / كه جفاها با وفا يكسان بود
entries.english: No, treacherous for acts of treacherous: the Pen has dried; and faithfulness for those acts of faithfulness: the Pen has dried.

entries.farsi: عفو باشد ليك كو فر اميد / كه بود بنده ز تقوى رو سپيد
entries.english: There may be pardon, but where is the glorious hope that through piety the servant of God may be illumined?

entries.farsi: دزد را گر عفو باشد جان برد / كى وزير و خازن مخزن شود
entries.english: If a robber be pardoned, he saves his life, how should he become a vizier and keeper of the treasury?

entries.farsi: اى امين الدين ربانى بيا / كز امانت رست هر تاج و لوا
entries.english: Come, O godly Amínu’ddín, for every tiara and ensign has grown from trustworthiness (amánat).

entries.farsi: پور سلطان گر بر او خاين شود / آن سرش از تن بدان باين شود
entries.english: If the Sultan’s son becomes a traitor to him, on that account his head will be severed from his body;

entries.farsi: ور غالم هندويى آرد وفا / دولت او را مى‏زند طال بقا
entries.english: And if a Hindu slave shows faithfulness, sovereignty will applaud him, ‘Long may he live!’

entries.farsi: چه غالم ار بر درى سگ با وفاست / ‏در دل سالار او را صد رضاست
entries.english: What of a slave? If a dog is faithful at a door, there are a hundred feelings of satisfaction with him in the heart of the master.

entries.farsi: زين چو سگ را بوسه بر پوزش دهد / گر بود شيرى چه پيروزش كند
entries.english: Since, because of this, he kisses the mouth of a dog, if he be a lion, how triumphant he will make him!

entries.farsi: جز مگر دزدى كه خدمتها كند / صدق او بيخ جفا را بر كند
entries.english: Robbers get nothing but pardon, except, to be sure, the robber who performs acts of service and whose sincerity uproots his treacherous,

entries.farsi: چون فضيل ره زنى كاو راست باخت / ‏ز انكه ده مرده به سوى توبه تاخت
entries.english: Like Fudayl, the brigand who played straight, because he ran with the strength of ten men towards repentance;

entries.farsi: و آن چنان كه ساحران فرعون را / رو سيه كردند از صبر و وفا
entries.english: And as the magicians blackened the face of Pharaoh by their fortitude and faithfulness.

entries.farsi: دست و پا دادند در جرم قود / آن به صد ساله عبادت كى شود
entries.english: They gave their hands and feet for the crime that entailed retaliation: how should that be attained by means of a hundred years’ devotional service?

entries.farsi: تو كه پنجه سال خدمت كرده‏اى / ‏كى چنين صدقى به دست آورده‏اى
entries.english: You who have served for fifty years, when have you brought into your possession such a sincerity as this?

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن درويش كه در هرى غالمان آراسته‏ى عميد خراسان را ديد و بر اسبان تازى و قباهاى زربفت و كالههاى مغرق و غير آن، پرسيد كه اينها كدام اميرانند و چه شاهانند گفتند او را كه اينها اميران نيستند اينها غالمان عميد خراسانند، روى به آسمان كرد كه اى خدا غالم پروردن از عميد بياموز، آن جا مستوفى را عميد گويند

sections.title_en: Story of the dervish who saw at Herat the well-equipped slaves of the Amid of Khurasan, mounted on Arab horses and wearing gold-embroidered coats, richly ornamented caps, etc. He asked, ‘what princes and what kings are these?’ On being told that they were not princes, but the slaves of the Amid of Khurasan, he turned his face to Heaven, crying, ‘O God, learn from the Amid how to take care of slaves!’ There the State-accountant is called Amid.

entries.farsi: آن يكى گستاخ رو اندر هرى / ‏چون بديدى او غالم مهترى
entries.english: A certain unmannerly at Herat, when he saw a nobleman’s slave

entries.farsi: جامه‏ى اطلس كمر زرين روان / ‏روى كردى سوى قبله‏ى آسمان
entries.english: Going about in satin raiment with a belt of gold, would turn his face to Heaven,

entries.farsi: كاى خدا زين خواجه‏ى صاحب منن / ‏چون نياموزى تو بنده داشتن
entries.english: And cry, ‘O God, why do You not learn from this bountiful Khwaja how to keep slave?

entries.farsi: بنده پروردن بياموز اى خدا / زين رئيس و اختيار شاه ما
entries.english: O God, let this rais (high dignitary) and chosen of our king teach You how to care for Your slave.’

entries.farsi: بود محتاج و برهنه و بى‏نوا / در زمستان لرز لرزان از هوا
entries.english: The dervish was needy and naked and without food: in winter he was trembling exceedingly from the air.

entries.farsi: انبساطى كرد آن از خود برى / ‏جراتى بنمود او از لمترى
entries.english: That man beside himself committed an impertinence: from grossness he displayed an audacity.

entries.farsi: اعتمادش بر هزاران موهبت / ‏كه نديم حق شد اهل معرفت
entries.english: He relied on the thousands of gifts, saying that the gnostic has become God’s boon-companion.

entries.farsi: گر نديم شاه گستاخى كند / تو مكن آن كه ندارى آن سند
entries.english: If the king’s boon-companion takes a liberty, do not you behave so, who have not the same support.

entries.farsi: حق ميان داد و ميان به از كمر / گر كسى تاجى دهد او داد سر
entries.english: God gave the waist, and the waist is better than the belt: if any one gives you a tiara, He gave the head.

entries.farsi: آن غالمان را شكنجه مى‏نمود / كه دفينه‏ى خواجه بنماييد زود
entries.english: The dervish continued his reproaches till a certain day when the king accused the Khwaja and bound him hand and foot,

entries.farsi: تا يكى روزى كه شاه آن خواجه را / متهم كرد و ببستش دست و پا
entries.english: He put those slaves to the rack, saying, ‘Show at once the Khwaja’s buried treasure;

entries.farsi: ر او با من بگوييد اى خسان / ‏ور نه برم از شما حلق و لسان
entries.english: Tell me his secret; O you rascals or I will cut your throats and your tongues.’

entries.farsi: مدت يك ماه شان تعذيب كرد / روز و شب اشكنجه و افشار و درد
entries.english: He tortured them for a month: the rack, torment, and anguish by day and by night.

entries.farsi: پاره پاره كردشان و يك غالم / ‏راز خواجه وانگفت از اهتمام
entries.english: He rent them to pieces, but from their anxiety not one slave betrayed the Khwaja’s secret.

entries.farsi: گفتش اندر خواب هاتف كاى كيا / بنده بودن هم بياموز و بيا
entries.english: A voice from Heaven said to him in his dream, ‘O sir, do you also learn how to be a slave, and come.

entries.farsi: اى دريده پوستين يوسفان / ‏گر بدرد گرگت آن از خويش دان
entries.english: O you, who have torn the coats of the Josephs, know that it is your own fault if the wolf tears you.

entries.farsi: ز انكه مى‏بافى همه ساله بپوش / ‏ز انكه مى‏كارى همه ساله بنوش
entries.english: Wear, all the year round, a garment of that cloth which you are weaving; eat and drink, all the year round, of that which you are sowing.

entries.farsi: فعل تست اين غصه‏هاى دم به دم / ‏اين بود معنى قد جف القلم
entries.english: These continual pangs are your own action: this is the meaning of ‘the Pen has dried,’

entries.farsi: كه نگردد سنت ما از رشد / نيك را نيكى بود بد راست بد
entries.english: ‘My Law (Sunna) does not turn aside from rectitude: good shall befall the good, evil the evil.’

entries.farsi: ر كن هين كه سليمان زنده است / ‏تا تو ديوى تيغ او برنده است
entries.english: Beware, do works, for Solomon is alive: so long as you are a devil, his sword is cutting;

entries.farsi: چون فرشته گشت از تيغ ايمنى است / ‏از سليمان هيچ او را خوف نيست
entries.english: When he becomes an angel, he is safe from the sword and has no dread of Solomon.

entries.farsi: حكم او بر ديو باشد نه ملك / ‏رنج در خاك است نه فوق فلك
entries.english: His sway is over the devil, not the angel: pain is on the earth, not above the sky.

entries.farsi: ترك كن اين جبر جمع منبالن / ‏تا خبر يابى از آن جبر چو جان
entries.english: Abandon this Necessitarianism, which is very empty, in order that you may know what the inmost secret of Necessity is.

entries.farsi: ترك كن اين جبر را كه بس تهى است / ‏تا بدانى سرّ سرّ جبر چيست
entries.english: Abandon this Necessitarianism of the idle party, in order that you may gain knowledge of the Necessity that is as the soul.

entries.farsi: ترك معشوقى كن و كن عاشقى / ‏اى گمان برده كه خوب و فايقى
entries.english: Abandon the state of being loved and adopt the practice of loving, O you who think that you are excellent and pre-eminent.

entries.farsi: اى كه در معنى ز شب خامش‏ترى / ‏گفت خود را چند جويى مشترى
entries.english: O you who really are more silent than Night, how long will you seek a purchaser for your words?

entries.farsi: سر بجنبانند پيشت بهر تو / رفت در سوداى ايشان دهر تو
entries.english: They nod their heads in your presence for you: your time is wasted in the passionate desire of them.

entries.farsi: تو مرا گويى حسد اندر مپيچ / ‏چه حسد آرد كسى از فوت هيچ
entries.english: You say to me, ‘Don’t indulge in envy,’ how should any one feel envy in consequence of losing nothing?

entries.farsi: خويش را تعليم كن عشق و نظر / كان بود چون نقش فى جرم الحجر
entries.english: O impudent man, instruction given to the worthless is like drawing a little design on a clod of earth.

entries.farsi: هست تعليم خسان اى چشم شوخ / ‏همچو نقش خرد كردن بر كلوخ
entries.english: Instruct yourself in love and insight; for that is like a design on a solid mass of stone.

entries.farsi: تا كنى مر غير را حبر و سنى / ‏خويش را بد خو و خالى مى‏كنى
entries.english: Your own self is the pupil that is faithful to you: the others perish: where will you seek them, where?

entries.farsi: نفس تو با تست شاگرد وفا / غير فانى شد كجا جويى كجا
entries.english: In order that you may make others erudite and eminent, you are making yourself evil-natured and empty.

entries.farsi: امر قُلْ زين آمدش كاى راستين / ‏كم نخواهد شد بگو درياست اين
entries.english: When your heart is united with that Eden, listen, speak on, and be not afraid of becoming empty.

entries.farsi: متصل چون شد دلت با آن عدن / ‏هين بگو مهراس از خالى‏شدن
entries.english: Hence the command, ‘Speak!’ came to him, saying, ‘O righteous one, it will not fail: this is an ocean.’

entries.farsi: أَنْصِ تُوا يعنى كه آبت را به الغ / ‏هين تلف كم كن كه لب خشك است باغ
entries.english: God said, ‘Be silent,’ that is, ‘do not waste your water in idle talk, for the orchard is dry-lipped.’

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد اى پدر / اين سخن را ترك كن پايان نگر
entries.english: This discourse has no end, O father: leave this discourse and consider the end.

entries.farsi: عاشقانت در پس پرده‏ى كرم / ‏بهر تو نعره زنان بين دم به دم
entries.english: I am not jealous that they stand in your presence: they are mocking you, they are not lovers.

entries.farsi: غيرتم نايد كه پيشت بيستند / بر تو مى‏خندند عاشق نيستند
entries.english: Behold your lovers behind the veil of the Bounty, crying aloud for you continually.

entries.farsi: عاشق آن عاشقان غيب باش / ‏عاشقان پنج روزه كم تراش
entries.english: Be the lover of those unseen lovers: do not cherish the lovers who last five days;

entries.farsi: كه بخوردندت ز خدعه و جذبه‏اى / ‏سالها ز يشان نديدى حبه‏اى
entries.english: For they have devoured you by means of a deceit and attraction, and during years you have never seen a grain from them.

entries.farsi: وقت صحت جمله يارند و حريف / ‏وقت درد و غم بجز حق كو اليف
entries.english: How long will you set up a show on the public road? You are footsore, and your desire has been fulfilled.

entries.farsi: چند هنگامه نهى بر راه عام / ‏گام خستى بر نيامد هيچ كام
entries.english: When you enjoy good health all of them are your friends and comrades, in the hour of pain and sorrow where is any familiar friend but God?

entries.farsi: وقت درد چشم و دندان هيچ كس / ‏دست تو گيرد بجز فرياد رس
entries.english: In the hour of eye-ache or toothache will anyone take your hand except Him who comes at the cry of distress?

entries.farsi: پس همان درد و مرض را ياد دار / چون اياز از پوستين كن اعتبار
entries.english: Therefore recollect that sickness and pain: take warning, like Ayaz from that sheepskin jacket.

entries.farsi: پوستين آن حالت درد تو است / ‏كه گرفته ست آن اياز آن را به دست
entries.english: Your experience of pain is the sheepskin jacket which Ayaz took into his hand.”

sections.title_fa: باز جواب گفتن آن كافر جبرى آن سنى را كه به اسالمش دعوت مى‏كرد و به ترك اعتقاد جبرش دعوت مى‏كرد و دراز شدن مناظره از طرفين كه ماده‏ى اشكال و جواب را نبرد الا عشق حقيقى كه او را پرواى آن نماند، و ذلك فضل اللهَّ يؤتيه من يشاء

sections.title_en: How the Necessitarian infidel again replied to the Sunni who was inviting him to accept Islam and abandon his belief in Necessity, and how the debate was prolonged on both sides; for this difficult and controversial matter cannot be decided except by the real love that has no further interest in it—“and that is God’s grace: He bestows it on whom He pleases.”

entries.farsi: كافر جبرى جواب آغاز كرد / كه از آن حيران شد آن منطيق مرد
entries.english: The Necessitarian infidel began his reply, by which that eloquent man was confounded;

entries.farsi: ليك گر من آن جوابات و سؤال / ‏جمله را گويم بمانم زين مقال
entries.english: But if I relate all those answers and questions, I shall be unable to get on with this Discourse.

entries.farsi: ز آن مهم‏تر گفتنيها هستمان / ‏كه بدان فهم تو به يابد نشان
entries.english: We have things of greater importance to say, whereby your understanding will obtain a better clue.

entries.farsi: اندكى گفتيم آن بحث اى عتل / ‏ز اندكى پيدا بود قانون كل
entries.english: We have told a little of that disputation, O fierce debater, from a little the principle of the whole is evident.

entries.farsi: همچنين بحث است تا حشر بشر / در ميان جبرى و اهل قدر
entries.english: Similarly, there is a disputation, till mankind is raised from the dead, between the Necessitarians and the partisans of Freewill.

entries.farsi: گر فروماندى ز دفع خصم خويش / ‏مذهب ايشان بر افتادى ز پيش
entries.english: If he had been incapable of refuting his adversary, their doctrines would have fallen out of sight.

entries.farsi: چون برون شوشان نبودى در جواب / ‏پس رميدندى از آن راه تباب
entries.english: Since they would not have had the means of escape in replying, they would therefore have recoiled from the way of perdition;

entries.farsi: چون كه مقضى بد دوام آن روش / مى‏دهدشان از داليل پرورش
entries.english: Inasmuch as their continuance in that course was destined, God feeds them with proofs,

entries.farsi: تا نگردد ملزم از اشكال خصم / ‏تا بود محجوب از اقبال خصم
entries.english: In order that he may not be silenced by his adversary’s difficult objection, and that he may be prevented from seeing his adversary’s success,

entries.farsi: تا كه اين هفتاد و دو ملت مدام / ‏در جهان ماند الى يوم القيام
entries.english: So that these seventy-two sects may always remain in the world till the Day of Resurrection.

entries.farsi: چون جهان ظلمت است و غيب اين / ‏از براى سايه مى‏بايد زمين
entries.english: Since this is the world of darkness and occultation, the earth is necessary for the shadow.

entries.farsi: تا قيامت ماند اين هفتاد و دو / كم نيايد مبتدع را گفت و گو
entries.english: These two-and-seventy will remain till the Resurrection: the heretic’s talk and argument will not fail.

entries.farsi: عزت مخزن بود اندر بها / كه بر او بسيار باشد قفلها
entries.english: The high value of a treasury is that there are many locks upon it.

entries.farsi: عزت مقصد بود اى ممتحن / ‏پيچ پيچ راه و عقبه و راه زن
entries.english: The greatness of the goal, O well-tried man, is the intricate windings of the way and the mountain-passes and the brigands.

entries.farsi: عزت كعبه بود و آن ناديه / ‏ره زنى اعراب و طول باديه
entries.english: The greatness of the Ka‘ba and its assembly-place is the brigandage of the Bedouins and the length of the desert.

entries.farsi: هر روش هر ره كه آن محمود نيست / ‏عقبه‏اى و مانعى و ره زنى است
entries.english: Every doctrine, every tenet, that is not praiseworthy is a mountain-pass and a barrier and a brigand.

entries.farsi: اين روش خصم و حقود آن شده / ‏تا مقلد در دو ره حيران شده
entries.english: This doctrine has become the adversary and bitter enemy of that, so that the imitator is in a dilemma;

entries.farsi: صدق هر دو ضد بيند در روش / ‏هر فريقى در ره خود خوش منش
entries.english: He sees that both the opponents are firm in their doctrine: every sect is pleased with its own path.

entries.farsi: گر جوابش نيست مى‏بندد ستيز / بر همان دم تا به روز رستخيز
entries.english: If it has no reply, it will cling obstinately to the same formula till the Day of Resurrection,

entries.farsi: كه مهان ما بدانند اين جواب / ‏گر چه از ما شد نهان وجه صواب
entries.english: Saying, “Our great authorities know the reply to this, although the right method is hidden from us.”

entries.farsi: پوز بند وسوسه عشق است و بس / ور نه كى وسواس را بسته‏ست كس
entries.english: The only muzzle for evil suggestions is Love; else, when has any one stopped temptation?

entries.farsi: عاشقى شو شاهدى خوبى بجو / صيد مرغابى همى‏كن جو به جو
entries.english: Become a lover; seek a fair favourite, hunt a waterfowl from river to river.

entries.farsi: كى برى ز آن آب كان آبت برد / كى كنى ز آن فهم فهمت را خورد
entries.english: How will you get water from that one who takes your water away? How will you apprehend from that one consumes your apprehension?

entries.farsi: غير اين معقولها معقولها / يابى اندر عشق با فر و بها
entries.english: In Love, glorious and resplendent, you will find intelligible things other than these intelligible things.

entries.farsi: غير اين عقل تو حق را عقلها ست / ‏كه بدان تدبير اسباب سما ست
entries.english: To God belong intelligences other than this intelligence of yours, by which the mediate celestial things are ruled;

entries.farsi: كه بدين عقل آورى ارزاق را / ز آن دگر مفرش كنى اطباق را
entries.english: For by this intelligence you procure the means of subsistence, by that other you make the tiers of Heaven a carpet.

entries.farsi: چون ببازى عقل در عشق صمد / عشر امثالت دهد يا هفت صد
entries.english: When you gamble away your intelligence in love of the Lord, He gives you ten like unto it or seven hundred.

entries.farsi: آن زنان چون عقلها درباختند / بر رواق عشق يوسف تاختند
entries.english: Those women of Egypt, when they gambled away their intelligences, sped onward to the pavilion of Joseph’s love.

entries.farsi: عقلشان يك دم ستد ساقى عمر / سير گشتند از خرد باقى عمر
entries.english: The cupbearer of life took away their intelligence in one moment: they drank their fill of wisdom all the rest of their lives.

entries.farsi: اصل صد يوسف جمال ذو الجلال / ‏اى كم از زن شو فداى آن جمال
entries.english: The beauty of the Almighty is the source of a hundred Josephs: O you, who are less than a woman, devote yourself to that beauty!

entries.farsi: عشق برد بحث را اى جان و بس / ‏كاو ز گفت و گو شود فرياد رس
entries.english: O soul, Love alone cuts disputation short, for it comes to the rescue when you cry for help against arguments.

entries.farsi: حيرتى آيد ز عشق آن نطق را / زهره نبود كه كند او ماجرا
entries.english: Eloquence is dumbfounded by Love: it dare not engage in altercation;

entries.farsi: كه بترسد گر جوابى وا دهد / گوهرى از لنج او بيرون فتد
entries.english: For he fears that, if he answer back, a pearl may fall out of his mouth.

entries.farsi: لب ببندد سخت او از خير و شر / تا نبايد كز دهان افتد گهر
entries.english: He closes his lips tight against good or evil lest the pearl should fall from his mouth,

entries.farsi: همچنان كه گفت آن يار رسول / ‏چون نبى بر خواندى بر ما فصول
entries.english: Even as the Companion of the Prophet said, “Whenever the Prophet recited sections to us,

entries.farsi: آن رسول مجتبى وقت نثار / خواستى از ما حضور و صد وقار
entries.english: At the moment of munificence that chosen Messenger would demand of us attentiveness and a hundred reverences.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه بر سرت مرغى بود / كز فواتش جان تو لرزان شود
entries.english: It is as when a bird is on your head, and your soul trembles for fear of its flitting,

entries.farsi: پس نيارى هيچ جنبيدن ز جا / تا نگيرد مرغ خوب تو هوا
entries.english: So you dare not stir from your place, lest your beautiful bird should take to the air;

entries.farsi: دم نيارى زد ببندى سرفه را / تا نبايد كه بپرد آن هما
entries.english: You dare not breathe; you suppress a cough, lest that huma should fly away;

entries.farsi: ور كست شيرين بگويد يا ترش / ‏بر لب انگشتى نهى يعنى خمش
entries.english: And if any one speaks sweet or sour to you, you lay a finger on your lip, meaning, and “Hush!”

entries.farsi: حيرت آن مرغ است خاموشت كند / بر نهد سر ديگ و پر جوشت كند
entries.english: Bewilderment is like that bird: it makes you silent: it puts the lid on the kettle and fills you with the boiling of love.

sections.title_fa: پرسيدن پادشاه قاصدا اياز را كه چندين غم و شادى با چارق و پوستين كه جماد است مى‏گويى تا اياز را در سخن آورد
sections.title_en: How the King (Mahnnid) purposely asked Arax,” (Why) art thou telling all this sorrow and joy
to a rustic shoe and a sheepskin jacket, which are inanimate?” (His purpose was) that he might induce Ayaz to speak.

entries.farsi: اى اياز اين مهرها بر چارقى / ‏چيست آخر همچو بر بت عاشقى
entries.english: “ 0 Ayaz, pray, why are these marks of affection, like a lover to his adored one, to a rustic shoe?

entries.farsi: همچو مجنون از رخ ليلى خويش / ‏كرده‏اى تو چارقى را دين و كيش
entries.english: Thou hast made a rustic shoe thy devotion and religion, as Majnun of his Layla’s face.

entries.farsi: با دو كهنه مهر جان آميخته / ‏هر دو را در حجره‏اى آويخته
entries.english: Thou hast mingled thy soul’s love with two old articles and hung them both in a chamber.

entries.farsi: چند گويى با دو كهنه نو سخن / ‏در جمادى مى‏دمى سر كهن
entries.english: How long wilt thou speak new words to two old things and breathe the ancient secret into a substance devoid of life

entries.farsi: چون عرب با ربع و اطالل اى اياز / مى‏كشى از عشق گفت خود دراز
entries.english: Like the Arabs, 0 Ayaz, thou art drawing out long and lovingly thy converse with the abodes and the traces of former habitation.

entries.farsi: چارقت ربع كدامين آصف است / ‏پوستين گويى كه كرته‏ى يوسف است
entries.english: Of what Asaf are thy shoon the abode? One would say that thy sheepskin jacket is the shirt of Joseph.”

entries.farsi: همچو ترسا كه شمارد با كشش / ‏جرم يك ساله زنا و غل و غش
entries.english: Like the Christian who recounts to his priest a year’s sins— fornication and malice and hypocrisy

entries.farsi: تا بيامرزد كشش زو آن گناه / ‏عفو او را عفو داند از اله
entries.english: In order that the priest may pardon his sins, for he regards his forgiveness as forgiveness from God.

entries.farsi: نيست آگه آن كشش از جرم و داد / ليك بس جادوست عشق و اعتقاد
entries.english: The priest has no knowledge of sin and pardon ; but love and firm belief are mightily bewitching.

entries.farsi: دوستى و وهم صد يوسف تند / اسحر از هاروت و ماروت است خود
entries.english: Love and imagination weave a hundred Joseph: in sooth they are greater sorcerers than Harut and Marut.

entries.farsi: صورتى پيدا كند بر ياد او / جذب صورت آردت در گفت و گو
entries.english: They cause a form to appear in memory of him: the attraction of the form leads you into (conversation with it).

entries.farsi: رازگويى پيش صورت صد هزار / آن چنان كه يار گويد پيش يار
entries.english: You tell a hundred thousand secrets in the form’s presence, just as a friend speaks in the presence of a friend.

entries.farsi: نه بد آن جا صورتى نه هيكلى / ‏زاده از وى صد أَ لَسْ تُ و صد بلى
entries.english: No form or shape is there; from it proceed a hundred “Am not I?” and a hundred “ Yeas.”

entries.farsi: آن چنان كه مادرى دل برده‏اى / ‏پيش گور بچه‏ى نو مرده‏اى
entries.english: As when a mother, distraught beside the grave of a child newly dead,

entries.farsi: رازها گويد به جد و اجتهاد / مى‏نمايد زنده او را آن جماد
entries.english: Utters heart-felt words earnestly and intensely: the inanimateseems to her to be alive.

entries.farsi: حى و قايم داند او آن خاك را / چشم و گوشى داند او خاشاك را
entries.english: She regards that dust as living and erect, she regards that rubbish as an eye and an ear.

entries.farsi: پيش او هر ذره‏ى آن خاك گور / گوش دارد هوش دارد وقت شور
entries.english: To her, at the moment when she is crazed, every atom of the earth in the grave seems to have hearing and intelligence.

entries.farsi: مستمع داند به جد آن خاك را / خوش نگر اين عشق ساحرناك را
entries.english: She believes with all her might that the earth is hearkening: look well at this Love that works magic!

entries.farsi: آن چنان بر خاك گور تازه او / دم‏به‏دم خوش مى‏نهد با اشك رو
entries.english: Fondly and with tears she lays her face, time after time, on the fresh earth of the grave in such wise

entries.farsi: كه به وقت زندگى هرگز چنان / ‏روى ننهاده‏ست بر پور چو جان
entries.english: As during his life she never laid her face on the son who was so dear to her ;

entries.farsi: از عزا چون چند روزى بگذرد / آتش آن عشق او ساكن شود
entries.english: When some days pass in mourning, the fire of her love sinks to rest.

entries.farsi: عشق بر مرده نباشد پايدار / عشق را بر حى جان افزاى دار
entries.english: Love for the dead is not lasting: keep your love on the Living One who increases spiritual life.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن ز آن گور خود خواب آيدش / ‏از جمادى هم جمادى زايدش
entries.english: Afterwards, indeed, from that grave slumber: from object is born in her the same insensibility,

entries.farsi: ز انك عشق افسون خود بربود و رفت / ‏ماند خاكستر چو آتش رفت تفت
entries.english: Because Love has carried off his enchantment and gone away: as soon as the fire is sped, ashes remain.

entries.farsi: آن چه بيند آن جوان در آينه / ‏پير اندر خشت مى‏بيند همه
entries.english: The Elder (Pir) beholds in the brick all that the young man beholds in the mirror.

entries.farsi: پير، عشق تست نه ريش سپيد / دستگير صد هزاران نااميد
entries.english: The Elder is thy love, not a white beard. It is he that gives a helping hand to thousands who are in despair.

entries.farsi: عشق صورتها بسازد در فراق / ‏نامصور سر كند وقت تالق
entries.english: In separation Love fashions forms; in the hour of union the Formless One puts forth his head,

entries.farsi: كه منم آن اصل هوش و مست / ‏بر صور آن حسن عكس ما بده‏ست
entries.english: Saving, “ I am the ultimate origin’ of sobriety and intoxication: the beauty in forms is reflected from Me.

entries.farsi: پرده‏ها را اين زمان برداشتم / ‏حسن را بى‏واسطه بفراشتم
entries.english: At this moment I have removed the veils: I have raised Beauty on high without intermediaries.

entries.farsi: ز انكه بس با عكس من دربافتى / ‏قوت تجريد ذاتم يافتى
entries.english: Because thou hast been much occupied with My reflexion, thou hast gained the power to contemplate My essence denuded .

entries.farsi: چون از اين سو جذبه‏ى من شد روان / ‏او كشش را مى‏نبيند در ميان
entries.english: When My pull is set in motion from this side, he does not see the priest intervening.”

entries.farsi: مغفرت مى‏خواهد از جرم و خطا / از پس آن پرده از لطف خدا
entries.english: He is craving forgiveness for his sins and trespasses from the grace of God behind the veil.

entries.farsi: چون ز سنگى چشمه‏اى جارى شود / سنگ اندر چشمه متوارى شود
entries.english: When a fountain gushes from a rock, the rock disappears in the fountain.

entries.farsi: كس نخواند بعد از آن او را حجر / ز انك جارى شد از آن سنگ آن گهر
entries.english: After that, no one calls it “ stone,” seeing that such a pure substance has gushed forth from the rock.

entries.farsi: كاسه‏ها دان اين صور را و اندر او / آن چه حق ريزد بدان گيرد علو
entries.english: 3285. Know that these forms are (like) bowls and acquire excellence through that which God pours into them.

sections.title_fa: گفتن خويشاوندان مجنون را كه حسن ليلى به اندازه اى است چندان نيست، از او نغزتر در شهر ما بسيار است يكى و دو و ده بر تو عرضه كنيم اختيار كن، ما را و خود را وارهان، و جواب گفتن مجنون ايشان را

sections.title_en: How the kinsfolk of Majnun said to him, “The beauty of Layla is limited, it is not so very great: in our city there are many fairer than she. We will show unto you one or two or ten: take your choice, and deliver us and yourself”; and how Majnun answered them.

entries.farsi: ابلهان گفتند مجنون را ز جهل / ‏حسن ليلى نيست چندان هست سهل
entries.english: The fools in their ignorance said to Majnun, “The beauty of Layla is not so very great, it is of slight account.

entries.farsi: بهتر از وى صد هزاران دل ربا / هست همچون ماه اندر شهر ما
entries.english: There are thousands of moon-like sweethearts fairer than she in our city.”

entries.farsi: گفت صورت كوزه است و حسن مى / ‏مى خدايم مى‏دهد از نقش وى
entries.english: He replied, “The form is a pot, and beauty is the wine: God is giving me wine from her form.

entries.farsi: مر شما را سركه داد از كوزه‏اش / ‏تا نباشد عشق اوتان گوش كش
entries.english: He gave you vinegar from her pot, lest love of her should pull you by the ears.”

entries.farsi: از يكى كوزه دهد زهر و عسل / ‏هر يكى را دست حق عز و جل
entries.english: The hand of God, the Almighty and Glorious, gives poison or honey to everyone from the same pot.

entries.farsi: كوزه مى‏بينى و ليكن آن شراب / ‏روى ننمايد به چشم ناصواب
entries.english: You see the pot, but the wine does not show itself to the wrong eye.

entries.farsi: قاصِ راتُ الطَّ رْفِ باشد ذوق جان / ‏جز به خصم خود بننمايد نشان
entries.english: Spiritual experience is the women who look modestly: it shows no sign but to its possessor.

entries.farsi: قاصِ راتُ الطَّ رْفِ آمد آن مدام / ‏وين حجاب ظرفها همچون خيام
entries.english: That wine is the women who look modestly, while these vessels screening it are like the tents.

entries.farsi: هست دريا خيمه‏اى در وى حيات / ‏بط را ليكن كالغان را ممات
entries.english: The great river is a tent, wherein is life for the duck, but death for crows.

entries.farsi: زهر باشد مار را هم قوت و برگ / ‏غير او را زهر او درد است و مرگ
entries.english: Venom also is the snake’s food and provision, its venom is anguish and death to others.

entries.farsi: صورت هر نعمتى و محنتى / ‏هست اين را دوزخ آن را جنتى
entries.english: The form of every blessing and affliction is a Hell to this one, a Paradise to that one.

entries.farsi: پس همه اجسام و اشيا تبصرون / ‏و اندر او قوت است و سم ال تبصرون
entries.english: Therefore you see all bodies and things, and there is food and poison in them —you do not see it.

entries.farsi: هست هر جسمى چو كاسه و كوزه‏اى / ‏اندر او هم قوت و هم دل سوزه‏اى
entries.english: Everybody resembles a bowl or a pot, wherein is both food and a heart-burning.

entries.farsi: صورت يوسف چو جامى بود خوب / ‏ز آن پدر مى خورد صد باده‏ى طروب
entries.english: The bowl is visible, the plenty in it is hidden: he who tastes it knows what he is eating or drinking from it.

entries.farsi: كاسه پيدا اندر او پنهان رغد / طاعمش داند كز آن چه مى‏خورد
entries.english: The form of Joseph was like a beauteous cup: from it his father drank a hundred exhilarating wines.

entries.farsi: باز اخوان را از آن زهر آب بود / كان در ايشان خشم و كينه مى‏فزود
entries.english: Again, to his brethren the draught they took from it was poisoned water which was increasing in them anger and hatred.

entries.farsi: باز از وى مر زليخا را شكر / مى‏كشيد از عشق افيونى دگر
entries.english: Again, to Zalikha from it was sugar: she was quaffing a different opiate from Love.

entries.farsi: غير آن چه بود مر يعقوب را / بود از يوسف غذا آن خوب را
entries.english: The nutriment which came from Joseph to that fair one was other than that which came to Jacob.

entries.farsi: گونه گونه شربت و كوزه يكى / ‏تا نماند در مى غيبت شكى
entries.english: The sherbets are various, but the pot is one, in order that no doubt may remain in you concerning the wine of the Unseen.

entries.farsi: باده از غيب است و كوزه زين جهان / ‏كوزه پيدا باده در وى بس نهان
entries.english: The wine belongs to the Unseen, the pot to this world: the pot is apparent; the wine in it is hidden:

entries.farsi: بس نهان از ديده‏ى نامحرمان / ‏ليك بر محرم هويدا و عيان
entries.english: Hidden from the eyes of the uninitiated, but manifest and evident to the adept.

entries.farsi: يا الهى سُكِّرَتْ أبصارنا / فاعف عنا اثقلت اوزارنا
entries.english: O my God, our eyes have been intoxicated. Forgive us: our burdens have been made heavy.

entries.farsi: يا خفيا قد مألت الخافقين / ‏قد علوت فوق نور المشرقين
entries.english: O concealed One who have filled from East to West and are exalted above the light of the Orient and the Occident,

entries.farsi: أنت سر كاشف اسرارنا / أنت فجر مفجر انهارنا
entries.english: You are an inmost Ground of consciousness revealing our inmost thoughts; You are a bursting Force that causes our dammed-up rivers to burst forth.

entries.farsi: أنت كالريح و نحن كالغبار / تختفى الريح و غبراها جهار
entries.english: O You whose Essence is hidden while Your gifts are sensible, You are as the water and we as the millstone.

entries.farsi: يا خفى الذات محسوس العطا / أنت كالماء و نحن كالرحا
entries.english: You are as the wind and we as the dust: the wind is hidden while the dust blown by it is plainly visible.

entries.farsi: تو بهارى ما چو باغ سبز خوش / ‏او نهان و آشكارا بخششش
entries.english: You are the Spring; we are fair as the verdant orchard: it is hidden while its bounty is manifest.

entries.farsi: تو چو جانى ما مثال دست و پا / قبض و بسط دست از جان شد روا
entries.english: You are as the spirit; we are like hand and foot: the closing and opening of the hand is due to the spirit.

entries.farsi: تو چو عقلى ما مثال اين زبان / ‏اين زبان از عقل دارد اين بيان
entries.english: You art as the reason, we are like this tongue: this tongue has its expression from the reason.

entries.farsi: تو مثال شادى و ما خنده‏ايم / ‏كه نتيجه‏ى شادى فرخنده‏ايم
entries.english: You art like the joy, and we are the laughter, for we are the result of blessed joy.

entries.farsi: جنبش ما هر دمى خود اشهد است / ‏كه گواه ذو الجلال سرمد است
entries.english: Our movement is really a continual profession of faith which bears witness to the Eternal Almighty One.

entries.farsi: گردش سنگ آسيا در اضطراب / ‏اشهد آمد بر وجود جوى آب
entries.english: The turning of the millstone in agitation is a profession of faith in the existence of the stream.

entries.farsi: اى برون از وهم و قال و قيل من / ‏خاك بر فرق من و تمثيل من
entries.english: O You who is beyond my conception and utterance, dust is on the head of me and my similes!

entries.farsi: بنده نشكيبد ز تصوير خوشت / ‏هر دمت گويد كه جانم مفرشت
entries.english: Your servant cannot refrain from depicting Your beauty: every moment he says to You, “May my soul be Your carpet!”

entries.farsi: همچو آن چوپان كه مى‏گفت اى خدا / پيش چوپان و محب خود بيا
entries.english: Like the shepherd who used to say, “O God, come to Your shepherd and lover,

entries.farsi: تا شپش جويم من از پيراهنت / ‏چارقت دوزم ببوسم دامنت
entries.english: That I may seek out the lice in Your smock and stitch Your shoes and kiss Your skirt.”

entries.farsi: كس نبودش در هوا و عشق جفت / ‏ليك قاصر بود از تسبيح و گفت
entries.english: There was none equal to him in passion and love, but he fell short in glorification and speech.

entries.farsi: عشق او خرگاه بر گردون زده / ‏جان سگ خرگاه آن چوپان شده
entries.english: His love pitched its tent on the sky: the Soul became the dog of that shepherd’s tent.

entries.farsi: چون كه بحر عشق يزدان جوش زد / بر دل او زد ترا بر گوش زد
entries.english: When the sea of Divine Love surged up, it struck upon his heart; it struck upon you ear.

sections.title_fa: حكايت جوحى كه چادر پوشيد و در وعظ ميان زنان نشست و حركتى كرد زنى او را بشناخت كه مرد است و نعره اى زد
sections.title_en: Story of Juhi, who put on a chador, went to hear the sermon, seated himself amongst the women,
and behaved in such a way that a certain woman knew he was a man and screamed.

entries.farsi: واعظى بد بس گزيده در بيان / ‏زير منبر جمع مردان و زنان
entries.english: There was a preacher, very fine in his exposition, under whose pulpit a great number of men and women were assembled.

entries.farsi: رفت جوحى چادر و روبند ساخت / ‏در ميان آن زنان شد ناشناخت
entries.english: Juhi went: he got a chador and veil and entered amongst the women without being recognised.

entries.farsi: سائلى پرسيد واعظ را به راز / موى عانه هست نقصان نماز
entries.english: Someone asked the preacher secretly, does pubic hair hinder prayer.

entries.farsi: گفت واعظ چون شود عانه دراز / پس كراهت باشد از وى در نماز
entries.english: The preacher replied, “an element of revulsion creeps in if the pubic hair is too long.

entries.farsi: يا به آهك يا ستره بسترش / ‏تا نمازت كامل آيد خوب و خوش
entries.english: It should, therefore be removed either with lime or shaved with a razor so that your prayer is perfectly happy and pleasant.

entries.farsi: گفت سائل آن درازى تا چه حد / شرط باشد تا نمازم كم بود
entries.english: The questioner said, “At what length is it required to shave it off?”

entries.farsi: گفت چون قدر جوى گردد به طول / ‏پس ستردن فرض باشد اى سئول
entries.english: He replied, “After the hair has reached the length of a barley seed it is mandatory to shave it off?”

entries.farsi: گفت جوحى زود اى خواهر ببين / ‏عانه‏ى من گشته باشد اين چنين
entries.english: At once Juhi said, “O sister, see if my hair has reached that length

entries.farsi: بهر خشنودى حق پيش آر دست / ‏كان به مقدار كراهت آمده‏ست
entries.english: For truth’s sake, place your hand and see if they have reached the proscribed length.”

entries.farsi: دست زن در كرد در شلوار مرد / كير او بر دست زن آسيب كرد
entries.english: The woman placed her hand inside the man’s trousers and his penis hurt her hand.

entries.farsi: نعره‏اى زد سخت اندر حال زن / ‏گفت واعظ بر دلش زد گفت من
entries.english: Thereupon the woman gave a loud scream: the preacher said, “My discourse has smitten her heart.”

entries.farsi: گفت نه بر دل نزد بر دست زد / واى اگر بر دل زدى اى پر خرد
entries.english: Juhi answered, “No, it has not smitten her heart, but her hand! Would to God that it had touched her heart!”

entries.farsi: بر دل آن ساحران زد اندكى / ‏شد عصا و دست ايشان را يكى
entries.english: When Divine Love struck a little upon the hearts of pharaoh’s magicians, staff and hand became one to them.

entries.farsi: گر عصا بستانى از پيرى شها / بيش رنجد كان گروه از دست و پا
entries.english: O king, if you take away the staff from an old man, he will be more grieved than that party was grieved by the amputation of their hands and feet.

entries.farsi: نعره‏ى ال ضَ يْرَ بر گردون رسيد / هين ببر كه جان ز جان كندن رهيد
entries.english: The cry, “No harm,” reached Heaven: “Listen, cut off, for our souls are delivered from the agony.

entries.farsi: ما بدانستيم ما اين تن نه‏ايم / ‏از وراى تن به يزدان مى‏زييم
entries.english: We have come to know we are not this body: beyond the body we are living through God.”

entries.farsi: اى خنك آن را كه ذات خود شناخت / ‏اندر امن سرمدى قصرى بساخت
entries.english: Oh, blest is he that has recognised his essence and built a palace in everlasting security.

entries.farsi: كودكى گريد پى جوز و مويز / پيش عاقل باشد آن بس سهل چيز
entries.english: A child weeps for walnuts and raisins; those are very trifling things in the view of a reasonable man.

entries.farsi: پيش دل جوز و مويز آمد جسد / طفل كى در دانش مردان رسد
entries.english: In the spirit’s view the body is walnuts and raisins, how should a child attain to the knowledge possessed by men?

entries.farsi: هر كه محجوب است او خود كودك است / ‏مرد آن باشد كه بيرون از شك است
entries.english: Whoever is veiled is really a child: the man is he who is beyond uncertainty.

entries.farsi: گر به ريش و خايه مردستى كسى / ‏هر بزى را ريش و مو باشد بسى
entries.english: Spiritual manhood does not consist in hair and genitals; every male-goat has a beard and plenty of hair.

entries.farsi: پيشواى بد بود آن بز شتاب / ‏مى‏برد اصحاب را پيش قصاب
entries.english: That goat is a bad leader: he is taking his followers quickly along to the butcher.

entries.farsi: ريش شانه كرده كه من سابقم / ‏سابقى ليكن به سوى مرگ و غم
entries.english: He has combed his beard, saying, “I am the foremost.” You are the foremost, but in the direction of death and anguish.

entries.farsi: هين روش بگزين و ترك ريش كن / ‏ترك اين ما و من و تشويش كن
entries.english: Listen, adopt travelling and abandon your beard: abandon this egoism and troubled thought,

entries.farsi: تا شوى چون بوى گل با عاشقان / ‏پيشوا و رهنماى گلستان
entries.english: That you may become like the scent of the rose to lovers their leader and guide to the Rose-garden.

entries.farsi: كيست بوى گل دم عقل و خرد / خوش قالووز ره ملك ابد
entries.english: What is the scent of the rose? The breath of reason and intelligence a sweet guide on the way to the Kingdom Everlasting.

sections.title_fa: فرمودن شاه به اياز بار دگر كه شرح چارق و پوستين آشكارا بگو كه تا خواجه تاشانت از آن اشارت پند گيرند كه الدين النصيحة
sections.title_en: How the King once more commanded Ayaz, saying “Give a clear explanation concerning your rustic shoes and sheepskin jacket in order that your fellow-servants may be admonished
by that indication; for, ‘Religion consists in sincere counsel’”

entries.farsi: سر چارق را بيان كن اى اياز / پيش چارق چيستت چندين نياز
entries.english: “O Ayaz, declare the mystery of the rustic shoes and why in the presence of the shoes you whow all this humility,

entries.farsi: اى اياز از تو غالمى نور يافت / ‏نورت از پستى سوى گردون شتافت
entries.english: So that your Sunqur and Bakyaruq may hear the inmost secret of your sheepskin jacket and rustic shoes

entries.farsi: تا نيوشد سنقر و بكيارقت / ‏سر سر پوستين و چارقت
entries.english: O Ayaz, slavery has gained lustre from you: your lustre has sped from lowliness towards heaven

entries.farsi: حسرت آزادگان شد بندگى / ‏بندگى را چون تو دادى زندگى
entries.english: Servitude has become an object of regret to the free, since you have given life to servitude.

entries.farsi: مومن آن باشد كه اندر جزر و مد / كافر از ايمان او حسرت خورد
entries.english: The true believer is he by whose true belief amidst the ebb and flow the infidel is made regretful.”

sections.title_fa: حكايت كافرى كه گفتندش در عهد بايزيد كه مسلمان شو و جواب گفتن او ايشان را
sections.title_en: Story of the infidel whom, in the time of Aba Yazíd, they invited to become a Moslem; and how he answered them.

entries.farsi: بود گبرى در زمان بايزيد / گفت او را يك مسلمان سعيد
entries.english: There was a certain infidel in the time of Bayazid: a blessed Moslem said to him,

entries.farsi: كه چه باشد گر تو اسالم آورى / ‏تا بيابى صد نجات و سرورى
entries.english: “How would it be if you profess Islam, so that you may gain a hundred salvations and sovereignties?”

entries.farsi: گفت اين ايمان اگر هست اى مريد / آن كه دارد شيخ عالم بايزيد
entries.english: He replied, “If this Faith, O disciple, is that which is held by Bayazid, the Shaykh of the world,

entries.farsi: من ندارم طاقت آن تاب آن / ‏كان فزون آمد ز كوششهاى جان
entries.english: I cannot endure the glowing heat thereof, which is too great for the strivings of my soul.

entries.farsi: گر چه در ايمان و دين ناموقنم / ‏ليك در ايمان او بس مومنم
entries.english: Although I feel no certainty as regards the Faith and Religion, yet I believe mightily in his Faith.

entries.farsi: دارم ايمان كان ز جمله برتر است / ‏بس لطيف و با فروغ و با فر است
entries.english: I hold the faith that that is higher than all: it is very beautiful, resplendent, and glorious.

entries.farsi: مومن ايمان اويم در نهان / ‏گر چه مهرم هست محكم بر دهان
entries.english: Inwardly I am a believer in his Faith, though a seal is set firmly on my mouth.

entries.farsi: باز ايمان خود گر ايمان شماست / ‏نه بدان ميل استم و نه مشتهاست
entries.english: Again, if indeed the Faith is your Faith, I have no inclination or desire for it.

entries.farsi: آن كه صد ميلش سوى ايمان بود / چون شما را ديد آن فاتر شود
entries.english: He that feels a hundred inclinations to believe— that languishes as soon as he sees you,

entries.farsi: ز انكه نامى بيند و معنيش نى / ‏چون بيابان را مفازه گفتنى
entries.english: Because he sees a name and no meaning in it, like calling the desert a safe place (mafáza).

entries.farsi: عشق او ز آورد ايمان بفسرد / چون به ايمان شما او بنگرد
entries.english: When he looks upon your Faith, his love is chilled from professing it.”

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن موذن زشت آواز كه در كافرستان بانگ نماز داد و مرد كافرى او را هديه داد
sections.title_en: Story of the harsh-voiced muezzin who called the Moslems to prayer in the land of the infidels and to whom a certain infidel offered presents.

entries.farsi: يك موذن داشت بس آواز بد / در ميان كافرستان بانگ زد
entries.english: A certain muezzin had a very harsh voice: he called the Moslems to prayer in the land of the infidels.

entries.farsi: چند گفتندش مگو بانگ نماز / كه شود جنگ و عداوتها دراز
entries.english: They said to him several times, “Do not give the call to prayer, for fighting and acts of hostility will be prolonged.”

entries.farsi: او ستيزه كرد و پس بى‏احتراز / گفت در كافرستان بانگ نماز
entries.english: He defied, and then without showing caution he gave the call to prayer in the land of the infidels.

entries.farsi: خلق خايف شد ز فتنه‏ى عامه‏اى / ‏خود بيامد كافرى با جامه‏اى
entries.english: The Muslims were in fear of a general insurrection; however, an infidel came up with a robe.

entries.farsi: شمع و حلوا با چنان جامه‏ى لطيف / ‏هديه آورد و بيامد چون اليف
entries.english: He brought candles and halwá and such a robe as gifts, and approached in a friendly pounder,

entries.farsi: پرس پرسان كاين موذن كو كجاست / ‏كه صال و بانگ او راحت فزاست
entries.english: Asking again and again, “Tell me, where is this muezzin, whose call and cry increases my pleasure?”

entries.farsi: هين چه راحت بود ز آن آواز زشت / ‏گفت كاوازش فتاد اندر كنشت
entries.english: “Eh, what pleasure was there from such a harsh voice?” He replied, “His voice penetrated into the church.

entries.farsi: دخترى دارم لطيف و بس سنى / ‏آرزو مى‏بود او را مومنى
entries.english: I have a comely daughter of very high estate: she desired a true believer.

entries.farsi: هيچ اين سودا نمى‏رفت از سرش / ‏پندها مى‏داد چندين كافرش
entries.english: Never would this passion go out of her head, so many infidels were exhorting her.

entries.farsi: در دل او مهر ايمان رسته بود / همچو مجمر بود اين غم من چو عود
entries.english: Love of the Faith had grown up in her heart: this grief was like a censer and I like the aloes-wood.

entries.farsi: در عذاب و درد و اشكنجه بدم / ‏كه بجنبد سلسله‏ى او دم به دم
entries.english: I was in torment and anguish and continually on the rack lest her passion should lead her.

entries.farsi: هيچ چاره مى‏ندانستم در آن / ‏تا فرو خواند اين موذن آن اذان
entries.english: I knew no remedy for it until this muezzin chanted the adhán (call to prayer).

entries.farsi: گفت دختر چيست اين مكروه بانگ / ‏كه به گوشم آمد اين دو چار دانگ
entries.english: My daughter said, ‘What is this detestable noise? It grates on my ear.

entries.farsi: من همه عمر اين چنين آواز زشت / ‏هيچ نشنيدم در اين دير و كنشت
entries.english: Never in all my life have I heard such a harsh voice in this Christian convent and church.’

entries.farsi: خواهرش گفتش كه اين بانگ اذان / ‏هست اعالم و شعار مومنان
entries.english: Her sister said to her, ‘This chant, namely the adhán, gives notice and is the watchword of the Faithful.’

entries.farsi: باورش نامد بپرسيد از دگر / آن دگر هم گفت آرى اى پدر
entries.english: She would not believe it, and asked someone else: that person too said, ‘Yes, O father.’

entries.farsi: باز رستم من ز تشويش و عذاب / ‏دوش خوش خفتم در آن بى‏خوف خواب
entries.english: When she became sure, her face turned pale and her heart grew cold to Islam.

entries.farsi: چون يقين گشتش رخ او زرد شد / از مسلمانى دل او سرد شد
entries.english: I was delivered from anxiety and torment: last night I slept sound in a peaceful sleep.

entries.farsi: راحتم اين بود از آواز او / هديه آوردم به شكر آن مرد كو
entries.english: This was the pleasure that came to me from his voice: in gratitude I bring gifts: where is the man?”

entries.farsi: چون بديدش گفت اين هديه پذير / كه مرا گشتى مجير و دستگير
entries.english: When he saw him, he said, “Accept the gift, for you have been my protector and saviour.

entries.farsi: آن چه كردى با من از احسان و بر / بنده‏ى تو گشته‏ام من مستمر
entries.english: Due to the benefit and kindness that you have done to me, I have become your slave perpetually.

entries.farsi: گر به مال و ملك و ثروت فردمى / ‏من دهانت را پر از زر كردمى
entries.english: If I were eminent in respect of property, possessions, and riches, I would fill your mouth with gold.”

entries.farsi: هست ايمان شما زرق و مجاز / راه زن همچون كه آن بانگ نماز
entries.english: “Your Faith is hypocrisy and falsehood: like that call to prayer, it waylays the seeker and prevents him from embracing Islam;

entries.farsi: ليك از ايمان و صدق بايزيد / چند حسرت در دل و جانم رسيد
entries.english: But many a regret has come into my heart and soul from the Faith and sincerity of Bayazid.”

entries.farsi: همچو آن زن كاو جماع خر بديد / گفت آوه چيست اين فحل فريد
entries.english: Similarly, the woman who observed sexual intercourse with the ass, she said: “Oh, what is this incomparable stallion?

entries.farsi: گر جماع اين است بردند اين خران / ‏بر كس ما مى‏ريند اين شوهران
entries.english: If this is sexual intercourse, these asses have won: these husbands are defecating on our vulvas.”

entries.farsi: داد جمله‏ى داد ايمان بايزيد / آفرينها بر چنين شير فريد
entries.english: Bayazid paid all that is due to the Faith: blessings be on such a peerless lion!

entries.farsi: قطره‏اى ز ايمانش در بحر ار رود / بحر اندر قطره‏اش غرقه شود
entries.english: If a single drop of his Faith enters into the ocean, the ocean will be submerged in his drop,

entries.farsi: همچو ز آتش ذره‏اى در بيشه‏ها / اندر آن ذره شود بيشه فنا
entries.english: As a spark of fire lands amidst the trees, the whole forest passes away in that mote;

entries.farsi: چون خيالى در دل شه يا سپاه / ‏كرد اندر جنگ خصمان را تباه
entries.english: Or as when an image appears in the heart of a king or his army, destroyed his enemies in war.

entries.farsi: يك ستاره در محمد رخ نمود / تا فنا شد گوهر گبر و جهود
entries.english: A star appeared in Mohammed, so that the substance of Magi and Jew passed away.

entries.farsi: آن كه ايمان يافت رفت اندر امان / ‏كفرهاى باقيان شد دو گمان
entries.english: He that received the Faith entered into security; the infidelities of the rest became two opinions.

entries.farsi: كفر صرف اولين بارى نماند / يا مسلمانى و يا بيمى نشاند
entries.english: At any rate, their first pure unbelief did not remain: it planted either Islam or a dread of it.

entries.farsi: اين به حيله آب و روغن كردنى است / ‏اين مثلها كفو ذره‏ى نور نيست
entries.english: This is a temporary varnishing: these similes are not equivalent to the spark of Divine light.

entries.farsi: ذره نبود جز حقيرى منجسم / ‏ذره نبود شارق ال ينقسم
entries.english: A mote is only a paltry bodily thing: a mote is not the indivisible Sun.

entries.farsi: گفتن ذره مرادى دان خفى / ‏محرم دريا نه‏اى اين دم كفى
entries.english: Know that calling it the Sun a mote has a hidden purpose, for you are not familiar with the Sea: at present you are the foam.

entries.farsi: آفتاب نير ايمان شيخ / ‏گر نمايد رخ ز شرق جان شيخ
entries.english: If the luminous sun of the Shaykh’s Faith should display itself from the Orient of the Shaykh’s spirit,

entries.farsi: جمله پستى گنج گيرد تا ثرى / ‏جمله بالا خلد گيرد اخضرى
entries.english: All below, down to the moist clay, would gain treasure, and all above would gain a verdant Paradise.

entries.farsi: او يكى جان دارد از نور منير / او يكى تن دارد از خاك حقير
entries.english: He has a spirit of resplendent light; he has a body of despicable earth.

entries.farsi: اى عجب اين است او يا آن بگو / كه بماندم اندر اين مشكل عمو
entries.english: Oh, I wonder whether he is this or that. Tell, uncle, for I am left helpless in this difficulty.

entries.farsi: گر وى اين است اى برادر چيست آن / ‏پر شده از نور او هفت آسمان
entries.english: O brother, if he is this, what is that?— for the Seven Heavens are filled with its light—

entries.farsi: ور وى آن است اين بدن اى دوست چيست / ‏اى عجب زين دو كدامين است و كيست
entries.english: And if he is that, what is this body, my friend? Oh, I wonder which of these two he is and who?

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن زن كه گفت شوهر را كه گوشت را گربه خورد شوهر گربه را به ترازو بر كشيد گربه نيم من بر آمد گفت اى زن گوشت نيم من بود و افزون اگر اين گوشت است گربه كو و اگر اين گربه است گوشت كو

sections.title_en: Story of the woman who told her husband that the cat had eaten the meat, the husband put the cat in the balance. Her weight amounted to half a “pound”, he said, “O wife, the meat weighed half a ‘pound’ and more. If this is the meat, where is the cat? Or if this is the cat, where is the meat?”

entries.farsi: بود مردى كدخدا او را زنى / ‏سخت طناز و پليد و ره زنى
entries.english: There was a man, a householder, who had a very sneering, dirty, and rapacious wife.

entries.farsi: هر چه آوردى تلف كرديش زن / ‏مرد مضطر بود اندر تن زدن
entries.english: Whatever he brought, his wife would consume it, and the man was forced to keep silence.

entries.farsi: بهر مهمان گوشت آورد آن معيل / ‏سوى خانه با دو صد جهد طويل
entries.english: That family man brought home, for a guest, meat which he had procured with infinite pains.

entries.farsi: زن بخوردش با كباب و با شراب / ‏مرد آمد گفت دفع ناصواب
entries.english: His wife ate it up with kabab and wine: the man came in; she put him off with useless words.

entries.farsi: مرد گفتش گوشت كو مهمان رسيد / پيش مهمان لوت مى‏بايد كشيد
entries.english: The man said to her, “Where is the meat? The guest has arrived: one must set nice food before a guest.”

entries.farsi: گفت زن اين گربه خورد آن گوشت را / گوشت ديگر خر اگر باشد هال
entries.english: “This cat has eaten the meat,” she replied: “hey, go and buy some more meat if you can!”

entries.farsi: گفت اى ايبك ترازو را بيار / گربه را من بر كشم اندر عيار
entries.english: He said, “O Aybak, fetch the balance: I will weigh the cat.

entries.farsi: بر كشيدش بود گربه نيم من / ‏پس بگفت آن مرد كاى محتال زن
entries.english: He weighed her. The cat was half a pound (mann). Then the man said, “O deceitful wife,

entries.farsi: گوشت نيم من بود افزون يك ستير / هست گربه نيم من هم اى ستير
entries.english: The meat was half a pound and one ounce (sitir) over; the cat is just half a pound, my lady.

entries.farsi: اين اگر گربست پس آن گوشت كو / ور بود اين گوشت گربه كو بجو
entries.english: If this is the cat, then where is the meat? Or, if this is the meat, where is the cat? Search for her!”

entries.farsi: بايزيد ار اين بود آن روح چيست / ‏ور وى آن روح است اين تصوير كيست
entries.english: If Bayazid is this, what is that spirit? And if he is that spirit, who is this bodily image?

entries.farsi: حيرت اندر حيرت است اى يار من / ‏اين نه كار تست و نه هم كار من
entries.english: It is bewilderment on bewilderment. O my friend, this is not your affair, nor is it mine either.

entries.farsi: حكمت اين اضداد را با هم ببست / ‏اى قصاب اين گردران با گردن است
entries.english: He is both, but in the corn-crop the grain is fundamental, while the stalk is derivative.

entries.farsi: هر دو او باشد و ليك از ريع زرع / ‏دانه باشد اصل و آن كه پره فرع
entries.english: Divine Wisdom has bound these contraries together: O butcher, this fleshy thigh-bone goes along with the neck.

entries.farsi: روح بى‏قالب نداند كار كرد / قالبت بى‏جان فسرده بود و سرد
entries.english: The spirit cannot function without the body; your body is frozen and cold without the spirit.

entries.farsi: قالبت پيدا و آن جانت نهان / ‏راست شد زين هر دو اسباب جهان
entries.english: Your body is visible, while your spirit is hidden from view: the business of the world is conducted by means of them both.

entries.farsi: خاك را بر سر زنى سر نشكند / آب را بر سر زنى در نشكند
entries.english: If you throw earth at someone’s head, his head will not be broken; if you throw water at his head, it will not be broken.

entries.farsi: گر تو مى‏خواهى كه سر را بشكنى / ‏آب را و خاك را بر هم زنى
entries.english: If you wish to break his head, you bring the earth and the water into contact with each other.

entries.farsi: چون شكستى سر رود آبش به اصل / ‏خاك سوى خاك آيد روز فصل
entries.english: When you have broken your head, its water returns to its source, and earth returns to earth on the day of separation.

entries.farsi: حكمتى كه بود حق را ز ازدواج / ‏گشت حاصل از نياز و از لجاج
entries.english: The providential purpose that God had—namely, humble supplication or obstinate disobedience—was fulfilled by means of the marriage.

entries.farsi: باشد آن گه ازدواجات دگر / ال سمع اذن و ال عين بصر
entries.english: Then there are other marriages that no ear has heard and no eye has seen.

entries.farsi: گر شنيدى اذن كى ماندى اذن / ‏يا كجا كردى دگر ضبط سخن
entries.english: If the ear had heard, how should the ear have remained or how should it have apprehended words anymore?

entries.farsi: گر بديدى برف و يخ خورشيد را / از يخى برداشتى اوميد را
entries.english: If the snow and ice were to behold the sun, they would despair of iciness;

entries.farsi: آب گشتى بى‏عروق و بى‏گره / ‏ز آب داود هوا كردى زره
entries.english: They would become water devoid of roots and knobs: the air, David-like, would make of the water a mail-coat,

entries.farsi: پس شدى درمان جان هر درخت / ‏هر درختى از قدومش نيك بخت
entries.english: And then it would become a life-giving medicine for every tree: every tree would be made fortunate by its advent.

entries.farsi: آن يخى بفسرده در خود مانده / ‏المساسى با درختان خوانده
entries.english: The frozen ice that remains within itself cries to the trees, Touch me not!

entries.farsi: ليس يالف ليس يولف جسمه / ‏ليس الا شح نفس قسمه
entries.english: Its body makes none its friend nor is it made a friend by any: its portion is naught but miserly selfishness.

entries.farsi: نيست ضايع زو شود تازه جگر / ليك نبود پيك و سلطان خضر
entries.english: It is not wasted, the heart is refreshed by it; but it is not the herald and lord of greenery.

entries.farsi: اى اياز استاره‏ى تو بس بلند / نيست هر برجى عبورش را پسند
entries.english: “O Ayaz, you are a very exalted star: not every sign of the zodiac is worthy of its transit.

entries.farsi: هر وفا را كى پسندد همتت / ‏هر صفا را كى گزيند صفوتت
entries.english: How should your lofty spirit be satisfied with every loyalty? How should your pureness choose every sincerity?”

sections.title_fa: حكايت آن امير كه غالم را گفت كه مى بيار غالم رفت و سبوى مى مى‏آورد در راه زاهدى بود امر معروف كرد زد سنگى و سبو را بشكست امير بشنيد و قصد گوشمال زاهد كرد، و آن قصه در عهد دين عيسى عليه السلام بود كه هنوز مى حرام نشده بود و ليكن زاهد تقززى مى‏كرد و از تنعم منع مى‏كرد

sections.title_en: Story of the Amír who bade his slave fetch some wine: the slave went off and was bringing a jug of wine, an ascetic was on the road admonished him that he should act righteously and threw a stone and smashed the jug; the Amír heard and resolved to punish the ascetic. That happened in the epoch of the religion of Jesus, on whom be peace, when wine
had not yet been declared unlawful; but the ascetic was showing an abhorrence and preventing from indulging themselves.

entries.farsi: بود اميرى خوش دلى مى باره‏اى / ‏كهف هر مخمور و هر بى‏چاره‏اى
