entries.english: A certain man had forty dirhems in his hand: every night he would throw one into the sea-water,

entries.farsi: با مسلمانان به كر او پيش رفت / ‏وقت فر او وانگشت از خصم تفت
entries.english: In order that the long agony suffered in deliberation might become grievous to the illusory soul.

entries.farsi: تا كه گردد سخت بر نفس مجاز / در تانى درد جان كندن دراز
entries.english: He advanced with the Moslems to attack, in the hour of retreat he did not fall back in haste before the enemy.

entries.farsi: زخم ديگر خورد آن را هم ببست / ‏بيست كرت رمح و تير از وى شكست
entries.english: He was wounded again, he bound up those too: twenty times were the spears and arrows broken by him.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن قوت نماند افتاد پيش / ‏مقعد صدق او ز صدق عشق خويش
entries.english: After that, no strength remained: he fell forward the seat of truth because his love was true.

entries.farsi: صدق جان دادن بود هين سابقوا / از نبى بر خوان رِجالٌ صدقوا
entries.english: Truth consists in giving up the soul. Listen, try to outstrip in the race! Recite from the Qur’án men who have been true.

entries.farsi: اين همه مردن نه مرگ صورت است / ‏اين بدن مر روح را چون آلت است
entries.english: All this dying is not the death of the form: this body is like an instrument for the spirit.

entries.farsi: آلتش بشكست و ره زن زنده ماند / نفس زنده‏ست ار چه مركب خون فشاند
entries.english: Oh, there is many a raw one whose blood was shed externally, but whose living fleshly soul escaped to yonder side.

entries.farsi: اى بسا خامى كه ظاهر خونش ريخت / ‏ليك نفس زنده آن جانب گريخت
entries.english: Its instrument was shattered, but the brigand was left alive: the fleshly soul is living though that on which it rode has bled to death. His horse was killed before his road was traversed: he became naught but ignorant and wicked and miserable.

entries.farsi: سب كشت و راه او رفته نشد / جز كه خام و زشت و آشفته نشد
entries.english: If a martyr were made by every bit of bloodshed, an infidel killed also would be a Bu Sa’id.

entries.farsi: گر به هر خون ريزيى گشتى شهيد / كافرى كشته بدى هم بو سعيد
entries.english: Oh, there is many a trusty martyred soul that has died in this world; it is going about like the living.

entries.farsi: اى بسا نفس شهيد معتمد / مرده در دنيا چو زنده مى‏رود
entries.english: The brigand spirit has died, though the body, which is its sword, survives: it is in the hand of that eager warrior.

entries.farsi: روح ره زن مرد و تن كه تيغ اوست / ‏هست باقى در كف آن غزو جوست
entries.english: The sword is that sword, the man is not that man; but this appearance is a cause of bewilderment to you.

entries.farsi: تيغ آن تيغ است مرد آن مرد نيست / ‏ليك اين صورت ترا حيران كنى است
entries.english: When the soul is transformed, this sword, namely, the body, remains in the hand of the action of the Beneficent.

entries.farsi: نفس چون مبدل شود اين تيغ تن / ‏باشد اندر دست صنع ذو المنن
entries.english: The one is a man whose food is entirely love; the other is a man hollow as dust.

sections.title_fa: صفت كردن مرد غماز و نمودن صورت كنيزك مصور در كاغذ و عاشق شدن خليفه‏ى مصر و ‏فرستادن خليفه اميرى را با سپاه گران به در موصل و قتل و ويرانى بسيار كردن بهر اين غرض
sections.title_en: How an informer described a girl and exhibited the picture of her on paper, and how the Caliph
of Egypt fell in love with it and sent an Amír with a mighty army to the gates of Mosul and made great slaughter and devastation for the purpose of obtaining the girl.

entries.farsi: آن يكى مردى است قوتش جمله درد / اين دگر مردى ميان تى همچو گرد
entries.english: An informer said to the Caliph of Egypt, “The King of Mosul is wedded to a houri.

entries.farsi: مر خليفه‏ى مصر را غماز گفت / ‏كه شه موصل به حورى گشت جفت
entries.english: He holds in his arms a girl like whom there is no beauty in the world.

entries.farsi: يك كنيزك دارد او اندر كنار / كه به عالم نيست مانندش نگار
entries.english: She does not admit of description, for her loveliness is beyond limits: here is her portrait on paper.”

entries.farsi: در بيان نايد كه حسنش بى‏حد است / ‏نقش او اين است كاندر كاغذ است
entries.english: When the Emperor saw the portrait on the paper, he became distraught and the cup dropped from his hand.

entries.farsi: نقش در كاغذ چو ديد آن كيقباد / خيره گشت و جام از دستش فتاد
entries.english: Immediately he dispatched to Mosul a captain with a very mighty army,

entries.farsi: كه اگر ندهد به تو آن ماه را / بر كن از بن آن در و درگاه را
entries.english: Saying, “If he will not give up that moon to you, raze his court and palace to the ground;

entries.farsi: ور دهد تركش كن و مه را بيار / تا كشم من بر زمين مه در كنار
entries.english: But if he gives her up, leave him alone and bring the moon, that on the earth I may embrace the moon.”

entries.farsi: پهلوان شد سوى موصل با حشم / ‏با هزاران رستم و طبل و علم
entries.english: The captain set out towards Mosul with his retinue and with thousands of heroes and drums and banners.

entries.farsi: چون ملخها بى‏عدد بر گرد كشت / ‏قاصد اهالك اهل شهر گشت
entries.english: With an army like innumerable locusts round the crops, he resolved to destroy the inhabitants of the city.

entries.farsi: هر نواحى منجنيقى از نبرد / همچو كوه قاف او بر كار كرد
entries.english: On every side he brought into hostile action a catapult like Mount Qáf.

entries.farsi: زخم تير و سنگهاى منجنيق / ‏تيغها در گرد چون برق از بريق
entries.english: Wounds by arrows and by stones from the catapult; swords amidst the dust, like lightning from a lightning-cloud.

entries.farsi: هفته‏اى كرد اين چنين خون ريز گرم / ‏برج سنگين سست شد چون موم نرم
entries.english: During a week he wrought such carnage in hot fight: stone towers became unsteady as soft wax.

entries.farsi: شاه موصل ديد پيكار مهول / ‏پس فرستاد از درون پيشش رسول
entries.english: The King of Mosul saw the terrible combat: then he sent an envoy from within to him,

entries.farsi: كه چه مى‏خواهى ز خون مومنان / ‏كشته مى‏گردند زين حرب گران
entries.english: To say, “What do you wish by shedding the blood of true believers? They are being killed in this grievous war.

entries.farsi: گر مرادت ملك شهر موصل است / ‏بى‏چنين خون ريز اينت حاصل است
entries.english: If your object is to gain possession of the city of Mosul, look now, it is achieved without bloodshed like this.

entries.farsi: من روم بيرون شهر اينك در آ / تا نگيرد خون مظلومان ترا
entries.english: I will go forth from the city: here it is for you, enter in, lest the blood of the oppressed lay hold of you;

entries.farsi: ور مرادت مال و زر و گوهر است / ‏اين ز ملك شهر خود آسان‏تر است
entries.english: And if your object is riches and gold and jewels, this is even easier than to take possession of the city.”

sections.title_fa: ايثار كردن صاحب موصل آن كنيزك را به خليفه تا خون ريزى مسلمانان بيشتر نشود
sections.title_en: How the lord of Mosul surrendered the girl to the Caliph in order that there might be no more shedding of Moslem blood.

entries.farsi: چون رسول آمد به پيشش پهلوان / ‏داد كاغذ اندر او نقش و نشان
entries.english: When the envoy came to the captain, he gave him the paper, on which the girl’s features were depicted,

entries.farsi: بنگر اندر كاغذ اين را طالبم / ‏هين بده ور نه كنون من غالبم
entries.english: “Look on the paper: this I require. Listen, give, or else I will take her by force, for I am the conqueror.”

entries.farsi: چون رسول آمد بگفت آن شاه نر / صورتى كم گير زود اين را ببر
entries.english: On the return of the envoy, that manly King said, “Take no account of a form, lead her away at once.

entries.farsi: من نيم در عهد ايمان بت پرست / ‏بت بر آن بت پرست اوليتر است
entries.english: I am not an idolater in the epoch of the true Faith: it is more fit that the idol should be in the hands of the idolater.”

entries.farsi: چون كه آوردش رسول آن پهلوان / ‏گشت عاشق بر جمالش آن زمان
entries.english: When the envoy brought her, the captain straightway fell in love with her beauty.

entries.farsi: دور گردونها ز موج عشق دان / ‏گر نبودى عشق بفسردى جهان
entries.english: Love is an ocean, on which the heavens are a flake of foam: like Zalikha’s desire for a Joseph.

entries.farsi: عشق بحرى آسمان بر وى كفى / ‏چون زليخا در هواى يوسفى
entries.english: Know that the wheeling heavens are turned by waves of Love: were it not for Love, the world would be frozen.

entries.farsi: كى جمادى محو گشتى در نبات / ‏كى فداى روح گشتى ناميات
entries.english: How would an inorganic thing disappear into a plant? How would vegetative things sacrifice themselves to become spirit?

entries.farsi: روح كى گشتى فداى آن دمى / ‏كز نسيمش حامله شد مريمى
entries.english: How would the spirit sacrifice itself for the sake of that Breath by the waft whereof a Mary was made pregnant?

entries.farsi: هر يكى بر جا ترنجيدى چو يخ / ‏كى بدى پران و جويان چون ملخ
entries.english: Each one would be stiff and immovable as ice: how should they be flying and seeking like locusts?

entries.farsi: ذره ذره عاشقان آن كمال / ‏مى‏شتابد در علو همچون نهال
entries.english: Every mote is in love with that Perfection and hastening upward like a sapling.

entries.farsi: سَبَّحَ للِهَِّ هست اشتابشان / ‏تنقيه‏ى تن مى‏كنند از بهر جان
entries.english: Their haste is “Glory to God!” They are purifying the body for the sake of the spirit.

entries.farsi: پهلوان چه را چو ره پنداشته / ‏شوره‏اش خوش آمده حب كاشته
entries.english: The captain deemed a pit to be like a road: to him the sterile soil appeared good, he sowed seed.

entries.farsi: چون خيالى ديد آن خفته به خواب / ‏جفت شد با آن و از وى رفت آب
entries.english: When that sleeper saw a (fantasised) image (of the beloved) in a dream, he coupled with it and (seminal) fluid flowed from him.

entries.farsi: چون برفت آن خواب شد بيدار زود / ديد كان لعبت به بيدارى نبود
entries.english: When the dream departed and he woke up at once, he saw that that doll was not (present) in wakefulness

entries.farsi: گفت بر هيچ آب خود بردم دريغ / ‏عشوه‏ى آن عشوه ده خوردم دريغ
entries.english: He said: “Alas! I have borne my water (semen) for nothing. Alas! I have swallowed the flirtatious tricks of that trickster (image).

entries.farsi: پهلوان تن بد آن مردى نداشت / ‏تخم مردى در چنان ريگى بكاشت
entries.english: That one was a captain of the body (only), he lacked (true) manliness: he sowed the seed of manliness in such (a place of) sand.

entries.farsi: مركب عشقش دريده صد لگام / ‏نعره مى‏زد الابالى بالحمام
entries.english: The steed of his love tore up a hundred bridles: he was shouting, “I care naught for death.

entries.farsi: ايش ابالى بالخليفة فى الهوى / ‏استوى عندى وجودى و التوى
entries.english: What should I care about the Caliph? In love, my life and death are the same to me.”

entries.farsi: اين چنين سوزان و گرم آخر مكار / مشورت كن با يكى خاوند گار
entries.english: Please, do not sow with such ardour and heat: take counsel with a master.

entries.farsi: مشورت كو عقل كو سيالب آز / در خرابى كرد ناخنها دراز
entries.english: Where is counsel, where is reason, the torrent of cupidity has extended its talons to destroy?

entries.farsi: بين ايدى سد و سوى خلف سد / پيش و پس كم بيند آن مفتون خد
entries.english: A barrier in front and a barrier behind; he that is fascinated by a cheek does not see before or behind.

entries.farsi: آمده در قصد جان سيل سياه / ‏تا كه روبه افكند شيرى به چاه
entries.english: The black torrent comes to take his life, so that a fox may hurl a lion into the well.

entries.farsi: از چهى بنموده معدومى خيال / ‏تا در اندازد اسودا كالجبال
entries.english: Something non-existent causes a phantom to appear in a well, in order that it may cast into it lions as mountains.

entries.farsi: هيچ كس را با زنان محرم مدار / كه مثال اين دو پنبه‏ست و شرار
entries.english: Do not have any one intimate with your womenfolk, for these two may be compared to cotton and sparks of fire.

entries.farsi: آتشى بايد بشسته ز آب حق / ‏همچو يوسف معتصم اندر رهق
entries.english: It needs a fire quenched by God’s water, one that like Joseph holds fast in evil temptation,

entries.farsi: كز زليخاى لطيف سرو قد / همچو شيران خويشتن را واكشد
entries.english: To withdraw itself as lions from a charming Zalikha tall and slender as a cypress.

entries.farsi: باز گشت از موصل و مى‏شد به راه / ‏تا فرود آمد به بيشه و مرج گاه
entries.english: He turned back from Mosul and went on his way till he encamped in a wooded meadowland.

entries.farsi: آتش عشقش فروزان آن چنان / ‏كه نداند او زمين از آسمان
entries.english: The fire of his love was blazing in such wise that he could not distinguish earth from heaven.

entries.farsi: قصد آن مه كرد اندر خيمه او / عقل كو و از خليفه خوف كو
entries.english: He sought to embrace that moon in her tent: where was his reason and his dread of the Caliph?

entries.farsi: چون زند شهوت در اين وادى دهل / ‏چيست عقل تو فجل ابن الفجل
entries.english: When lust beats the drum in this vale, what is your reason? A radish and the son of a radish.

entries.farsi: صد خليفه گشته كمتر از مگس / ‏پيش چشم آتشينش آن نفس
entries.english: To his fiery eye a hundred Caliphs seemed at that moment less than a gnat.

entries.farsi: چون برون انداخت شلوار و نشست / ‏در ميان پاى زن آن زن پرست
entries.english: He jumped up (and ran) naked of buttocks to the (army) ranks, grasping a fiery (flashing) scimitar in his hand.

entries.farsi: چون ذكر سوى مقر مى‏رفت راست / ‏رستخيز و غلغل از لشكر بخاست
entries.english: When (his) penis was going straight toward the place (of pleasure), a commotion and outcry arose from the army.

entries.farsi: بر جهيد و كون برهنه سوى صف / ذو الفقار همچو آتش او بكف‏
entries.english: He jumped up (and ran) naked of buttocks to the (army) ranks, grasping a fiery (flashing) scimitar in his hand.

entries.farsi: ديد شير نر سيه از نيستان / ‏بر زده بر قلب لشكر ناگهان
entries.english: He saw that a fierce black lion from the jungle had suddenly rushed upon the centre of the army;

entries.farsi: تازيان چون ديو در جوش آمده / ‏هر طويله و خيمه اندر هم زده
entries.english: The Arab horses were demoniacally excited, every stable and tent was in confusion;

entries.farsi: شير نر گنبد همى‏كرد از لغز / در هوا چون موج دريا بيست گز
entries.english: The fierce lion from the covert was bounding twenty ells into the air, like billows of the sea.

entries.farsi: پهلوان مردانه بود و بى‏حذر / پيش شير آمد چو شير مست نر
entries.english: The captain was manful and intrepid: he advanced, like a furious lion, to meet the lion.

entries.farsi: زد به شمشير و سرش را بر شكافت / ‏زود سوى خيمه‏ى مه رو شتافت
entries.english: He struck it with his sword and clove its head; at once he hastened to the tent of the beauty.

entries.farsi: چون كه خود را او بدان حورى نمود / مردى او همچنان بر پاى بود
entries.english: When he showed himself to the (woman as lovely as an) houri, his (organ of) virility was erect in the same manner (as before).

entries.farsi: با چنان شيرى به چالش گشت جفت / ‏مردى او مانده بر پاى و نخفت
entries.english: He joined in battle with such a lion: (yet) his (organ of) virility remained erect and did not lie down.

entries.farsi: آن بت شيرين لقاى ماه رو / در عجب درماند از مردى او
entries.english: That moon-faced idol, sweet of countenance, was amazed at his virility.

entries.farsi: جفت شد با او به شهوت آن زمان / ‏متحد گشتند حالى آن دو جان
entries.english: She became joined with him (eagerly) at that time with lust: those two souls immediately became united.

entries.farsi: ز اتصال اين دو جان با همدگر / مى‏رسد از غيبشان جانى دگر
entries.english: Through the union of these two souls with one another, there will come to them from the Unseen World another soul.

entries.farsi: رو نمايد از طريق زادنى / ‏گر نباشد از علوقش ره زنى
entries.english: It will appear by the road of birth, if there is nothing to waylay its conception.

entries.farsi: ليك اندر غيب زايد آن صور / چون روى آن سو ببينى در نظر
entries.english: Wherever two persons unite in a love or hate, a third will certainly be born;

entries.farsi: هر كجا دو كس به مهرى يا به كين / ‏جمع آيد ثالثى زايد يقين
entries.english: But those forms are born in the Unseen World: when you go there, you will see them in clearly.

entries.farsi: آن نتايج از قرانات تو زاد / هين مگرد از هر قرينى زود شاد
entries.english: That progeny is born of your associations: beware, do not rejoice too soon in any associate.

entries.farsi: كز عمل زاييده‏اند و از علل / ‏هر يكى را صورت و نطق و طلل
entries.english: Remain in expectation of the appointed time: recognise the truth of the promise that the offspring shall join their parents;

entries.farsi: منتظر مى‏باش آن ميقات را / صدق دان الحاق ذريات را
entries.english: For they are born of action and causes: each one hath form and speech and dwelling-place.

entries.farsi: بانگشان در مى‏رسد ز آن خوش حجال / ‏كاى ز ما غافل هال زوتر تعال
entries.english: Their cry is coming from those delightful bowers— “O you who have forgotten us, listen, come with all speed!”

entries.farsi: منتظر در غيب جان مرد و زن / ‏مول مولت چيست زوتر گام زن
entries.english: The soul of man and woman is waiting in the Unseen: why are you delaying? Step forward at once.

entries.farsi: راه گم كرد او از آن صبح دروغ / ‏چون مگس افتاد اندر ديگ دوغ
entries.english: He lost his way and, by that false dawn, fell like a gnat into the pot of buttermilk.

sections.title_fa: پشيمان شدن آن سر لشكر از جنايت كه كرد و سوگند دادن او آن كنيزك را كه به خليفه باز نگويد از ‏آن چه رفت
sections.title_en: How that military chief repented of the sin which he had committed and begged the girl not to tell
the Caliph anything of what had happened.

entries.farsi: داد سوگندش كه اى خورشيد رو / با خليفه زين چه شد رمزى مگو
entries.english: He was absorbed in that for a while, afterwards he repented of that grievous crime,

entries.farsi: چند روزى هم بر آن بد بعد از آن / ‏شد پشيمان او از آن جرم گران
entries.english: And begged her, saying, “O you whose face is like the sun, do not give the Caliph any hint of what has passed.”

entries.farsi: چون بديد او را خليفه مست گشت / ‏پس ز بام افتاد او را نيز طشت
entries.english: When the Caliph saw her he became distraught, and then too his secret was exposed to all.

entries.farsi: ديد صد چندان كه وصفش كرده بود / كى بود خود ديده مانند شنود
entries.english: He saw a hundred times as beautiful as he had described her: how in truth should seeing be like hearing?

entries.farsi: وصف تصوير است بهر چشم هوش / صورت آن چشم دان نه ز آن گوش
entries.english: Description is a picture for the eye of intelligence: know that the form belongs to the eye, not to the ear.

entries.farsi: كرد مردى از سخن دانى سؤال / ‏حق و باطل چيست اى نيكو مقال
entries.english: A certain man asked an eloquent person, “What are truth and falsehood, O man of goodly discourse?”

entries.farsi: گوش را بگرفت و گفت اين باطل است / ‏چشم حق است و يقينش حاصل است
entries.english: He took hold of his ear and said, “This is false: the eye is true and possesses certainty.”

entries.farsi: آن به نسبت باطل آمد پيش اين / ‏نسبت است اغلب سخنها اى امين
entries.english: The former is relatively false as compared with the latter: most sayings are relative, O trusty one.

entries.farsi: ز آفتاب ار كرد خفاش احتجاب / ‏نيست محجوب از خيال آفتاب
entries.english: If the bat screens itself from the sun, it is not screened from the fancy of the sun.

entries.farsi: خوف او را خود خيالش مى‏دهد / آن خيالش سوى ظلمت مى‏كشد
entries.english: Even the idea of the sun puts fear into the bat: that fancy leads it towards the darkness.

entries.farsi: آن خيال نور مى‏ترساندش / ‏بر شب ظلمات مى‏چفساندش
entries.english: That idea of the light terrifies it and causes it to become attached to the night of gloom.

entries.farsi: از خيال دشمن و تصوير اوست / ‏كه تو بر چفسيده‏اى بر يار و دوست
entries.english: It is from the idea and the picture of your enemy that you have become attached to your comrade and friend.

entries.farsi: موسيا كشفت لمع بر كه فراشت / ‏آن مخيل تاب تحقيقت نداشت
entries.english: O Moses, the revelation given to you illumined the mountain; the fancy conceiving could not endure your real experience.

entries.farsi: هين مشو غره بدان كه قابلى / ‏مر خيالش را و زين ره واصلى
entries.english: Listen be not deluded by that you are able to conceive the fancy thereof and by this means can attain.

entries.farsi: از خيال حرب نهراسيد كس / ‏ال شجاعه قبل حرب اين دان و بس
entries.english: No one was ever terrified by the mere idea of war: there is no bravery before war. Know this, and it is enough.

entries.farsi: بر خيال حرب هيز اندر فكر / مى‏كند چون رستمان صد كر و فر
entries.english: Possessed with the idea of war, the coward makes, in his thoughts, a hundred heroic attacks.

entries.farsi: نقش رستم كان به حمامى بود / قرن حمله‏ى فكر هر خامى بود
entries.english: The antagonist in the mind of every raw is the picture of Rustam that may be in a bath-house.

entries.farsi: اين خيال سمع چون مبصر شود / هيز چه بود رستمى مضطر شود
entries.english: When this idea derived from hearing becomes visible, what of the poltroon? A Rustam is compelled.

entries.farsi: جهد كن كز گوش در چشمت رود / آن چه كان باطل بده‏ست آن حق شود
entries.english: Endeavour that it may pass from your ear into your eye, and that what has been unreal may become real.

entries.farsi: ز آن سپس گوشت شود هم طبع چشم / ‏گوهرى گردد دو گوش همچو پشم
entries.english: After that, your ear will become connatural with your eye: the two ears, as wool, will become of pure substance;

entries.farsi: بلكه جمله‏ى تن چو آيينه شود / جمله چشم و گوهر سينه شود
entries.english: Nay, your whole body will become like a mirror: it will become all eye and pure spiritual substance.

entries.farsi: گوش انگيزد خيال و آن خيال / ‏هست دالله‏ى وصال آن جمال
entries.english: The ear rouses a fancy, and that fancy is the go-between to union with that Beauty.

entries.farsi: آن خليفه‏ى گول هم يك چند نيز / ريش گاوى كرد خوش با آن كنيز
entries.english: Endeavour that this fancy may increase, so that the go-between may become a guide for Majnun.

entries.farsi: جهد كن تا اين خيال افزون شود / تا دالله رهبر مجنون شود
entries.english: That foolish Caliph, too, was mightily infatuated for awhile with that girl.

entries.farsi: ملك را تو ملك غرب و شرق گير / چون نمى‏ماند تو آن را برق گير
entries.english: Suppose the empire is the empire of the West and the East: since it will not remain, deem it to be a lightning-flash.

entries.farsi: مملكت كان مى‏نماند جاودان / ‏اى دلت خفته تو آن را خواب دان
entries.english: O you, whose heart is slumbering, know that the kingdom that does not remain unto everlasting is a dream.

entries.farsi: تا چه خواهى كرد آن باد و بروت / ‏كه بگيرد همچو جالدى گلوت
entries.english: Consider what you will do with that vanity and vainglory; for it will grip your throat like an executioner.

entries.farsi: هم در اين عالم بدان كه مأمنى است / ‏از منافق كم شنو كاو گفت نيست
entries.english: Know that even in this world there is a safe refuge: do not listen to the hypocrite who says there is none.

sections.title_fa: ‏حجت منكران آخرت و بيان ضعف آن حجت زيرا حجت ايشان بدين باز مى‏گردد كه غير اين نمى‏بينيم
sections.title_en: The argument of those who disbelieve in the after-life, and a demonstration of the weakness of that argument, since their argument amounts to “We do not see any other than this.”

entries.farsi: حجتش اين است گويد هر دمى / گر بدى چيزى دگر هم ديدمى
entries.english: This is his argument: he says at every moment, “If there were anything else, I should have seen it.”

entries.farsi: گر نبيند كودكى احوال عقل / ‏عاقلى هرگز كند از عقل نقل
entries.english: If a child does not see the various aspects of reason, will a rational person ever abandon reason?

entries.farsi: ور نبيند عاقلى احوال عشق / ‏كم نگردد ماه نيكو فال عشق
entries.english: And if a rational person does not see the various aspects of Love, the auspicious moon of Love does not wane.

entries.farsi: حسن يوسف ديده‏ى اخوان نديد / از دل يعقوب كى شد ناپديد
entries.english: Joseph’s beauty was not seen by the eyes of his brethren, when did it disappear from the heart of Jacob?

entries.farsi: مر عصا را چشم موسى چوب ديد / چشم غيبى افعى و آشوب ديد
entries.english: The eye of Moses regarded the staff as wood; the eye of the Invisible beheld a serpent and panic.

entries.farsi: چشم سر با چشم سر در جنگ بود / غالب آمد چشم سر حجت نمود
entries.english: The eye of the head was in conflict with the eye of the heart: the eye of the heart prevailed and displayed the proof.

entries.farsi: اين سخن پايان ندارد در كمال / ‏پيش هر محروم باشد چون خيال
entries.english: The eye of Moses regarded his hand as a hand, to the eye of the Invisible it was a manifest light.

entries.farsi: چشم موسى دست خود را دست ديد / پيش چشم غيب نورى بد پديد
entries.english: This matter hath no limit in perfection, it seems like a fancy to everyone that is deprived.

entries.farsi: چون حقيقت پيش او فرج و گلوست / ‏كم بيان كن پيش او اسرار دوست
entries.english: Since to him the reality is the pudendum and the gullet, do not expound the mysteries of the Beloved to him.

entries.farsi: پيش ما فرج و گلو باشد خيال / ‏الجرم هر دم نمايد جان جمال
entries.english: To us the genitals and the stomach are an idea; consequently the Soul displays His beauty at every moment.

entries.farsi: هر كه را فرج و گلو آيين و خوست / ‏آن لكم دين وَ لِيَ دِينِ بهر اوست
entries.english: Anyone who is addicted the genitals and the stomach, for him is “To you your religion and to me my religion.”

entries.farsi: با چنان انكار كوته كن سخن / ‏احمدا كم گوى با گبر كهن
entries.english: Cut short your talk with such skepticism: do not converse, O Ahmad, with the ancient infidel.

sections.title_fa: ‏آمدن خليفه نزد آن خوب روى براى جماع
sections.title_en: How the Caliph came next to that one of beautiful face for the sake of (sexual) intercourse.

entries.farsi: آن خليفه كرد راى اجتماع / ‏سوى آن زن رفت از بهر جماع
entries.english: The Caliph decided on a meeting; he went to that woman for the sake of (sexual) intercourse.

entries.farsi: ذكر او كرد و ذكر بر پاى كرد / قصد خفت و خيز مهرافزاى كرد
entries.english: He called her and (his) penis became erect; he made the intention for (moving) back and forth with love-increaser.

entries.farsi: چون ميان پاى آن خاتون نشست / ‏پس قضا آمد ره عيشش ببست
entries.english: When he sat between the legs of that lady, then the Decree (of God) came (and) blocked the way to his pleasure.

entries.farsi: خشت و خشت موش در گوشش رسيد / خفت كيرش شهوتش كلى رميد
entries.english: The rustling (sounds) of a mouse reached his ear: his penis lay down; his lust was completely scared away;

entries.farsi: وهم آن كز مار باشد اين صرير / كه همى‏جنبد به تندى از حصير
entries.english: His imagination (was) that this grating sound was from a snake which was violently moving (out) from the (straw) mat.

sections.title_fa: ‏خنده گرفتن آن كنيزك را از ضعف شهوت خليفه و قوت شهوت آن امير و فهم كردن خليفه از خنده‏ى كنيزك
sections.title_en: How laughter took hold of that maidservant because of the weakness of the Caliph’s sexual desire compared to the strength of the Captain’s lust, and how the Caliph understood about the maidservant’s laughter.

entries.farsi: زن بديد آن سستى او از شگفت / ‏آمد اندر قهقهه خنده‏ش گرفت
entries.english: The woman saw his weakness (and), from astonishment, began bursting out laughing; laughter (completely) took hold of her.

entries.farsi: يادش آمد مردى آن پهلوان / ‏كه بكشت او شير و اندامش چنان
entries.english: She remembered the virility of that Captain who killed the lion and (with) his (male) organ like that.

entries.farsi: غالب آمد خنده‏ى زن شد دراز / جهد مى‏كرد و نمى‏شد لب فراز
entries.english: The woman’s lauaghter overpowered and was prolonged: she tried har but her lips wuld not shut.

entries.farsi: سخت مى‏خنديد همچون بنگيان / ‏غالب آمد خنده بر سود و زيان
entries.english: She kept laughing violently like beng-eaters: her laughter overpowered gain or loss.

entries.farsi: هر چه انديشيد خنده مى‏فزود / همچو بند سيل ناگاهان گشود
entries.english: Everything that she thought of increased her laughter, as a flood-gate is suddenly opened.

entries.farsi: گريه و خنده غم و شادى دل / ‏هر يكى را معدنى دان مستقل
entries.english: Weeping and laughter and sorrow and joy of heart— know that each one has an independent source.

entries.farsi: هر يكى را مخزنى مفتاح آن / ‏اى برادر در كف فتاح دان
entries.english: Each one has a store-house: know, O brother that the key thereof is in the hand of the Opener.

entries.farsi: هيچ ساكن مى‏نشد آن خنده زو / پس خليفه طيره گشت و تند خو
entries.english: Her laughter was never ceasing: then the Caliph became enraged and fierce.

entries.farsi: زود شمشير از غالفش بر كشيد / گفت سر خنده واگو اى پليد
entries.english: He quickly drew his scimitar from its sheath and said, “Declare the secret cause of your laughter, O foul woman!

entries.farsi: در دلم زين خنده ظنى اوفتاد / راستى گو عشوه نتوانيم داد
entries.english: From this laughter a suspicion has come into my heart: tell the truth, you cannot cajole me.

entries.farsi: ور خالف راستى بفريبيم / ‏يا بهانه‏ى چرب آرى تو به دم
entries.english: And if you deceive me with falsehoods or idly utter glib excuses,

entries.farsi: من بدانم در دل من روشنى است / ‏بايدت گفتن هر آن چه گفتنى است
entries.english: I shall know there is light in my heart: you must tell everything that ought to be told.

entries.farsi: ر دل شاهان تو ماهى دان سطبر / گر چه گه گه شد ز غفلت زير ابر
entries.english: Know that in the heart of kings there is a mighty moon, though sometimes it is overclouded by forgetfulness.

entries.farsi: ك چراغى هست در دل وقت گشت / ‏وقت خشم و حرص آيد زير طشت
entries.english: In the heart there is a lamp with which one goes about; at times of anger and concupiscence it is put under the basin.

entries.farsi: آن فراست اين زمان يار من است / ‏گر نگويى آن چه حق گفتن است
entries.english: That clairvoyance accompanies me just now: unless you tell that which it is your duty to tell,

entries.farsi: من بدين شمشير برم گردنت / ‏سود نبود خود بهانه كردنت
entries.english: I will sever your neck with this scimitar: evasion will not avail you at all.

entries.farsi: ور بگويى راست آزادت كنم / ‏حق يزدان نشكنم شادت كنم
entries.english: And if you tell the truth, I will set you free: I will not violate the duty I owe to God, I will make you glad.”

entries.farsi: هفت مصحف آن زمان بر هم نهاد / خورد سوگند و چنين تقرير داد
entries.english: At the same moment he placed seven Qur’ans one on the top of another and swore an oath and thus confirmed his promise.

sections.title_fa: فاش كردن آن كنيزك آن راز را با خليفه از بيم زخم شمشير و اكراه خليفه كه راست گو سبب اين ‏خنده را و گر نه بكشمت
sections.title_en: How the girl disclosed the secret to the Caliph in fear of a blow of the sword, and how she was
forced by the Caliph, “Give a true account of the cause of your laughter or else I will kill you.”

entries.farsi: زن چو عاجز شد بگفت احوال را / مردى آن رستم صد زال را
entries.english: When the woman became helpless, she related what had happened to the manliness of that Rustam who was the son of a hundred Zals.

entries.farsi: شرح آن گردك كه اندر راه بود / يك به يك با آن خليفه وانمود
entries.english: She described to the Caliph, point by point, the bride-chamber that was on the route,

entries.farsi: باز اين سستى اين ناموس كوش / ‏كاو فرو مرد از يكى خش خشت موش
entries.english: His killing the lion and returning to the tent with the penis erect like the horn of a rhinoceros;

entries.farsi: شير كشتن سوى خيمه آمدن / ‏و آن ذكر قايم چو شاخ كرگدن
entries.english: (And) again, the weakness of this striver for honour (the male organ) which dropped dead because of the rustling (sounds) of a mouse.

entries.farsi: رازها را مى‏كند حق آشكار / چون بخواهد رست تخم بد مكار
entries.english: God is ever making the hidden things manifest: since they will grow up, do not sow bad seed.

entries.farsi: آب و ابر و آتش و اين آفتاب / ‏رازها را مى‏برآرد از تراب
entries.english: Rain and clouds and fire and this sun are ever bringing up the hidden things from the earth.

entries.farsi: اين بهار نو ز بعد برگ ريز / هست برهان وجود رستخيز
entries.english: This new springtide after the fall of the leaves is a proof of the existence of the Resurrection.

entries.farsi: در بهار آن سرها پيدا شود / هر چه خورده‏ست اين زمين رسوا شود
entries.english: In spring the secrets are revealed: whatever this Earth has eaten is exposed to view.

entries.farsi: بر دمد آن از دهان و از لبش / ‏تا پديد آرد ضمير و مذهبش
entries.english: It shoots up from her mouth and lips in order that she may bring to light her hidden mind and way.

entries.farsi: سر بيخ هر درختى و خورش / ‏جملگى پيدا شود آن بر سرش
entries.english: The secret of the root of every tree and its nutriment— the whole of that is plainly shown forth on its top.

entries.farsi: هر غمى كز وى تو دل آزرده‏اى / ‏از خمار مى بود كان خورده‏اى
entries.english: Every sorrow whereby you are sore in heart is the headache arising from the wine that you have drunk;

entries.farsi: ليك كى دانى كه آن رنج خمار / از كدامين مى بر آمد آشكار
entries.english: But how should you know from which wine that headache has arisen manifest?

entries.farsi: اين خمار اشكوفه‏ى آن دانه است / ‏آن شناسد كاگه و فرزانه است
entries.english: This crop-sickness is the blossom of that seed: he that is sagacious and wise will recognise it.

entries.farsi: شاخ و اشكوفه نماند دانه را / نطفه كى ماند تن مردانه را
entries.english: The bough and its blossom do not resemble the seed: how should semen resemble the body of man?

entries.farsi: نيست ماننده هيوال با اثر / دانه كى ماننده آمد با شجر
entries.english: The matter does not resemble the product: when did the seed resemble the tree?

entries.farsi: نطفه از نان است كى باشد چو نان / ‏مردم از نطفه‏ست كى باشد چنان
entries.english: Semen is of bread, how should it be like bread? Man comes from semen, how should he be like it?

entries.farsi: جنى از نار است كى ماند به نار / از بخار است ابر و نبود چون بخار
entries.english: The Jinni is from fire, how should he resemble fire? The cloud is from vapour, but it is not like vapour.

entries.farsi: از دم جبريل عيسى شد پديد / كى به صورت همچو او بد يا نديد
entries.english: Jesus was produced from the breath of Gabriel, when was he like him in form or comparable?

entries.farsi: آدم از خاك است كى ماند به خاك / ‏هيچ انگورى نمى‏ماند به تاك
entries.english: Adam is of earth, how should he resemble earth? No grape resembles the vine.

entries.farsi: كى بود دزدى به شكل پاى دار / كى بود طاعت چو خلد پايدار
entries.english: How should robbery look like the foot of the gallows? How should piety be like the everlasting abode?

entries.farsi: هيچ اصلى نيست مانند اثر / پس ندانى اصل رنج و درد سر
entries.english: No origin resembles its product; therefore you cannot know the origin of pain and headache.

entries.farsi: ليك بى‏اصلى نباشد اين جزا / بى‏گناهى كى برنجاند خدا
entries.english: But this punishment is not without an origin: how should God inflict pain without any sin?

entries.farsi: آن چه اصل است و كشنده‏ى آن شى است / ‏گر نمى‏ماند به وى هم از وى است
entries.english: That which is the origin and bringer-on of that thing— if it does not resemble it, still it originates from it.

entries.farsi: پس بدان رنجت نتيجه‏ى زلتى است / آفت اين ضربتت از شهوتى است
entries.english: Know, then, that your pain is the result of some lapse this woe with which you art stricken arises from some lust.

entries.farsi: سجده كن صد بار مى‏گو اى خدا / نيست اين غم غير در خورد و سزا
entries.english: If you cannot discern that sin by means of consideration, at once make humble entreaty and seek pardon.

entries.farsi: گر ندانى آن گنه را ز اعتبار / زود زارى كن طلب كن اغتفار
entries.english: Prostrate yourself a hundred times and keep saying, “O God, this pain is nothing but my due and desert.

entries.farsi: اى تو سبحان پاك از ظلم و ستم / ‏كى دهى بى‏جرم جان را درد و غم
entries.english: O You who are transcendent in holiness and free from injustice and oppression, how should You inflict grief and pain upon the soul when it has not sinned?

entries.farsi: من معين مى‏ندانم جرم را / ليك هم جرمى ببايد گرم را
entries.english: I do not know my sin definitely, but there must be a sin for the anguish.

entries.farsi: جون بپوشيدى سبب را ز اعتبار / دايما آن جرم را پوشيده دار
entries.english: Since You have concealed the cause from my consideration, do You always keep my sin concealed;

entries.farsi: كه جزا اظهار جرم من بود / كز سياست دزدى‏ام ظاهر شود
entries.english: For it would be retribution to disclose my sin, so that my thievery should be made manifest by punishment.”

sections.title_fa: عزم كردن شاه چون واقف شد بر آن خيانت كه بپوشاند و عفو كند و او را به او دهد و دانست كه آن فتنه جزاى او بود و قصد او بود و ظلم او بر صاحب موصل كه وَ مَنْ أَساءَ فَعَلَيْهاو إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِالْمِرْصادِ و ترسيدن كه اگر انتقام كند آن انتقام هم بر سر او آيد چنان كه اين ظلم و طمع بر سرش آمد

sections.title_en: How the monarch, on being acquainted with that act of treachery, resolved to conceal and pardon it and give her to him, and recognised that the tribulation was a punishment inflicted on him and was his attempt and the wrong which he had done to the lord of Mosul; for “and who does evil, it is against himself” and “lo, your Lord is on the watch”; and how he feared that, if he should avenge himself, the vengeance would recoil on his own head,
as this injustice and greed had recoiled upon him.

entries.farsi: شاه با خود آمد استغفار كرد / ياد جرم و زلت و اصرار كرد
entries.english: The monarch came to himself. He asked pardon and confessed his sin and lapse and persistence.

entries.farsi: گفت با خود آن چه كردم با كسان / ‏شد جزاى آن به جان من رسان
entries.english: He said to himself, “The retribution for what I did to certain persons has reached my soul.

entries.farsi: قصد جفت ديگران كردم ز جاه / ‏بر من آمد آن و افتادم به چاه
entries.english: From power I made an attempt on the concubine of another: that recoiled upon me and I fell into the pit.

entries.farsi: من در خانه‏ى كسى ديگر زدم / ‏او در خانه‏ى مرا زد الجرم
entries.english: I knocked at the door of another person’s house: consequently he knocked at the door of my house.”

entries.farsi: هر كه با اهل كسان شد فسق جو / اهل خود را دان كه قواد است او
entries.english: Whoever seeks to commit adultery with people’s wives, know that he is a pimp to his own wife;

entries.farsi: ز انكه مثل آن جزاى آن شود / چون جزاى سيئه مثلش بود
entries.english: For that is paid back by the like, since the retribution for an evil act is an act like unto it.

entries.farsi: غصب كردم از شه موصل كنيز / غصب كردند از من او را زود نيز
entries.english: Inasmuch as you have made a cord and pulled one like it towards yourself, you are a wittol and worse.

entries.farsi: چون سبب كردى كشيدى سوى خويش / ‏مثل آن را پس تو ديوثى و بيش
entries.english: “I took the king of Mosul’s concubine from him by force she was soon taken by force from me too.

entries.farsi: اوك امين من بد و الالى من / ‏خاينش كرد آن خيانتهاى من
entries.english: My treacherous deeds made a traitor of him who was my trusted friend and servant.

entries.farsi: نيست وقت كين گزارى و انتقام / ‏من به دست خويش كردم كار خام
entries.english: It is no time to inflict punishment and avenge myself: I prepared the disaster with my own hand.

entries.farsi: گر كشم كينه بر آن مير و حرم / ‏آن تعدى هم بيايد بر سرم
entries.english: If I wreak vengeance on the Amír and the woman that trespass also will come on my head,

entries.farsi: همچنانك اين يك بيامد در جزا / آزمودم باز نه زمايم و را
entries.english: Just as this one has come in retribution: I have tried Him, I will not try Him again.

entries.farsi: درد صاحب موصلم گردن شكست / ‏من نيارم اين دگر را نيز خست
entries.english: The grief of the lord of Mosul has broken my neck: I dare not wound this other man as well.

entries.farsi: داد حق‏مان از مكافات آگهى / ‏گفت ان عدتم به عدنا به
entries.english: God hath given us information concerning retribution: He hath said, ‘If you repeat it, We shall repeat it.’

entries.farsi: چون فزونى كردن اينجا سود نيست / ‏غير صبر و مرحمت محمود نيست
entries.english: Since in this case it is useless to commit excess, nothing but patience and mercy is praiseworthy.

entries.farsi: ربنا انا ظلمنا سهو رفت / ‏رحمتى كن اى رحيميهات زفت
entries.english: O our Lord, verily we have done wrong, a fault has occurred: perform an act of mercy, O You whose mercifulness is mighty!

entries.farsi: عفو كردم تو هم از من عفو كن / ‏از گناه نو ز زالت كهن
entries.english: I have pardoned, do You also pardon me — the new sin and the old lapses!”

entries.farsi: گفت اكنون اى كنيزك وامگو / اين سخن را كه شنيدم من ز تو
entries.english: He said, “Now, O girl, do not relate this tale which I have heard from you.

entries.farsi: با اميرت جفت خواهم كرد من / ‏اللهَّ اللهَّ زين حكايت دم مزن
entries.english: I will unite you with the Amír: for God’s sake, for God’s sake, do not breathe a word of this story,

entries.farsi: تا نگردد او ز رويم شرمسار / كاو يكى بد كرد و نيكى صد هزار
entries.english: Lest he become ashamed to face me; for he has done one bad deed and a hundred thousand good.

entries.farsi: بارها من امتحانش كرده‏ام / ‏خوب‏تر از تو بدو بسپرده‏ام
entries.english: Many times have I put him to the test: I have entrusted him with fairer than you are.

entries.farsi: در امانت يافتم او را تمام / ‏اين قضايى بود هم از كرده‏هام
entries.english: I found him perfect in fidelity; this too was a judgment resulting from things done by me.”

entries.farsi: پس بخود خواند آن امير خويش را / كشت در خود خشم قهر انديش را
entries.english: Then he summoned his Amír to his presence: he extinguished in himself the wrath that meditates a violent revenge.

entries.farsi: كرد با او يك بهانه‏ى دل پذير / كه شده‏ستم زين كنيزك من نفير
entries.english: He made an agreeable excuse to him, saying, “I have become disinclined to this slave-girl,

entries.farsi: ز آن سبب كز غيرت و رشك كنيز / مادر فرزند دارد صد ازيز
entries.english: Because the mother of my children is terribly agitated by jealousy and envy of the girl.

entries.farsi: مادر فرزند را بس حقهاست / او نه در خورد چنين جور و جفاست
entries.english: The mother of my children has many claims: she does not deserve such injustice and unkindness.

entries.farsi: رشك و غيرت مى‏برد خون مى‏خورد / زين كنيزك سخت تلخى مى‏برد
entries.english: She is nursing envy and jealousy, she is suffering anguish, and she is feeling great bitterness on account of this girl.

entries.farsi: چون كسى را داد خواهم اين كنيز / پس ترا اوليتر است اين اى عزيز
entries.english: Since I wish to give this girl to some person, it is most fitting to you, O dear friend;

entries.farsi: عقد كردش با امير او را سپرد / كرد خشم و حرص را او خرد و مرد
entries.english: For you did hazard your life for the sake of her: it would not be fair to give her to anyone but you.”

entries.farsi: كه تو جان‏بازى نمودى بهر او / خوش نباشد دادن آن جز به تو
entries.english: He gave her in marriage and handed her over to him: he crushed anger and cupidity to atoms.

sections.title_fa: بيان آن كه نَحْ نُ قَسَ مْناكه يكى را شهوت و قوت خران دهد و يكى را كياست و قوت انبيا و فرشتگان دهد ‏سر ز هوا تافتن از سرورى است ترك هوا قوت پيغمبرى است تخمهايى كه شهوتى نبود بر آن جز قيامتى نبود

sections.title_en: Explaining that the words “We have apportioned” mean that He bestows on one the lust and strength of asses and on another the intelligence and strength of the prophets and the angels. “To turn the head away from sensual desire is nobility; to abandon sensual desire is the strength that belongs to prophethood.” “The seeds that are not sown in lust—
their fruit only appears at the Resurrection.”

entries.farsi: گر بدش سستى نرى خران / ‏بود او را مردى پيغمبران
entries.english: If he was deficient in the masculinity of asses, he possessed the manliness of the prophets.

entries.farsi: ترك خشم و شهوت و حرص آورى / ‏هست مردى و رگ پيغمبرى
entries.english: It is manliness and the nature of prophethood to abandon anger and lust and greed.

entries.farsi: مرده‏اى باشم به من حق بنگرد / به از آن زنده كه باشد دور و رد
entries.english: Let the masculinity of the ass be lacking in his nature: God calls him the great Spiritual Sovereign.

entries.farsi: نرى خر گو مباش اندر رگش / ‏حق همى‏خواند الغ بگلر بگش
entries.english: If I am a dead man and God looks on me favourably, my case is better than the living man who is far from God and rejected by Him.

entries.farsi: مغز مردى اين شناس و پوست آن / ‏آن برد دوزخ برد اين در جنان
entries.english: Recognise this to be the kernel of manliness, and that to be the husk: the latter leads to Hell, the former to Paradise.

entries.farsi: حفت الجنة مكاره را رسيد / حفت النار از هوا آمد پديد
entries.english: “Paradise is encompassed with things disliked” has come; “Hell-fire is encompassed with sensual desire” has been declared.

entries.farsi: اى اياز شير نر ديو كش / ‏مردى خر كم فزون مردى هش
entries.english: “O Ayaz, fierce demon-killing lion, the manliness of the ass is inferior, the manliness of Reason superior,

entries.farsi: آن چه چندين صدر ادراكش نكرد / لعب كودك بود پيشت اينت مرد
entries.english: That which so many eminent persons did not apprehend was to your child’s play: lo, here is the man!

entries.farsi: اى بديده لذت امر مرا / جان سپرده بهر امرم در وفا
entries.english: O you who have felt the delight of my command and have loyally devoted your life for the sake of my command,

entries.farsi: داستان ذوق امر و چاشنيش / ‏بشنو اكنون در بيان معنويش
entries.english: Now listen to the tale of the savour and relish of the command in the spiritual exposition thereof.”

sections.title_fa: دادن شاه گوهر را ميان ديوان و مجمع به دست وزير كه اين چند ارزد و مبالغه كردن وزير در قيمت ‏او و فرمودن شاه او را كه اكنون اين را بشكن و گفتن وزير كه اين را چون بشكنم الى آخر القصه
sections.title_en: How the King, in the midst of the Diwan and assembly-place, put a pearl in the hand of the Vizier and asked him what it was worth; and how the Vizier gave an extremely high estimate of its value;
and when the King commanded him to break it, answered, “How should I break it?” and so forth.

entries.farsi: شاه روزى جانب ديوان شتافت / جمله اركان را در آن ديوان بيافت
entries.english: One day the King hastened to the Diwan: in the Diwan he found all the courtiers.

entries.farsi: گوهرى بيرون كشيد او مستنير / پس نهادش زود در كف وزير
entries.english: He produced a radiant pearl and immediately put it in the palm of the Vizier.

entries.farsi: گفت چون است و چه ارزد اين گهر / گفت به ارزد ز صد خروار زر
entries.english: “How about this pearl?” he asked, “and what is it worth?” He replied, “is worth more than a hundred ass-loads of gold.”

entries.farsi: گفت بشكن گفت چونش بشكنم / ‏نيك خواه مخزن و مالت منم
entries.english: He said, “Break it!” “How should I break it?” he replied: “I am a well-wisher to your treasury and riches.

entries.farsi: چون روا دارم كه مثل اين گهر / كه نيايد در بها گردد هدر
entries.english: How should I deem it allowable that a priceless pearl like this should go to waste?”

entries.farsi: گفت شاباش و بدادش خلعتى / ‏گوهر از وى بستد آن شاه و فتى
entries.english: “Well said!” exclaimed the King and presented him with a dress of honour; the generous King took the pearl from him,

entries.farsi: كرد ايثار وزير آن شاه جود / هر لباس و حله كاو پوشيده بود
entries.english: The munificent monarch bestowed on the Vizier every garment and robe that he wore.

entries.farsi: ساعتيشان كرد مشغول سخن / ‏از قضيه‏ى تازه و راز كهن
entries.english: For a while he engaged them in conversation concerning new event and old mystery.

entries.farsi: بعد از آن دادش به دست حاجبى / ‏كه چه ارزد اين به پيش طالبى
entries.english: Afterwards he put it into the hand of a chamber lain, saying, “What is it worth to a would-be purchaser?

entries.farsi: گفت ارزد اين به نيمه‏ى مملكت / ‏كش نگه دارد خدا از مهلكت
entries.english: He replied, “It is worth half a kingdom: may God preserve it from destruction!”

entries.farsi: گفت بشكن گفت اى خورشيد تيغ / ‏بس دريغ است اين شكستن را دريغ
entries.english: “Break it,” said he. “O you whose sword is like the sun he replied, “Alas, it is a great pity to break it.

entries.farsi: قيمتش بگذار بين تاب و لمع / ‏كه شده‏ست اين نور روز او را تبع
entries.english: Let alone its value, mark its splendour and brilliancies: this daylight has become second to it.

entries.farsi: دست كى جنبد مرا در كسر او / كى خزينه‏ى شاه را باشم عدو
entries.english: How should my hand make a movement to break it? How should I be an enemy to the King’s treasure-house?”

entries.farsi: شاه خلعت داد و ادرارش فزود / پس دهان در مدح عقل او گشود
entries.english: The King gave him a robe of honour and increased his stipend, and then opened his mouth in praise of his intelligence;

entries.farsi: بعد يك ساعت به دست مير داد / در را آن امتحان كن باز داد
entries.english: After a short time he who was making the trial again handed the pearl to the Minister of Justice (Mir-i dad).

entries.farsi: او همين گفت و همه ميران همين / ‏هر يكى را خلعتى داد او ثمين
entries.english: He said the same, and all the Amirs said the same: he bestowed a costly robe of honour on every one. .

entries.farsi: جامگيهاشان همى‏افزود شاه / ‏آن خسيسان را به برد از ره به چاه
entries.english: The King was raising their salaries; he brought those base wretches from the Way to the pit.

entries.farsi: اين چنين گفتند پنجه شصت امير / جمله يك يك هم به تقليد وزير
entries.english: All the fifty or sixty Amirs, one by one, spoke like this in imitation of the Vizier.

entries.farsi: گر چه تقليد است استون جهان / ‏هست رسوا هر مقلد ز امتحان
entries.english: Though imitation is the pillar of the (present) world, every imitator is disgraced on being put to the trial.

sections.title_fa: رسيدن گوهر از دست به دست آخر دور به اياز و كياست اياز و مقلد ناشدن ايشان را و مغرور ناشدن او به كال و مال دادن شاه و خلعتها و جامگيها افزون كردن و مدح عقل مخطئان كردن، كه نشايد مقلد را مسلمان داشتن، مسلمان باشد اما نادر باشد كه مقلد ثبات كند بر آن اعتقاد و مقلد از اين امتحانها به سالمت بيرون آيد كه ثبات بينايان ندارد الا من عصمه اللهَّ زيرا حق يكى است و آن را
ضد بسيار غلط افكن و مشابه حق، مقلد چون آن ضد را نشناسد از آن رو حق را نشناخته باشد اما حق با آن ناشناخت او چو او را به عنايت نگاه دارد آن ناشناخت او را زيان ندارد
sections.title_en: How the pearl, from hand to hand, came round at last to Ayaz; and the sagacity of Ayaz, and how he did not act in conformity with them and was not beguiled by the King’s giving them goods and riches and increasing their robes of honour and salaries and praising the intelligence of

those erring men; for one ought not to regard the imitator as a Moslem: he may be a Moslem, but it rarely happens that he holds fast to his faith and comes off safely from the trials — for he lacks the steadfastness of the clairvoyant——except those whom God preserves; because the Truth is one, and its contrary is very deceptive and like unto it. Since the imitator does
not know the contrary, on that account he cannot have known the Truth; but when, notwithstanding his ignorance, God preserves him by favour, that ignorance does him no harm.

entries.farsi: اى اياز اكنون نگويى كاين گهر / چند مى‏ارزد بدين تاب و هنر
entries.english: “Now, O Ayaz, will not you say how much a pearl of this splendour and excellence is worth?”

entries.farsi: گفت افزون ز آنچ تانم گفت من / ‏گفت اكنون زود خردش در شكن
entries.english: He replied, “More than I am able to say.” He said, “Now break it immediately into small fragments.”

entries.farsi: سنگها در آستين بودش شتاب / ‏خرد كردش پيش او بود آن صواب
entries.english: He had stones in his sleeve: he quickly reduced it to dust, that seemed to him the right course.

entries.farsi: يا بخواب اين ديده بود آن پر صفا / كرده بود اندر بغل دو سنگ را
entries.english: Or that entirely sincere man had dreamed of this and put the two stones under his arm,

entries.farsi: هر كه را فتح و ظفر پيغام داد / پيش او يك شد مراد و بى‏مراد
entries.english: Like Joseph to whom at the bottom of the well his ultimate fortunes was revealed by God.

entries.farsi: همچو يوسف كه درون قعر چاه / ‏كشف شد پايان كارش از اله
entries.english: To whomsoever He has announced victory and triumph — to him success and failure are one.

entries.farsi: هر كه پايندان وى شد وصل يار / او چه ترسد از شكست و كارزار
entries.english: To whomsoever the favour of the Friend has become a surety — what fear should he have of defeat and combat

entries.farsi: چون يقين گشتش كه خواهد كرد مات / ‏فوت اسب و پيل هستش ترهات
entries.english: When it has become certain to him that he will checkmate, the loss of his horse and elephant is a trifle to him

entries.farsi: گر برد اسبش هر آن كه اسب جوست / ‏اسب گور و نه كه پيش آهنگ اوست
entries.english: If his horse be taken by anyone who desires to take the horse, let the horse go; is not he, the winner?

entries.farsi: مرد را با اسب كى خويشى بود / عشق اسبش از پى پيشى بود
entries.english: How should there be an affinity between a man and a horse? His love for the horse is for the purpose of getting in front.

entries.farsi: بهر صورتها مكش چندين زحير / بى‏صداع صورتى معنى بگير
entries.english: Do not endure all this anguish for the sake of forms: grasp the reality without headache on account of a form.

entries.farsi: هست زاهد را غم پايان كار / تا چه باشد حال او روز شمار
entries.english: The ascetic feels anxiety concerning his latter end: what will be his plight on the Day of Reckoning;

entries.farsi: عارفان ز آغاز گشته هوشمند / از غم و احوال آخر فارغ‏اند
entries.english: The gnostics, having become conscious of the beginning, are free from anxiety and the ultimate conditions.

entries.farsi: بود عارف را همين خوف و رجا / سابقه دانيش خورد آن هر دو را
entries.english: The gnostic had the same fear and hope as the ascetic, his knowledge of the past devoured both those.

entries.farsi: ديد كاو سابق زراعت كرد ماش / ‏او همى‏داند چه خواهد بود چاش
entries.english: He perceived that in the past he had sown pulse: he knows what the produce will be.

entries.farsi: عارف است و باز رست از خوف و بيم / ‏هاى و هو را كرد تيغ حق دو نيم
entries.english: He is a gnostic and has been delivered from fear and dread: the sword of God has cut lamentation asunder

entries.farsi: بود او را بيم و اوميد از خدا / خوف فانى شد عيان گشت آن رجا
entries.english: Formerly he had from God fear and hope: the fear has passed away and the hope has come into clear view.

entries.farsi: چون شكست او گوهر خاص آن زمان / ‏ز آن اميران خاست صد بانگ و فغان
entries.english: When he broke that choice pearl, thereupon from the Amirs arose a hundred clamours and outcries—

entries.farsi: كاين چه بى‏باكيست و اهلل كافر است / ‏هر كه اين پر نور گوهر را شكست
entries.english: “What recklessness is this? By God, whoever has broken this luminous pearl is an infidel”—

entries.farsi: قيمتى گوهر نتيجه‏ى مهر و ود / بر چنان خاطر چرا پوشيده شد
entries.english: And the whole company in their ignorance and blindness had broken the pearl of the King’s command.

entries.farsi: و آن جماعت جمله از جهل و عما / در شكسته در امر شاه را
entries.english: The precious pearl, the product of love and affection— why was it veiled from hearts like those?

sections.title_fa: تشنيع زدن امرا بر اياز كه چرا شكستش و جواب دادن اياز ايشان را
sections.title_en: How the Amirs reviled Ayaz, saying, “Why did he break it?” and how Ayaz answered them.

entries.farsi: گفت اياز اى مهتران نامور / امر شه بهتر به قيمت يا گهر
entries.english: Ayaz said, “O renowned princes, is the King’s command more precious or the pearl?

entries.farsi: امر سلطان به بود پيش شما / يا كه اين نيكو گهر بهر خدا
entries.english: In your eyes is the command of the sovereign or this goodly pearl superior? For God’s sake!

entries.farsi: اى نظرتان بر گهر بر شاه نه / ‏قبله تان غول است و جاده‏ى راه نه
entries.english: O you whose gaze is upon the pearl, not upon the King, the ghoul is your object of desire, not the highway.

entries.farsi: من ز شه بر مى‏نگردانم نظر / من چو مشرك روى نارم با حجر
entries.english: I will never avert my gaze from the King, I will not turn my face towards a stone, like the polytheist.

entries.farsi: بى‏گهر جانى كه رنگين سنگ را / بر گزيند پس نهد شاه مرا
entries.english: Devoid of the pearl is the soul that prefers a coloured stone and puts my King behind.”

entries.farsi: پشت سوى لعبت گل رنگ كن / ‏عقل در رنگ آورنده دنگ كن
entries.english: Turn your back towards the rose-coloured doll; lose your reason in Him who bestows the colour.

entries.farsi: اندر آ در جو سبو بر سنگ زن / ‏آتش اندر بو و اندر رنگ زن
entries.english: Come into the river, dash the pitcher against the stone, and set fire to scent and colour.

entries.farsi: گر نه‏اى در راه دين از ره زنان / ‏رنگ و بو مپرست مانند زنان
entries.english: If you are not one of the brigands on the Way of the Religion, do not be addicted, like women, to colour and scent.

entries.farsi: سر فرو انداختند آن مهتران / ‏عذر جويان گشته ز آن نسيان به جان
entries.english: Those princes cast down their heads, craving with their soul to be excused for that forgetfulness.

entries.farsi: از دل هر يك دو صد آه آن زمان / ‏همچو دودى مى‏شدى تا آسمان
entries.english: At that moment from the heart of each one two hundred sighs were going, like a smoke, to heaven.

entries.farsi: كرد اشارت شه به جالد كهن / ‏كه ز صدرم اين خسان را دور كن
entries.english: The King made a sign to the ancient executioner, as though to say, “Remove these vile wretches from my seat of honour.

entries.farsi: اين خسان چه اليق صدر من‏اند / كز پى سنگ امر ما را بشكنند
entries.english: How are these vile wretches worthy of my seat of honour, when they break my command for the sake of a stone?

entries.farsi: امر ما پيش چنين اهل فساد / بهر رنگين سنگ شد خوار و كساد
entries.english: For the sake of a coloured stone my command is held contemptible and cheap by evil-doers like these.”

sections.title_fa: ‏قصد شاه به كشتن امرا و شفاعت كردن اياز پيش تخت سلطان كه العفو اولى
sections.title_en: How the King was about to kill the Amirs, and how Ayaz made intercession before the royal throne, saying, “it is better to forgive.”

entries.farsi: پس اياز مهر افزا بر جهيد / پيش تخت آن الغ سلطان دويد
entries.english: Then Ayaz, who was abounding in love, sprang up and ran to the throne of that mighty Sultan.

entries.farsi: سجده‏اى كرد و گلوى خود گرفت / ‏كاى قبادى كز تو چرخ آرد شگفت
entries.english: He made a prostration and spoke with bated breath’, saying, “O Emperor at whom the celestial sphere is astounded,

entries.farsi: اى همايى كه همايان فرخى / ‏از تو دارند و سخاوت هر سخى
entries.english: O Huma from whom humas have auspiciousness, and every generous man generosity,

entries.farsi: اى كريمى كه كرمهاى جهان / ‏محو گردد پيش ايثارت نهان
entries.english: O Noble One before whose self-sacrifice acts of nobility in the world are hidden (eclipsed) and disappear,

entries.farsi: از غفورى تو غفران چشم سير / روبهان بر شير از عفو تو چير
entries.english: O Lovely One whom the red rose beheld and tore its shirt in shame,

entries.farsi: اى لطيفى كه گل سرخت بديد / از خجالت پيرهن را بر دريد
entries.english: Forgiveness is fully content with your forgivingness: because of your pardon the foxes prevail over the lion.

entries.farsi: جز كه عفو تو كه را دارد سند / هر كه با امر تو بى‏باكى كند
entries.english: Whosoever treats your command with insolence, whom should he have to support him except your pardon?

entries.farsi: غفلت و گستاخى اين مجرمان / ‏از وفور عفو تست اى عفو الن
entries.english: The heedlessness and irreverence of these sinners arise from the abundance of your pardon, O mine of pardon.”

entries.farsi: دايما غفلت ز گستاخى دمد / كه برد تعظيم از ديده رمد
entries.english: Heedlessness always grows up from irreverence, for reverence will remove the inflammation from the eye.

entries.farsi: غفلت و نسيان بد آموخته / ‏ز آتش تعظيم گردد سوخته
entries.english: The heedlessness and wicked forgetfulness he has learned will be consumed by the fire of reverence.

entries.farsi: هيبتش بيدارى و فطنت دهد / سهو و نسيان از دلش بيرون جهد
entries.english: Awe will bestow on him wakefulness and keen wittedness: negligence and forgetfulness will leap forth from his heart.

entries.farsi: وقت غارت خواب نايد خلق را / تا بنربايد كسى زو دلق را
entries.english: Folk do not fall asleep at the time of a raid, lest anyone should carry off his cloak.

entries.farsi: خواب چون در مى‏رمد از بيم دلق / ‏خواب نسيان كى بود با بيم حلق
entries.english: Since sleep is banished by fear for one’s cloak, how should the sleep of forgetfulness be with fear for one’s throat?’

entries.farsi: ال تواخذ ان نسينا شد گواه / ‏كه بود نسيان به وجهى هم گناه
entries.english: Do not punish if we forget is evidence that forgetfulness too, in a certain way, is sinful,

entries.farsi: ز انكه استكمال تعظيم او نكرد / ور نه نسيان در نياوردى نبرد
entries.english: Because he did not attain to complete reverence, or else forgetfulness would not have assailed him

entries.farsi: گر چه نسيان ال بد و ناچار بود / در سبب ورزيدن او مختار بود
entries.english: Although forgetfulness was necessary and inevitable, he was a free agent in employing the means;

entries.farsi: كه تهاون كرد در تعظيمها / تا كه نسيان زاد يا سهو و خطا
entries.english: For he showed remissness in his feelings of reverence, so that I forgetfulness was born or negligence and trespass.

entries.farsi: همچو مستى كاو جنايتها كند / گويد او معذور بودم من ز خود
entries.english: Like the drunken man who commits sins and says, “I was excused from myself ”

entries.farsi: گويدش ليكن سبب اى زشت كار / از تو بد در رفتن آن اختيار
entries.english: “But,” says he to him, “the cause, in the loss of that power to choose, proceeded from you, O evil-doer.

entries.farsi: بى‏خودى نامد به خود تش خواندى / ‏اختيارت خود نشد تش راندى
entries.english: Your senselessness did not come of itself, you invited it; your power to choose did not go of itself, you drove it away.

entries.farsi: گر رسيدى مستيى بى‏جهد تو / حفظ كردى ساقى جان عهد تو
entries.english: If intoxication had come without exertion on your part, the spiritual Cup-bearer would have kept your covenant.

entries.farsi: عفوهاى جمله عالم ذره‏اى / ‏عكس عفوت اى ز تو هر بهره‏اى
entries.english: He would have been your backer and intercessor: I am devoted to the sin of him who is intoxicated by God.”

entries.farsi: پشت دارت بودى او و عذر خواه / ‏من غالم زلت مست اله
entries.english: “The forgiveness of the whole world is a mote— the reflection of your forgiveness, O you from whom comes, every fortune.

entries.farsi: عفوها گفته ثناى عفو تو / نيست كفوش أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتقوا
entries.english: Forgiveness sings the praise of your forgiveness: there is no peer to it. O people, beware!

entries.farsi: جانشان بخش و ز خودشان هم مران / ‏كام شيرين تواند اى كامران
entries.english: Grant them their lives, neither banish them from yourself: they are your sweet desire, O you who brings desire to fruition.

entries.farsi: از فراق و هجر مى‏گويى سخن / ‏هر چه خواهى كن و ليكن اين مكن
entries.english: Have mercy on him that beheld your face: how shall he endure I the bitter separation from you?

entries.farsi: رحم كن بر وى كه روى تو بديد / فرقت تلخ تو چون خواهد كشيد
entries.english: You are speaking of separation and banishment: do what I you will but do not this.

entries.farsi: تلخى هجر از ذكور و از اناث / ‏دور دار اى مجرمان را مستغاث
entries.english: A hundred thousand bitter sixtyfold deaths are not comparable to separation from your face.

entries.farsi: صد هزاران مرگ تلخ شصت تو / نيست مانند فراق روى تو
entries.english: Keep the bitterness of banishment aloof from males and females, O you whose help is besought by sinners!

entries.farsi: بر اميد وصل تو مردن خوش است / ‏تلخى هجر تو فوق آتش است
entries.english: It is sweet to die in hope of union with you; the bitterness of banishment from you is worse than fire.”

entries.farsi: كان نظر شيرين كننده‏ى رنجهاست / ‏ساحران را خون بهاى دست و پاست
entries.english: Amidst Hell-fire the infidel is saying, “What pain should I feel if He were to look on me?”

entries.farsi: گبر مى‏گويد ميان آن سقر / چه غمم بودى گرم كردى نظر
entries.english: For that look makes pains sweet: it is the blood-price to the magicians for the amputation of their hands and feet.

sections.title_fa: َتفسير گفتن ساحران فرعون را در وقت سياست كه ال ضَ يْرَ إِنَّا إِلى‏ رَبِّنا مُنْقَلِبُون
sections.title_en: Commentary on the Saying of Pharaoh’s magicians in the hour of their punishment,
“it is no harm, for lo, we shall return unto our Lord.”

entries.farsi: نعره‏ى ال ضَ يْرَ بشنيد آسمان / ‏چرخ گويى شد پى آن صولجان
entries.english: Heaven heard the cry, “it is no harm”: the celestial sphere became a ball for that bat.

entries.farsi: ضربت فرعون ما را نيست ضير / لطف حق غالب بود بر قهر غير
entries.english: “The punishment inflicted by Pharaoh is no harm to us: the grace of God prevails over the violence of others.

entries.farsi: گر بدانى سر ما را اى مضل / ‏مى‏رهانيمان ز رنج اى كوردل
entries.english: If you should know our secret, O misleader, you are delivering us from pain, O man whose heart is blind.

entries.farsi: داد ما را فضل حق فرعونيى / ‏نه چو فرعونيت و ملكت فانيى
entries.english: Listen, come and from this quarter behold this organ pealing ‘Oh, would that my people knew!’

entries.farsi: هين بيا زين سو ببين كاين ارغنون / ‏مى‏زند يا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يعلمون
entries.english: God’s bounty has bestowed Pharaohship on us, not a perishable one like your Pharaohship and kingdom.

entries.farsi: سر بر آر و ملك بين زنده و جليل / ‏اى شده غره به مصر و رود نيل
entries.english: Lift up your head and behold the living and majestic kingdom, O you who have been deluded by Egypt and the river Nile.

entries.farsi: گر تو ترك اين نجس خرقه كنى / ‏نيل را در نيل جان غرقه كنى
entries.english: If you will take leave of this filthy tattered cloak, you will drown the Nile in the Nile of the spirit.

entries.farsi: هين بدار از مصر اى فرعون دست / ‏در ميان مصر جان صد مصر هست
entries.english: Listen, O Pharaoh, hold your hand from Egypt: there are a hundred Egypts within the Egypt of the Spirit.

entries.farsi: تو انا رب همى‏گويى به عام / ‏غافل از ماهيت اين هر دو نام
entries.english: You say to the common, ‘I am a Lord,’ being unaware of the essential natures of both these names.

entries.farsi: رب بر مربوب كى لرزان بود / كى انا دان بند جسم و جان بود
entries.english: How should a Lord be trembling for that which is lorded over? How should one who knows ‘I’ be in bondage to body and soul?

entries.farsi: نك انا ماييم رسته از انا / از اناى پر بالى پر عنا
entries.english: Lo, we are the real ‘I,’ having been freed from the unreal ‘I,’ from the ‘I’ that is full of tribulation and trouble.

entries.farsi: آن انايى بر تو اى سگ شوم بود / در حق ما دولت محتوم بود
entries.english: To you, O cur, that ‘I’-hood was baleful, in regard to us it was irreversibly ordained felicity.

entries.farsi: گر نبوديت اين انايى كينه كش / ‏كى زدى بر ما چنين اقبال خوش
entries.english: Unless you had had this vindictive ‘I’-hood, how should such fortune have bidden us welcome?

entries.farsi: شكر آنك از دار فانى مى‏رهيم / ‏بر سر اين دار پندت مى‏دهيم
entries.english: In thanksgiving for our deliverance from the perishable abode we are admonishing you on this gallows.

entries.farsi: دار قتل ما براق رحلت است / ‏دار ملك تو غرور و غفلت است
entries.english: The gallows (dár) on which we are killed is the Buraq on which we ride; the abode (dár) possessed by you is delusion and heedlessness.

entries.farsi: اين حياتى خفيه در نقش ممات / ‏و آن مماتى خفيه در قشر حيات
entries.english: This is a life concealed in the form of death, while that is a death concealed in the husk of life.

entries.farsi: مى‏نمايد نور نار و نار نور / ور نه دنيا كى بدى دار الغرور
entries.english: Light seems as fire, and fire as light: else, how should this world have been the abode of delusion?”

entries.farsi: هين مكن تعجيل اول نيست شو / چون غروب آرى بر آ از شرق ضو
entries.english: Beware, do not make haste: first become naught, and when you sink rise from the radiant East!

entries.farsi: از انايى ازل دل دنگ شد / اين انايى سرد گشت و ننگ شد
entries.english: The heart was dumbfounded by the eternal “I”-hood: this “I”-hood became insipid and shameful.

entries.farsi: ز آن اناى بى‏انا خوش گشت جان / ‏شد جهان او از انايى جهان
entries.english: The spirit was made glad by that “I”-hood without “I” and sprang away from the “I”-hood of the world.

entries.farsi: از انا چون رست اكنون شد انا / آفرينها بر اناى بى‏عنا
entries.english: Since it has been delivered from “I,” it has now become “I”: blessings on the “I” that is without affliction;

entries.farsi: كاو گريزان و انايى در پى‏اش / ‏مى‏دود چون ديد وى را بى‏وى‏اش
entries.english: For it is fleeing from its unreal “I”-hood, and the real “I”-hood is running after it, since it saw it to be selfless.

entries.farsi: طالب اويى نگردد طالبت / ‏چون بمردى طالبت شد مطلبت
entries.english: If you seek the real “I”-hood, it will not become a seeker of you: when you have died to self will that which you seek become your seeker.

entries.farsi: زنده‏اى كى مرده شو شويد ترا / طالبى كى مطلبت جويد ترا
entries.english: If you are living, how should the corpse-washer wash you? If you are seeking, how should that which you seek go in search of you?

entries.farsi: اندر اين بحث ار خرد ره بين بدى / ‏فخر رازى راز دان دين بدى
entries.english: If the intellect could discern the way in this question, Fakhr-i Razí would be an adept in religious mysteries;

entries.farsi: ليك چون من لم يذق لم يدر بود / عقل و تخييالت او حيرت فزود
entries.english: But since he was whoso has not tasted does not know, his intelligence and imaginations increased his perplexity.

entries.farsi: كى شود كشف از تفكر اين انا / آن انا مكشوف شد بعد از فنا
entries.english: How should this “I” be revealed by thinking? That “I” is revealed after passing away from self (faná).

entries.farsi: مى‏فتد اين عقلها در افتقاد / در مغاكى حلول و اتحاد
entries.english: These intellects in their quest fall into the abyss of incarnation (hulúl) and ittihád.

entries.farsi: اى اياز گشته فانى ز اقتراب / ‏همچو اختر در شعاع آفتاب
entries.english: O Ayaz who have passed away in union like the star in the beams of the sun—

entries.farsi: بلكه چون نطفه مبدل تو بتن / ‏نه از حلول و اتحادى مفتتن
entries.english: Nay, transmuted, like semen, into body— you are not afflicted with hulúl and ittihád.

entries.farsi: عفو كن اى عفو در صندوق تو / سابق لطفى همه مسبوق تو
entries.english: “Forgive, O you in whose coffer Forgiveness is (contained) and by whom all precedents of mercy are preceded.

entries.farsi: من كه باشم كه بگويم عفو كن / ‏اى تو سلطان و خالصه‏ى امر كن
entries.english: Who am I that I should say ‘Forgive,’ O you who are the sovereign and quintessence of the command Be?

entries.farsi: من كه باشم كه بوم من با منت / ‏اى گرفته جمله منها دامنت
entries.english: Who am I that I should exist beside you, O you whose skirt all ‘I’s’ have clutched?

sections.title_fa: مجرم دانستن اياز خود را در اين شفاعت‏گرى و عذر اين جرم خواستن و در آن عذر گويى خود را َّهمجرم دانستن، و اين شكستگى از شناخت عظمت شاه خيزد كه انا أعلمكم باهلل و اخشاكم هلل و قال الل ُتعالى إِنَّما يَخْ شَ ى اللهََّ مِنْ عِ بادِهِ الْعُلَماء

sections.title_en: How Ayaz deemed himself culpable for thus acting as intercessor and begged pardon for this offence and deemed himself culpable for begging pardon; and this self-abasement arises from knowledge of the majesty of the King; for, ‘I know God better than you and fear Him more than you,’ and the High God hath said, ‘None fears God but those of His servants
that are possessed of knowledge.’

entries.farsi: من كى آرم رحم خلم آلود را / ره نمايم حلم علم اندود را
entries.english: How should I bring mercy to you who art moved with anger, and point out the path of clemency to you who art endued with knowledge?

entries.farsi: صد هزاران صفع را ارزانى‏ام / ‏گر زبون صفعها گردانيم
entries.english: If you subject me to the indignity of (receiving) cuffs, I am deserving of a hundred thousand cuffs.

entries.farsi: آن چه معلوم تو نبود چيست آن / ‏و انچه يادت نيست كو اندر جهان
entries.english: What should I say in your presence? Should I give you information or recall to your mind the method of lovingkindness?

entries.farsi: من چه گويم پيشت اعالمت كنم / ‏يا كه وا يادت دهم شرط كرم
entries.english: What is that which is unknown to you? And where in the world is that which you dost not remember?

entries.farsi: اى تو پاك از جهل و علمت پاك از آن / ‏كه فراموشى كند بر وى نهان
entries.english: O you who are free from ignorance and whose knowledge is free from that forgetfulness should cause to be hidden from it,

entries.farsi: هيچ كس را تو كسى انگاشتى / ‏همچو خورشيدش به نور افراشتى
entries.english: You have deemed a nobody to be somebody and have exalted him, like the sun, with light.

entries.farsi: چون كسم كردى اگر البه كنم / ‏مستمع شو البه‏ام را از كرم
entries.english: Since you have made me somebody, graciously listen to my supplication if I supplicate (you);

entries.farsi: ز انكه از نقشم چو بيرون برده‏اى / ‏آن شفاعت هم تو خود را كرده‏اى
entries.english: For, inasmuch as you have transported me from the form, it is you that have made that intercession unto yourself.

entries.farsi: چون ز رخت من تهى گشت اين وطن / ‏تر و خشك خانه نبود آن من
entries.english: Since this home has been emptied of my furniture, nothing great or small in the house belongs to me.

entries.farsi: هم دعا از من روان كردى چو آب / ‏هم ثباتش بخش و دارش مستجاب
entries.english: You have caused the prayer to flow forth from me like water: do you accordingly give it reality and let it be granted.

entries.farsi: هم تو بودى اول آرنده‏ى دعا / هم تو باش آخر اجابت را رجا
entries.english: You were the inspirer of the prayer in the beginning: be you accordingly the hope for its acceptance in the end,

entries.farsi: تا زنم من الف كان شاه جهان / ‏بهر بنده عفو كرد از مجرمان
entries.english: In order that I may boast that the King of the world pardoned the sinners for his slave’s sake.

entries.farsi: درد بودم سر بسر من خود پسند / كرد شاهم داروى هر دردمند
entries.english: I was a pain, entirely self-satisfied: the King made me the remedy for every sufferer from pain.

entries.farsi: دوزخى بودم پر از شور و شرى / ‏كرد دست فضل اويم كوثرى
entries.english: I was a Hell filled with woe and bale: the hand of his grace made me a Kawthar.

entries.farsi: هر كه را سوزيد دوزخ در قود / من برويانم دگر بار از جسد
entries.english: Whomever Hell has consumed in vengeance, I cause him to grow anew from his body.”

entries.farsi: كار كوثر چيست كه هر سوخته / ‏گردد از وى نابت و اندوخته
entries.english: What is the work of Kawthar by which every one that has been burned is made to grow and becomes reintegrated?

entries.farsi: قطره قطره او منادى كرم / ‏كانچه دوزخ سوخت من باز آورم
entries.english: Drop by drop it proclaims its bounty, saying, “I restore that which Hell has consumed.”

entries.farsi: هست دوزخ همچو سرماى خزان / ‏هست كوثر چون بهار اى گلستان
entries.english: Hell is like the cold of autumn; Kawthar is like the spring, O rose-garden.

entries.farsi: هست دوزخ همچو مرگ و خاك گور / هست كوثر بر مثال نفخ صور
entries.english: Hell is like death and the earth of the grave; Kawthar resembles the blast of the trumpet.

entries.farsi: اى ز دوزخ سوخته اجسامتان / ‏سوى كوثر مى‏كشد اكرامتان
entries.english: O you whose bodies are consumed by Hell, the kindness is leading you towards Kawthar.

entries.farsi: ال الن اربح عليهم جود تست / ‏كه شود زو جمله ناقصها درست
entries.english: Since Your mercy, O Self-subsistent Living One, said, “I created the creatures that they might profit by Me,”

entries.farsi: چون خلقت الخلق كى يربح على / ‏لطف تو فرمود اى قيوم حى
entries.english: “Not that I might profit by them” is Your munificence, by which all defective things are made whole,

entries.farsi: عفو كن زين بندگان تن پرست / ‏عفو از درياى عفو اوليتر است
entries.english: Pardon these body-worshipping slaves: pardon from the ocean of pardon is more worthy.

entries.farsi: عفو خلقان همچو جو و همچو سيل / هم بدان درياى خود تازند خيل
entries.english: Creaturely pardon is like a river and like a torrent: the troop run towards their ocean.

entries.farsi: بازشان وقت سحر پران كنى / ‏تا به شب محبوس اين ابدان كنى
entries.english: Every night from these individual hearts the pardons come to You, O King, like pigeons.

entries.farsi: عفوها هر شب از اين دل پاره‏ها / چون كبوتر سوى تو آيد شها
entries.english: At the hour of dawn You cause them to fly away again, and imprison them in these bodies till nightfall.

entries.farsi: پر زنان بار دگر در وقت شام / ‏مى‏پرند از عشق آن ايوان و بام
entries.english: Once more, at eventide, flapping their wings they fly off in passionate longing for that palace and roof.

entries.farsi: تا كه از تن تار وصلت بگسلند / پيش تو آيند كز تو مقبلند
entries.english: In order that they may snap the thread that unites them with the body, they come before You, for by You they are endowed with fortune—

entries.farsi: بانگ مى‏آيد تَعالَوْا ز آن كرم / ‏بعد از آن رجعت نماند آن حرص و غم
entries.english: Flapping their wings, secure from falling back headlong, in the air and saying, “Truly unto Him we are returning.”

entries.farsi: پر زنان ايمن ز رجع سر نگون / ‏در هوا كه إِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجعون
entries.english: From that Bounty comes the call, “Come! After that returning desire and anxiety are no more.

entries.farsi: بس غريبيها كشيديد از جهان / ‏قدر من دانسته باشيد اى مهان
entries.english: As exiles in the world you suffered many indignities: you will have learned to value Me, O nobles.

entries.farsi: زير سايه‏ى اين درختم مست ناز / هين بيندازيد پاها را دراز
entries.english: Listen now; stretch your legs beneath the shade of this tree of Mine in the intoxication of delight,

entries.farsi: پايهاى پر عنا از راه دين / ‏بر كنار و دست حوران خالدين
entries.english: Stretch your legs, which are fatigued by the Way of Religion, resting for ever on the bosoms and hands of the houris,

entries.farsi: حوريان گشته مغمز مهربان / ‏كز سفر باز آمدند اين صوفيان
entries.english: The houris amorously and fondly say, ‘These Sufis have returned from their travels.

entries.farsi: صوفيان صافيان چون نور خور / مدتى افتاده بر خاك و قذر
entries.english: The Sufis pure as the light of the sun, who for a long time had fallen into earth and filth,

entries.farsi: بى‏اثر پاك از قذر باز آمدند / همچو نور خور سوى قرص بلند
entries.english: Have come back stainless and undefiled, as the sunlight to the lofty orb.’”

entries.farsi: اين گروه مجرمان هم اى مجيد / جمله سرهاشان به ديوارى رسيد
entries.english: “This company of sinners likewise, O glorious— all their heads have come against a wall.

entries.farsi: بر خطا و جرم خود واقف شدند / گر چه مات كعبتين شه بدند
entries.english: They have become aware of their fault and sin, although they were defeated by the King’s two dice.

entries.farsi: رو به تو كردند اكنون اه كنان / ‏اى كه لطفت مجرمان را ره كنان
entries.english: Now they turn their faces towards you, uttering cries of lamentation. O you whose clemency is making way for sinners,

entries.farsi: راه ده آلودگان را العجل / ‏در فرات عفو و عين مغتسل
entries.english: Speedily grant the defiled ones admission into the Euphrates of pardon and the fountain a washing-place,

entries.farsi: تا كه غسل آرند ز آن جرم دراز / در صف پاكان روند اندر نماز
entries.english: That they may wash themselves clean of that prolonged sin and join in prayer among the ranks of the purified—

entries.farsi: اندر آن صفها از اندازه برون / ‏غرقگان نور نحن الصافون
entries.english: Among those innumerable ranks plunged in the light of ‘We are they that stand in line.’”

entries.farsi: چون سخن در وصف اين حالت رسيد / هم قلم بشكست و هم كاغذ دريد
entries.english: When the discourse reached the description of this state, at once the pen broke and the paper tore.

entries.farsi: بحر را پيمود هيچ اسكره‏اى / ‏شير را برداشت هرگز بره‏اى
entries.english: Did any saucer measure the sea? Did a lamb ever carry off a lion?

entries.farsi: گر حجاب استت برون رو ز احتجاب / ‏تا ببينى پادشاهى عجاب
entries.english: If you are veiled, cease being veiled, that you may behold the marvellous sovereignty (of God).

entries.farsi: گر چه بشكستند جامت قوم مست / ‏آن كه مست از تو بود عذريش هست
entries.english: Although the drunken fellows broke Your cup, there is an excuse for him that is intoxicated by You.

entries.farsi: مستى ايشان به اقبال و به مال / ‏نه ز باده‏ى تست اى شيرين فعال
entries.english: Is not their intoxication with fortune and riches of Your wine, O You whose actions are sweet?

entries.farsi: اى شهنشه مست تخصيص تواند / عفو كن از مست خود اى عفومند
