entries.english: O Emperor, they are intoxicated with Your election: pardon him that is intoxicated with You, O Pardoner!

entries.farsi: لذت تخصيص تو وقت خطاب / ‏آن كند كه نايد از صد خم شراب
entries.english: The delight of being elected by You at the moment of Your addressing them has an effect that is not produced by a hundred jars of wine.

entries.farsi: چون كه مستم كرده‏اى حدم مزن / ‏شرع مستان را نبيند حد زدن
entries.english: Since You have intoxicated me, do not inflict a penalty: the Law does not see fit to inflict a penalty on the intoxicated.

entries.farsi: چون شوم هشيار آن گاهم بزن / ‏كه نخواهم گشت خود هشيار من
entries.english: Inflict it at the time when I become sober; for indeed I shall never become sober.

entries.farsi: هر كه از جام تو خورد اى ذو المنن / ‏تا ابد رست از هش و از حد زدن
entries.english: Whoever has drunk of Your cup, O Gracious One, is forever delivered from self consciousness and from the infliction of penalties.

entries.farsi: خالدين فى فناء سكرهم / ‏من تفانى فى هواكم لم يقم
entries.english: Their intoxication consists in a state of unconsciousness of self (faná), abiding for ever: he that passes away from self in love for You will not arise.

entries.farsi: فضل تو گويد دل ما را كه رو / اى شده در دوغ عشق ما گرو
entries.english: Your grace says to our heart, “Go, O you who have become in pawn to the buttermilk of My love.

entries.farsi: چون مگس در دوغ ما افتاده‏اى / ‏تو نه‏اى مست اى مگس تو باده‏اى
entries.english: You have fallen, like a gnat, into My buttermilk: O gnat, you are not intoxicated, you are the wine.

entries.farsi: كركسان مست از تو گردند اى مگس / ‏چون كه بر بحر عسل رانى فرس
entries.english: O gnat, the vultures become intoxicated by you, when you ride on the ocean of honey.

entries.farsi: كوه‏ها چون ذره‏ها سر مست تو / نقطه و پرگار و خط در دست تو
entries.english: The mountains are tipsy with you like motes; the point and the compass and the line are in your hand.

entries.farsi: فتنه كه لرزند از او لرزان تست / ‏هر گران قيمت گهر ارزان تست
entries.english: The torment at which they tremble is trembling at you: every costly pearl is cheap to you.”

entries.farsi: گر خدا دادى مرا پانصد دهان / ‏گفتمى شرح تو اى جان و جهان
entries.english: If God gave me five hundred mouths, I would sing in description of you, O soul and world;

entries.farsi: يك دهان دارم من آن هم منكسر / در خجالت از تو اى داناى سر
entries.english: I have one mouth, and even that one is crushed with shame before you, O knower of the mystery.

entries.farsi: صد هزار آثار غيبى منتظر / كز عدم بيرون جهد با لطف و بر
entries.english: In truth I am not more crushed than non-existence, from the mouth of which these peoples have come.

entries.farsi: منكسرتر خود نباشم از عدم / ‏كز دهانش آمده‏ستند اين امم
entries.english: A hundred thousand impressions of the Unseen World are waiting to spring forth graciously and kindly from non-existence.

entries.farsi: از تقاضاى تو مى‏گردد سرم / ‏اى بمرده من بپيش آن كرم
entries.english: Because of your urgency my head is reeling: oh, I am dead in the presence of that bounty.

entries.farsi: رغبت ما از تقاضاى تو است / ‏جذبه‏ى حق است هر جا رهرو است
entries.english: Our desire arises from your urgency: wherever there is a wayfarer, it is the pull of God.

entries.farsi: آب حيوان قبله‏ى جان دوستان / ‏ز آب باشد سبز و خندان بوستان
entries.english: Does the dust leap upward without a wind? Does a ship voyage without the sea?

entries.farsi: خاك بى‏بادى به بالا بر جهد / كشتى‏يى بحر پا در ره نهد
entries.english: None died in the presence of the Water of Life: compared with your water the Water of Life is dregs.

entries.farsi: پيش آب زندگانى كس نمرد / پيش آبت آب حيوان است درد
entries.english: The Water of Life is the goal of those to whom life is dear: by water the garden is green and smiling.

entries.farsi: مرگ آشامان ز عشقش زنده‏اند / دل ز جان و آب جان بر كنده‏اند
entries.english: Those who quaff the cup of death are living through His love: they have torn their hearts away from life and the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: آب عشق تو چو ما را دست داد / آب حيوان شد به پيش ما كساد
entries.english: When the water of Your love gave us its hand, the Water of Life became worthless in our sight.

entries.farsi: ز آب حيوان هست هر جان را نوى / ليك آب آب حيوانى توى‏
entries.english: Every soul derives freshness from the Water of Life, but You are the Water of the Water of Life.

entries.farsi: هر دمى مرگى و حشرى دادى‏ام / تا بديدم دست برد آن كرم‏
entries.english: You did bestow on me a death and a resurrection continually, that I might experience the conquering power of that bounty.

entries.farsi: همچو خفتن گشت اين مردن مرا / ز اعتماد بعث كردن اى خدا
entries.english: This dying became to me as sleeping, from my confidence that You, O God, wouldst raise me from the dead.

entries.farsi: هفت دريا هر دم ار گردد سراب / گوش گيرى آوريش اى آب آب‏
entries.english: If the Seven Seas become a mirage at every moment, You will take it by the ear and bring it, O Water of the water.

entries.farsi: عقل لرزان از اجل و آن عشق شوخ / سنگ كى ترسد ز باران چون كلوخ‏
entries.english: Reason is trembling with fear of death, but Love is bold: how should the stone be afraid of rain as the clod?

entries.farsi: از صحاف مثنوى اين پنجمست / در بروج چرخ جان چون اجنمست‏
entries.english: This is the Fifth of the Scrolls of the Masnavi: it is like the stars in the zodiacal signs of the spiritual sky.

entries.farsi: هر دمى مرگى و حشرى دادى‏ام / تا بديدم دست برد آن كرم‏
entries.english: Not every sense can find its way by the star: except the mariner acquainted with the star.

entries.farsi: ره نيابد از ستاره هر حواس / جز كه كشتيبان استاره شناس‏
entries.english: The lot of the others is naught but looking: they are ignorant of its auspiciousness and conjunction.

entries.farsi: آشنايى گير شبها تا به روز / با چنني استاره‏هاى ديو سوز
entries.english: During the nights till daybreak make yourself familiar with devil-burning stars like these,

entries.farsi: هر يكى در دفع ديو بد گمان / هست نفط انداز قلعه‏ى آسمان‏
entries.english: Everyone is hurling naphtha from the fortress of Heaven to drive away the evil-thinking devil.

entries.farsi: اختران با ديو همچون عقرب است / مشترى را او ولى الاقرب است‏
entries.english: To the devil the stars are as a scorpion, to the buyer it is the next of kin.

entries.farsi: قوس اگر از تير دوزد ديو را / دلو پر آب است زرع و ميو را
entries.english: If the Bow pierces the devil with an arrow, the Bucket is full of water for the crops and fruit.

entries.farsi: حوت اگر چه كشتى غى بشكند / دوست را چون ثور كشتى مى‏كند
entries.english: Though the Fish wreck the ship of error, for the friend it is sowing like the Bull.

entries.farsi: شمس اگر شب را بدرد چون اسد / لعل را زو خلعت اطلس رسد
entries.english: If the Sun rends Night to pieces, like a lion, there comes from it a satin robe of honour for the ruby.

entries.farsi: هر وجودى كز عدم بنمود سر / بر يكى زهر است و بر ديگر شكر
entries.english: Every existence that emerged from non-existence is poison to one and sugar to another.

entries.farsi: دوست شو وز خوى ناخوش شو برى / تا ز خمره‏ى زهر هم شكر خورى‏
entries.english: Be a friend and become quit of sour qualities, so that you may eat sugar even from a jar of poison.
A poison did no harm to Faruq because to him the antidote, discrimination (faruqí), was sweet as candy.
